An amplifier is integrated at the output, producing sensing signals of approximately 0.88 V for silicone oil and 24.27 V for deionized water; the maximum output variance exceeds 23 V, outperforming all previously reported work. This design offers a new approach for precise droplet localization in active-matrix digital microfluidics.

title:
Influences of Voltage Cycling on Variations in HfO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -based 3D Trench FeRAM
authors: Weiwen Pan, Fei Yu, Peiyuan Du, Wanmei Zhang, Yutai Man, Xingcheng Jin, Chongyong Guo, Hongbo Li, Huan Liu, Bing Chen, Ran Cheng, Yan Liu, Xiao Yu, Genquan Han
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, we experimentally investigated the variation behavior of HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based 3D trench ferroelectric random access memory (FeRAM) under different voltage cycling conditions. The remanent polarization (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf>) and coercive field (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>) were extracted to evaluate device-to-device (D2D) and cycle-to-cycle (C2C) variations. The planar-dominated capacitors showed a gradual decrease in variation when the cycling voltage and number increased. In contrast, the trench-dominated capacitors showed irregular changes during cycling. To understand the cause, cross-sectional transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and energy-dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) analyses were performed. The results revealed that the ferroelectric layer at the trench sidewall was thinner and suffered from strong interdiffusion between the electrode and ferroelectric materials. These structural and compositional differences degrade the ferroelectric quality and lead to larger variation in trench-dominated devices. This work clarifies the relationship between cycling behavior and variability in 3D FeRAMs and provides guidance for improving their reliability through interface and material engineering.

title: Plant Leaf Disease Detection Using Deep Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Jose P, Vonesh Kumar K, Chittibabu Y
abstract: Plant diseases lead to major losses in crop yield and quality, posing a serious threat to global food security. This study presents a smart and clear plant disease detection framework that combines Deep Learning with Explainable Artificial Intelligence. We use CNN architectures like VGG16, ResNet50, and EfficientNet for automatic feature extraction and classification, reaching over 95 percentage accuracy on the PlantVillage dataset. To improve transparency, Grad-CAM and LIME create visual explanations that highlight areas affected by disease. This approach blends high accuracy with interpretability, offering a reliable tool for early disease diagnosis and sustainable farming.

title: Manufacturing-Oriented Dual Neural Surrogate for Trench MOSFET Fabrication Process and Device Optimization
authors: Mingqiang Geng, Jianming Guo, Yuting Sun, Yixian Song, Dawei Gao, Dong Ni
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents a dual-recurrent neural network (RNN) surrogate model for end-to-end semiconductor manufacturing process optimization targeting 150 nm gate-length N-type trench MOSFETs. The forward RNN accurately predicts device electrical characteristics with 98% accuracy, while the inverse RNN infers critical process parameters with 95% precision, effectively avoiding the local-optima issues of conventional multi-objective optimization. Validated against simulated and experimental data of a 150 nm trench MOSFET, our approach establishes a robust inverse-design methodology for global optimization of multi-stage fabrication parameters. This data-driven framework enables precise process control and improved device yield, highlighting its significance for advanced manufacturing.

title: Analysis of the Impact of Service Quality Factors on Customer Satisfaction: A Study of Sri Lankan Freight Forwarding Selection
authors: Thanusha Withana, Chathumi Kavirathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study aims to analyze the impact of service quality factors on customer satisfaction in the selection of sea and air freight forwarders in Sri Lanka, a sector that plays a vital role in supporting the country's import and export performance. The research addresses the problem of fragmented service quality criteria and the absence of a consolidated, context specific framework for evaluating freight forwarders in the Sri Lankan logistics industry. A mixed method research design was adopted, employing a systematic literature review guided by the PRISMA methodology to synthesize evidence from fifty two peer reviewed studies. The reviewed literature was analyzed across three dimensions chronological trends, regional focus, and industry specific applications. The study identifies several critical service quality factors influencing customer satisfaction, including cost effectiveness, reliability, responsiveness, tangibility, timeliness, and operational efficiency. The findings reveal that cost and operational efficiency remain the most influential determinants of customer satisfaction in freight forwarder selection, while technological capabilities and sustainability related practices are gaining increasing importance. The study concludes that enhancing service quality performance across these key dimensions can improve customer satisfaction and operational efficiency competitiveness of Sri Lankan freight forwarders, offering practical implications for industry stakeholders and future research directions.

title: Dual-Loss Trained Compact Cascaded ANN for Custom-Range BSIM-CMG Parameter Extraction
authors: Sidhant V Vazarkar, Abdullah M. H Cachhi, Shreyan Ghosh, Girish Pahwa
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents a novel deep learning framework that combines dual-loss training with floating normalization for BSIM-CMG model parameter extraction. The framework addresses the fundamental challenge of non-unique parameter-to-characteristic mappings through a cascaded forward-inverse neural network architecture trained with both parameter supervision and curve reconstruction constraints. A flexible floating normalization scheme enables user-defined extraction ranges, allowing multiple valid parameter sets to be extracted from identical I-V characteristics. The dual-loss training mechanism provides superior convergence by simultaneously enforcing parameter accuracy and forward-inverse consistency. Validated with 200 K Monte Carlo samples across 3 nm, 7 nm, and 14 nm FinFET technology nodes, the framework achieves RMSE below 3.75% while offering unprecedented flexibility compared to conventional fixed-range extraction methods.

title: Dynamic Response Characterisation of PDMS-MWCNTs Piezoresistive Sponge for Human Motion Monitoring
authors: Abhinav Sharma, Shivanku Chauhan, Shubham Kumar Mishra, Sabyasachi Talukdar, Mohd. Zahid Ansari
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Flexible sensors have gained considerable attention in recent years for human motion monitoring. Particularly, sponge based, porous piezoresistive flexible pressure sensors are particularly promising. The flexible pressure sensor applications in human motion monitoring require the sensor to be tested against the dynamic response. In this paper, we prepared a flexible pressure sensor based on Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) with multi walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) together. The sensor was tested in real time to measure its sensitivity and conducted a dynamic response analysis. The sensitivity analysis showed that at low pressure values, the sensor shows a linear response with a sensitivity value to be 3.03% kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> in the range of 0-20 kPa. Additionally, to mimic the dynamic response, we tested the sensor against dynamic loading conditions, along with repeated cycles at fixed pressure values. The sensor response showed the clear differentiation between applied pressure values as well as stable performance over numerous cycles.

title: LandChain: A Blockchain-Powered Land Marketplace Using Hyperledger Fabric and IPFS
authors: Israt Jahan Anika, Masnin Juairia, Md. Sadique Mahmud, Md. Aminur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Property transactions in the majority of jurisdictions remain reliant on scattered records, hand-verified and paper files that are slow to validate and easy to dispute. Users cannot easily check title, boundaries, or counterparty authenticity; agencies have duplicate files and conflicting identities and third-party middlemen introduce expense and complexity. We present LandChain, a Blockchain-Powered Land Marketplace which makes each step of a property transaction verifiable while preserving current offline legal processes. Users are authenticated with an X. 509 certificate issued by a Fabric CA which is linked with their national ID; the permissioned ledger stores immutable ownership and event history and government-approved files are anchored in IPFS with hashes secured on-chain. From the moment when a user first transacts on the land's advertisement web page to when the owner-name is finally updated, the system includes: identity verification, seller-buyer direct communication, offline deed signing, auto-authentication and user profile updates in ledger. LandChain reduces intermediaries and builds trust through open provenance. The result is a secure, auditable and human-centered way of land transfer.

title: Enhancing Learning Path Recommendation via Multi-task Learning
authors: Afsana Nasrin, Lijun Qian, Pamela Obiomon, Xishuang Dong
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: Personalized learning is a student-centered educational approach that adapts content, pace, and assessment to meet each learner's unique needs. As the key technique to implement the personalized learning, learning path recommendation sequentially recommends personalized learning items such as lectures and exercises. Advances in deep learning, particularly deep reinforcement learning, have made modeling such recommendations more practical and effective. This paper proposes a multi-task LSTM model that enhances learning path recommendation by leveraging shared information across tasks. The approach reframes learning path recommendation as a sequence-to-sequence (Seq2Seq) prediction problem, generating personalized learning paths from a learner's historical interactions. The model uses a shared LSTM layer to capture common features for both learning path recommendation and deep knowledge tracing, along with task-specific LSTM layers for each objective. To avoid redundant recommendations, a nonrepeat loss penalizes repeated items within the recommended learning path. Experiments on the ASSIST09 dataset show that the proposed model significantly outperforms baseline methods for the learning path recommendation.

title: SALG-QCNN-SER: A Noise-Robust Quantum Convolutional Neural Network with Enhanced Cross-Domain Generalization for Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Md. Rifat Hossen, Md. Sarwar Hosain, Akif Mahdi, Tarun Debnath, Md. Najmul Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Emotion recognition in speech is essential for human-computer interaction. This study introduces a refined hybrid Quantum Convolutional Neural Network (QCNN) framework designed to substantially enhance Speech Emotion Recognition (SER). It addresses major challenges such as speaker variability, noise robustness, and cross-domain applicability that often limit classical deep learning models. By Utilizing quantum principles like superposition and entanglement within a 6-qubit, 8-layer parameterized circuit, the model derives highdimensional, noise-resistant features from speech signals. The method includes sophisticated preprocessing steps, such as bandpass filtering and energy-based Voice Activity Detection (VAD), followed by Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) extraction. Evaluated on a diverse dataset of 4,515 samples across eight emotions (Angry, Calm, Disgust, Fear, Happy, Neutral, Sad, Surprised), the QCNN achieved an accuracy of 86 %, setting a new benchmark. It surpasses classical approaches such as a CNN with attention <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(77 \%)$</tex>, a traditional QCNN <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(77.87 \%)$</tex>, and a hybrid quantum-classical network <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(76 \%)$</tex>. The model demonstrated balanced performance, with precision scores ranging from 0.79 to 1.0 (including a perfect 1.0 for Fear) and recall scores from 0.82 to 0.94. These results underscore the promise of quantum-enhanced neural networks in capturing complex, nonlinear speech emotion patterns and establishing a new standard for robust, generalizable SER systems.

title: VL-BrainNet: A VGG16–BiLSTM-Based Framework for Accelerated Brain Tumor Diagnosis
authors: S M Masfequier Rahman Swapno, Md Basitur Rahman Bappi, A B M Shawkat Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deep Learning (DL)-based automated systems have emerged as powerful tools for medical image analysis. However, existing models often face limitations when applied to brain tumor classification because the irregular organ shapes, varying sizes, overlapping regions, and low-contrast boundaries significantly affect the performance and generalizability of current classification techniques. To address this challenge, we propose VL-BrainNet, an advanced VGG16 and BiLSTM-based classification network for brain tumor. Here, we introduce an advanced architecture that combines VGG16 for robust spatial feature extraction with BiLSTM for bidirectional context modelling (e.g., across slices/feature sequences), yielding richer, more separable representations and stronger generalization. Using the Kaggle dataset (7,023 images), VL-BrainNet is trained, validated, and tested <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{8 0 \% - 1 0 \% - 1 0 \%}$</tex> split) to classify the four major brain tumors (glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and normal), achieving substantially higher training performance of 99.93% and validation accuracy of 98.67%. Moreover, VL-BrainNet achieved a test accuracy of 98.19% in 5-fold cross-validation. The model also outperformed state-of-the-art methods in terms of higher precision, recall, and F1 scores, reflecting its robust classification quality. Thus, VL-BrainNet has substantial practical implications for the reliable delineation of brain organs in clinical workflows (e.g., radiotherapy planning), thereby contributing to improved healthcare outcomes in the management of brain tumor detection.

title: Zero Trust-Based Cyber-Physical Security for Financial Fraud Detection in Engineering Projects
authors: Rakesh Ranjan Swain, Joshila Grace, Manisha Chandna, Divya Sharma, Nivedan Mahato, Kuthalingam Venkadeshwaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: As banks come up with new ways to digitize their systems, the implementation of new cybersecurity measures becomes critical. ZTA’s implementation would ensuring there is real-time verification of access requests to inter-connected systems, thus minimizing exposure to the cyber threats. Most contemporary fraud detection systems still utilize manually configured, static, centralized basis, which does not adapt to changing attack scenarios, and are inefficient in rule outputs. Most of all, such mechanisms are privacy invasive. AAD-FRL is the new cyber security fraud detection framework proposed in this thesis. It integrates ZTA and distributed fraud detection poly systems and principles of ZTA to optimally detect and prevent fraud. Using federated learning and encapsulated with reinforcement learning, AAD-FRL can conduct real-time, and lac data fraud detection and prevention across different financial systems without compromising user privacy. AADFRL, when operating within a ZTA paradigm, amplifies the Zero Trust principles of the banking security, strengthens proactive, real-time fraud detection and prevention systems, increases anomaly detection, and misclassification detection, and reframes the security architecture of the systems. The proposed AAD-FRL method accomplished a detection accuracy of 95%, a false positive rate of 3%, a false negative rate of 4%, and an average response time of 70 milliseconds, reflecting excellent real-time performance in fraud detection.

title: An Integrated Approach to Forecast Bitcoin Price Incorporating Economic Factors, Strategic Commodities, and X Tweets
authors: Swattic Ghose, Rubaiyet Hossain Jawwad, Nafiz Imtiaz Rafin, Yamin Bin Yahiya, Faiyaz Bin Khaled, Md. Golam Rabiul Alam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Bitcoin is known for its volatility. So, we conducted a diverse market analysis to construct a conclusive market feature dataset and a hybrid model architecture to find better insight and make better forecasts. One key factor that other research overlooked was the market impact of AMD and NVIDIA. These two most prominent tech companies provide consumerlevel GPUs mostly used for cryptocurrency mining. Focusing on these gaps, we conducted an extensive study of the potential markets and their impact on our model training. We provided an extensive data analysis of the impact of crude oil, gold, AMD, NVIDIA, S&P500, NASDAQ, and sentiment analysis of 85 million tweets altogether on Bitcoin price. The volume of tweets that we collected through web scraping is substantial to the other studies. From our comparative analysis, we deduced that A bidirectional LSTM works best for predicting the Bitcoin trend compared to other deep learning and time series models. Thus, we chose Bidirectional LSTM as our base model and used it to build a hybrid architecture ensemble model with Random Forest Regressor. For the proposed model, we have deployed four simultaneous Bayesian Optimized Bidirectional LSTM models, each with its distinct input features, and trained the Random Forest Regressor model using the predictions from those four models. The trained Random Forest model was used to select the best forecast we obtained from the four Bi-LSTM models. Our findings indicate that Bidirectional LSTM predicts more accurately when sentiment analysis and other macroeconomic aspects are incorporated.

title: An Explainable Ensemble Learning Framework for Crop Classification with Optimized Feature Pyramids and Deep Networks
authors: Syed Rayhan Masud, SK Muktadir Hossain, Md. Ridoy Sarkar, Mohammad Sakib Mahmood, Md. Kishor Morol, Rakib Hossain Sajib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Agriculture is increasingly challenged by climate change, soil degradation, and resource depletion, and hence requires advanced data-driven crop classification and recommendation solutions. This work presents an explainable ensemble learning paradigm that fuses optimized feature pyramids, deep networks, self-attention mechanisms, and residual networks for bolstering crop suitability predictions based on soil characteristics (e.g., pH, nitrogen, potassium) and climatic conditions (e.g., temperature, rainfall). With a dataset comprising 3,867 instances and 29 features from the Ethiopian Agricultural Transformation Agency and NASA, the paradigm leverages preprocessing methods such as label encoding, outlier removal using IQR, normalization through StandardScaler, and SMOTE for balancing classes. A range of machine learning models such as Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Support Vector Machines, Decision Trees, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and a new Relative Error Support Vector Machine are compared, with hyperparameter tuning through Grid Search and cross-validation. The suggested “Final Ensemble” meta-ensemble design outperforms with 98.80% accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, compared to individual models such as K-Nearest Neighbors (95.56% accuracy). Explainable AI methods, such as SHAP and permutation importance, offer actionable insights, highlighting critical features such as soil pH, nitrogen, and zinc. The paradigm addresses the gap between intricate ML models and actionable agricultural decision-making, fostering sustainability and trust in AI-powered recommendations

title: Bioinspired Fractal Nanoneedle Arrays for Flexible and Controlled Drug Delivery
authors: Ananya Bhattacharjee, Ratul K Baruah
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study advances transdermal drug delivery by optimizing hollow micro/nanoneedles (MN/NN) as a minimally invasive alternative to conventional methods. Structural strength analysis using COMSOL FEA examines variations in base design, tip diameter, and length. A spiderweb-inspired silicon hexagonal array coated with PMMA shows linear strength gains under 1-5 kPa loads, with results up to third-order arrays confirming the feasibility of higher-order designs for improved performance. Fractal, web-based nanoneedles, when arranged in robust, scalable hexagonal arrays, enhance mechanical strength and drug delivery efficiency for use in flexible electronics.

title: DenseNet201-Based Deep Learning Approach for Automated Classification of Peanut Plant Leaf Diseases
authors: Boyapati Bharathi, Premkumar Dhanasekar, S. Kaliappan, Avani Mittal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In this work, an auto system for classification of peanut plant leaf disease based on the DenseNet201 deep learning model is introduced. The research utilizes a large dataset of 822 images, divided into three equally distributed classes: Normal Leaf, Diseased Leaf, and Dead Leaf. By using transfer learning, designing dense layers specific to the problem, and applying careful dropout, the new model performs optimally. The dataset was then pre-processed in a systematic way; resized to 224x224 pixels, normalized, and augmented to make the model more robust. Training for 15 epochs with dynamic learning rate management achieved a very good accuracy of 92%. It achieved high precision and recall scores on all higher classes, with scores above 0.90. Specifically, the system reported ideal classification for Normal Leaf samples and robust accuracy in classifying Dead and Diseased classes. Confusion matrix analysis results reported negligible misclassification patterns, such as between the Dead and Diseased classes. This study provides a trustworthy automated disease detection tool for peanut farming that can potentially increase the efficiency of crop monitoring and, hence, aid in timely intervention strategies.

title: A Compact Ultra-Wideband MIMO Antenna for Mmwave Applications
authors: Zijian Dong, Zhenzhen Jiang, Mark Leach, Jiafeng Zhou
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents an ultrawideband (UWB) millimetre-wave (mmWave) multiple-input, multiple-output (MIMO) antenna employing a defected ground structure (DGS) covering the 5G NR bands n257, n258, n259, n260, and n262. The proposed design has a compact volume of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28 \times 26 \times 0.832 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex> and achieves an operating bandwidth from 23.71 to 51.99 GHz, corresponding to a 74.71% fractional bandwidth. The MIMO configuration exhibits strong diversity performance; an envelope correlation coefficient below 0.06 and diversity gain above 9.97 dB. The antenna achieves radiation and total efficiencies exceeding 90% and 80%, respectively, and provides a maximum gain of 6.45 dBi. The measured and simulated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex>-parameter results are in good agreement, showing the proposed design can be used for 5G mm wave applications.

title: DREAM: Drain Stress-Induced Reliability Enhancement Method for IGZO-FETs and Performance Improvement of IGZO-based M3D System from 77K to 373K
authors: Zhidong Tang, Yanbo Su, Jianshi Tang, Kexin Wang, Zhixing Jiang, Yuyao Lu, Yijia Fan, Yibei Zhang, Yue Xi, Dong Wu, Bin Gao, He Qian, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: For the first time, we have developed a Drain stress-induced Reliability EnhAncement Method (DREAM) for IGZO-FETs, demonstrating significantly improved positive bias temperature instability (PBTI) reliability across a wide temperature range from 77 K to 373 K. Specifically, the ΔV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> of IGZO-FETs is reduced from 210 mV to 40 mV in PBTI measurements respectively (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ox</inf>=3 MV/cm for t<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">stress</inf> =10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> s at T=300 K). By applying DREAM to an IGZO-based monolithic 3D (M3D) system design, the performance and reliability is enhanced, where the typical neural network classification accuracy can be improved by over 10%.

title: Leveraging AI in Library Science: Enhancing Access to Political Science Literature and Resources
authors: Mahmoud Khalifa, Ammar A. Jalamneh, Amany A. Khodair
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper investigates how artificial intelligence (AI) can enhance access to political science literature within library systems, while critically examining the challenges associated with its implementation. Adopting a broad definition of AI that encompasses a range of technologies and practices, the study explores how AI-driven tools such as intelligent search engines, recommendation systems, and open data infrastructures are reshaping the curation, indexing, and dissemination of political knowledge. These technologies offer new opportunities for evidence-based analysis, policy-oriented research, and user engagement, thereby transforming both library science and political science research methodologies. However, the integration of AI also raises significant concerns regarding equity, transparency, and governance. Issues such as algorithmic bias, platform dependency, and unequal access to advanced tools may intensify existing disparities across institutions and regions. Drawing on case studies and current initiatives in Library and Information Science (LIS), the paper highlights practical implications and outlines future research directions for the responsible and expandable deployment of AI in political science libraries. Ultimately, AI is positioned not merely as a technical enhancement, but as a political and epistemological force within the evolving ecosystem of knowledge production.

title: CGP Scaled 3D 2T0C DRAM with Record Write Speed of 400 ps and High Temperature Stability
authors: Tianyue Fu, Qianlan Hu, Xiong Xiong, Qijun Li, Wenjie Zhao, Shiyuan Liu, Yanqing Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: As data-intensive applications, particularly in artificial intelligence (AI), demand ever-increasing high-density and high-speed memory, three-dimensional (3D) integration has emerged as a crucial solution. In this work, we present a high-density, 3D 2T0C DRAM architecture based on amorphous oxide semiconductor (AOS) transistors. The device is fabricated using a comprehensive back-end-of-line (BEOL) compatible process with a low thermal budget of less than 300 °C. We demonstrate aggressive contact-gate-pitch (CGP) scaling to 140 nm and successfully stack two AOS transistor layers to form the 3D structure. The fabricated 3D DRAM cell exhibits robust thermal stability, with excellent data retention performance up to 125 °C. Furthermore, the device achieves a record-fast write speed of 400 ps, highlighting its superior performance. These results provide a comprehensive path toward future ultra-high-density and high-speed memory technologies.

title: CALM: Cost-efficient, model Agnostic, Low-latency Modular LLM Deployment framework
authors: Ashish Bansal, Deepkamal Kaur Gill, Rajeev Joshi, Hung Pham, Lee Zamparo, Wenxuan Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The rapid evolution of deep learning and generative AI has positioned large language models (LLMs) as critical enablers of innovation across industries. This paper underscores the critical importance of architecting optimized LLM deployment strategies that not only meet stringent service-level agreements (SLAs) but also enable faster time to market and meet return-on-investment expectations for enterprises through reduced inference costs. We identify three core challenges faced by AI practitioners: (1) the prohibitive and often unsustainable costs of leveraging thirdparty hosted LLMs for advanced applications such as customer service automation, (2) the need to optimize deployments for specific use cases to meet dynamic SLA requirements, and (3) ensuring always-on production reliability without compromising scalability or development velocity. To address these issues, we introduce CALM - a purpose-built, cost-aware deployment methodology that integrates dynamic autoscaling, workload-aware dedicated tenancy, and intelligent resource orchestration. Our system supports a local development and deployment model that simplifies the end-to-end lifecycle while enabling efficient hardware utilization and robust production performance. Empirical results show inference cost reductions of up to 5x compared to managed hosted solutions in high-load environments. Finally, we provide practical guidelines and deployment patterns that enable AI teams to build and operate scalable, performant, and cost-optimized LLM applications—accelerating their ability to deliver value in production environments.

title: QGMR: A New Quantum Mutation Testing Operator
authors: Sanskar Shah, Sangharatna Godboley, P. Radha Krishna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Mutation testing is an important technique for assessing the quality of test cases and software systems. It involves introducing changes into the code and identifying those changes. In the context of quantum computing, where programs are shown as quantum circuits composed of quantum gates, the traditional mutation testing techniques face unique challenges due to the inherent complexity of quantum algorithms and operations. We propose a novel strategy for higher-order mutation testing of quantum programs. Our main objective is to enhance the reliability of the quantum program. We propose a mutator to replace quantum gates in a quantum program. It identifies the gate combinations and their syntactic equivalence. We compare circuit state vectors to identify equivalent gate combinations and present the results for efficient mutation generation.

title: Standard CMOS-Compatible Low-Power Ternary Logic with Drain Resistance
authors: Minsu Lim, Jiwon Chang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: We propose a silicon (Si) CMOS-compatible ternary logic (T-CMOS) technology achieved by a simple modification of conventional MOSFETs. By introducing an additional drain resistance, the on-current is limited to a gate-independent constant level, thereby enabling a stable third logic state in the voltage transfer curve (VTC). TCAD simulations calibrated to experimental transfer characteristics of planar FETs and GAAFETs confirm robust ternary operation with low static power consumption and short propagation delay. Among the devices, GAAFET-based T-CMOS achieves the most favorable performance, demonstrating superior current scalability and a large static noise margin (SNM) of 149 mV/dec at V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf> = 0.7 V. Furthermore, the circuit supports hybrid operation, switching between ternary and binary logic depending on the supply voltage.

title: An Adaptive Security Model Based on User Behavior Analysis for Enhancing Protection Against Cyber Attacks
authors: Mohammed Elrasheed Mohammed Salih, Adil Bushra Yahya Yousif, Siddiq Babikir, Osama El Abdein, Ahmed Arbab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: With cyberattacks increasingly exploiting human vulnerabilities, traditional security systems based on static rules fail to adapt to individual user behaviors. These systems treat all users the same, overlooking differences in awareness, roles, and usage patterns. This research presents an adaptive security model that analyzes user behavior in real-time using artificial intelligence (AI). The model classifies users by risk level and dynamically applies tailored security responses, such as alerts or access limitations. By integrating behavioral science and machine learning, the system proactively identifies anomalies and adjusts defenses accordingly. Evaluated in a simulated environment, the model reduced successful attacks by 42%, cut false alarms by 68%, and responded to threats in under 3.2 hours. It proved capable of adapting to changing user patterns within minutes, enhancing both security and user experience. This behavior-aware approach represents a significant step toward intelligent, personalized cybersecurity systems that protect against modern, human centered threats..

title: Deep Learning Model Splitting for Distributed Inference in Resource-Constrained Edge Environments for Wildlife Monitoring
authors: Md. Auhidur Rahman, Davide Adami, Stefano Giordano
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deep neural networks have achieved remarkable success in computer vision tasks such as object detection, classification and segmentation. However, deploying these models in real time on resource-constrained edge devices poses significant challenges due to high computational and memory requirements. Split computing enables distributed inference by partitioning the model across multiple devices, thereby balancing workload and reducing per-device overhead. In this work, we split our custom trained YOLOv8m object detection model for European wildlife animal classification into two components: the backbone (backbone.pt) and the neck-head (neck_head.pt). After verifying the correctness of the split model, we deploy it across three NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8 GB devices two acting as gRPC clients executing the backbone, and one as a gRPC server completing inference using the neck-head layers. To address the communication bottleneck caused by transmitting high-dimensional intermediate feature maps (P3, P4, P5) over limitedbandwidth edge networks, we compare two tensor serialization strategies: repeated float fields and serialized byte arrays, evaluated in both FP32 and FP16 formats. Our experiments show that the use of byte-based serialization with FP16 reduces network latency by up to 85 % and improves the end-to-end frame rate by over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex>, achieving up to 9 FPS. In contrast, traditional TCP-based backbone feature maps tensor transmission yields less than 1 FPS, making it infeasible for real-time applications. These results demonstrate that efficient tensor serialization and gRPC-based communication are key enablers for scalable, low-latency, distributed inference in edge AI systems.

title: Readiness Dimensions for Digital Twin Adoption and Process Optimization in Sri Lanka's Large-Scale Apparel Sector
authors: Dulanga Thennakoon, Chalani Oruthotaarachchi, Amila Withanaarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Industry 4.0 positions Digital Twin (DT) technology as critical for manufacturing efficiency through real-time virtual replicas, enabling process optimization. This study examines how organizational readiness dimensions for Digital Twin adoption predict process optimization outcomes in Sri Lanka's large-scale apparel manufacturing sector, which faces substantial barriers to digital transformation despite contributing significantly to the national GDP. Using data from 179 industry professionals analyzed through PLS-SEM, the research model examined four readiness dimensions: Technological Infrastructure, Organizational Capability, Workforce Competency, and Financial Resources, alongside environmental moderators including Market and Competitive Pressure and Government Support. Results reveal that Financial Resources and Technological Infrastructure significantly predict process optimization, while Market Pressure critically moderates the Organizational Capability relationship, demonstrating that competitive intensity activates organizational capabilities for performance gains. Government support shows limited influence, revealing institutional gaps. The model explains substantial variance in process optimization outcomes. Findings emphasize that financial capacity and technological infrastructure drive DT adoption with competition-dependent organizational effects. Recommendations include prioritizing strategic technology investments, strengthening adaptive organizational structures, and implementing targeted policy interventions to address sector-specific barriers to digital transformation in emerging economies.

title: Bridging Local and Contextual Features with IChOA-CNN-LSTM for Robust Textual Emotion Recognition
authors: Asif Ahammad Miazee, Ashraful Goni
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The explosive growth of digital communication has transformed social media into a vast repository of human emotion, yet the noisy, informal nature of online language continues to challenge existing natural language processing (NLP) systems. This paper introduces IChOA-CNN-LSTM, a optimised hybrid deep learning architecture designed to bridge the persistent gap between local feature extraction and longrange contextual understanding in textual emotion recognition. The framework integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for capturing fine-grained n-gram patterns, bidirectional long short-term memory networks (BiLSTM) for modeling sequential dependencies, and a multi-head attention mechanism optimized through the Improved Chimp Optimization Algorithm (IChOA) for feature selection. The model achieves an accuracy of 78.19%, outperforming conventional CNN, LSTM, and state-of-the-art hybrid baselines by up to 18%. This research lays a scalable foundation for future multimodal and context-aware emotion analysis.

title: Enhanced Near-Field Power Beam Scanning in E-Plane and H-Plane Using mmWave Leaky-Wave Antenna
authors: Shan Han, Yuan Ding, George Goussetis, Symon K. Podilchak, Lei Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents a two-dimensional (2-D) near-field-focusing (NFF) leaky-wave antenna (LWA) array formed by stacking 16 one-dimensional (1-D) NFF LWAs with 5.45 mm spacing and element-dependent feed phases. The derived aperture phase distribution is obtained from the finite-distance pathlength condition, and applied as element-dependent feed phases. The proposed excitation synthesizes a converging aperture field that produces a compact focal spot with a 6 dB power-density enhancement compared with a conventional far-field focusing (FFF) configuration at the same distance. While the NFF design exhibits a lower far-field gain (18.4 dBi) than the FFF case (23.9 dBi), it still maintains strong directional radiation and stable far-field characteristics. This demonstrates that NFF and highquality far-field performance can be simultaneously achieved. The resulting dual-function aperture is well suited for wireless power transfer and communication applications, offering a compact and efficient solution for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems.

title: Temporal Personal Fashion AI System with Multi-Component Integration
authors: Raveesha Harshani, Navodya Manamendra, Pabasara Rajapaksha, Shyamantha Godaarawa, Buddika Harshanath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The rapid growth of e-commerce has reshaped fashion retail but left gaps in personalized sizing, styling, and grooming solutions. This study presents an integrated, AIpowered ecosystem that addresses these challenges through four interoperable, cloud-based modules. First, wardrobe intelligence uses LSTM networks with multi-head attention to track personal style trends over time and recommends sustainable, wardrobe-centric choices, outperforming standard prediction methods. Second, accurate anthropometric measurements are extracted from dual-view images using EfficientNet-B3, enabling privacy-focused and precise body sizing across brands. Third, occasion-based accessory recommendations are generated by combining ResNet-50 for clothing attribute extraction, Transformer-based cultural modeling, and reinforcement learning to adapt to user feedback. Fourth, specialized CNNs offer comprehensive grooming advice—analyzing facial shape, skin, dental, hair, and nail health—validated by expert professionals. The ecosystem leverages large, diverse datasets and federated learning to ensure scalability and user privacy, resulting in notable reductions in return rates, measurable user savings, and high satisfaction across large deployments.

title: An Intelligent Machine and Deep Learning Framework for Fake News Detection
authors: Amit Sheikh, Soyad Adnan Hossain Pranto, Souhardyo Bhattacharjee, Anamika Rani Nath, S M Samim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of fake news in the digital era has become a critical challenge, undermining public trust and spreading misinformation across online platforms. Accurate and automated detection of fake news has become essential to ensure the reliability of digital information. In this study, we have performed a comparative evaluation of classical machine learning models and a deep learning-based approach for fake news detection. Logistic Regression and Naïve Bayes classifiers have been implemented, achieving accuracies of 98% and 93%, respectively, which has demonstrated the effectiveness of traditional methods in text classification tasks. To better capture semantic relationships and sequential dependencies in textual data, a Long Short-Term Memory network has been developed. The LSTM model has achieved superior performance with 99% accuracy, which has highlighted the advantages of deep learning in modeling complex linguistic patterns. The dataset has consisted of verified fake and real news articles, which have been preprocessed using tokenization, normalization, sequence padding, and feature extraction techniques such as TF-IDF to optimize classification performance. Experimental results have indicated that LSTMbased approaches have not only improved classification accuracy but also offered robustness against noisy and unstructured text, providing a scalable and reliable framework for automated online news verification. This work has contributed to the growing body of research on fake news detection and has served as a benchmark for future studies integrating deep learning and natural language processing techniques.

title: Design and Analysis of a Symmetric Thirteen-Level Inverter for THD Minimization with Reduced Component Count
authors: Bivas Sarkar, Md. Mohi Uddin Mohin, Mohammad Ibrahim, Shahariar Ahmed Himo, Md. Sabbir Alam, Monjila Afrin Dorothi, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Multilevel inverters (MLIs) have emerged as a pivotal technology for high-efficiency power conversion in renewable energy systems, owing to their superior output waveform quality and reduced dv/dt. However, conventional thirteen-level topologies often incur high component counts and control complexity, limiting large-scale integration. This research proposes a novel symmetric thirteen-level inverter that employs only nine power switches and uses the Optimum Nearest Level Control (ONLC) to minimize the total harmonic distortion (THD). Comprehensive MATLAB/Simulink simulations demonstrate a peak output of 600 V and a THD of 6.24% using a resistive load. The inverter's streamlined architecture reduces cost, simplifies control, and enhances reliability. Key advantages include lower conduction and switching losses, simplified gatedriver requirements, and superior harmonic performance. Comparative analysis confirms the proposed topology's economic feasibility and technical superiority over state-of-theart thirteen-level inverters.

title: Performance Degradation of Monocrystalline PV Modules Under Progressive Uniform Shading Conditions: An Experimental Study
authors: Md. Sabbir Alam, Shahariar Ahmed Himo, Md. Ashraful Islam, Md. Mohi Uddin Mohin, Mst. Umme Habiba, ASM Shihavuddin, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) modules frequently experience partial shadowing in real-world installations, which significantly reduces energy production. The effect of uniform partial shadow, which covers 0%, 10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80%, and 90% of the module surface area, on the performance of 20W monocrystalline solar modules in outdoor conditions is investigated experimentally in this work. The panels generate 7.82©9.40 W at 10% shading but drop to 3.215.08 W at 90% shading, indicating that higher shading levels disproportionately reduce available power. The results show a significant fall in maximum power with increased shading. From 58.29% at 10% shading to almost 80% at 90%, power loss increases dramatically, with peak sensitivity between 10% and 50% shading. According to this study's results, the shadow has a greater impact on current, reducing it by almost 46% (from 0.369 A to 0.201 A). While the voltage drops by just 11% (from 23.08 V to 20.46 V), this indicates that current loss is the leading cause of the power decline. In addition to providing crucial information for solar system design, performance forecasts, and shade mitigation strategies in both urban and rural environments, this work offers quantitative insights into the consequences of uniform shadowing.

title: LLM is Not All You Need: A Systematic Evaluation of ML vs. Foundation Models for text and image based Medical Classification
authors: Meet Raval, Tejul Pandit, Dhvani Upadhyay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The combination of multimodal Vision-Language Models (VLMs) and Large Language Models (LLMs) opens up new possibilities for medical classification. This work offers a rigorous, unified benchmark by using four publicly available datasets covering text and image modalities (binary and multiclass complexity) that contrasts traditional Machine Learning (ML) with contemporary transformer-based techniques. We evaluated three model classes for each task: Classical ML (LR, LightGBM, ResNet-50), Prompt-Based LLMs/VLMs (Gemini 2.5), and Fine-Tuned PEFT Models (LoRA-adapted Gemma3 variants). All experiments used consistent data splits and aligned metrics. According to our results, traditional machine learning (ML) models set a high standard by consistently achieving the best overall performance across most medical categorization tasks. This was especially true for structured text-based datasets, where the classical models performed exceptionally well. In stark contrast, the LoRA-tuned Gemma variants consistently showed the worst performance across all text and image experiments, failing to generalize from the minimal fine-tuning provided. However, the zero-shot LLM/VLM pipelines (Gemini 2.5) had mixed results; they performed poorly on text-based tasks, but demonstrated competitive performance on the multiclass image task, matching the classical ResNet-50 baseline. These results demonstrate that in many medical categorization scenarios, established machine learning models continue to be the most reliable option. The experiment suggests that foundation models are not universally superior and that the effectiveness of Parameter-Efficient FineTuning (PEFT) is highly dependent on the adaptation strategy, as minimal fine-tuning proved detrimental in this study.

title: Khelte Khelte Shikhi: A Proposed HCI Framework for Gamified Interactive Learning with Minecraft in Bangladeshi Education Systems
authors: Mohd Ruhul Ameen, Akif Islam, Momen Khandokar Ope
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Game-based learning shows real promise for engaging students in well-resourced classrooms, but what about the millions who study in schools with far fewer opportunities? We propose a practical framework for bringing Minecraft Education Edition into Bangladesh's 130,000 schools, where 55% lack reliable internet, rural areas receive only 12-16 hours of electricity per day, computer access in rural schools is just 8%, and student-teacher ratios can reach 52:1. Our approach addresses these constraints directly through three deployment tiers: cloudbased multiplayer for urban schools with stable infrastructure (15%), local-area network (LAN) solutions with optional solar power for semi-urban schools <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(30 \%)$</tex>, and fully offline, turnbased modes using refurbished hardware for rural contexts (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 5 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>. We provide eight curriculum-aligned Minecraft worlds with complete Bangla localization, covering topics from Lalbagh Fort reconstruction to monsoon flood simulation. The interface supports first-time users through progressive complexity, culturally familiar metaphors rooted in local farming and architecture, and accessibility features such as keyboard-only controls and 200% text scaling. We outline evaluation benchmarks including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \%}$</tex> learning gains, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0 \%}$</tex> transfer-task mastery, System Usability Scale scores above 70, and a per-student cost below two dollars per hour. Although not yet empirically validated, this work synthesizes game-based learning theory, HCI principles, and contextual analysis to offer implementable specifications for pilot testing in resource-constrained settings. It is presented as a design-oriented conceptual framework rather than a field deployment, providing an implementation-ready blueprint for future empirical validation.

title: AI-Based Forecasting Model for Sustainable Development in Arab and GCC Countries
authors: Nabil El Kadhi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper predicts the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) Index of Arab and GCC nations based on high-quality AI-processed time-series forecasts to predict its value in 2027 using SDG data (2000 -2022) of the SDG Transformation Center. Findings indicate that GCC countries have better performance when compared to non-GCC countries because of better institutional capacities, governance and investments on sustainability. The United Arab Emirates is expected to be top of the list with a score of above 75 in 2026 , and this is due to its efforts on diversification, renewable energy, and digital governance systems. The SDG Index of Saudi Arabia is expected to increase to 72.5 in 2026 with a steady rise in 2018 (63.7) in relation to Vision 2030 and moderately in Oman and Qatar, focusing on green employment and inclusive society. On the other hand, Kuwait and Bahrain are disadvantaged by their delayed reform and by its dependence on oil. Comparative experiments show that cross-validation has a considerable increase in model performance relative to the 80/20 split, with a 35-60 per cent reduction in MAE and RMSE and an average of 0.9 in R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in a stable economy. As an example, the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of Saudi Arabia increased by 0.72 to 0.96, and RMSE decreased by 3.34 to 1.13 in Oman. These findings demonstrate the strength of cross-validation in SDG forecasting and the urgency of the implementation of transformational and innovation-led policies to maintain long-term developments towards the 2030 Agenda in the Arab and GCC worlds.

title: Folding the Vision: Towards Efficient Global Context Representation on Edge Devices
authors: Ang Li, Shouxiang Ni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Traditional efficient CNNs face limitations in capturing global dependencies due to local receptive fields, while ViTs suffer from quadratic complexity. Recent works attempt to combine convolution and attention mechanisms, yet still struggle with computational overhead that limits inference speed on mobile devices. In this work, we propose FoldNet, a pure convolutional architecture that achieves global context representation through hardware-efficient operations. First, we propose the Fold Convolution that reorganizes spatial pixels into the channel dimension, capturing global spatial attention via channel interactions, with lower computational costs compared to ViTs. Second, we design the Partial Re-parameterization Module that applies the structural re-parameterization technique to a subset of feature channels. This approach achieves nearly identical performance compared to full-channel processing while significantly minimizing redundant computations and memory accesses. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of FoldNet over existing state-of-the-art methods. For example, on ImageNet-1K, our Foldnet-S1 achieves 79.0% Top-1 accuracy, while only has 1.0 ms inference speed on mobile devices. Comprehensive experimental results on high-resolution downstream tasks show that FoldNet outperforms existing efficient models, maintaining a superior trade-off between inference speed and accuracy.

title: A Routh Zero-Location Testing Method for Integer Polynomials With Any Singularity Profile
authors: Yuval Bistritz, Ruth Weinberg
abstract: The paper presents an integer-preserving algorithm to determine the zero location (ZL) with respect to the imaginary axis for any polynomial with Gaussian-integer coefficients. It extends the so-called optimal integer Routh stability test, previously limited to normal conditions, to integer polynomials with any singularity profile. The primary advantage of integer arithmetic lies in its inherent error-free quality. This characteristic is particularly crucial for the general ZL case because it involves workarounds for abnormal and singular cases that are triggered by correct identification of the value 0 - an occurrence that can be obscured by rounding errors in floating-point arithmetic. Running the algorithm with integers, prevents the risk of false acceptance or rejection of these workarounds. Since in practice any non-integer polynomial can be scaled into an integer polynomial, the new algorithm paves the way for an error-free solution to the classical Routh-Hurwitz problem in its most general possible setting.

title: Guide for the redesign of websites not accessible to blind people: MiUV study case_supp1-3690147.docx
authors: Jose Rafael Rojano-Caceres
abstract: This research proposes a guideline that outlines the redesign process of a web system by compiling a set of tools and techniques aimed at improving accessibility for users with visual functional diversity (blindness). It is worth mentioning that the study adheres to the W3C (World Wide Web Consortium) standards using automatic evaluation programs and screen readers. The paper presents the selection process for these tools and techniques, which constitute the guideline composed of five stages: (1) training on accessibility issues, (2) initial system exploration, (3) evaluation with users and experts, (4) requirements analysis, and (5) recoding. It also specifies the artifacts to be produced at each stage and how they are used in subsequent phases. The guideline was applied to the institutional system “MiUV” which is used by the Universidad Veracruzana community, including faculty members, administrative staff and students—some of whom have visual functional diversity. The results showed that the proposed process was effective in guiding the redesign to improve accessibility. However, opportunities for improvement were also identified in each phase of the redesign process.

title: Real-time Dual Sensing of cTnI and Myoglobin via Printable Graphitized Electrodes
authors: Suhaib Imtiyaz, Mohsina Afrooz, Parvathy Nair, Sonal Fande, Khairunnisa Amreen, Sanket Goel
abstract: Acute myocardial infarction (AMI) has emerged as a serious global health concern. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), health authorities report approximately 100 million cases annually worldwide, with about 9 million associated mortalities. As AMI can affect individuals regardless of their age or prior health status, making early and pre-symptomatic detection is crucial for clinical intervention. Targeting AMI biomarkers such as cardiac troponin I (cTnI) and myoglobin is therefore essential for timely diagnosis and treatment. Hence, the development of ultra-sensitive and highly specific sensors is crucial. Herein, a laser-induced graphene (LIG)-based electrochemical biosensor capable of dual detection of two pivotal AMI biomarkers, cTnI and myoglobin, is presented, hence addressing the need for simultaneous biomarker monitoring. Electrochemical analytical techniques such as Cyclic voltammetry (CV) and differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) techniques were employed, achieving limits of detection (LoD) and quantification (LoQ) of 0.035 ng/mL and 0.105 ng/mL for cTnI, and 1.39 ng/mL and 4.25 ng/mL for myoglobin, respectively. The fabricated electrodes exhibited excellent stability and reproducibility, supported by physicochemical characterizations confirming detailed electrode morphology. Furthermore, this dual-detection platform was successfully tested for real-time detection in human blood serum, yielding high recovery values and demonstrating its potential for practical, real-world applications.

title: Interannual Linkage Between LOD and ENSO: Insights From Geodetic Observations, Geophysical Models, and Climate Indices
authors: Małgorzata Wińska, Dominika Staniszewska, Justyna Śliwińska-Bronowicz, Jolanta Nastula
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: This study addresses interannual linkages between variations in Earth rotation (Length of Day, LOD), atmospheric dynamics (AAM), and key climate indices related to El Niño Southern Oscillation (ENSO) events, highlighting that the mechanisms and strength of these couplings at interannual scales are still not fully quantified or consistently characterized across different datasets. This study investigates interannual linkages between Length of Day (LOD), Atmospheric Angular Momentum (AAM), and key climate indices, including the Southern Oscillation Index (SOI), Northern Oscillation Index (NOI), Global Mean Temperature (GMT), and Global Mean Sea Level (GMSL). Using advanced statistical and spectral methods, we analyze interannual oscillations (with periods between 2 and 8 years) along with variations of geodetic, atmospheric, and climatic time series to quantify correlations, lead–lag relationships, and phase coherence with respect to El Niño Southern Oscillation (ENSO) events. Strong dynamical coupling and significant bidirectional Granger causality is observed between LOD and AAM, as well as robust in-phase correlations with major climate indices, particularly during pronounced El Niño and La Niña episodes. Our findings highlight the potential of LOD and AAM as integrative geodetic indicators for monitoring and forecasting the impacts of climate variability, underscoring the necessity of considering Earth rotation dynamics in climate system analysis.

title: GenoPIM: Enhancing Genome Alignment using Digital Processing-in-Memory
authors: Dhruv Kulkarni, Sai Manoj Pudukotai Dinkarrao
abstract: Genome alignment is an emerging application in the field of genomics. There have been several hardware chips that have been introduced that aim to optimize Genome sequencing, but none of them utilize Look-up table based approaches. We implement a Look-up-Table based PIM (Processing-in-Memory) chip for Dynamic Programming based algorithms, such as Needleman-Wunsch. Our results indicate up to 4.5× higher substrate-level throughput potential than a CPU baseline under an architectural upper-bound model, with substantially lower estimated energy per DP cell than contemporary PIM designs and a naive GPU baseline.

title: Common-Mode Analysis and Suppression for Asymmetrical Six-Phase PMSM Drive Under Single-Phase Open-Circuit Fault
authors: Guangyu Wang, Hongyang Liu, Zicheng Liu, Junnan You, Rongpei Zhu, Changhao Huang, Dong Jiang, Ronghai Qu
abstract: Compared with healthy electric drive systems, open-circuit faults break the symmetry of the electric drive system, thereby inducing unique common-mode (CM) characteristics. As a result, traditional definitions and analyses of CM voltage (CMV) for health-symmetrical electric drive systems are difficult to quantitatively evaluate the CM noise of fault-asymmetric electric drive systems or guide the design of suppression methods. Therefore, this article models and analyzes the CMV under single-phase bridge open-circuit fault, revealing the formation mechanism of CMV in fault-asymmetric electric drive systems and achieving an accurate definition of CMV. On this basis, an optimized CMV suppression method suitable for faulty electric drive systems is proposed. It employs a dynamic zero-sequence injection scheme to actively cancel the CMV generated by the remaining healthy bridges, and adjusts the carrier phase based on the unique CMV characteristics of the faulty phase to suppress the CMV of the system. The experimental results verify the effectiveness of the proposed CMV modeling and analysis method for the fault-asymmetric system. Meanwhile, compared with traditional methods, the proposed CMV suppression method achieves lower CMV and CM electromagnetic interference (EMI) under single-phase open-circuit fault, providing a reference for the CM evaluation and optimal design of fault-asymmetric electric drive systems.

title: Flow-Induced Vibration-Based Liquid Flow Rate Measurement With Optical Carrier Microwave Interferometry
authors: Songlin Li, Ting Xue, Yan Wu, Bin Wu
abstract: Accurate liquid flow rate measurement is essential for efficient energy management and sustainable development. In this paper, a novel non-intrusive liquid flow rate measurement method based on optical carrier microwave interferometry (OCMI) combined with flow-induced vibration (FIV) is developed. The theoretical principle of FIV is presented, and the liquid flow rate mathematical model based on FIV is established. In the experiment, the sensing fiber is helically wrapped around the outer surface of the pipeline to effectively capture FIV signals. The Fabry-Perot interferometers (FPIs) are categorized into two types: sensing FPIs incorporating the helical fiber and reference FPIs without it. Experimental results demonstrate that sensing FPIs exhibit a strong response to liquid flow rate variations through pipeline vibrations, with a pronounced quadratic relationship between the dip frequency and the flow rate. The fitted curve achieves high accuracy, yielding a mean absolute error and root mean square error of less than 4 kHz and 5 kHz, respectively. The comparative analysis between sensing and reference FPIs not only corroborates the validity of the derived theoretical model but also confirms the effectiveness and reliability of the proposed approach, which presents a promising, non-intrusive solution for liquid flow rate monitoring in practical industrial applications.

title: A Framework for Energy-Efficient Hybrid Transceiver Design in Multi-Hop Communications
authors: Hanyu Yang, Heng Liu, Shiqi Gong, Chengwen Xing, Nan Zhao, Dusit Niyato
abstract: In this paper, we propose a general energy efficiency (EE) optimization framework for the hybrid analog-digital transceivers design in multi-hop communication systems. The analog and digital beamforming matrices are jointly optimized considering two kinds of practical power constraint models, i.e., sum power with box eigenvalue constraints (SPBECs) and multiple weighted power constraints (MWPCs), and unit-modulus constraints on analog beamforming matrices. For both the SPBECs and MWPCs cases, to tackle the challenging problem involving highly-coupled variables, an effective decoupling approach is first proposed. Specifically, a set of auxiliary variables are introduced to equivalently transform the original problem into a decoupled form with respect to the variables of each node. Then, for each node, we propose an efficient two-stage analog and digital beamforming optimization algorithm. To be specific, we optimize the analog beamforming matrices in the first stage by jointly exploiting the matrix-monotonic optimization framework and channel-alignment strategy. Then, we optimize the digital beamforming matrices in the second stage based on the multi-node water-filling methodology. Furthermore, in order to compute the parameters involved in the multi-node water-filling solutions for the SPBECs case, we propose two novel strategies, i.e., the Dinkelbach based strategy and the per-node penalty based strategy, which derive the parameters in closed-forms and offer clear physical interpretations. Moreover, the per-node penalty based strategy is effectively extended to the MWPCs case by additionally employing the Lagrangian duality theory. Simulation results demonstrate the superior performance and high efficiency of our proposed algorithms.

title: Decentralized Online Learning with Hard Constraints: A Doubly-Bounded Queue Approach
authors: Yituo Liu, Weiyi Qin, Wei Bao, Juncheng Wang, Jianxiong Guo, Min Zhou
abstract: We consider decentralized online convex optimization with time-varying constraints and conduct performance analysis using two stringent metrics: network dynamic regret with respect to the online global solution benchmark, and hard constraint violation that does not allow any compensated violation over time and learners. We propose an efficient algorithm called Constrained Online Learning with Doubly-bounded Queue (COLDQ), which introduces a novel virtual queue that is both lower and upper bounded, allowing tight control of the constraint violation without needing the Slater’s condition. We prove via a new Lyapunov drift analysis that COLDQ provides O(T 1+Vx/2 ) network dynamic regret and O(min{T 3+Vx/4 ; TVg }) hard constraint violation, where Vx and Vg capture the dynamics of the loss and constraint functions. For the first time, the two bounds smoothly approach to the best-known O(T 1/2 ) regret and O(1) violation, as the dynamics of the losses and constraints diminish, under both centralized and decentralized settings. For strongly convex loss functions, COLDQ provides O(log T) static regret and O(min{T1/2 (log T)1/2 , TVg }) hard constraint violation. Simulation results based on synthetic and canonical datasets demonstrate that COLDQ substantially outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches for both convex and non-convex loss functions.

title: AAIML 2026 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)

title: IntentQA: Intent Question Answering in Videos by Cognitive Context Reasoning_supp1-3690561.pdf
authors: Ping Wei
abstract: Video understanding requires intelligent agents to transcend mere recognition of visual facts and comprehend the underlying intents behind human actions-often termed the “dark matter” of social intelligence. To bridge the gap between visual observation and intent reasoning, we introduce a novel task, IntentQA, and contribute a large-scale VideoQA dataset specifically tailored for this purpose. However, recognizing that standard metrics may overestimate capabilities due to dataset biases, we go beyond simple accuracy to rigorously evaluate model robustness. We augment the benchmark by generating five distinct contrast sets via Large Language Models (LLMs) and introducing a “Contrast Performance Decline” metric. We propose the X-CaVIR (eXplainable Context-aware Video Intent Reasoning) framework, which leverages three types of “Cognitive Context” to enhance video analysis: i) Situational Context via a cross-modal Video Query Language (VQL) module, ii) Contrastive Context via a Contrastive Learning module, and iii) Commonsense Context via a Commonsense Reasoning module. Crucially, to overcome the opacity of traditional black-box models, we refine the integration of LLMs within X-CaVIR by employing a transparent pipeline that synergizes video captions with VQA model outputs. This approach not only improves performance by effectively utilizing rich commonsense knowledge but also renders the reasoning process explicitly interpretable. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our components, the superiority of X-CaVIR over state-of-the-art baselines, and its stability against perturbations on the contrast sets. The dataset and codes are open-sourced at: https://github.com/JoseponLee/IntentQA.

title: Evaluating and Enhancing Large Language Models for Islamic Question Answering
authors: Md. Naimul Haque, Mostahid Hasan Fahim, M. Reza Selim, Md. Minhajul Haque, MD. Fuad Al Amin, Md. Ataullha, M. Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study investigates how large language models (LLMs) perform when applied to Islamic question answering. We investigate two models, Mistral-7B and LLaMA-3.2-3B, assessing their ability to answer multiple-choice and short-answer questions derived from a curated dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3, 0 0 0}$</tex> Islamic questions. Unlike general-purpose evaluations, our work emphasizes the unique challenges posed by religious content, including the necessity to align with authentic sources and avoid interpretive errors. The study applies prompt engineering, targeted fine-tuning and Retrieval Augmented Generation(RAG) to enhance output quality. Evaluation was conducted using manual and LLM-as-a-judge approach. Results show significant improvements with prompt engineering: LLaMA-3.2-3B improved from 38.78 % to 64.19 %, while Mistral-7B improved from 41.84 % to 70.05 %. Fine-tuning further raised Mistral-7B performance to 68.11 %. Still, the accuracy is not acceptable in such a sensitive field. Hence, we implemented a RAG pipeline to supply grounded context from authentic religious sources. However, retrieval quality, chunking limitations, and embedding mismatch led to frequent irrelevant or missing evidence. These issues also caused mistakes in nuanced religious concepts. As a result, RAG produced only a modest accuracy improvement, remaining far below expectations.

title: PoultryNet: A Lightweight CNN Student Model for Chicken Disease Classification via Knowledge Distillation from Vision Transformer
authors: Md Parvez Kabir, Rasedul Islam, Jhion Adbar Opi, Md Aminur Rahman Joy, Arefin Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Poultry diseases seriously endanger the poultry industry, leading to economic losses, as well as undermining food security. While there are various precise heavyweight disease detection models, there are few fast, lightweight, as well as deployable, solutions that are appropriate for real-world farm deployments. This research bridges this gap by introducing PoultryNet, a tailored lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) trained as a student network through Knowledge Distillation (KD) from a Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16) teacher. While the teacher attains 98.83 % accuracy on a Kaggle chicken disease dataset containing four disease classes, PoultryNet achieves a reasonable test accuracy of 95.93%, presenting a fast and resource-optimized alternative. Extensive experiments confirm that PoultryNet not only surpasses the best classification performance on all four classes of diseases but also requires a much smaller model size (15.6 MB) as well as a smaller number of parameters (4 million) yet attaining over 95 % teacher- network compression. It is contrasted with baseline CNN-based architectures, including MobileNetV2, MobileNetV3, SqueezeNet, ShuffleNet, as well as EfficientNet, demonstrating higher efficiency as well as comparable or improved performance. Weighted precision, recall, F1-score, as well as Cohen's Kappa, also affirm its high strength. This work presents the capability of distillation-based knowledge aiding fast, precise, as well as deployable, poultry disease identification, offering an efficient farmer-deployed tool capable of scaling up to extensive health monitoring.

title: Design and Evaluation of an Anonymous Mobile Mental Health Application for College Students
authors: Kurikala Hasini, Mokshita Vp, Rajanikanth Aluvalu, Mallellu Sai Prashanth
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This article develops an evaluation and design plan for a mobile application that offers instant, anonymous, and personalised mental health support for college students. The existing college counselling services face challenges from stigma and lack of available time slots, and existing mental health mobile applications do not provide engagement and retention features and do not smoothly transition to support from a formal institution. Our solution is a mobile application, "Mentally Aware," which incorporates three core features: an AI-driven triage and recommendation system; an anonymous, encrypted peer-to-peer chat network staffed by trained student volunteers; and direct API integration with campus counselling systems for seamless support scheduling. This paper outlines a comprehensive human-centred design process which includes qualitative user interviews (n=20), iterative design with prototypes, and a formal usability study (n=15). We anticipate high usability metrics, a significant increase in students’ willingness to seek support, reduced time and effort to book a professional appointment, and decreased effort to book a professional appointment. We aim to provide a scalable hybrid human-AI support model, an operational framework for anonymous peer communications, and a secure, validated pathway for integrating digital tools into the traditional university mental health systems.

title: Comparison of Feature-Based Clustering Methods on Unlabeled Marine Acoustic Images
authors: Omar Mammadov, Rajab Iskandarli, Ismayil Kheyirbayov, Nahla El Zant El Kadhi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Marine acoustic images present unique challenges for clustering due to heterogeneous textures, irregular object boundaries, and strong noise artifacts. The absence of ground-truth labels further necessitate unsupervised approaches. In this work, we perform a comparative evaluation of feature-based clustering methods on unlabeled acoustic imagery. The dataset consists of 10,600 marine acoustic images. Four feature extraction strategies are investigated: flattened grayscale vectors, embeddings from a pretrained ResNet-50, unsupervised convolutional autoencoder features, and manually extracted low-level statistical descriptors. These representations are combined with dimensionality reduction techniques (PCA, UMAP, t-SNE) and clustering algorithms (k-means, DBSCAN, HDBSCAN, AHC, and SOM). Performance is assessed using the Silhouette coefficient, Calinski–Harabasz index, and Davies–Bouldin index. To provide a balanced evaluation across these metrics, we introduce a unified comparison score, which integrates the normalized forms of all three indices. For each feature extraction strategy, we identify the five most effective combinations and analyze their outcomes in detail. The results demonstrate that manually engineered features combined with UMAP and k-means deliver the most robust clusters, while deep embeddings from ResNet-50 and autoencoders achieve comparable performance when paired with manifold reduction. Notably, flattened pixel representations consistently underperform, confirming the necessity of feature learning for acoustic imagery.

title: NextRAG: A Bilingual Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Financial Question-Answering
authors: Sayed Ashraful Islam Opin, Raiyan Ahmed Thakur, Ahsanul Karim Raiyan, Muhammad Rafsan Kabir, Shafin Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Financial institutions and regulators produce large volumes of complex, multilingual documents, making access to critical financial information challenging. Limited financial literacy further exacerbates this issue in countries like Bangladesh. This study introduces NextRAG, a bilingual Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework for financial knowledge retrieval and question answering in the Bangladeshi context.
