This research contributes a Structured and Repeatable Benchmarking Framework (SRBF) and a practitioner-focused decision matrix. Our findings provide actionable guidance for optimizing AI deployment strategies, recommending persistent VM clusters for heavy model training and serverless architectures for lightweight, cost-sensitive inference tasks.

title: An Integrated Socio-Technical Framework for Antifragile Project Organizations: A New Paradigm for Decision-Making Under Uncertainty
authors: Aawag Mohsen Alawag, Basheer M. Al-Ghazali, Saleem Ahmed Al-Azazi, Mohammed Abdulrazzaq Alaghbari, Fares Mohammed Shabaan, Maryam Althawadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In an era defined by persistent volatility, traditional project management paradigms focused on robustness and resilience prove insufficient. This paper introduces the concept of an anti-fragility system's ability to benefit from and improve in response to stressors and uncertainty as a necessary evolution for project-based organizations. We propose a novel Integrated Socio-Technical Framework that operationalizes antifragility by synthesizing principles from Complex Adaptive Systems (CAS), High-Reliability Organizations (HROs), and Socio-Technical Systems (STS) theory. The framework is built upon the joint optimization of two interdependent pillars: a 'social' system (comprising adaptive leadership, an antifragile culture, and strategic HRM) and a 'technical' system (including adaptive project management and digital enablers like the Digital Twin). By integrating these systems, organizations can develop the dynamic capabilities and organizational ambidexterity necessary to not only survive but also thrive in the face of disruption. This conceptual paper provides a practical roadmap for shifting from a process-centric focus to a value-delivery model, enabling project organizations to build a sustainable competitive advantage in an increasingly unpredictable world.

title: Optimized YOLOv12 for Robust Object Detection in Complex Satellite Scenes
authors: Sadek Al Prince, Gazi Mehraj Sakib, Mohammad Shoaib Uddin, Mohammed Shorfuddin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Object detection in satellite imagery remains challenging due to small object sizes, dense backgrounds, illumination variation, and multi-scale spatial patterns. To address these challenges, this paper presents an optimized YOLOv12 framework enhanced with Residual Attention Local Aggregation Networks (R-ALAN), an Adaptive Multi-Scale Fusion (AMSF) module, and region-aware A2C2f attention. A curated 19,254-image dataset covering eight infrastructure and environmental categories is introduced. Extensive experiments show that the optimized YOLOv12 achieves Precision = 0.85, Recall = 0.74, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F} 1=0.79, \text{mAP} {@} 0.5=0.78$</tex>, outperforming YOLOv10 and YOLOv11. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of each module, while external validation on subsets of DIOR and DOTA demonstrates improved generalization. Grad-CAM and attention visualizations improve interpretability. This revised framework provides a robust solution for remote-sensing applications including environmental monitoring, urban planning, and disaster response. Our results demonstrate that YOLOv12 is capable of attaining a better balance between localization accuracy and detection completeness, making it a competitive solution for realtime remote sensing.

title: A Wearable Inertial Sensing Platform for Personalized Health and Motion Monitoring
authors: Sachintha Adhikari, Praveen Rathnayaka, Sanjula Pathirana, Thevindu Dilmith Samarasekera, Roshan Godaliyadda, Parakrama Ekanayake, Chanaka Senanayake, Asela Ratnayake, Vijitha Herath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Accurate measurement of human joint kinematics is fundamental to modern health diagnostics, rehabilitation, and performance optimization. Although vision-based motion capture systems can achieve high accuracy in controlled laboratory environments, their reliance on cameras, controlled lighting, and unobstructed line-of-sight limits their ability to accurately capture true biomechanical motion, including segment-level kinematics and coordinated muscle group responses during dynamic tasks. To address these limitations, this paper presents a synchronized inertial measurement unit (IMU)-based wearable platform for field-deployable joint angle analysis in wearable motion monitoring applications. The proposed system integrates high-rate inertial sensors, precision timing, a real-time operating system, and a high-speed local data logging framework to ensure temporally consistent and lossless biomechanical data acquisition. Sensor orientation is estimated using a quaternion-based Extended Kalman filtering algorithm, and relative joint angles are computed from synchronized body segment orientations. Experimental validation involved 2D planar motion and a clean and jerk task to extract knee and elbow joint angles for evaluation of system performance. Accuracy was assessed by comparing IMU derived joint angles with those obtained from a video-based pose estimation method. The preliminary results of the tests showed a comparable trajectory in controlled experimental environments. These findings provide preliminary support for the feasibility of the proposed wearable framework for motion analytics beyond laboratory environments.

title: Developing a Decision Support Framework to Minimize Loss Mileage and Its Impact on Sri Lanka's FMCG Transportation Sector: Delphi-ISM Approach
authors: Induwara Sahasraka, Janaka Wijayanayake, Chathumi Kavirathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector in Sri Lanka depends on responsive transport, yet loss mileage (non-value-adding distance) increases fuel, labor, and maintenance costs and reduces fleet utilization. This paper proposes a decision support framework (DSF) to minimize loss mileage using a case-based Delphi-interpretive structural modeling (ISM) approach, triangulated with observations from transport management system (TMS) logs. A purposive expert panel (a panel of ten experts, each with more than 10 years of experience) refined 20 loss-mileage factors and provided pairwise judgments to form the structural self-interaction matrix, which was transformed into ISM reachability matrices and a MICMAC based driver-dependence classification. The results indicate that structural/ network and coordination/ technology factors such as distribution-network design, collaboration/consolidation practices, and logistics IT adoptionact as primary drivers, while operational issues including lowcapacity utilization and loading/unloading delays are dependent outcomes. The DSF converts these insights into staged intervention priorities: address key drivers first, mid-level enablers (e.g., maintenance reliability and driver capability), and monitor outcomes through routine TMS reporting. Facevalidity evaluation with the expert panel confirmed the framework's usability and practical relevance for Sri Lankan FMCG transport, and future work should quantify relationship strengths and embed DSF rules as TMS alerts.

title: Pre-Implementation Readiness for AI in the Success of ERP Implementation: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: N.T.D.T. Sandamini, T.D.K.L. Galappaththi, K.D.N. Dhananjana, M.B.J.M.P.N. Jayasekara, Ruwan Wickramarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems is reshaping enterprise operations, yet implementation failure rates remain high due to inadequate pre-implementation readiness. This study conducts a systematic literature review to examine readiness factors influencing the success of AI-enabled ERP implementations. Based on the analysis of 39 peer-reviewed studies published between 2014 and 2025, five dominant readiness dimensions are identified: technological, organizational, environmental, agile-process, and AI-ethical readiness. These dimensions are synthesized into an integrated Technology, Organization, Environment, Agility, AI Readiness (TOE-A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>IR) framework, extending traditional ERP readiness models by incorporating adaptability and ethical governance aligned with Industry 5.0 principles. The findings indicate that readiness for AI-enabled ERP is not a static condition, but a dynamic, multidimensional capability shaped by technology, leadership, agility, and ethical awareness. This study contributes by consolidating fragmented literature, advancing readiness theory for intelligent ERP systems, and offering a structured framework to support informed and responsible AI-driven ERP transformations.

title: Optimization and Benchmarking of Distribution Costs in a Sri Lankan Paint Manufacturing Company Using Vehicle Routing Problem Method
authors: WDT Alwis, PM Edirisinghe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This research examines the application of Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) algorithms to optimize the distribution operations of a well-recognized Paint manufacturer in Sri Lanka. The company's logistics relied generally on a manual process, and the logistics were done based on 21 regions island wide. Even though, the process is operationally smooth, it is inefficient in terms of mileage, fleet utilization, and fuel consumption. By using the Clarke-Wright Savings Algorithm and the Sweep Algorithm, two well established heuristic approaches are considered for this study in terms of developing a theoretical optimization model that tailored to the company's specific operational context and to provide a benchmark for the logistical operational cost of the company. Further, it gives an insight into the digital tools that can be utilized in their route planning. Substantial efficiency gains were revealed by a two-month comparative analysis of operational data. The data reported that in February, optimized routing achieved a 38.82% of mileage reduction <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(45,680 \text{km}$</tex> saved), while in the month of March, the figure increased up to 56.07% where it saved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$91,735 \text{km}$</tex>. It also contributed to reduce the number of trucks used in logistic operations and refined algorithmic performance. These savings are translated into fuel reductions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7, 0 2 7}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4, 1 1 3}$</tex> liters, respectively. Moreover, it amounted to cost saving of over 7 million rupees. Additionally, the optimized model suggested a potential 23.8% reduction in fleet size, importantly lowering maintenance, labor, and operational overhead. This study concludes that a hybrid approach while combining algorithmic planning with human operational insight. That can also preserve the company's service reliability and achieve long-term efficiency, cost savings, and sustainability.

title: Generative Rickshaw Art: Stylistic Fine-Tuning of Diffusion Models with Low-Rank Adaptation
authors: Susmita Mondal Sristi, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Generative diffusion models such as Stable Diffusion have achieved remarkable success in producing high-quality images across diverse artistic domains. However, most existing applications remain centered on globally recognized styles, while regional folk traditions remain underrepresented. This work introduces Rickshaw Art, a vibrant Bangladeshi folk-art tradition, into diffusion-based generative pipelines through lightweight finetuning with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). A curated dataset of authentic Rickshaw Art paintings was used to adapt Stable Diffusion v1.5 and SDXL, enabling the selective transfer of stylistic motifs onto objects and scenes. Prompt engineering strategies were employed to control style placement, ensuring that elements such as garments, architecture, and decorative objects could be adorned in Rickshaw Art patterns without overwhelming entire scenes. Model performance was evaluated using both quantitative measures and qualitative human assessment, demonstrating strong stylistic fidelity and cultural authenticity. Beyond technical contributions, this study highlights the role of generative AI in cultural preservation, extending underexplored art forms into digital creative ecosystems. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to integrate Bangladeshi Rickshaw Art into diffusion-based generative modeling, contributing both cultural novelty and technical value.

title: Privacy-Preserving Real-Time Crowd Counting on IoT Enabled Edge Devices Using YOLOv8n and Norfair
authors: W.T.D. Thilini Hansika, M.A. Pramod Madhawa, N.T.M. Sajith, M.N.M. Aashiq, M.N.A. Hinas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The implementation of smart crowd management and intelligent infrastructure systems necessitates real-time crowd monitoring to ensure safety, efficiency and responsiveness. Recent developments in deep learning methods and IoT devices have opened new avenues for addressing real-world problems. This study presents a lightweight framework for real-time crowd counting, that utilizes YOLOv8n model for human head detection and the Norfair tracking algorithm for multi-object tracking. The complete system is deployed on a Raspberry Pi 5 edge device connected to a USB camera for video input. Integrating deep learning models on edge devices significantly reduces reliance on high bandwidth data communication and cloud processing. A ThingsBoard-based dashboard provides real-time occupancy visualization and historical crowd data. Experimental evaluations conducted across diverse indoor and outdoor environments demonstrate up to 94% counting accuracy under varying lighting and crowd conditions. The proposed edge-AI solution is scalable, cost-effective and privacy-preserving, as it avoids raw video data transmission for smart crowd monitoring applications.

title: Monitoring Mental Stress Using Multimodal Physiological Signals from Wearable Device
authors: Md Rokonuzzaman Mim, Ashraf Al- Khalique, Mohammod Abdul Motin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Mental stress is closely linked to numerous medical and psychological disorders; early detection can help individuals take proactive steps to protect their well-being and maintain a creative and effective response to daily challenges. With the advent of wearable technologies and intelligent systems, machine learning-based approaches to stress detection have emerged as a promising solution to support early interventions. This study examines the feasibility of utilizing blood volume pulse (BVP) and electrodermal activity (EDA) signals collected from a wearable sensor to monitor stress automatically using machine learning models. Various machine learning algorithms were explored and evaluated for stress classifications. To improve classification performance, a comprehensive analysis of features in both the time domain and frequency domain was conducted. Using publicly available datasets, the proposed approach achieved a binary classification accuracy of 90.00 % and an F1-score of 0.88 on BVP data, while EDA-based classification attained an accuracy of 83.41 % and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 0.81. For the combined data using both BVP and EDA signals, the model achieved an accuracy of 95.10 % and an F1-score of 0.94, highlighting the effectiveness of multimodal integration. This study also explores a multimodal framework that integrates various domains of features, demonstrating promising potential in advancing the development of intelligent and real-time health-monitoring systems using wearable devices.

title: DMX: A Weighted Softmax Fusion of Heterogeneous CNNs for Burmese Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Faruk Ahmed, Saifuddin Sagor, Dewan Mamun Raza, Md Masum Billah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Burmese grape (Baccaurea ramiflora) crops are highly susceptible to leaf diseases, which significantly impact yield and quality. Accurate and early detection of these diseases is critical, yet conventional visual inspection and single-model deep learning approaches often lack robustness, fail to generalize under natural field conditions, and are sensitive to class imbalance. To overcome these challenges, we propose DMX, a novel weighted softmax score fusion framework that strategically combines three complementary convolutional neural networks, DenseNet201, MobileNetV2, and Xception, leveraging their unique strengths in depth, efficiency, and feature extraction. The framework assigns optimized weights to individual model predictions, integrating diverse feature representations for enhanced robustness and precision. A primary dataset of 4,063 field-collected images was preprocessed with resizing to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224 \times 3$</tex> pixels, normalization, and quality enhancement, and class imbalance was mitigated using SMOTE. Comprehensive ablation studies highlight the contribution of each base model and input resolution, confirming the superiority of the weighted ensemble over single models. Experimental results demonstrate that DMX achieves 99.33 % overall accuracy, with per-class F1-scores up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0 0}$</tex>, outperforming all individual models. These findings establish DMX as a highly precise, computationally efficient, and generalizable framework, offering a practical solution for real-world deployment in smart agriculture.

title: Securing Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks Using Quantum Sensing: Open Issues, Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Ubaid Ullah, Lubna Luxmi Dhirani
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs) face persistent challenges such as high attenuation, limited bandwidth, and environmental interference, which constrain their reliability and performance. This survey investigates the potential of quantum sensing technologies to overcome these limitations. We systematically review and analyze quantum-enhanced detection methods, entanglement-based communication protocols, and quantum magnetometry for underwater applications. Our study highlights how quantum techniques can conceptually improve signal detection, extend communication range, and enhance positioning accuracy. By comparing existing surveys, identifying key research gaps, and outlining a roadmap for practical implementation, this work provides a comprehensive perspective on advancing UWSNs through quantum technologies.

title: Exploring Key Drivers of User Satisfaction in Mobile E-Grocery Applications: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Muhammad Lizuardi, Sastra Dinar Bayu Sasmita, Christopher Joshua Leksana, Stefanus Rumangkit
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study seeks to examine the determinants of user satisfaction in post-purchase behavior of mobile e-grocery applications by investigating Performance Expectancy, Effort Expectancy, Behavioral Intention, and Intention to Recommend. A quantitative methodology was utilized, relying on primary data as the principal source. Data were gathered via an online questionnaire. The sample had 256 respondents, as calculated by SPSS Sample Power. This research employed a stratified random sample method, analyzed with SPSS software. The analysis utilized Partial Least Squares-Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The results demonstrate that performance expectancy and effort expectancy do not significantly affect behavioral intention. perceived usefulness significantly positively affects behavioral intention, which in turn significantly and positively effects the intention to recommend.

title: Nile Tilapia Pathogens Detection Using CNN Model Infused with Efficient Channel Attention Mechanism
authors: Md. Enamul Haque, Alok Das, Mohammad Nurul Huda, Md. Rayhan Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Nile Tilapia, a highly resilient fish species crucial to aquaculture, faces significant health threats from various infectious diseases. These diseases are caused by bacterial, viral, and parasitic pathogens that affect fish health, productivity, and overall sustainability, as the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) reported. Parasitic infections from monogeneans, streptococcosis from Streptococcus agalactiae, columnaris disease from Flavobacterium columnare, Tilapia Lake Virus (TiLV), and Motile Aeromonad Septicemia (MAS) from Aeromonas species and so on. Effective disease treatment in aquaculture depends on prompt and accurate identification due to the overlapping symptoms of these infections. Though reliable, traditional diagnostic methods, like PCR and histology, can be time-consuming and require specialized equipment and expertise, limiting their accessibility. In this study, we used several pre-trained deep learning models to address the identification challenge, including VGG16, MobileNetV2, DenseNet201, ResNet101, and ResNet152 for disease detection in Nile Tilapia. Additionally, we proposed a customized CNN model enhanced with an Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) mechanism to focus on the most relevant features in the input data. Our proposed model demonstrated strong generalization capabilities, achieving a training accuracy of 99.85%, validation accuracy of 93.44%, and 96.86% test accuracy with precision 96.08%, recall 95.8%, and f1-score of 95.88% on the Enhancing Disease Detection in Nile Tilapia (EDDNT) dataset. For the ablation study we have employed Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), Squeeze and Excitation Module (SE-Net) with CNN model and Dense layers.

title: Electrohysterogram-Based Identification of Gestational Period Variations in Uterine Electrical Activity
authors: Muhammad Omar Cheema, Anum Rashid, Zia Mohy-Ud-Din, Jahan Zeb Gul, Hassan Ali, Muhammad Zeeshan Ul Haque
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Electrohysterography (EHG) is a noninvasive technique of measuring uterine electrophysiological activity, through which the functional maturation of the myometrium in pregnancy is assessed. This paper focuses on examining gestational age-related changes in the electrical behavior of uterine during pregnancy, through the application of high-order signal decomposition and feature selection processes. The records of EHG were obtained in 24 pregnant women (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22-43$</tex> weeks of gestation) in a BIOPAC MP 36 system under standardized clinical conditions. The records of each of the three channels were first processed by a bandpass filter (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.08-4.0 ~\text{Hz}$</tex>) to isolate uterine activity and decomposed by Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) via Continuous Wavelet Transformer (CWT) validation. Various temporal and spectral characteristics were calculated out of every mode, such as energy, root mean square (RMS), instantaneous frequency, and entropy. The statistical comparison demonstrated that the energy of the third VMD mode of Channel 1 (M3 energy) had a strong positive correlation with the gestational age (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.4528, p=0.0263$</tex>), and it showed a significant progressive increase in the energy of the uterine electrical energy at term. The KruskalWallis test (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}=0.1877$</tex>) was not significant because of a small sample, but the Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex> indicated a statistically significant change in the position of physiological escalation of contractile readiness (week 37-38, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d=-0.15$</tex>). These observations indicate that VMD-based features have the capacity to represent significant electrophysiological maturation of the uterus and indicate their possible use as quantitative biomarkers of gestational monitoring and preterm risk.

title: Design of a Cost-Effective Analog Solar Charge Controller Based on LM358 for Off-Grid Applications with Integrated Battery Protection
authors: Ratin Barua, Rakibul Hasan, Aditi Bhattacharjee, Mohammad Tazrian Taharin, Md. Imam Uddin Bhuiyan, Ratul Barua, Bivas Paul, Dolon Mutsuddi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents the design, construction, and evaluation of a cost-effective analog solar charge controller optimized for off-grid photovoltaic (PV) systems. The controller employs the LM358 operational amplifier configured as a voltage comparator to regulate charging and discharging through relay actuation. Essential protections, including overvoltage cutoff, deep discharge prevention, and short-circuit isolation, are achieved using simple, reliable circuitry implemented on a custom PCB with cost-effective components. Experimental testing under real sunlight demonstrated stable charging performance, with a peak current of 3.5 A and effective regulation for a 12 V lead-acid battery. This work addresses the gap in providing an affordable, repairable alternative to available PWM and expensive controllers for mini standalone systems, especially for rural markets. To the best of our knowledge, it is among the first documented LM358-based analog Solar Charge Controllers (SCC) integrating multiple protections in one design. The unique application of LM358 in this context offers an accessible approach to solar energy management where simplicity, affordability, and durability are paramount.

title: Analysis of Double Gate AlGaN/GaN HEMT with Increasing Quantum Wells for Emerging Electronics Applications
authors: Padmakshya Kar, Nitish Kumar, Niranjan Jyoti Borah, Trupti R. Lenka, Sudhanshu Choudhary, Hieu P. T. Nguyen
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This research presents a detailed DC analysis of a normally-on double-gate AlGaN/GaN HEMT integrated with one, two, and three quantum wells in the channel. The study shows how embedding multiple quantum wells improves carrier confinement and enhances gate control. As the number of wells increases, the device exhibits a favourable shift in threshold voltage, higher drain current, and optimized channel modulation. The appearance of a second slope in transfer characteristics confirms sequential activation of the wells, enabling multi-channel transport. While a minor drop in transconductance is observed for the two quantum well case, the three quantum well structure balances confinement and mobility, offering superior current capability for emerging high-power and RF electronics applications.

title: Innovative Technologies, Smart Manufacturing, and Machine Learning in Semiconductor Supply Chain
authors: Tsung Yao Wen, Yuanbo Wang, Adrian Crisan, Angelo Pinto
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Modern semiconductor supply chains face unprecedented complexity that requires smarter strategies across companies. Leverage of automation tools and new advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) can be beneficial for enhanced efficiency and stability. To this end, this paper introduces a practical framework focused on three key areas: engineering, planning, and operations. With the automated processes and Machine Learning (ML) integration in each component, manufacturers can significantly improve overall workflow efficiency and system resilience. By pinpointing high-impact business areas for optimization, the proposed framework has demonstrated its ability to identify issues in a timely manner, enable targeted technology deployment, and provide actionable insights for building intelligent and adaptive supply chain solutions in volatile markets.

title: Design and Analysis of Button Shaped Microstrip Patch Antenna for Wireless Capsule Endoscopy in Biotelemetry
authors: Sumaya Sraboni, Fateha Samad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper is designed based on a unique microstrip patch antenna for wireless capsule endoscopy (WCE). The dimension of the proposed antenna is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \times 25 \times 0.9 \text{mm}^{3}$</tex> where the selected material of the substrate is FR-4 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{\mathrm{r}}=4.3$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\delta=0.025$</tex>). The patch of this antenna is prepared by the copper material. Moreover, a partial modified ground plane has been designed on the substrate. For WCE, a capsule is introduced including the dimension of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$26 \times 11 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. In this work, the capsule is made of polyimide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\epsilon_{\mathrm{r}}=3.5$</tex>) material. CST Studio Suite software is used for designing and analyzing the antenna for free space, into the capsule shell, and inside phantom model. The proposed antenna gives resonant frequency of 5.576 GHz with - 10 dB, for the bandwidth of 3 GHz where the range is from 5.5 GHz to 6 GHz. For this antenna, omnidirectional radiation pattern has been observed and it shows circular polarization inside the phantom model. In addition, the maximum radiation efficiency is found as −28.4 dB. The specific absorption rate (SAR) in the gastrointestinal tract is also measured to ensure that it does not harm any body tissues during the operation.

title: Reviewing Model Collapse and Countermeasures
authors: Xihao Xie, Beichen Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Driven by massive amounts of web-scale data, generative AI (GenAI) has achieved remarkable progress, enabling various applications in diverse sectors. The advances of GenAI have actuated practitioners to use AI-synthesized data for training next-generation AI models. Undeniably, using synthetic data has alleviated the increasing stringent demand for data supply. Unfortunately, it also introduces a new critical issue: in a selfconsuming cycle between model and data, the model ultimately collapse, raising more trustworthiness concerns to GenAI. In recent years, increasingly more studies have investigated the phenomenon of model collapse (MC) and explored potential solutions to mitigate it. However, the review of the phenomenon of MC still remains blank. To fill this gap, this paper provides an up-to-date overview of these studies for consolidating and reviewing the progress of MC in different application scenarios and countermeasures for mitigating MC. We also highlight challenges and future research opportunities.

title: Investigation of Switching Characteristics of Ferroelectric Tunnel Junctions by TCAD Simulation
authors: Jingchao Lei, Qiang Zhang, Di Lu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: A systematic, quantitative study of switching characteristics of ferroelectric tunneling junctions (FTJs) is investigated by using technology computer-aided design (TCAD) simulations. This study reveals the relationship between the on-off ratio, one of the most key FTJ performance factor, and multiple design factors, such as work function / carrier concentration of the electrodes, and electron affinity / remnant polarization (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf>) of the ferroelectric layer. Given that the remnant polarization of the ultrathin ferroelectric layer has a fundamental significance for the performance of the FTJs yet difficult to be directly measured, we show the deduction of the P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> of actual Cu-BaTiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>-La<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.67</inf>Sr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.33</inf>MnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> FTJs by TCAD simulation, as an application. Furthermore, a MOSFET-FTJ 1 transistor - 1 resistor (1T1R) structure was simulated, providing references for the future construction of FTJ arrays in practical applications.

title: AI-Based Flight Trajectory Optimization Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Achyutha S Nijigal, Balasubramanyam B S, Sripad Kulkarni S
abstract: Flight trajectory optimization is a major challenge in modern aviation due to increasing air traffic density, rising fuel costs, and strict operational constraints. Traditional trajectory planning approaches rely on static optimization techniques that are unable to adapt effectively to dynamic airspace environments. In this paper, an AI-based flight trajectory planning framework is proposed using Reinforcement Learning (RL). The trajectory optimization problem is formulated as a Markov Decision Process where multiple RL agents are trained with different reward functions to optimize fuel consumption, arrival delay, and a balanced objective combining both. Experimental results show that the balanced agent achieves an effective trade-off between fuel efficiency and delay reduction. The proposed framework demonstrates the potential of reinforcement learning for adaptive and intelligent air traffic management systems.

title: Hierarchical Vision-Controlled Quadruped Robot for Autonomous Human Detection and Localization in Disaster Response
authors: Faiaz Ahsan, Najeeb Ahmed Bhuiyan, Md. Al Tas Did Ul, Md. Jubair Ahamed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Search-and-rescue (SAR) operations in collapsed buildings and rubble-strewn environments pose significant challenges for autonomous systems, where wheeled and aerial robots often fail due to limited mobility or sensing constraints. To address this gap, we present a compact spider-inspired quadruped robot designed for confined-space SAR. The robot combines multi-degree-of-freedom (DOF) legged locomotion with onboard computer vision and Global Positioning System (GPS)-based reporting. A hierarchical perception-control pipeline integrates real-time human detection and tracking using a lightweight object detection model on a high-level device with gait execution through inverse kinematics on a low-level device. The system autonomously alternates between search and track states, enabling both exploratory coverage and persistent victim following in cluttered spaces. Upon target lock, GPS localization is employed to provide situational awareness to rescuers. Experiments conducted demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of integrating adaptable legged mobility, vision-guided detection, and localization into a lightweight robotic platform. The proposed approach establishes a practical foundation for deploying quadruped robots as ground assets in disaster response missions.

title: Deep Learning-Based Blood Cell Classification for Cancer Detection Using ResNet50
authors: Poonam Sharma, Priyanka Nanda, Meenakshi Sharma, Shikha Verma, Lakshay Kheterpal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research focuses on ResNet50, a deep learning algorithm used for the classification of blood cells automatically, with the types including Basophil, Erythroblast, Monocyte, Myeloblast, and Seg_Neutrophil. The high-resolution 5000 images present in the used dataset belong to Kaggle and were pre-processed using resizing, normalization, and augmentation. Twenty epochs of training were done using the Adam optimizer and binary cross-entropy loss. The evaluation provided a total accuracy of 86% and precision, recall, and F1-scores of over 0.85 for each class. The number of misclassified results from Erythroblast to Monocyte and vice versa was very few due to overlapping morphological features in the confusion matrix. Analysis of the training and validation losses revealed stable learning with little over-fitting. The research establishes that deep learning can successfully automate the classification of blood cells and has potential in speeding up the process of manual diagnosis. Future improvement can be made with ensemble learning, more complete data collection, and integration in real-time with the clinical setting to improve the generalizability and explainability of the model, and make cancer detection with AI more reliable and accessible.

title: Requirement-Aware ATS Optimization through an LLM-Driven Approach
authors: Iffat Ara, Md. Sazzadur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) are essential for matching resumes with job postings, but existing systems often rely on keyword-based methods that miss semantic and contextual relationships. Recent Large Language Model (LLM)-based approaches have been applied to resume-job matching, yet they do not significantly improve performance because they cannot fully leverage relational context. To address these limitations, this study proposes a deep learning pipeline that combines Graph Attention Networks (GAT) with DistilBERT embeddings and LLaMA 7B feedback to capture semantic relationships between resumes and job postings. The pipeline begins by generating contextual embeddings with DistilBERT, followed by a GAT module to model relational interactions. LLaMA 7B feedback is then incorporated to refine semantic alignment between resumes and job postings. A job-level based fixed weighting scheme is applied, where skills are emphasized for junior roles and experience/expertise for senior roles. This weighting guides a second GAT aggregation step, allowing the model to refine role-specific embeddings before computing the final similarity score. Experimental results show that the pipeline improves cosine similarity scores by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 1 2 \%}$</tex> compared to the GAT baseline and achieves category-specific gains. The proposed pipeline outperforms existing LLM-based approaches and provides more robust, scalable, and requirement-aware ATS scoring.

title:
Investigation of Oxidative Doping in Multilayer WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    FETs
authors: Suwen Song, Xin Feng, Zeyang Ren, Weihang Zhang, Xianhe Liu, Hong Zhou, Zhihong Liu, Yue Hao, Jincheng Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, a novel spatially controlled oxidative doping technique is proposed to prepare the high performance 2D p-type WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FETs, which are based on the bottom-contact and back-gated configuration. To find out the optical device, five kinds of device structure are adopted with full top WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation, full bottom WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation, dual-side WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation, patterned top-layer WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> oxidation and patterned bottom-layer WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> oxidation. It is found that all the oxidative doping WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FETs show a high hole injection efficiency with a maximum on-state current of 35.1 μA/μm on the dual-side WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FET. Besides, the patterned top oxidation WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FET achieves a good gate control (SS:142mV/dec) and 4-fold current growth of 10.9 μA/μm, offering an optimal performance. This study provides key insights for designing high-performance 2D p-type transistors.

title: Towards Real-Time Anomaly Detection in Robotic Manipulators Through Multi-Sensor Fusion with Machine Learning
authors: Khandokar Sakhawat Hossen, Sumaiya Kabir Shashin, Tawfiq Mahmud Khan, Ariful Islam Riad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Anomalies within robotic applications can arise from hardware faults, environmental changes or unexpected operational deviations. Current research in this field has typically employed single-sensor monitoring or computationally intensive deep learning approaches, limiting the scalability of resourceconstrained low-power embedded systems in the industrial arena. To provide a more efficient and affordable solution in anomalous system detection, this study propose a multi-sensor fusion framework that combines multiple data streams like current, vibration, and temperature modalities with supervised machine learning models. The experimental dataset was collected from a 4DOF hobby servo-based robotic manipulator, where the proposed models were trained and validated for anomaly classification. A comparative evaluation of multiple classifiers is conducted, where the Random Forest (rf) model achieves the best performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8 \%}$</tex> accuracy and an f1-score of 97. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach provides a balance between computational complexity and detection reliability, making it well-suited for real-time application in industrial robotic systems.

title: Acceptance of Artificial Intelligence and Future Italian Physicians’ Perspectives: a cross-sectional descriptive survey
authors: Valentina De Nicolò, Davide La Torre, Ignazio Iacone
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly shaping clinical reasoning and healthcare delivery, yet evidence on physicians’ acceptance, trust, and ethical expectations remains limited. This nationwide cross-sectional study investigated the perceptions of 293 Italian physicians, the majority of whom were hospital-based specialists, regarding the integration of AI into clinical practice. Although 78.5% had previously used AI tools, only 4.8% had received formal training, revealing a significant educational gap. Respondents expressed a cautiously optimistic attitude toward AI, particularly recognizing its potential to enhance diagnostic accuracy and support therapeutic decision-making, while showing moderate agreement on its ability to reduce workload. Persistent reservations were linked to limited trust in autonomous AI outputs and fears of professional displacement. Factor analysis identified three clusters influencing acceptance: primary facilitators such as usability and clinical relevance; secondary enablers including trustworthiness, safety, transparency, and privacy protection; and minor inhibitors related to insufficient expertise, limited peer endorsement, and perceived loss of autonomy. Ethical expectations were notably high, with strong agreement on the need for defined medico-legal responsibilities, independent ethical evaluation, and validation of clinical reliability. Participants also emphasized the importance of equity in AI deployment, regulatory harmonization, and targeted professional education. Overall, Italian physicians appear open to the adoption of AI in medicine, provided that human oversight, fairness, and trust remain central. Bridging the current gaps in training, governance, and ethical regulation is essential to foster a healthcare environment in which AI complements—rather than replaces—human clinical judgement.

title: Enhancing Network Security with a Two-Layered Machine Learning-Based Intrusion Detection Framework
authors: Zakia Sultana, Md. Saiful Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) face significant exposure to cyber-attacks because of their limited processing capabilities and distributed operating environment. Existing intrusion detection systems (IDS) typically offer either lightweight anomaly detection at the edge or depend on cloud based deep learning models that require substantial computational resources, which restricts their suitability for real-time use and for handling multiple attack categories. This study focuses on developing a hybrid IDS that can deliver fast detection at the edge while also achieving reliable multi-class attack classification in the cloud, all with reduced computational overhead. The proposed design includes a feedforward neural network at the edge for binary classification (Normal vs. Intrusion) and an encoder decoder model in the cloud for identifying different attack types, namely Flooding, TDMA, Grayhole, and Blackhole. Experiments conducted on the WSN-DS dataset show an overall accuracy of 99.57%, with macro-average precision, recall, and F1-score all above 0.94. The results indicate that the system is scalable, energy efficient, and well suited for real-time WSN applications that require both speed and high detection accuracy.

title: From Sensors to Decisions: A Distributed Software-Hardware Stack for Hydroponic Cultivation
authors: Komninos Lazaros Lekkas, Antonios Mavrakis, Achilles D. Boursianis, Charalampos Bratsas, Sotirios K. Goudos, Maria S. Papadopoulou
abstract: Hydroponic cultivation offers substantial gains in water-management efficiency, plant growth rate, and input utilization, yet dependable operation requires continuous supervision of chemical and environmental variables (pH, Total Dissolved Solids/Electrical Conductivity TDS/EC, water temperature, flow, water level, ambient temperature, and relative humidity T/RH). Manual monitoring is error-prone and slow to react, while many low-cost systems lack real-time sensing, automated control, and integrated analytics, limiting reliability and scalability. This work presents Hydroponic Ecosystem (HECO), a low-cost, IoT-enabled hydroponics platform that unifies multi-parameter environmental variables, local safety control, and cloud-backed supervision with a single mobile application for managing multiple installations. Long-running trials demonstrate stable telemetry, responsive closed-loop actuation, and robust behavior under transient sensor and network faults. The modular design of the platform, secure messaging, and edge-prioritized safety make it a practical foundation for scalable, data-driven hydroponic management and a reusable reference for embedded IoT control systems.

title: Gate-All-Around Nanosheet Oxide Semiconductor Transistor with Crystalline InGaOx
authors: Masaharu Kobayashi, Anlan-Chen, Ki-Woong Park, Kota Sakai, Sunbin Hwang, Xingyu Huang, Takuya Saraya, Toshiro Hiramoto, Takanori Takahashi, Mutsunori Uenuma, Yukiharu Uraoka
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, we introduce our recent research progress on gate-all-around nanosheet (GAA NS) oxide semiconductor (OS) FETs for LSI applications such as 3D DRAM and monolithic 3D integration. We demonstrated performance enhancement of poly-crystalline InOx FET by Ga-doping in ALD process and anneal with mobility of 81cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/Vs, novel technique of selective crystallization for the InGaOx in an OS stack, and process integration and device operation of a GAA NS InGaOx (IGO) FET. Transconductance of 689μS/μm has been achieved in the GAA NS IGO FETs. Bias-stress reliability is significantly improved by GAA NS FET. This work provides a direction for scalable high-performance OS FETs toward LSI applications.

title: DTKD: A Distance-Based Framework for Efficient Maintenance of Top-k Dominating Queries on Incomplete Data
authors: Kh. Khaleduzzaman, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Efficiently handling Top-K Dominance (TKD) queries on incomplete data is key for real-world applications where decisions rely on finding the best objects in large datasets with missing values. Traditional methods struggle because they rely heavily on pairwise comparisons, which increase complexity and lead to cyclic dominance problems, particularly with large sparse datasets. This often slows things down, reduces accuracy, and makes it difficult to manage large-scale datasets effectively. In this paper, we offer Distance-Based TKD (DTKD), a new framework that boosts both the speed and accuracy of TKD query processing. We use an Ideal Point to pick the Top-K dominant objects, with Euclidean Distance (ED) as a simple way to figure out the rankings. Unlike older approaches, DTKD skips pairwise comparisons, cutting down on computing effort and improving how well it scales with different data sizes and levels of sparsity. This work introduces a smart factor normalization technique that tackles the unevenness caused by missing data. This keeps distance calculations fair and consistent, even when some attributes are incomplete, ensuring steady and reliable results across various conditions. We tested this approach on real-world and synthetic datasets, showing that it saves a lot of processing time while still delivering accurate dominance outcomes. These findings highlight its potential for practical tools, such as decision support or recommender systems, where data gaps are a common challenge. The ability to handle diverse data scenarios makes the algorithm a promising solution for modern data-driven approaches, especially for incomplete data.

title: Development of Quantum Computers with Electron Spins in Silicon Quantum Dots
authors: Takashi Nakajima
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Silicon is an excellent platform for integrating billions of quantum dots that serve as artificial atoms on a chip. We aim to use the electron spin state in a quantum dot as a qubit to realize a quantum computer, which potentially outperforms traditional supercomputers in certain tasks. However, practical quantum computation requires precise control of fragile qubit states and quantum error correction. We have investigated dominant error sources and implemented qubit control with low error rates, which opens the path toward fault-tolerant quantum computation.

title: Flexible electrochemical devices integrating zinc-manganese oxide battery with symmetric supercapacitor
authors: Zanxiang Nie, Pritesh Hiralal, Gehan A.J. Amaratunga, Yue Liu, Shiwei Liu, Shiqiang Luo
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The realization of a unified energy storage device that synergistically combines the high-power attributes of supercapacitors with the high-energy characteristics of batteries represents a pivotal advancement toward enabling robust and versatile energy solutions for flexible electronics. Herein, we present a novel approach involving the electrodeposition of Zn and MnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> onto activated carbon electrodes within a symmetric supercapacitor configuration. This strategy facilitates the seamless integration of the supercapacitor with a Zn-MnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> battery system. The resultant integrated hybrid device exhibits superior overall performance compared to its individual battery and supercapacitor counterparts operating in isolation. Furthermore, the incorporation of this auxiliary battery system is demonstrated to serve as an effective mechanism for preventing overcharging in aqueous supercapacitors through precise control of the charging voltage.

title: AMAF: An Adaptive and Coupling-Sensitive Framework for Cross-Language Software Maintainability Analysis
authors: P. Vishaliney
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The assessment of software maintainability is vital for controlling development costs and technical debt, thereby supporting long-term software evolution. However, existing maintainability measures and composite indices are typically based on isolated metrics, fixed aggregation schemes, or language-specific tools. As a result, they fail to adequately capture cognitive effort, design-level dependencies, and the nonlinear maintainability risks inherent in modern multi-language systems. This study proposes the Adaptive Maintainability Assessment Framework (AMAF), a language-independent and interpretable composite measure of software maintainability. AMAF integrates cognitive complexity, structural complexity, software size, maintainability indicators, and coupling within a unified framework. The framework employs dynamic metric weighting to adaptively increase or decrease the influence of individual metrics, along with a non-linear coupling penalty to model disproportionate maintenance costs arising from intercomponent dependencies. The use of a language-neutral intermediate representation enables the consistent extraction of metrics across Java, C, and C# using a common methodology. Empirical evaluation, based on functionally equivalent implementations and sensitivity analysis, demonstrates that AMAF provides a stable, consistent, and more realistic assessment of maintainability compared to conventional linear aggregation models.

title: A Slope Calibration Technique for Time-mode Delay Tap Accuracy Improvement
authors: Aikaterini I. Chronopoulou, Konstantinos P. Pagkalos, Spyridon Vlassis
abstract: In this paper, a calibration topology for the unity delay tap suitable for time-mode signal processing is proposed. The delay tap is based on the constant slope discharging capacitor concept which is used in time-domain time-mode signal processing. The proposed topology calibrates the impact of both process and temperature variations improving the dynamic range and the accuracy of the delay tap. It contains a digital loop that calibrates the discharging slope. The novelty of this work is that the slope calibration is achieved by adjusting the supply voltage of a driving CMOS inverter avoiding the perturbation of the signal path. All circuits are designed using the 65 nm TSMC process under a supply voltage of 1.2 V. The current consumption of the delay tap is 50μW for a sampling frequency 100MHz. The accuracy of the delay tap after calibration is better than 2.5ps for 80% of the input full scale range. The input range of the delay tap dramatically improves compared to the uncalibrated tap.

title: Location Allocation for Parcel Lockers with P-Median Model: A Case Study from Türkiye
authors: Elifnaz Olgac, Ali Karasan, Ihsan Kaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: As delivery technologies have advanced, novel last mile delivery (LMD) channels have been emerged in recent years. Among these channels, parcel lockers stand out, as they reduce the number of failed delivery attempts in addition to offering cost-effective and efficient delivery experience. Location selection is a key concept for establishing a parcel locker network, as it directly influences ability of the demand coverage, required initial investment and compliance to any additional constraints. For this reason, the location allocation problem for parcel lockers should be addressed with a systematic and quantitative methodology. This study focuses on parcel locker location selection for Beşiktaş, a district of İstanbul, Türkiye which is densely populated and presents highly challenging urban conditions. Within this context, parcel locker allocation problem is solved with a modified p-median model, while the demand points are obtained from geographical information systems and the candidate service points are identified through k -means clustering algorithm. The results obtained provide a fundamental basis for establishing a parcel locker system in Beşiktaş. Moreover, traditional capacitated p-median model is enhanced with additional constraints to satisfy the demand coverage, providing a more effective initial solution than the traditional model.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of a Slotted Dual-Band MIMO Antenna for X-Band Satellite Communication
authors: Hrittik Raj Barua, Arpan Dey, Imtiaz Akber Chowdhury
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study introduces a dual-band MIMO antenna designed for X-band satellite communication systems. The antenna is fabricated on a Rogers RO4003 substrate with overall dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 ~\text{mm} \times 30 ~\text{mm} \times 0.508 ~\text{mm}$</tex>. The radiating element, composed of copper, incorporates I-shaped and U- shaped stubs along with a centrally embedded circular slot to enhance resonance characteristics. Excitation is provided through a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0} \boldsymbol{\Omega}$</tex> microstrip feed line. Electromagnetic simulations were performed using CST Studio Suite, and the results demonstrate dual resonances at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 6 5 ~ G H z}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9. 5 9 5}$</tex> GHz. The proposed design achieves a return loss of -27.63 dB, a gain of 2.98 dBi, and a directivity of 6.59 dBi for 8.65 GHz and a return loss of -33.985 dB, a gain of 5.51 dBi, and a directivity of 7.08 dBi for 9.595 GHz respectively. These outcomes confirm the antenna's suitability for compact, high-performance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{X}$</tex>-band satellite communication applications. Furthermore, the analysis was extended to MIMO configurations, where different alignments for MIMO antennas were represented and analyzed via simulation. The Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC) remains consistently low across the observed frequency range, reaching values as small as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.4025 \times 10^{-5}$</tex> between 8.65 and 9.595 GHz.

title: Depolarization Field Induced Threshold Voltage Variability in Junctionless Non-Volatile Memory Devices
authors: Dhruv Ganesh, Neeraj Samant, Aarya Ameet Agavekar, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work presents a detailed analysis of depolarization field (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dep</inf>) induced threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>) variability in junctionless (JL) ferroelectric (Fe) field-effect transistors (JL FeFETs) for non-volatile memory applications through well calibrated simulations. Statistical simulations were performed to examine the impact of process induced fluctuation in film thickness (T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">si</inf>) and Fe parameters such as Fe thickness (T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">fe</inf>), coercive field (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>), and remanent polarization (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf>) on the threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>) of programmed (PRG) and erased (ERS) states. The results show that a high channel doping (N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>) and higher gate work-function (φ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>) enhance E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dep</inf> in the pristine state, leading to polarization suppression and wider V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> dispersion due to Fe parameters variation. In contrast, reducing the N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf> and tuning the φ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf> near to mid-gap values mitigate E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dep</inf>, improving polarization stability and lowering σV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> corresponding to PRG and ERS states. The results, for the first time, highlight the crucial role of controlling E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dep</inf> in achieving reliable and scalable JL FeFET based memory device.

title: Malaria Detection from RBC Images Using Multi-Functional CNN with Explainable AI
authors: Md. Mahatab Sakib, Md. Omaer Goni, Md. Monjor Morshed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deep Learning techniques are widely implemented to extract various features from image data. To detect malaria parasites from Red Blood Cell images we can leverage this technology. Several prior studies relied on generic CNNs and neighboring models to capture mainstream features from RBC. Whereas some works omitted attention mechanisms and interpretability. This led to the need for a specific architecture to integrate most of the important abilities of machine learning models i.e., decision explainability, accuracy enhancement by fine-tuning, omitting redundant data etc. through a novel framework. The primary objective is to capture multi-scale features from image data while keeping less complexity that uses multi-branch CNN architecture based on truncated ResNet18 & evaluate its performance using crossvalidation. Neighboring models such as plain ResNet18, CNN and ResNet50 lag in confirming multi-scale reasoning and attention mechanisms, vital for detecting a parasite in a cell. Total 26,161 accurate image samples were split into 80% for 5-fold training & 20% for testing over them. The proposed model reached average test accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.61 \% \pm 0.06 \%$</tex> and a nearby precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score. A clinician can verify the model's logic by help of Grad-CAM as Explainable AI which generates heatmaps over the most important regions influencing the model's decision. It also offers a compact model consisting of 1.08 million parameters that requires a smaller number of resources. Socially, this automated model ought to fasten treatment in regions with scarce resources, thereby supporting global disease control goals.

title: Forecasting India's Exports to the United States Under Exchange Rate Fluctuations Using VECM and LSTM Models
authors: Dulanji Samarakoon, Dilushika Anton Michael, Ruwantha Perera, Yasmi Rajapaksha, Kavindi Vinurika, Anuradha Hewaarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: As a leading economy in South Asia, India plays a central role in shaping the region's trade dynamics, making its export patterns and currency movements highly influential for neighbouring countries and overall regional economic stability. This study explores the relationship between Indian exports to the United States and the USD/INR exchange rate. These two factors have a strong positive relationship with a correlation of 0.83, suggesting a potential long-run relationship suitable for multivariate modelling. A Vector Error Correction Model (VECM) was initially employed to analyse this relationship, but the results were unreliable. Alternatively, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) deep learning model was used, which better captured the nonlinear patterns and offered higher performance accuracy with an R -squared of 0.7536. The findings highlight how currency changes can affect export performance, offering useful insights for business leaders, investors, traders, and policymakers not only in India but also in South Asian countries, especially Sri Lanka and Bangladesh. This research supports the idea that combining classical statistical models with modern methods can improve understanding of real-world economic trends. The study also opens the door for future research on how changes in U.S. trade policies and tariffs may affect exports from South Asia across different countries.

title: An Improved End-to-End Deep Learning-Based Approach to Steering Angle Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Md. Shakib Hasan Rudro, Faria Parvin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles rely heavily on accurate perception of roads and control systems to ensure safe navigation in a dynamic environment. The traditional modular approach to the system is developing perception, prediction, and planning in separate models then connecting them with the framework. This increases the likelihood of error propagation, computational cost, and probability of the vehicle drifting away from the lane. However, an end-to-end learning approach, where the system takes raw images as input and delivers control actions as output, can be a viable alternative. This study proposes a deep learning-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model and a transfer learning-based VGG16 model for end-to-end steering angle prediction and compares them to the state-of-the-art NVIDIA PilotNet baseline. Additionally, it introduces temporal information to the model to ensure vehicle stability on the lane. Microsoft AirSim was used for data acquisition, evaluation, and testing of the proposed model. During the test run, the CNN and VGG16 model achieved an autonomy of 92 % and 94 %, respectively. Both the proposed CNN and VGG16 models achieve up to 10 % higher autonomy than the NVIDIA PilotNet baseline while requiring approximately 40 % fewer training samples. This highlights the potential for CNN-based end-to-end autonomous driving systems.

title: Robust Dual-Domain ANN-Based Compact MOSFET Modeling Across Technology Nodes
authors: Abdullah M.H Cachhi, Sidhant V Vazarkar, Shreyan Ghosh, Girish Pahwa
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents an artificial neural network model for the current-voltage and capacitance-voltage characteristics of advanced transistor technology nodes. We propose a dual-domain loss function combining ratio and log-scale error terms for multi-scale current modeling. Different activation functions are evaluated, including tanh, ISRU (alpha values: 0.5, 1.0, 2.0), and Mish. The results show that tanh delivers the best current-voltage performance with smooth third-order derivatives, while Mish significantly outperforms other activations for capacitance-voltage modeling through self-regularization, superior gradient flow, and infinite differentiability. Training on BSIM-CMG data for 1000 epochs with early stopping achieves high accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: An Efficient Algorithmic Approach to Modernize Photo Negatives to Digital Images
authors: Waleej Haider, Muhammad Nadeem, Muhammad Kashif Shaikh, Sallar Khan, Haris Ahmed, Abdul Hameed Pitafi
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Many families have preserved images of their forebears in the form of photo negatives so that they might introduce their children to their elders and have them always remain in their minds. But the technique to transform photo negatives into images is already obsolete. It is important to give application based solution to convert these photo negatives into digital photos so that individuals can preserve these assets. On the other hand, it is difficult to find or detect images of particular persons from a big data. To cope up with the problems, an application based solution named “SMART IMAGESHOP” has been proposed. It is enable to transform negative images into high-definition photos. With advanced search functionalities, the application can get input image(s) from the gallery or using camera of the device and find all the relevant photos from a big data. This application is based on two algorithms to perform both tasks. The first algorithms not only facilitates users to obtain a photo negative as an input and transform it into a positive photo but also offers the features to crop, resize and resolution improvement of the created photo. The second algorithm is able to detect all photos from the big data based on input image(s). To test the performance of the proposed algorithms, 50 photo negatives from the dataset was used as an input. The application has successfully transformed all the input negatives into digital photos with high resolution. With a user-friendly interface, cross-platform compatibility, and a focus on user comfort, the “SMART IMAGESHOP” stands as an effective and innovative solution in image enhancement and management.

title: Detecting AI-Generated Paraphrases in Bengali: A Comparative Study of Zero-Shot and Fine-Tuned Transformers
authors: Md. Rakibul Islam, Most. Sharmin Sultana Samu, Md. Zahid Hossain, Farhad Uz Zaman, Md. Kamrozzaman Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can produce text that closely resembles human writing. This capability raises concerns about misuse, including disinformation and content manipulation. Detecting AI-generated text is essential to maintain authenticity and prevent malicious applications. Existing research has addressed detection in multiple languages, but the Bengali language remains largely unexplored. Bengali's rich vocabulary and complex structure make distinguishing human-written and AI-generated text particularly challenging. This study investigates five transformer-based models: XLM-RoBERTa-Large, mDeBERTaV3-Base, BanglaBERT-Base, IndicBERT-Base and MultilingualBERT-Base. Zero-shot evaluation shows that all models perform near chance levels (around 50% accuracy) and highlight the need for task-specific fine-tuning. Fine-tuning significantly improves performance, with XLM-RoBERTa, mDeBERTa and MultilingualBERT achieving around 91% on both accuracy and F1-score. IndicBERT demonstrates comparatively weaker performance, indicating limited effectiveness in fine-tuning for this task. This work advances AI-generated text detection in Bengali and establishes a foundation for building robust systems to counter AI-generated content.

title: AI Adoption and Organizational Agility: Rethinking Business Development for the Digital Era
authors: Abeer Tarawneh, Mohammed H. M. Qeshta, Mohammed Abusweilem, Amineh A. Khaddam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper seeks to respond to the issue of urgency of the application of artificial intelligence (AI) in altering the approach of business development in the digital era. It puts the implementation of AI as one of the contributors in enhancing organizational agility, the capacity to sense and respond swiftly to change, and consequently prosper in turbulent and unpredictable markets. We synthesize the already available literature on AI adoption and organizational agility to come up with a new conceptual model. The model assumes that AI-based capabilities (e.g. automation, decision-making augmentation, generative AI) may help an organization to be more agile strategically, operationally, and on the marketplace, which in turn will result in the realization of the quality of business development. The article introduces a new model, which relates the adoption of AI, agility, and the business development outcomes. What we propose is that the adoption of AI enhances agility (e.g. speed, flexibility, adaptability), and that agility mediates AI effects on business development success, and situational considerations like organizational culture and leadership preparedness precondition agility. This theoretical work sheds light on the AI-agility nexus, that can be utilized in subsequent research works and practitioners as a roadmap in reconsidering the business development in the digital age.

title: DynBrainNet: A Dynamic Lightweight Residual Network with Grad-CAM for Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Basharat Hussain, Basit Hussain, Saqib Hussain, Sania Zehra, Shah Faisal, Tasnimul Ferdous Tamim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Brain tumor classification is crucial for prompt treatment and improved patient prognosis. Deep learning techniques have shown promising results in medical image classification; however, challenges remain in balancing the accuracy, computational efficiency, and model interpretability. This study proposes a novel lightweight deep learning architecture, DynBrainNet, designed for accurate and interpretable brain tumor classification using magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). DynBrainNet incorporates dynamic residual convolutional layers, Learned Group Convolutions (LGConv), early exit strategies, and hyperparameter optimization through grid search to achieve efficient and scalable performance. The model was assessed using a publicly accessible dataset consisting of 24,832 MRI images categorized into four categories: glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and no tumor. DynBrainNet outperformed established transfer learning models, including ResNet50, InceptionV3, VGG16, EfficientNetV2B2, and Xception, achieving the highest classification accuracy of 95.3 % among the models tested. The Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (GradCAM) method was used to create heatmaps that clarify the model's predictions and can be used for clinical applications. The experimental results verified that the proposed DynBrainNet model provides a computationally efficient, interpretable, and robust framework for the diagnosis of brain tumors.

title: Real-World Detection of Full and Partial Bangladeshi Banknotes: A Deep Learning Based Comparative Analysis
authors: Md Nazmul Hossen, Samim Ahmed, Mohammad Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study evaluates the automatic classification of Bangladeshi banknotes, focusing on full and partial-note detection to support assistive technology for visually impaired individuals. Unlike previous works that primarily considered full notes in controlled datasets, our research addresses challenging real-world conditions, including complex backgrounds, diverse orientations, folds, and occlusions. The dataset covers multiple denominations, including newly issued 1000, 50, and 20 Taka notes, ensuring practical relevance. To evaluate recognition performance, we compared five deep learning architectures: EfficientNetB0, ResNet50, MobileNetV2, Xception, and a custom CNN. The experimental results show that EfficientNetB0 achieves the highest validation accuracy (99.03%) and test accuracy (97.90%), demonstrating superior generalization and robustness. ResNet50 also performs competitively with strong test accuracy (96.85%), while MobileNetV2 balances efficiency and accuracy, making it suitable for mobile deployment. Xception provides moderate performance, whereas the custom CNN shows signs of overfitting with weaker generalization. These findings highlight the impact of partial-note images on recognition accuracy and underscore the effectiveness of advanced feature extractors such as EfficientNetBO. The results provide a foundation for the development of lightweight real-time currency recognition systems to improve financial accessibility for visually impaired communities in Bangladesh.
