title: Expanded Deep Embedding Clustering With Adversarial Learning and Adaptive Graph Constraint
authors: Xiaoyu Zheng, Shiyuan Han, Jin Zhou, C. L. Philip Chen, Yingxu Wang, Yuehui Chen, Lin Wang, Tao Du, Cheng Yang, Bowen Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: The autoencoder (AE) is an efficient feature extraction tool that learns latent representations from raw data by minimizing the reconstruction loss. Building upon the AE architecture, deep clustering models are designed to jointly optimize the deep neural network and perform unsupervised clustering. However, existing methods directly impose the clustering objective on the latent features produced by the AE network, thereby neglecting the potential conflict between data clustering and data representation. Specifically, data clustering aims to enhance data aggregation, whereas data representation focuses on ensuring that latent features faithfully reflect the manifold structure of the raw data. To address this issue, this article proposes an innovative expanded deep embedding clustering (E-DEC) model, in which the AE network is employed to seek better latent representations, and a novel residual expansion module (REM) is integrated to construct an expanded feature space that better serves clustering tasks. Furthermore, adversarial learning between the soft cluster assignments and a prior one-hot distribution is adopted in lieu of the conventional Kullback–Leibler (KL) divergence, so as to enhance the discrimination of different clusters and avoid the degeneracy problem. Finally, an entropy regularization technique is incorporated to adaptively refine the affinity graph throughout the clustering process, thereby reducing the sensitivity of clustering performance to the initial affinity graph. Extensive experiments on real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed model over state-of-the-art deep clustering methods.

title: Performance of an IRS-Assisted Underwater Optical Communication System With Energy Harvesting
authors: Himani Mehta, Rohit Kumar, Shankar Prakriya
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Underwater Internet of Things (IoT) sensor nodes face critical energy limitations, as battery replacement or recharging is often impractical. Underwater optical wireless communication (UOWC) offers high-speed data transmission but suffers from severe impairments such as path loss, turbulence, and pointing errors. Intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) have recently emerged as a promising solution to enhance signal quality and extend coverage in challenging environments. This paper presents the performance analysis of an UOWC system featuring downlink energy harvesting (EH) at an IoT sensor node and uplink data transmission assisted by an IRS. A novel composite channel model, incorporating both direct and IRS-reflected links, is proposed and characterized for the UOWC environment. Closed-form expressions are derived for key performance metrics including energy outage probability (EOP), outage probability (OP), and ergodic rate (ER). Extensive analysis is conducted for various water types, demonstrating that the proposed IRS-assisted system significantly outperforms conventional setups without IRS, enabling reliable operation of IoT sensor nodes at greater depths.

title: Decoupling Axial Strain and Torsional Twist in Polarimetric Optical Fiber Sensors through Stokes Parameter Analysis
authors: Ana Teixeira, Vinícius Piaia, Paulo Robalinho, Susana Silva, Orlando Frazão
abstract: Polarimetric optical fiber sensors offer a promising approach for simultaneous measurement of multiple physical parameters, such as strain and twist, due to their sensitivity to changes in birefringence. However, their practical application is often restricted by cross-sensitivity between different parameters. In this work, we experimentally investigate the cross-sensitivity of a polarimetric sensor to axial strain and torsional twist. Two independent setups were used to apply controlled strain and twist to a 0.20-meter fiber section, while monitoring the output state of polarization via Stokes parameters. The response of the Stokes parameters S2 and S3, as well as the orientation angle Ψ and ellipticity angle α, were analyzed to construct sensitivity matrices correlating optical changes to mechanical inputs. A cross-sensitivity analysis was performed using the inverse of these matrices, enabling the discrimination of strain and twist contributions in combined loading scenarios. The results demonstrate a clear linear response and provide a quantitative basis for decoupling strain and twist, advancing the development of robust multi-parameter sensing systems. Therefore, this work shows the feasibility of decoupling strain and twist effects using polarimetric interrogation, supporting the potential of such sensors for multi-parameter sensing in practical environments.

title: A detailed analysis and design of a 1kV piezo-driver with precise fast-time response for hydraulic system
authors: Nassim Rayane Bennacer, Christian Martin, Loris Pace, Pascal Bevilacqua
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents the study of a 1kV DC/AC unipolar converter designed to drive piezoelectric actuators with a precise time response of 1ms, used in a hydraulic system. The selected topology includes a PWM current control to adjust the time response and enables energy recovery from the actuator. This paper presents a detailed analysis, design, and control strategy of the proposed topology. Simulation and experimental results are also included in this work to evaluate time response accuracy, alongside efficiency measurements and loss estimation of the DC/AC converter. The experimental results demonstrate a time response adjustment accuracy of 96.52%(corresponding to 1.036ms), with loss estimation accuracy of 99.04%.

title: Automated Mode Locking in an All-Normal-Dispersion Fiber Laser Using Polarization Feedback.
authors: Ebin Joseph, Simon Tremblay, Hanan Anis
abstract: We experimentally demonstrate real-time automation of nonlinear polarization evolution (NPE)-based mode locking (ML) in an all-normal-dispersion (ANDi) fiber laser cavity using polarization feedback alone. The system not only achieves stable mode locking but also distinguishes between fundamental (FML), harmonic (HML), and Q-switching (QS) regimes. The automation setup combines an electronic polarization controller (EPC) with a Raspberry Pi 4 (RPi). Output polarization measurements are used to calculate the first Stokes parameter (S1); its response to small bias perturbations provides both fitness and a regime classifier. FML is identified by a dominant polarization switch followed by a quiet plateau, HML by a polarization switch preceded by strong pre-switch variation, while QS is flagged by a low S1 baseline with isolated spikes. This technique is reliable, easy to implement, and low in cost, making it well suited for moving NPE-based mode-locking from research laboratories toward practical and commercial applications. In practice, the controller converges within a few closed-loop refinement stages: FML is typically achieved within two to three genetic algorithm (GA) iterations, HML within a few additional refinements, while QS requires longer stabilization using adaptive dithering.

title: Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This volume contains the papers presented at iWAT2026: 2026 International Workshop on Antenna Technology held on March 25-27, 2026 in Liverpool.

title: Enhancing TV APPs Authoring With LLM Agents
authors: Paulo Victor Borges, Daniel de S. Moraes, Simone D. J. Barbosa, Sergio Colcher
abstract: Developing interactive hypermedia applications using the Nested Context Language (NCL) presents significant challenges due to its declarative nature and strict syntactic and semantic requirements. This paper introduces a multi-agent tool powered by large language models (LLMs) to assist in the authoring of NCL 3.0 documents. The proposed tool leverages agents specialized in generating, validating, and refining NCL elements, ensuring syntactic correctness and structural alignment. The tool’s usability, workflow efficiency, and perceived user satisfaction were evaluated through a structured observational study. This work contributes to the field of multimedia systems by providing a scalable, efficient, and user-friendly solution for NCL development, paving the way for the broader adoption of declarative languages in digital TV and multimedia applications.

title: Robust Variational Identification of LPV Systems With Asymmetric Measurement Noise
authors: Xiang Chen, Ke Li, Peng Su, Fei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper considers the problem of parameter identification for linear parameter-varying (LPV) systems with asymmetric noise. Commonly employed noise distributions exhibit symmetric statistical properties that make it difficult to describe asymmetric measurement noise. This leads to a degradation in the performance of the identification algorithm. To cope with the asymmetric noise, the measurement noise is characterized by the generalized hyperbolic skew Student’s t (GHS) distribution. Then, the robust variational algorithm for the LPV system is proposed within the Bayesian framework. The variational Bayesian (VB) algorithm estimates the parameters of the model and the asymmetric noise from the measured data. The proposed algorithm is validated through a numerical example and a mass–spring–damper system. The results and findings show that the proposed algorithm can effectively address the problem caused by asymmetric measurement noise. It can accurately identify the model parameters and suppress the influence of the asymmetric noise.

title: Design and Analysis of a Novel Permanent-Magnet Vernier Machine as an Electromagnetic Damper
authors: Congqi Lu, Fengbin Cai, Qifang Lin, Yurong Huang, Yuhao Wang, Xinhua Guo, Shuangxia Niu
abstract: This article addresses the limitations of conventional electromagnetic dampers, such as low torque density and nonlinear damping characteristics, by proposing a novel dual-excitation permanent magnet Vernier machine (DEPMVM) integrated with a resonant circuit. The rotor of the DEPMVM combines spoke-type magnets and a central magnetic ring to enhance torque density (38.27 kNm/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>) and power factor (0.88) under motor operating mode. The resonant circuit compensates for the synchronous reactance of armature winding in the generator mode, improving the linearity of the damping force versus rotational speed. The flux barrier effect is further mitigated through split-teeth stator design. In addition, with the parallel flux paths, the sum of the dominant harmonic torques in the DEPMVM is increased by 26.99% compared with the conventional model. Experimental results indicate that under the optimal resonant parameters (R = 2 Ω, C = 0.9 mF), the fitting degree (FD) of the T-n curve reaches 0.953. Prototype testing reveals a back-EMF deviation of 3.9%.

title: El Niño/La Niña-driven spatiotemporal heterogeneity of the Eastern Tropical South Pacific Oxygen Minimum Zone with satellite-based long-term records
authors: Zhang Yifan, Bai Yan, He Xianqiang, Li Teng, Gong Fang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The Eastern Tropical South Pacific (ETSP) contains one of the largest oxygen minimum zones (OMZs) globally and is strongly influenced by El Niño-La Niña variability. Yet, long-term OMZ depth variability and its controlling processes remain poorly understood due to limited continuous observations. We develop a machine learning-based inversion model to estimate the depth of hypoxia (DOH, the shallowest OMZ depth) using satellite products and biogeochemical Argo oxygen profiles from 2008 to 2019. The model combines physical and biogeochemical predictors, including satellite-derived net community production, to represent the nonlinear regulation of subsurface hypoxia. Validation against independent observations (n = 809) demonstrates robust performance, with a coefficient of determination of 0.86 and a root mean square error of 22.7 m. Using this framework, we reconstruct a 17-year (2003-2019) monthly DOH dataset for the ETSP, enabling a high-frequency satellite-based assessment of OMZ vertical variability across recurrent El Niño-La Niña events. The reconstructed record reveals pronounced contrasts between offshore and nearshore systems. During 2003-2012, thermocline-driven offshore DOH shoaling contrasted with nonlinear nearshore fluctuations modulated by biological processes. The extreme 2012-2016 El Niño interrupted OMZ expansion, triggering pan-ETSP DOH deepening through divergent mechanisms—intensified stratification offshore versus reduced biological oxygen consumption nearshore. Following 2017, rapid offshore shoaling contrasted with delayed nearshore recovery. For the first time, we reveal the high-frequency temporal evolution of OMZ depth (DOH) in response to recurrent El Niño/La Niña events, and elucidate the El Niño/La Niña-driven heterogeneous hypoxia responses and associated environmental controls between nearshore and offshore upwelling systems.

title: Improving phase difference estimation by optimizing the condition number
authors: Yuwei Qiao, Datong Liu, Liansheng Liu, Yu Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Phase difference estimation is a core component of measurement and calibration in multi-channel systems. For two channels with the same nominal frequency, a joint sinusoidal model with seven parameters is employed to estimate the phase difference, thereby mitigating estimation bias from minor frequency mismatches. The parameters are obtained by solving the least-squares (LS) problem via singular value decomposition (SVD). Analysis shows that when the system matrix has a high condition number (CN), matrix computations are prone to illconditioning, leading to numerical instability in the iterative process. To alleviate this problem, this paper derives a closed-form scaling factor that minimizes the system matrix CN while preserving the LS solution, thereby ensuring numerical stability. Based on different amplitude relationships between channels, the CN under coherent sampling can be optimized to the range of (14, 110). Simulation results show that the ill-conditioned risk of the original SVD-LS algorithm increases significantly as the CN enters the medium-to-high range. After introducing the scaling factor derived in this paper, the CN of the system matrix decreases significantly, the iterative stability and convergence rate are systematically improved, and the LS optimal solution remains unchanged.

title: SelfBlending: Artificial Intelligence-driven Augmentation with Hand Interactions for Seamless Reality Blending in Virtual Environments
authors: Ahmed Elsharkawy, Bocheon Gim, Aya Ataya, SeungJun Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Accessing real-world objects during immersive virtual reality (VR) experiences remains challenging, as current cross-reality systems often rely on predefined interaction steps, tracking devices/markers, or fixed object setups. They also lack support for personalized object recall, where users can add, remove, or modify real-world items blended into the virtual environment (VE). Many head-mounted displays (HMDs) include passthrough technology to switch between virtual and real worlds, but it often disrupts immersion by requiring a full shift from virtual to real. Thus, maintaining an optimal balance between virtuality and reality is difficult. To address these challenges, we developed SelfBlending, a framework that uses AI-based hand tracking to let users label physical objects through freehand gestures, then blends the selected item into the VE using object recognition, enabling interaction with the relevant real-world object. SelfBlending was evaluated against two common interaction conditions: the default passthrough feature in VR HMDs and the conventional approach of physically removing the HMD to access real-world objects. Results from seated, single-object interactions with tabletop-placed items showed that SelfBlending enhanced user experience by boosting presence, supporting efficient physical interaction, and improving cross-reality continuity. It also enabled selective interaction with real objects while minimizing the disruption of VR experience.

title: THz Transistor Design and Applications
authors: M. J. W. Rodwell, Miguel Urteaga, Yihao Fang, Logan Whitaker, Johann Rode, Amirreza Alizadeh, Utku Soylu, Zach Griffith, Yuya Nemoto
abstract: We consider the present performance, design principles, and prospects for further improvement, in THz transistor technologies, addressing how transistor characteristics limit IC performance, and showing several 100-300 GHz IC and systems examples.

title: Critical End-Effect at Sea-to-Land Transition of Three Core Submarine Cables
authors: W. Poradowski, H. Brakelmann, G.J. Anders, T. Dong
abstract: At the sea-to-land transition or offshore interfaces, three-core submarine cables are separated into individual single-core cables arranged in a flat configuration. This separation, combined with protective layers over metallic sheaths that impart semiconductive properties and cause spatial bonding, results in localized increases in circulating sheath currents in the submarine portion of the installation. Despite potential thermal consequences in offshore cable projects, this specific issue has received limited attention in the existing literature; consequently, this paper presents a novel examination of this end-effect phenomenon. An analytical model is developed in the paper and cross-validated with a comprehensive three-dimensional Finite Element Method (FEM) model and PI-section simulations, which represent fundamentally different approaches. The study addresses practical mitigation strategies for end effects in submarine cable installations and addresses a critical gap in existing methods for rating calculations of submarine power cables.

title: Development of a Bespoke Hybrid Electron Paramagnetic Resonance (EPR) Spectrometer for Simultaneous In Situ Neutron Diffraction Studies
authors: Kai Silver, Heungjae Choi, Michael Barter, Steve C. Cripps, Ron I. Smith, Emma Richards, Damien M. Murphy, Martin O. Jones, Adrian Porch
abstract: We present the design, construction, and commissioning of the first hybrid Electron Paramagnetic Resonance (EPR) spectrometer engineered to operate within a Neutron Powder Diffractometer (NPD), enabling simultaneous measurements of spin properties and crystal structure. Developed for the Polaris instrument at the ISIS Neutron and Muon Source (U.K.), the system was specifically designed to function within the constraints of a neutron beamline. Achieving in situ EPR capability within the diffractometer required several necessary compromises to the diffraction geometry. The finite gap between the two halves of the electromagnet body reduces detector visibility, limiting coverage for a proportion of scattering angles. The retained magnetic return-path introduce restricted regions in 2<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\theta$</tex-math></inline-formula> that require reorientation of the instrument to access. Furthermore, the change in sample geometry from a conventional vertical cylindrical configuration to a skewed horizontal cylinder had a detrimental effect on diffraction peak shape and width. The aluminium microwave cavity, although thinned to 0.1 mm to maximise neutron transparency, introduces additional background Bragg reflections. Despite these trade-offs, simultaneous EPR and neutron diffraction data were successfully acquired. Key innovations include a bespoke electromagnet compatible with beamline access, a thinwalled X-band quasi-elliptical microwave cavity, a remotely tuneable waveguide coupling scheme, and a modular support structure optimised to minimise parasitic scattering. The system was benchmarked using standard paramagnetic reference samples, demonstrating EPR sensitivity comparable to commercial laboratory instruments. This work establishes a new class of multimodal instrumentation, enabling operando investigations of materials in which coupled spinlattice phenomena govern functionality, including batteries, catalysts, and quantum materials.

title: EDTM 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)

title: AI-Driven Multimodal Framework for Environmental Monitoring and Conservation
authors: Jayabhaduri Radhakrishnan
abstract: Climate change-induced environmental degradation, pollution, deforestation, and ocean litter require smart, scalable, and real-time monitoring systems. Conventional methods based on manual data gathering or fixed sensor networks are usually characterized by spatially limited coverage, latency, and inefficiency in high-resolution environmental analysis. This article suggests a modular and scalable framework taking advantage of Artificial Intelligence (AI), edge computing, and multi-modal data streams-satellite imagery, unmanned aerial systems (UAS)/unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and Internet of Things (IoT) sensors together for combined environmental monitoring and conservation. The framework has six major layers: data acquisition, ingestion and storage, preprocessing and feature engineering, AI/ML model processing, deployment, and visualization with real-time alerts. It enables the classification of water bodies and vegetation with CNNs, marine debris identification using YOLOv5, forecasting of air quality using LSTM models in time-series, and classification of pollution levels with Random Forests. Edge deployment on NVIDIA Jetson Nano allows for real-time inference with a latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$65 ~\text{ms} /$</tex> frame and power usage of 6.5 W, guaranteeing cost-effective field deployment. From the cloud perspective, Google Vertex AI allows for scalable model retraining and inference in sub-second latency. Visualization dashboards created with Grafana and Google Data Studio provide minute-by-minute reports, whereas the alert system provides 95.2 % accuracy with response times less than three seconds. The suggested system showcases real-world relevance through several case studies and proves its ability to enable responsive, energy-conscious, and scalable environmental intelligence. The current work provides a foundation for future AI-driven conservation projects and the implementation of sustainable environmental policies.

title: Wind Power Forecasting Using Deep LSTM and Hybrid Quantum Neural Networks
authors: Insaf Ghouaoua, Mohamed Tarek Khadir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Wind power has become a crucial source of renewable energy, yet its variability poses significant challenges to maintaining a stable balance between electricity generation and demand. Accurate forecasting is therefore essential to support efficient grid management and enable the integration of renewable resources. This study evaluates two advanced approaches for wind power forecasting: a deep Long Short-Term Memory model and a deep hybrid Quantum Neural Network. Both models were trained and tested on wind power data to assess their predictive performance. Results show that the Long Short-Term Memory model successfully captured temporal patterns and produced accurate forecasts, while the deep hybrid Quantum Neural Network achieved higher overall performance with fewer computational resources. The originality of this work lies in the design and evaluation of a hybrid quantum-classical forecasting model applied to wind power prediction, demonstrating its potential to improve accuracy and efficiency compared to conventional methods. These findings suggest that quantum machine learning could play a strategic role in future energy systems by enhancing reliability and reducing the cost of renewable energy integration.

title: BERTopic for Disaster Response Needs Assessment: Insights from Twitter(X) Data
authors: Jessa O. Poe, Jeffrey S. Ingosan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Assessment of community needs is essential in disaster response, as it guides Local Government Units and responders in identifying priorities and delivering appropriate assistance to the affected communities. Social media platforms, particularly Twitter(X), were considered as a valuable tool for obtaining information on public concerns and needs during disasters. However, the large volume of data and noise found on social media posed challenges in accurately identifying relevant information. The emergence of unsupervised machine learning approaches, such as topic modeling, enabled the efficient analysis of large datasets. This study investigated the use of BERTopic, a topic modeling technique, to identify and analyze community needs expressed in Twitter(X) data. Historical data were preprocessed, embedded using SentenceTransformers, transformed with class-based TFIDF representations, and clustered using GPU-accelerated cuML to improve the efficiency of both UMAP and HDBSCAN. Several hyperparameter tuning experiments were conducted, and coherence scores were used to evaluate the performance and assess the quality of the generated topics. These results highlighted the importance of combining unigrams and bigrams in enhancing topic coherence. The identified categories were: “Shelter and Evacuation”, “Food and Water”, “Clothing”, “Health and Medical”, “Search and Rescue”, and “Power and Communications”. The study also integrated a Large Language Model (LLM) and manual validation to support topic labeling and enhance interpretability. These results highlight the potential of integrating BERTopic, a transformer-based topic modeling, into disaster response, providing valuable insights to support the assessment of community needs.

title: Prompt, Assert, Repeat: Test-Driven Prompting as a Quality Assurance Strategy for Code-Generating LLMs
authors: Brahma Reddy Korraprolu, Mohan Gangadhar Gudey, Y. Raghu Reddy, Dhiraj SM Varanasi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Code-generating LLMs are increasingly used in software development, but compiling code does not imply functional correctness; outputs must be tested. This paper studies a lightweight QA strategy where unit tests are embedded in the prompt to guide code generation, followed by execution against a fixed test suite. Using the Mostly Basic Python Problems (MBPP) benchmark (964 problems, 3 assertions each), we evaluate twelve open-source LLMs with (i) plain prompts and (ii) assertionaugmented prompts, generating five samples per configuration and measuring pass@1 and pass@5. We find that including assertions often yields large correctness gains (frequently 20-30 points in pass@5), but the effect is model and problem-type dependent, with notable degradations for some instruction-tuned models. Across problem types, conditional-logic tasks are consistently easier, while recursion-heavy categories (e.g., dynamic programming/tree traversal) remain challenging. Based on these results, we outline SQA guidance on when test-driven prompting and limited resampling are effective for improving correctness in LLM-assisted programming.

title: NextSeed: An IoT Based Precision Agriculture System for Soil Analysis and Crop Recommendation
authors: Md. Abdur Rakib, Md. Mohidul Alam, Dabasis Das, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir, Md. Mahbubur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Agriculture is an important sector for the economy and food security of Bangladesh, which is facing several challenges due to climate change, lack of cultivable land and inadequate access to advanced farming technologies. To overcome the above challenges, this paper proposes NextSeed, which is an IoT based precision agriculture system that integrates realtime soils monitoring with machine learning for intelligent crop suggestion. The system uses a 7-in-1 soil sensor to measure NPK nutrients, pH, moisture, temperature and electrical conductivity, and some environmental sensors for rainfall, water level and atmospheric conditions. Data goes through anomaly detection before the cloud processing where a Naive Bayes classifier performs 99.77% accuracy in crop prediction. The system also offers fertilizer recommendations and allows automated irrigation control depending on soil conditions and water availability. NextSeed helps to cover significant gaps in solutions on the market by providing integrated automation that combines soil nutrition analysis with crops prediction and water management for agricultural decision support.

title: MOOD-TUNES: A Music & Movie Recommendation System Based on People's Facial Expressions and Hand Gestures
authors: Md. Jisan Ahmed, Sajadul Islam Akash, Isha Das, Md. Shahriar Hussain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In this digital era, music or movies are essential part of our day to day life. They are very powerful in expressing emotion, distressing, and communication with society and culture. However, people have problems finding appropriate music and movies that go with their moods. That's why we have prepared a project for you that, while innovative, makes it easy by creating a recommendation system that provides perfect content based on your mood via facial expressions and hand gestures. Our Project reads expressions and gestures in realtime, and we have developed it with deep learning approaches integrating two different models. One is the traditional CNN model, and another is the CNN model with the MediaPipe pipeline. The training accuracy of Model-1(CNN) is 86%, and the validation accuracy is 84%. The training accuracy of Model2 (CNN with MediaPipe) is 98%. We have integrated these two models into a website named “Mood-Tunes.” We also trained an attentional neural networks model but implemented the first two models mentioned above. Our models will help users find their preferred movie or music according to their mood.

title: A Performance Comparison of Machine Learning Techniques for Cholesterol Prediction
authors: Mishaal Ahmed, Muhammad Ajmal Naz, Manzar Ahmed, Mohiuddin Bukhari, Asif Nawaz, Ibrar-ul-Haque
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Hypercholesterolemia is an important in cardiovascular disease and it has high risk, but it remains undetected due to unavailability of modern equipment in laboratory for proper screening tests. About 29 million U.S. adults approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \text{1 4 \%}$</tex> of the total population suffer from cholesterol issues and this indicate that the high demand of alternative screening testing method. To address these major issue we proposed non-invasive method by using ML models to predict the cholesterol level by using method using the NHANES survey data as an input. The data of patient include demographics (age, sex and race/ethnicity), lifestyle factors (dietary intake, physical activity and smoking), anthropometric measures (BMI, waist circumference, blood pressure) and self-reported health history (diabetes, hypertension). The digital data outcome is defined by clinical thresholds (total cholesterol <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant 240 \text{mg} / \text{dL}$</tex> or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{LDL} \geqslant 160 \text{mg} / \text{dL}$</tex>). The data preprocessing involves inputting missing values, encoding categorical variables and scaling numeric features. In first stage we train five classifiers (logistic regression, random forest, XGBoost, SVM, k-NN) using stratified 5 -fold cross-validation, tuning hyperparameters. The performance was tested based on accuracy, sensitivity, specificity and area under the ROC curve (AUC). The results outline that XGBoost model shows the highest discrimination (AUC/ROC ~ 0.85) with high sensitivity, outperforming logistic regression (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.68$</tex>) as compared to others. The main data related to predictors included age, BMI, blood pressure, and diet quality, consistent with known risk factors. These results shows that by using survey data meaningfully stratify cholesterol risk can be reduce. Finally, we outline the public health implications based on a non- invasive ML-based screening tool for testing and improving preventive care.

title: Class-Wise Streaming for Detecting Minority Class Drift in Machine Learning Classifiers
authors: Dileesha Kannangara, Uthayasanker Thayasivam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Timely and effective detection of concept drift is essential for maintaining machine learning model performance in non-stationary environments. Among these models, classification systems are the most prevalent, and they often face a unique challenge: class imbalance. Typically, the minority class is not only underrepresented but also the most critical to predict accurately, as misclassifications of the minority class can lead to severe consequences.For example, in cancer detection, malignant cases are both rare and vital to identify. Therefore, it is crucial to pay special attention to drifts affecting the minority class. Despite this, most existing drift detection methods overlook changes affecting only the minority class, and this challenge remains largely underexplored in current research. To address this gap, we propose a heuristic methodology that can be applied to any existing concept drift detector through class-wise streaming.Our experiments show that in 84.26% of all evaluations, our method either outperformed or matched the performance of the conventional approach.Through this, we offer an important initial step toward a practical approach for improving the efficiency of drift detection, particularly when the drift occurs in the minority class.

title: An LLM-Based Framework for Generating Synthetic Legal Contract Benchmark Datasets: A LowResource Language Case Study
authors: Zofran Aqsha, Nur Hanis Sabrina Suhaimi, Said Ah Saad, Muhamad Helmi Md Said, Muhammad Harieth Zaini, Afrina Rosa Mohamad Sattar, Maisarah Mustaffa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) in low-resource legal domains is impeded by the scarcity of high-fidelity benchmarks required for rigorous evaluation. While generic web-scraped datasets exist, the reasoning density and domain-specific accuracy required for high-stakes legal drafting are often lacking. To address this, a novel Human-in-theLoop (HITL) framework for constructing high-fidelity legal benchmark datasets is presented, utilizing a case study on Malay Tenancy Contracts. A methodology similar to process supervision is employed via directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) to codify expertdefined contract drafting workflows. Subsequently, a state-of-theart (SOTA) LLM iteratively amends a legal contract template according to these workflows, with the outputted clauses validated by legal practitioners at every intermediate DAG node. The efficacy of this framework is demonstrated through the construction of a pilot benchmark, MaLe-400, consisting of 400 synthetically generated, expert-verified Malay Tenancy Contracts. Preliminary analysis using Google Gemini 2.5 indicates that this approach demonstrates SOTA LLM reliability in producing Malay legal clauses while systematically capturing localized legal nuances often missed by SOTA models, thereby establishing a reproducible methodology for creating gold-standard benchmarks in resource-constrained legal domains.

title: Word Sense Disambiguation: Resolving Lexical Ambiguity in Word-Level Translation - Tamil-to-English
authors: Sajishvar Mohan, Joshua John Thevathas, Jananie Jarachanthan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Word Sense Disambiguation is a vital task in resolving lexical ambiguity, which is one of the major challenges in NLP. In Tamil-to-English translation, this challenge occurs when a single Tamil word has multiple English meanings. The correct meaning depends on the context in which the word appears. This research constructs a robust WSD approach for Tamil-to-English word-level translation. The model utilizes Tamil-specific syntactic and semantic structures to improve contextual understanding. By incorporating linguistic rules and context analysis, the work aims to improve translation accuracy. The main contribution of the research will be that this kind of effort has great demand in low-resource languages like Tamil due to poor availability of NLP tools. This improvement can significantly enhance translation models. It also facilitates better communication and knowledge sharing between Tamil and English speakers. Compared to existing WSD systems such as Word Overlapping <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(71 \%)$</tex>, Cumulative Similarity (84.3 %), WSDPCS (78.3 %), and Google Translate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(79.7 \%)$</tex>, the proposed hybrid model achieved a notable improvement with 82.5 % accuracy in target word identification and 90 % accuracy in gloss sense prediction.

title: Secure Searchable Attribute-Based Encryption Scheme for Multimodal Data in Satellite Internet
authors: Ran Hou, Jiayi Liu, Zheyu Zhang, Xiaofei Zhang, Zhen Xu, Yushu Wu, Zhiquan Liu, Yongwang Liu, Tiejun Wu
abstract: With the continuous optimization of data transmission technologies in Satellite Internet, the transmission objects have expanded from initially simple text data to multimodal data, which faces multiple challenges, such as privacy protection and efficient processing. To address these issues, we propose the searchable attribute-based encryption scheme for multimodal data in Satellite Internet (SABE-MD-SI). Firstly, by specifically extracting and quantifying the features of multimodal data, the fusion of cross-modal data is achieved, effectively enabling the fusion and protection of heterogeneous multimodal data in Satellite Internet. Secondly, the scheme adopts searchable encryption technology to allow ground or satellite nodes to search multimodal data in an encrypted state, thereby enhancing the security of data transmission in Satellite Internet. Finally, the scheme incorporates an attribute update function based on attribute-based encryption technology, ensuring that data access permissions can be dynamically adjusted as required while achieving fine-grained access control. The privacy and security analysis indicates that the security of the ciphertext has been rigorously verified.

title: Design and Development of an IoT-Integrated Automated Liquid and Pill Dispensing System for Real-Time Remote Supervision by Doctors and Caregivers
authors: Muhammad Al Muktadir, Md Shihabul Islam, Rabiul Haque Sajid, Ridwan Kader, Md. Abu Shoaib, Md. Faisal Rahman Badal
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Patients requiring long-term medication for conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, or heart disease often struggle with adherence owing to forgetfulness, stress, busy schedules, illiteracy, or polypharmacy. This non-adherence increases the risk of health deterioration. To address this, this paper proposes an Automated Medicine Dispenser System (AMDS) that improves adherence and patient safety and enables real-time remote supervision by doctors and caregivers. Unlike previous systems, the proposed AMDS handles both liquid and pill medications, employing a PID controller for accurate control of liquid dispensing volumes, achieving 99% success rate for the pill type medicine dispensing and a mean volumetric delivery ratio approximately 95 % for liquid type medicine dispensing, while providing timely notifications, dose confirmation, and cloudbased monitoring at a low cost.

title: AutiScan: A Multimodal Deep Learning Framework for Early Autism Spectrum Disorder Detection
authors: Md Mahbubur Rahman, Md. Abdur Rakib, Mohammad Mahfuzul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Imagine a world where it is possible to have a quickaccess screening for Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) through the use of a smartphone, taking advantage of the combined strength of a child's face and behavior. Instead of depending on the classical and time-consuming clinical evaluation, we introduce AutiScan a novel multimodal deep learning framework based on the combination of facial image analysis and the responses to behavioral questionnaires for the early identification of ASD. The proposed approach consists of hybrid ViT and Swin Transformer for concatenated feature extraction from visual and attentive BiLSTM network for modeling behavioral pattern. Through this multimodal fusion, this system is able to attain a test accuracy of 99.32%, which is significantly better than unimodal baselines (imaged only: 93.33%, text only: 89.5%). Implemented via a mobile and web application, AutiScan enables real-time, scalable screening and provides a valid tool for early ASD detection, and reasonable in combination with standard diagnostic procedures.

title: Chip-to-Package Level Thermal Analysis of GaN HEMT with Thermally Conductive Epoxy Integration
authors: Puteri Nur Aisyah Abd Rahim, Amir Murtadha Mohamad Yussof, M.F. Abdullah, Norhana Arsad, Iskandar Yahya, Norazreen Abd Aziz
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Gallium Nitride High Electron Mobility Transistor (GaN HEMT) is a transistor that is recognised for its faster switching speed and high efficiency in a compact size that can handle the high frequency and high power application. However, with the miniaturisation of the component, thermal management is still a challenge due to localised hotspot formation which can cause reduction in device reliability. In this study, thermal behaviour was studied at chip-level and package-level. Currently there’s no software that can support the thermal simulation from chip-level to package-level. To address this issue, the study of thermal behaviour in chip-level for GaN HEMT was carried out in Silvaco Atlas by simulation the hotspot formation in active region, which is the gate-drain access region. The package-level simulation was done in ANSYS Icepak where a block of heat source was replicated to behave as GaN and also a GaN chip model was placed in D2PAK package in the simulation. The maximum temperature when simulating a dummy block is lower compared to a modeled GaN HEMT which is 148.78 °C while the peak temperature with a modeled GaN chip is 166.7 °C. A novel thermally conductive epoxy was also introduced inside the package to increase the heat dissipation as a part of thermal management. Even though the exact composition of the epoxy is beyond the scope of this paper, the findings in this paper bridges the thermal behaviour of GaN HEMT on the chip-level and the package-level.

title: An Ease and Expedite Decision Support System for Optimized Fertilizers Application - The Case Study of a Blueberry Farm
authors: António D. Costa, João A. Padrão, Guilherme C. Inocêncio, Tânia M. Lima, Pedro D. Gaspar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper presents a decision support system for fertilizer optimization in blueberry farming, developed using Microsoft Excel Solver for broad accessibility. The methodology applies a linear programming model that integrates complete nutrient constraints and fertilizer-plant-soil interactions, tested in a real-world Portuguese blueberry farm. Results demonstrate how cost-effective fertilizer use can maintain optimal crop yield and quality for small-and medium-sized producers. The proposed system bridges the gap between research and practical application by offering an adaptable, low-cost solution. Contributions include a validated optimization model for fertigation, sensitivity analysis, and identified potential for wider deployment across varied agricultural contexts.

title: Smart Computing Approaches for Pulmonary Tuberculosis Diagnosis: A Decision Support Perspective
authors: Mohammad Nishat Akhtar, Sheikh Mohamad Naim Shikh Shatir, Elmi Abu Bakar, A.Amudha, P.Jeevananthan, S. Varalakshmi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Pulmonary tuberculosis (PTB) is a worldwide health problem; hence, accurate, fast, and economical diagnostic methods are needed. Expert interpretation, expensive expenses, and long turnaround times are typical of conventional diagnostic methods. This research proposes an Intelligent Tuberculosis Diagnostic Support System (ITDSS) that leverages ML, AI, and IoT to address these constraints. Using a cloud-based multi-agent decision support system, ITDSS allows seamless data exchange and real-time diagnosis, optimizing healthcare operations. CNNs evaluate chest X-rays, and NLP interprets clinical data in the system. According to experiments, the ITDSS software helps doctors make quicker, more accurate diagnoses and streamline the identification process. Intelligent computing improves TB detection accuracy and reach, according to the findings. The ITDSS is a smart, scalable system that improves healthcare delivery in resource-limited locations and early illness detection.

title: Integrating Employer Branding Strategies To Attract Job Applications Through Company Reputation In The Healthcare Industry Among Generation Z
authors: Nova Nur Anisa, Velani Choirunisa, Oktavia Aini Budiningsih, Kartika Aprilia Benhardy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Considering corporate reputation as a mediator variable, this study intends to examine how company branding tactics affect Generation Z's desire to seek for jobs in the healthcare sector. The healthcare industry in Indonesia is currently experiencing rapid growth, driven by increased public awareness of healthcare services and the growing dominance of Generation Z in the productive age group. This research uses a quantitative approach, utilizing a questionnaire distributed to more than 200 respondents aged 21– 28 years. The results show that employer branding has a positive and significant effect on both corporate reputation and the intention to apply for jobs. Moreover, corporate reputation is proven to mediate the relationship between employer branding and job application intention

title: Accurately Allocating Network Slice in 5G Networks with Explainability Analysis
authors: Avishek Chowdhury, Emon Palit, Dhrubajyoti Das
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Network slice allocation plays a pivotal role in optimising the Quality of Service (QoS) and delivery of differentiated services in modern communication systems. This study focuses on the development and evaluation of a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) classifier to predict network slices based on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) such as packet delay, packet loss rate, technology support, and other network parameters. The MLP model was designed with two hidden layers containing 8 and 4 neurons, using the Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU) activation function and adaptive learning with the Adaptive Moment Estimation (Adam) optimiser. The model demonstrates rapid convergence, achieving a high level of accuracy in a minimal number of epochs. To interpret the model's predictions, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) was utilised, providing both global and local explanations. SHAP force plots highlighted the impact of individual features on specific predictions, while summary bar and violin plots emphasised “Packet Delay” and “Technology Supported” as the most significant contributors across the dataset. These visualisations revealed how feature values influenced the model's output, improving transparency and interoperability. The integration of SHAP explanations offers valuable insights into model behaviour, ensuring trust and accountability in predictions. This study sets the groundwork for further exploration of interpretable machine learning in realworld network optimisation scenarios.

title: Transformer-Based Multimodal Fusion Technique for Bengali Aggressive Meme Classification
authors: Anirban Barua, Binoy Das Gupta, Hasan Murad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Memes have become a familiar feature on social media, but their quick rise has led to concerns about how easily they can be used to spread harmful content and hate speech. It becomes particularly challenging to handle such content in lowresource languages like Bengali due to script complexity, and existing approaches mostly use conventional multimodal fusion techniques. In this work, we present an efficient multimodal meme classification framework that identifies aggressive memes and categorizes them into five classes based on their targeted hate group. Our method combines textual embeddings from MuRIL with visual embeddings from CLIP to capture crossmodal relationships. Moreover, we utilize a compact transformerbased fusion strategy for aligning image and text features, achieving notable improvements over common fusion methods across multiple encoder combinations. We also explore the use of state-of-the-art multimodal large language models for this task. We achieve a weighted F1 score of 0.772 using our proposed method, indicating its effectiveness for aggressive meme classification in the Bengali language.

title: A Commercial-Grade Reliable 40nm eRRAM Macro Featuring a Dual-Oxide Resistive Switching Layer
authors: Zhenchao Sui, Chengxiang Ma, Kaimeng Liu, Xiangchao Ma, Jianshi Tang, Huaqiang Wu, Xing Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents an embedded resistive random-access memory (eRRAM) macro fabricated on a commercial 40nm CMOS technology platform. Through a comprehensive product-level data validation, we first established a baseline HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based RRAM that optimizes the trade-off between the forming and pre-cycling times during the chip probing (CP) stage. Subsequently, a novel dual-oxide resistive switching layer (RSL), enabled by an optimized atomic layer deposition (ALD) process, was developed. This dual-oxide RSL significantly enhanced the retention performance of the RRAM macro, achieving a 38.9% improvement in the post-bake memory window on the wafer level without degrading other performance metrics. The optimized 40nm RRAM macro demonstrates commercial-grade performance for the display driver integrated circuit (DDIC) application, including endurance >1K cycles, data retention >10 years at 85°C, read disturb immunity >10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">9</sup> cycles, and passes the solder reflow test at 260°C for 900s according to JEDEC standard [1]. Therefore, this work provides a highly competitive embedded non-volatile memory (eNVM) solution and promotes the commercialization of RRAM technology.

title: Robust Epileptic Seizure Detection via Multi-Artifact EEG Denoising and SE-CNN
authors: Tanvir Ahsan Showrov, Md. Julkar Nain Siam, Najmus Shakif Ayaan, Md. Sakir Hossain, S.M. Sadakatul Bari
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Epilepsy is a persistent neurological disorder distinguished by recurrent seizure episodes, making prompt detection crucial for treatment and preventing injuries. Machine Learning (ML) models trained on electroencephalogram (EEG) signals have great promise for automating seizure detection, but their reliability is seriously hampered by artifacts introduced during EEG acquisition. Despite significant research on artifact removal, the challenge of addressing a wide variety of artifacts simultaneously remains unexplored. In this paper, seven different types of realistic motion and environmental artifacts are considered to investigate their impacts on the performance of epilepsy seizure detection. We leverage wavelet decomposition, ensemble empirical mode decomposition (EEMD), and a hybrid FFT Masking + EEMD method for artifact suppression. The squeeze-and-excitation convolutional neural network (SE-CNN) is exploited as a classifier. The results demonstrate that motion noise notably degrades the classification accuracy of the seizure detection model, reducing it from 98.75 % (clean signals) to 88.75 % (contaminated signals). The EEMD methods provide the most consistent improvements by preserving crucial EEG features, achieving an accuracy of 96.25 %, which is a 7.5 % improvement compared to the contaminated case. Finally, the proposed SE-CNN model achieves an AUC of more than 99 %, which is 0.27 % and 2.95 % higher than the temporal convolutional network (TCN) and random forest (RF), respectively.

title: Bengali Cyberbullying Detection in Social Media Comments Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Md. Abdul Hai, Sultana Jahan Soheli, Md. Tanvir Ahmed, Abu Rayhan, Ashikur Rahman Khan, Md Mahbubul Alam, M. Shamim Kaiser
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Facebook and Twitter are the most popular social media sites where cyberbullying is common. It is defined as the exploitation of digital communication to harass, intimidate, or degrade people, and this may cause serious psychological and social harm. Cyberbullying can occur at any time and is more difficult to avoid as opposed to traditional bullying. In this paper, we aim to identify cyberbullying in Bengali social media comments, a lowresource language, and to detect abusive content in depth. To address the problem, we trained and evaluated five models: Bangla-BERT, m-BERT, LSTM, Bi-LSTM, and SVM with a labelled dataset of Bengali social media comments. The models were trained to label the comments as abusive and non-abusive. The models were trained to classify comments as abusive or non-abusive. Experimental results show that Bangla-BERT achieved the highest accuracy (94 %), followed by m-BERT (91 %), Bi-LSTM (89 %), LSTM (87 %), and SVM (76 %). Although the Bangla-BERT is better than the other models, it can still be improved with bigger and more varied datasets. The study illustrates the usefulness of machine learning in cyberbullying detection in Bengali and also leads to safer online communication, although it also indicates future directions of low-resource language processing.

title: A Transparent Conformal Miniaturized Slot Antenna for Wi-Fi 6E Applications
authors: Jinfeng Lin, Yejun He, Guodong Liao, Chaoyun Song, Yi Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This article introduces a transparent conformal slot antenna designed for optimal visual clarity. It uses a polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA) substrate in combination with an indium tin oxide (ITO) conductive layer, achieving impressive electromagnetic performance while maintaining high optical transparency. The antenna effectively operates within the 5.76 to 7.21 GHz band, covering the Wi-Fi 6E spectrum. It features a return loss of less than 10 dB, a gain of bigger than 5.8 dBi, and a stable bidirectional radiation pattern, with a half-power beamwidth of approximately 60° in the E-plane. The antenna is highly flexible, maintaining its performance even when bent around cylindrical structures of various radii. Additionally, a metal back cavity is incorporated to significantly broaden the impedance bandwidth, extending the operating range from 4.6 to 7.23 GHz. This improvement enhances the antenna's versatility and effectiveness for a wider range of practical applications. The proposed antenna can be integrated in smart home systems and other applications that require discreet wireless connectivity.

title: Ensemble Machine Learning Models for Treatment Response Prediction and Adaptive Patient Allocation in Cancer Clinical Trials
authors: Umme Saima Tasnim, Shanjida Hossain, Md Mehedi Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Traditional clinical trial methodologies face limitations in predicting individual patient responses to oncological therapeutics, necessitating advanced predictive approaches for personalized treatment selection. To develop and validate machine learning models for predicting treatment response and implement adaptive randomization algorithms to optimize patient outcomes in clinical trials. This retrospective cohort study analyzed 2,156 patients from phase II-III oncology trials (20182024). Multiple machine learning algorithms including logistic regression, random forest, support vector machines, XGBoost, and ensemble methods were developed using clinical, molecular, and laboratory features. External validation was performed on 856 independent patients. The ensemble model achieved superior discriminative performance (AUC-ROC: 0.862, 95% CI: 0.8370.887) compared to traditional clinical scoring (AUC-ROC: 0.698, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>). External validation maintained robust performance (AUC-ROC: 0.831). Mutation burden and performance status were the most predictive features. The adaptive randomization algorithm allocated 68.4 % of patients to optimal treatments versus 50 % with conventional randomization, improving overall response rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.3 \%(\mathbf{p}<0.001)$</tex>. Machine learning significantly enhances clinical trial efficiency and patient outcome prediction, demonstrating clear clinical utility for treatment optimization and patient stratification in precision oncology.

title: Proactive VLSI Congestion Prediction Using a Hybrid Residual U-Net with Squeeze-and-Excitation Blocks
authors: Hasibul Rahman, Zakia Sultana, Fabbiha Ishrar, Arif Istiaque
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Routing congestion is a critical challenge in modern Very Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) design, as it directly impacts timing closure, power efficiency, and manufacturability. In the placement and routing (P&R) stage of VLSI design, accurate congestion prediction is essential to ensure routability, timing closure, and efficient resource utilization. Traditional Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools rely on computationally expensive placement and routing iterations to identify congestion hotspots, which prolongs design closure cycles and increases resource consumption. To address this problem, we propose a deep learning-based framework for early congestion prediction using features derived from the CircuitNet-N14 benchmark dataset. Our approach integrates RUDY, RUDY_pin, and macro-region features with ground truth congestion maps to construct a rich feature representation of circuit layouts. A Residual U-Net enhanced with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) blocks is employed to learn mappings between placement features and congestion heatmaps. Experimental results on multiple benchmark designs demonstrate that our model achieves competitive accuracy, with a Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) of 0.9724 and a normalized RMSE (nRMSE) of 0.0123, while significantly reducing prediction time compared to conventional EDA flows. These results show that our framework can serve as a fast, accurate proxy for costly P&R iterations, helping the industry accelerate design closure and reduce computational overhead.

title: A New Localization Algorithm for a Two-Layer Magnetic Sensor Array in Capsule Endoscopy
authors: Omid Yaghoobian, Khan A. Wahid
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate localization of wireless capsule endoscopes (WCE) is essential for gastrointestinal (GI) diagnostics, yet natural peristalsis introduces abrupt capsule motion that challenges conventional optimization-based methods. We propose a magnetic localization framework that integrates a particle filter (PF) with a two-layer sensor array. The PF provides global search capability, reducing sensitivity to poor initial estimates caused by sudden movements, while the two-layer array improves spatial sensitivity and robustness when the capsule shifts away from one layer. Sensor placement and inter-layer separation are further optimized using the Cramér-Rao Lower Bound (CRLB) to minimize uncertainty within the sensing volume. Experimental and simulation results show that the proposed system—optimized for realistic magnet and sensor characteristics-achieves 2 mm position and 2.4° orientation accuracy across a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \times 15 \times 25 \text{cm}^{3}$</tex> region. Compared with single-layer arrays and local-optimizer-based approaches, the method demonstrates superior accuracy and robustness under dynamic motion, highlighting its potential for practical WCE tracking.

title: Study on the Fluid–Structure Interaction at Different Layout in Wafer Mold Encapsulation Process
authors: Ainnur Hanim Othman, Mohammad Hafifi Hafiz Ishak, Mohd Sharizal Abdul Aziz, Muhammad Naqib Nashrudin, Muhammad Aqil Azman
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study investigates the effect of die aspect ratio on epoxy molding compound (EMC) flow and mechanical response during wafer-level encapsulation using a coupled CFD–FEA simulation. Two aspect ratios, 1:1 and 1:2, were analyzed by varying die width while keeping height and length constant. Results showed that the 1:1 configuration achieved faster mold filling due to lower flow resistance, while the 1:2 geometry exhibited delayed progression. Structural analysis revealed higher global deformation in the 1:2 case, but more localized deformation and sharper stress peaks in the 1:1 model. Overall, die aspect ratio strongly influences EMC flow and stress distribution, providing design guidance for optimizing die layout and improving encapsulation reliability.

title: Federated Edge Learning MAC Cross-Layer Optimization in 6G Networks : The Case for Radial Basis Functions Optimization
authors: Spyridon Louvros, Anupkumar Pandey, Brijesh Shah, Yashesh Buch
abstract: This paper studies the impact of retransmission strategies on federated edge learning performance in wireless edge networks for 5G Advanced and emerging 6G systems. Hybrid automatic repeat request mechanisms are examined in multi device learning scenarios, where unreliable transmissions affect model convergence and latency. Retransmission selection is formulated as a global optimization problem balancing delay, energy efficiency and update reliability under uncertain channel conditions. A radial basis function-based optimization framework is proposed, showing improved learning robustness and communication efficiency compared to conventional approaches.

title: Decision Analytics in Climate Diplomacy: Pathways Toward Global Sustainability
authors: Muhammed Nawar Hussein, Abdulrhman Huseein Ali, Fouad Jameel Khalaf, Humam Mahdi Salih, Zeyad Khalaf Ismail, Marwan Mohammed Abbood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This electronic Climate change is one of the most pressing global challenges, with profound economic, political, and environmental consequences. This study aims to analyze the role of climate diplomacy as a mechanism for enhancing international cooperation and promoting sustainability. The research hypothesis assumes that climate diplomacy is a key tool for reducing emissions, integrating climate policies into development plans, and fostering technological innovation. It relies on negotiations, agreements, and multilateral dialogues to balance national interests. The study concludes that its effectiveness remains constrained by tensions and limited financing, recommending stronger commitments, expanded climate finance, and deeper regional cooperation to ensure global sustainability.

title: Tea Leaf Disease Detection and Classification Using Modified InceptionV3 with Transfer Learning Approach
authors: E. Afreen Banu, MD.Asma, S.Dhivya, S.Nooray Sashmi, Yashika Bhaghal, Girish Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Disease identification and categorization of tea leaves are exceptionally vital in the management of the crop health and production in the tea farms. This research introduces an automated approach that utilizes deep learning, specifically a modified InceptionV3 architecture, to classify five distinct categories of tea leaf conditions. The diseases are Anthracnose, Bird’s Eyespot, Gray Blight, Healthy, and Red Leaf Spot. The study used a dataset of 517 images, aggressive preprocessing, and careful data augmentation to improve the performance of the model. The model architecture used in this work is a transfer learning architecture with some modifications, such as adding dense layers, dropout, and batch normalization, and it has an overall accuracy of 91%. The model was able to accurately identify all classes with F1 scores between 0.89 and 0.93; the model performed best in the classification of healthy leaf samples. The results of the evaluation using the confusion matrix and the classification metrics also indicated that there was very low cross-classification between the disease categories, and thus, the model is suitable for practical use. This research finding is very important in the field of agricultural automation in that it provides an optimal, precise, and scalable approach to tea leaf disease identification with the potential of minimizing losses and enhancing disease control in tea growing.

title: CertiChain: A Cryptographic Ledger for Immutable Academic Credential Verification
authors: Gimhani Samindika Dissanayake, Sudesh Chamara Jayathunge Bandara, Shyamali Saranga Karunadasa, Bevan Kanishka Liyanage, Lakshika Ruwani Samarasinghe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Academic credential fraud is a pervasive issue undermining the integrity of higher education institutions globally. Traditional methods of verification, relying on centralized databases or paper certificates, are vulnerable to specialized forgery and internal data manipulation. This paper presents “CertiChain,” a cryptographic ledger simulation designed to demonstrate the application of blockchain principles to credential storage. Built using React and the Web Crypto API, CertiChain implements a tamper-evident linked list structure secured by SHA-256 hashing. The system provides an interactive visualization of the “Avalanche Effect,” allowing auditors to observe how a minor alteration in student data catastrophically breaks the cryptographic chain of trust. This report details the mathematical foundations of the ledger, analyzes the TypeScript implementation of the hashing protocol, and evaluates the system's efficacy as security control against data tampering.

title: Assessment of YOLO Models for Real-Time Vehicle Detection in the Complex Urban Settings of Bangladesh
authors: Ha Meem Hossain, Pritam Nath, Md Imtiaz Uddin, Ishrat Jahan Eiste, Mahitun Nesa Mahi, Syed Nasibur Rahman Ratul, Md Naim Uddin Mozumdar, Asif Mohammed Saad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study assesses the efficacy of YOLOv8 and YOLOv11 architectures, including medium (m) and extra-large (x) variants, for real-time vehicle detection in Bangladesh's complex urban environments. A custom dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1920 \times 1080$</tex> images was compiled, annotated with 23 classes encompassing local vehicles such as Desi Nosimon, Leguna, and Battery Rickshaw. Data were collected using mobile cameras, annotated with LabelImg, and divided into 75 % training, 5 % validation, and 20 % testing sets. Models were trained at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$640 \times 640$</tex> resolution and evaluated using precision, recall, F1 score, mean average precision (mAP@0.5), and inference time. Results indicate that YOLOv8x achieves the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 0.733 and F1 score of 0.681, with an inference time of 40.255 ms, while YOLOv8m balances accuracy (mAP@0.5: 0.709) with a faster 12.1 ms. YOLOv11m variants exhibit competitive performance but higher latency (e.g., 16.6 ms for YOLOv11m). Medium variants are recommended for real-time applications, enhancing road safety through autonomous driving and traffic enforcement. Qualitative analysis reveals challenges with rare classes, necessitating dataset refinement. Future research will explore additional models and optimize datasets to improve detection accuracy in diverse Bangladeshi road conditions.

title: First-Principles DFT+U Study of Orthorhombic Rivalry in Ferroelectric Hafnia–Zirconia
authors: Mochamad Januar, Min-Hung Lee
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Ferroelectric Hf<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1−x</inf>Zr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> (HZO) is a leading candidate for silicon-technology-compatible non-volatile memory, yet the microscopic origin of its polarization remains under debate due to the near-degeneracy of polar orthorhombic phases. Here, we employ first-principles DFT+U calculations to systematically compare the structural, electronic, and ferroelectric properties of the Pca2<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> and Pmn2<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> polymorphs across a range of Hf/Zr stoichiometries. Equation-of-state analysis confirms mechanical stability for both phases, with Pmn2<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> exhibiting slightly larger lattice constants. Energetic trends reveal that phase stability is highly sensitive to Zr content, whereas the spontaneous polarization (∼56–60 µC cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup>) and wide band gap (∼6 eV) remain remarkably robust. This resilience originates from the fact that substitutional Zr modifies orbital hybridization but preserves the underlying O−2p to Hf/Zr−d charge-transfer mechanism in both polymorphs. These findings show that stoichiometry dictates phase stability, while ferroelectric functionality remains robust against cation ratio and polymorphic competition, offering symmetry- and orbital-guided principles for designing hafnia–zirconia in future memory and logic devices.

title: Post-Disaster Damage Assessment using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: C Valliyammai, A K Vaishnavi, Sri Vaishnavi Devi, D Vijay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The damage assessment is significant for the response and recovery after a disaster to avoid the time-consuming process of manually assessing and validating property damage before providing relief to affected people. To forecast the grade of damage caused by natural disasters, an efficient damage assessment system is proposed. The multi-class classification using deep learning estimates the degree of damage to structures with fundamental factors from the Gorkha earthquake dataset, including building location, age, construction details, and secondary usage. The features that are important in identifying building seismic risks are extracted using Spearman Correlation Coefficient, Extra Tree Classifier, and Mutual Information. The Principal Component Analysis is used to identify the important features that help in damage assessment. The deep learning models CNN, LSTM, and RNN are used to predict the damage grade of the buildings after the disaster. The experimental results prove that RNN combined with Mutual Information achieves $97.58 \%$ training accuracy and 99% testing accuracy when compared with other deep learning models, which helps in planning efficient strategies for disaster management.

title: Breaking the Trade-Off: A Floating-Gate Field-Plated Split-Gate MOSFET for High-Frequency and High-Reliability Power Conversion
authors: Yiming Zhang, Ran Tao, Dawei Gao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: As power electronics push toward higher frequencies and power densities, conventional split-gate MOSFETs (CSG-MOS) face a critical trade-off between low capacitance and poor reliability due to electric field crowding. This work breaks this bottleneck by introducing a floating-gate field-plated MOSFET (FGP-MOS) that retains the removed central gate region as a capacitively-coupled field plate. Unlike prior arts requiring complex process modifications, the FGP-MOS is fabricated without any additional steps. TCAD simulations confirm that the structure simultaneously achieves a record-low specific on-resistance (0.41 mΩ•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), superior breakdown voltage, and 15.4% lower switching loss than conventional MOSFETs, while extending short-circuit withstand time by 16% and drastically suppressing hot-carrier degradation. This design offers a scalable pathway to high-frequency, high-reliability power devices without process overhead.

title: AI-Based Urban Risk Modeling: Leveraging Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Real-Time Structural Stress Simulation of a Structure
authors: Mohammad Arafat Alamgir, Dipra Barua, MST. Khadija Khanom, Md Shahriar Ahmed, MD. Fardin Ahmed, Md. Osman Goni, Mayaz Uddin Gazi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and efficient prediction of structural stress in urban high-rise clusters is essential for seismic risk assessment and resilience planning in urban areas. This study proposes a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework that embeds elasticity and strength constraints directly into the learning process to ensure physically consistent and interpretable predictions. A curated dataset of 2,500 samples with 11 engineered features was used to benchmark the PINN against conventional machine learning models, including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, Support Vector Regression, and Multilayer Perceptrons. The results demonstrate that the proposed PINN achieved superior performance (MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{5. 0 9}, \mathbf{R M S E} \approx \mathbf{2. 2 5}$</tex>, MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1.81, \mathbf{R}^{2} \approx \mathbf{0. 9 3}$</tex>), outperforming all baselines while maintaining consistency with the established physical laws, with physics-residual violations remaining below 3%. The framework shows potential for near-real-time deployment in digital-twin or rapid assessment workflows, although further validation on larger and real-world datasets is required. Although the study is limited by the dataset size and simplified material assumptions, it establishes a robust physics-grounded baseline and outlines clear pathways for improvement using expanded and real-world datasets, ensemble or hybrid modeling, and region-specific applications. Overall, the findings highlight the novelty and practical utility of physics-informed learning for advancing scalable and reliable urban risk management systems.

title: Automated Carotid Intima-Media Complex Segmentation Using Multi-Stage Deep Learning with Attention-Guided Feature Fusion
authors: Abdus Salam, Oishi Jyoti, Md. Nahiduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of mortality worldwide, with carotid intima-media thickness (CIMT) serving as a critical biomarker for early atherosclerotic detection. Traditional manual measurement approaches suffer from significant inter-observer variability and time-intensive analysis protocols. This study presents a novel automated segmentation framework combining YOLOv9-based region of interest detection with a hybrid ResNet-50/SelfONN-UNet++ architecture enhanced by squeeze-and-excitation attention mechanisms. Evaluation on the CUBS dataset comprising 2,176 carotid ultrasound images demonstrates robust performance metrics: Dice coefficient of 85.32%, intersection over union of 74.41%, and sensitivity of 86.87%. The proposed methodology reduces false negative rates compared to conventional approaches while maintaining clinical accuracy standards. These findings establish a foundation for standardized automated CIMT assessment in cardiovascular risk stratification protocols.

title: On the Limited Availability Group Model for Multirate Poisson Traffic
authors: Marinos Vlasakis, Maria Kourtesi, Iskanter-Alexandros Chousainov, Irene Keramidi, Dimitris Uzunidis, Orestis Zestas, Ioannis Moscholios, Michael Logothetis
