abstract: Loss models for finite-capacity systems play a fundamental role in teletraffic theory, particularly for dimensioning multirate service-classes. Among them, the Limited Availability Group (LAG) model constitutes an important extension of the classical Erlang Multirate Loss Model for systems consisting of multiple separate resources that accommodate Poisson arriving calls. This paper presents a review of the LAG model with emphasis on clarifying the model’s assumptions and the intermediate steps required for the call blocking probability determination through analytical tutorial examples. Furthermore, the accuracy of the LAG model is evaluated against simulation results for small-capacity systems, demonstrating satisfactory performance.

title: Analysis of the Impact of Large-Scale Solar Power Plant Integration on Protection, Stability, and Short-Circuit Dynamics in the National Transmission Grid
authors: Mehdi Kolahkaj, Leila Abkar, Mohammad Khazabi, Esmail Fathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: Large-scale solar integration poses significant challenges to protection coordination, transient stability, and short-circuit levels in transmission grids. This paper analyzes these effects through detailed modeling of multiple solar penetration scenarios and proposes a multi-level optimization framework that jointly addresses operational planning, dynamic security, and adaptive protection. Results show up to a 55.3 % increase in short-circuit current at the point of connection, reduced coordination time intervals, and increased rotor angle deviations exceeding 15° under high solar penetration. The findings highlight the necessity of adaptive protection and integrated dynamic security assessment to ensure reliable operation of grids with high shares of inverter-based resources.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Business: A Systematic Review of Applications in Leadership, Sales, and Management
authors: Sakher M.A Alnajdawi, Saed Adnan Mustafa, Mahmoud Alghizzawi, Hazem Alkasassbeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly recognized as a transformative force in business, reshaping how organizations lead, sell, and manage in an era of rapid digital change. This paper presents a systematic review of 132 studies published between 2015 and 2025, examining AI’s applications in leadership, sales, and management through the PRISMA framework. The findings reveal that in leadership, AI supports predictive talent analytics, adaptive digital leadership, and inclusive decision-making. In sales, AI enhances demand forecasting, customer engagement, personalization, and dynamic pricing models. In management, AI strengthens human resource practices, improves supply chain resilience, enables advanced financial risk analysis, and supports strategic planning under uncertainty. Across domains, three key themes emerge: AI improves decision-making accuracy, facilitates personalization, and introduces new ethical and transparency challenges. While AI consistently augments efficiency and competitiveness, its effectiveness depends on being implemented as a complement to human judgment rather than a replacement. The review contributes to theory by extending leadership, marketing, and management frameworks into AI-driven contexts and provides practical guidance for organizations seeking to adopt AI responsibly. It concludes by highlighting future research directions, particularly in generative AI, explainable AI, and human–AI collaboration.

title: Extending UTAUT for Blockchain Adoption in Power Plant Asset Management: Towards UTAUT4-BPP
authors: Miftahol Arifin, Elisa Kusrini, Winda Nur Cahyo, Imam Djati Widodo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The escalating digitalization of power-plant operations has heightened the necessity for transparent, secure, and compliant asset management systems. Blockchain technology offers promising capabilities for traceability, auditability, and operational efficiency. However, existing adoption frameworks such as UTAUT, UTAUT2, and UTAUT3 do not sufficiently capture the behavioral and institutional complexities inherent in regulated energy sectors. This paper introduces UTAUT4-BPP (Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology for Blockchain in Power Plants)—a conceptual extension of UTAUT formulated to elucidate blockchain adoption within power-plant asset management. The model amalgamates elements from the Theory of Planned Behavior and institutional theory, while incorporating three novel constructs: Transparency and Data Provenance Security (TDPS), Regulatory and Compliance Alignment (RCA), and Operational Risk and Efficiency (ORE). Collectively, these constructs embody transparency-driven trust, regulatory legitimacy, and operational resilience, crucial factors for technological adoption in asset-intensive environments. The proposed framework delineates eight theoretical hypotheses connecting UTAUT’s core constructs with these contextual extensions. It proposes a three-phase validation roadmap comprising expert judgment, PLS-SEM testing, and agent-based simulation. UTAUT4-BPP redefines technology acceptance as a socio-technical-institutional phenomenon that links performance expectancy, regulatory alignment, and risk governance. This framework advances theoretical understanding in technology adoption research and provides practical guidance for utilities, regulators, and suppliers aiming to implement blockchain for sustainable and compliant asset management.

title: A Multi-Criteria Decision Framework for Selecting Sustainable Local Materials in Post-Crisis Reconstruction
authors: Sufyan Sufyan, Amin Saif, Farag Qasem, Aq Ahdal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Post-crisis reconstruction often defaults to imported materials, undermining long-term sustainability and local economic recovery. Although vernacular materials can reduce embodied emissions and strengthen local livelihoods, decision makers lack a transparent way to weigh competing criteria and justify choices. This paper presents a four-phase quantitative decision framework that integrates the Analytic Hierarchy Process to derive criteria weights from experts and the Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution to order prospective materials. The framework is demonstrated on a flood-affected housing scenario comparing bamboo and rammed earth. The analysis quantifies decision thresholds and shows how shifts in priorities—such as higher emphasis on initial cost versus durability—change the preferred option. Sensitivity analysis tests robustness and clarifies the drivers of each recommendation. By making trade-offs explicit, the framework operationalizes the "build back better" principle, enabling stakeholders to make defensible, locally appropriate material selections for a resilient and sustainable recovery.

title: Enhancing Bangla Sentiment Analysis Through Data Balancing and Preprocessing: A Comparative Insight from Classical and Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Md. Anowar Hossen Labu, Most. Tazfia Sultana, Md. Mahmudul Hassan, Md. Afridi Chowdhury, Adiba Mahjabin Nitu, Md. Arshad Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Sentiment analysis in Bangla remains a challenging task due to the scarcity of labeled data, class imbalance, and the noisy nature of user-generated social media text. Unlike the languages with extensive resources such as English, Bangla lacks large-scale, high-quality datasets that can support robust natural language processing models. In this study, we focus on the SentNoB dataset, which contains 12,575 comments across 13 domains, annotated into positive, negative, and neutral classes. A key limitation of this dataset is the scarcity of neutral samples, which significantly affects model performance and generalization. Initial experiments confirmed these challenges, with Support Vector Machine (SVM) achieving 66 % accuracy and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) reaching only 57.86 % of accuracy. These results highlight the adverse effects of both class imbalance and noisy textual features. To address these issues, the authors enriched the dataset with 2,000 additional neutral and 200 negative samples collected from social media, followed by extensive preprocessing that included text cleaning, normalization, stopword removal, and noise reduction. Retraining with this improved dataset yielded substantial gains, with SVM achieving 76.94% and BiLSTM reaching 78.37 % accuracy. The authors findings demonstrate that dataset balancing and robust preprocessing are critical for Bangla sentiment analysis and emphasize the complementary strengths of classical machine learning and deep learning in low-resource contexts.

title: Attention-Guided Invariant Feature Learning for Clothes-Changing Person Re-Identification
authors: Raisa Begum, Kaushik Deb
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Person re-identification is crucial in advanced surveillance systems, striving to identify and track a query person across cross-camera views, times, and locations within a gallery of images. The presence of occlusion, multiple camera views, background interference, etc., induce further complexities in the approach. Nonetheless, notable progress has been procured on conventional person re-id tasks inadvertently presuming that query's clothes are static during the entire retrieval procedure within a short time period. However, we cannot overlook addressing the cloth-changing aspect in case of re-id over a long period of time. Cloth-invariant feature learning is the key to withdrawing its bias while handling clothing changing Re-ID (CC Re-ID). To improve the reliability of surveillance systems, in this paper, we propose an innovative framework called Non-Cloth Relevant Feature Mining (NCR-FM) to decouple clothing information and ID-inherent details in order to secure robust re-id of individuals regardless of alterations in their clothing. Separate feature mining modules are employed to squeeze the precise identity and clothing information. The mined pieces of information are further polished and disjointed by aiding feature blending and level-based pyramid approaches followed by specialized classifiers. The end-to-end collective training with the weighted sum of the multi-functional criterion functions gradually strips the clothing bias from potential biometric clues. Extensive experiments on publicly available benchmark cloth-changing dataset, PRCC further validates the disentanglement potency of the proposed framework over the recent approaches, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 2. 9 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 3. 1 \%}$</tex> of Top-1 and mAP CC Re-ID scores respectively.

title: Big Data Genomic Analysis and Understanding the Pathogenicity of Protozoan Naegleria Genome Through Insilico Approach (Karachi Strain)
authors: Rabia Faizan, Rafia Wasi, Fareen
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: This study provides a comprehensive genomic and proteomic characterization of the Naegleria fowleri Karachi_NF001 strain, revealing critical insights into its pathogenic mechanisms, evolutionary lineage, and immunogenic potential. Phylogenetic analysis demonstrated a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90-100 \%$</tex> sequence similarity with other N. fowleri strains, supporting conserved genetic lineage and regional adaptation. Eight Open Reading Frames (ORFs) were identified, with ORF135 being the longest at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6, 6 3 3 ~ b p}$</tex> and potentially encoding a protein of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4 0 ~ k D a}$</tex>. Conserved domain analysis revealed functionally significant domains, including nucleotide transferase-related (pfam10127, COG3641), cytochrome oxidase assembly (SURF1, Shy1), calcium signaling-related (pfam06449, pfam01365), and membrane interaction (MIR domain, pfam02815), all with E -values <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1.0 \mathrm{E}-10$</tex>, indicating strong functional conservation. The protein-protein interaction (PPI) network comprised 232 interactions among 51 proteins, with a high clustering coefficient of 0.751 and key hubs involved in protein kinase activity (e.g., D2V5S4_NAEGR) and cyclic AMP turnover. Functional enrichment highlighted Reactome pathway involvement in heme biosynthesis and iron metabolism, KEGG pathway activation in purine/pyrimidine metabolism, and STRING clusters related to phosphodiesterases and transporters. Immunogenicity prediction identified multiple peptides with potential vaccine value, including one with high HLA-DR7 (Class II) binding affinity of 0.87 and several with Class I affinities ranging from 0.25 to 0.34; these peptides were also non-toxic and nonallergenic with immunogenicity scores <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.6$</tex>. These findings collectively advance the understanding of N. fowleri's molecular biology, support future epitope-based vaccine development, and provide a reference for targeted therapeutics in the context of Primary Amoebic Meningoencephalitis (PAM).

title: Benchmarking GAN-Based Synthetic Data Augmentation for Task-Driven Handwritten Digit Recognition
authors: Jannathul Maowa Hasi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study evaluates the practical utility of synthetic handwritten digits generated by four Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) architectures: DCGAN, CGAN, Conv-CGAN, and WGAN-GP. The models are assessed using both established image-quality metrics—Fréchet Inception Distance (FID), Inception Score (IS), and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM). Moreover, a task-oriented evaluation is employed, in which a lightweight Optical Character Recognition network (OCRNet) is trained on generated synthetic data. Conv-CGAN consistently produces the most realistic and semantically coherent samples across all metrics. When these samples are combined with the MNIST training set(real data), OCRNet's accuracy improves from an intentionally constrained baseline of 89.5 % (trained only on real data) to 94.1 % (trained on real and synthetic data together). This demonstrates that the generated data provides meaningful new information beyond the original dataset. Our findings show that conditional generative models with enhanced capacity can deliver both visually high-quality images and functionally useful training signals for downstream recognition. This work highlights the importance of evaluating generative models by their real-world impact and points to the broader potential of synthetic data in resource-limited machine learning settings.

title: EfficientNet with Channel-Spatial Attention for Accurate Brain Tumor Detection
authors: Nagifa Nujhat Easha, Mohiuddin Ahmad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and reliable classification of brain tumors from MRI scans is essential for timely clinical intervention, yet the heterogeneous appearance of tumors and the presence of visually similar anatomical structures make this task highly challenging. To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight attention-guided framework that integrates EfficientNet-B0 with a Convolutional Block Attention Module to enhance both channel-wise and spatial feature discrimination. The incorporation of attention refinement enables the model to focus on tumor-relevant regions while reducing background interference, improving robustness across diverse MRI patterns. Evaluated on a benchmark brain tumor dataset, the proposed approach achieves superior performance compared to standard CNN baselines, attaining a test accuracy of 97.1 % and a macro-averaged AUC-ROC of 0.987. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining principled model scaling with attention mechanisms for advancing automated brain tumor analysis and demonstrate the potential of the proposed framework as a reliable and scalable clinical decision-support tool.

title: AI-Driven RF-DETR Decision System for Real-Time PPE Monitoring in Yemen’s Development Projects
authors: Ezzaldeen Al-Tayar, Mogeeb A. Saeed, Amin Saif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Occupational safety monitoring remains a critical challenge in fragile development contexts such as Yemen, where construction activities are often implemented under limited oversight and scarce digital resources. This paper presents the development and implementation of a decision-support system for real-time monitoring of personal protective equipment (PPE) compliance, utilizing a transformer-based computer vision model. Building upon prior conceptual studies on trustworthy and explainable detection frameworks, the research advances toward a fully operational application through the deployment of the Roboflow Detection Transformer (RF-DETR Small). A localized dataset representing Yemeni worksites was created, comprising over two thousand annotated images across multiple PPE and non-compliance classes. The model was trained and evaluated under resource-constrained conditions, achieving high accuracy and robustness in detecting helmets, vests, and safety hazards, with precision and recall exceeding ninety percent. The resulting system demonstrates the feasibility of integrating AI-enabled safety analytics into low-infrastructure environments to support timely decision-making, reduce occupational risks, and strengthen safety accountability in humanitarian and development projects. The outcomes underline the strategic value of transformer architectures for sustainable safety management and contribute practical evidence toward data-driven decision-aid systems in engineering project environments.

title: The Impact of Adherence to Corporate Governance Principles on Enhancing the Quality of Financial Reports : Applied Study on Non-Financial Sector Companies Listed on the Bahrain Bourse
authors: Nadya Alshareeda, Ammar Alsammarraee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study investigates the effect of corporate governance compliance on the quality of financial reports in non-financial companies listed on the Bahrain Bourse. A descriptive-analytical method was applied, using a questionnaire to measure governance adherence and the accounting conservatism model to evaluate reporting quality. Data were analyzed through SPSS. The analysis indicates that the firms evaluated showed high compliance with the rules of good governance and the quality of corporate financial reporting significantly benefits from these norms. The study suggests further development of governance practices, training of companies to raise awareness of these matters, and implementation of an annual public ranking of levels of compliance. Firms which adhere to these governance standards should also receive incentives.

title: Deep Learning and Ensemble Techniques in Fake Image Detection
authors: Md. Nabil Hasan Mumat, Rakibul Hasan Showrov, Nashita Nawar Prapti, Md. Shahriar Hussain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The growth of deep learning has resulted in the creation of deepfake images that are remarkably realistic, posing substantial risks to data integrity, security, and privacy. Detecting such manipulated content is crucial for preventing misuse in social media, journalism, and authentication systems. This study employed advanced feature extraction techniques and neural network architectures to explore machine learning-based deepfake detection. It analyzes spatial inconsistencies, such as artifacts and unnatural blending, through models like EfficientNet-B3, ConvNeXt, Swin Transformer, ViT, and ensemble architectures combining features from ConvNeXt, Swin Transformer, ViT, and EfficientNet-B3 with an NN-Classifier using soft voting. The models were trained, validated, and tested on comprehensive datasets, achieving the highest accuracy with soft voting: 99.43% in training and validation and 99.43% in testing. These findings illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed methods in enhancing detection accuracy and addressing generalization challenges, ultimately leading to reliable systems that combat misinformation and deepfakes.

title: HPSO-GO: A Hybrid Meta-Heuristic Approach for Multi-Objective Real-Time Cloud Resource Scheduling
authors: D.P.B.S. Lakshitha, H.M.K.T. Gunawardane, K.P.N. Jayasena
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Resource Scheduling in Cloud Computing has become an important NP-Hard problem due to the increasing heterogeneity of workloads and the strict requirements imposed by Service Level Agreements (SLAs). Traditional algorithms are computationally simple, however, they often fail to adapt to the dynamic nature of cloud environments. On the other hand, traditional meta-heuristic algorithms such as Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) can produce good initial solutions, but usually experience “premature convergence,” whereby the swarm becomes “stuck” within a local optimum. To resolve these issues, this work proposes a Hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization with Genetic Operators (HPSO-GO) algorithm for efficient cloud resource scheduling. In HPSO-GO framework, the population diversity is continuously monitored, and when stagnation occurs, genetic operators such as crossover and mutation are triggered to introduce diversification and enable the scheduler to escape local optima. The effectiveness of HPSO-GO was evaluated through extensive simulations using the CloudSim simulation toolkit and real workload traces from the Alibaba Cluster dataset. The performance of HPSO-GO was evaluated against six different baseline algorithms: First-Come-First-Served (FCFS), Round Robin, Min-Min, Min-Max and Ant Colony Optimization along with the standard PSO. The results show that HPSO-GO achieves statistically significant performance improvements over standard PSO, and that it exceeds the static Min-Min heuristic algorithm. Additionally, in terms of total energy consumed, HPSO-GO is by far the highest performer of all the high-throughput scheduling algorithms, consuming less than 156.26 kWh of energy to complete all of its tasks. Through HPSO-GO's use of an HPSO along with a genetic algorithm, the average CPU utilisation for HPSO-GO was also higher than for standard PSO <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(31.64 \%$</tex> vs 22.24%). The findings provide strong support that integrating swarm intelligence with evolutionary operators provides a viable solution for efficiently scheduling cloud computing resources in real-time for multi-objective optimization in dynamic cloud environments.

title: Detecting Fresh & Rotten Fruits: A Comprehensive Study Using Deep & Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Fahmida Tahmin, Md. Musfiqur Rahman Mridha, Sakib Imtiaz, Fatin Nibbrash Nakib, Md. Shahid Ahammed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The global production of fresh fruits and the spoiled percentage pose significant challenges to public health, the economy, the environment, and food security. Accurate and efficient recognition of fruit quality, especially at the margin between fresh and rotten, is therefore essential. This study explores the application of advanced Deep Learning (DL) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques for the automated classification of fresh and rotten fruits. Several pretrained DL models as feature extractors, including members of the DenseNet family, Xception, and ResNet50V2, are evaluated alongside ML classifiers such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), and a custom DL-based classifier. Experiments are conducted on a publicly available Kaggle dataset comprising two classes: fresh and rotten. The primary contribution of this work is the demonstration of superior performance by the DenseNet169SVM model in binary fruit classification, which achieves the highest accuracy of 99.76 % with the lowest error rate. The findings highlight the potential of DL-ML models for reliable fruit quality assessment, contributing to enhanced food safety, waste reduction, and supply chain optimization. The proposed approach can be extended to other perishable food categories for broader food quality monitoring. Future work may involve real-time deployment and integration with smart agricultural or retail systems.

title: High-Performance Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Using Transfer Learning with Feature Fusion and Attention
authors: Md. Johir Uddin Bhuiyan, Habiba Akter Rimi, Md Asaduzzaman, Hashibul Ahsan Shoaib, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Anika Sayeema
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Brain tumor classification from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) plays a critical role in clinical diagnosis and treatment planning, yet manual interpretation is often time-consuming and prone to variability. While recent deep learning methods have improved classification accuracy, many approaches depend solely on final-layer features, which may neglect subtle tumor characteristics. To address this challenge, we propose a novel framework that integrates transfer learning with feature fusion and attention mechanisms for high-performance brain tumor classification. The model combines intermediate and deep feature representations to capture both local and global tumor patterns, while channel-wise attention enhances focus on diagnostically relevant features. Experiments on a publicly available brain tumor MRI dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves superior results compared to established baselines, reaching an overall accuracy of 96.8 %, F1-score of 96.6 %, and ROC-AUC of 98.8 %. In addition, the framework maintains competitive computational efficiency, with inference times comparable to lightweight architectures. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed approach to provide reliable, accurate, and efficient diagnostic support in clinical settings, offering a practical step toward integrating AI-based systems into neuro-oncology workflows.

title: Big Data-Driven Approach to Customer Churn Prediction in the Telecom Industry
authors: Mishel Rossmaree, Achala Aponso
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Customer churn prediction is a major challenge for the telecommunications industry, where attrition rates often exceed 25 % and directly impact revenue. This study proposes a scalable churn prediction pipeline built on Apache Spark and Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS), containerized using Docker to ensure reproducibility and deployment portability. Using the widely adopted Telco Customer Churn dataset, three machine learning models: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, and Random Forest were trained and evaluated. Among them, Random Forest achieved the best overall performance with an accuracy of 75.86 % and recall of 0.76, while Logistic Regression recorded the highest F1 score (0.74). All models trained in under eight seconds, confirming the system's computational efficiency. The pipeline integrates feature engineering, imbalance-aware evaluation using ROC-AUC and precision-recall curves, and model interpretation through feature importance. By emphasizing scalability, automation, and interpretability, this work demonstrates a deployment-ready framework for telecom churn analytics and outlines future directions such as hyperparameter optimization, real-time streaming, and explainable AI integration.

title: Development of a Flip-Chip Bonding Process for Scalable Silicon-Based Quantum Processors
authors: V. K. H. Van, C. Bohémier, S. S. Ramirez, R. Wreyford, A. Dash, A. Dickie, S. A. Yianni, F. E. Hudson, W. H. Lim, A. S. Dzurak, K. W. Chan
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The scalability of quantum processors relies on the efficient integration of control infrastructure with qubit devices. Flip-chip bonding (FCB) offers a compact and high-density interconnect solution for interconnecting quantum devices with cryogenic control electronics. In this work, we develop and demonstrate an FCB process compatible with silicon metal–oxide–semiconductor (SiMOS) structures. Indium micro-bumps were used to assemble SiMOS daisy chain and quantum dot (QD) devices, and the bonding integrity was evaluated through I—V measurements, electron microscopy, finite element simulations, and quantum transport spectroscopy. The developed FCB recipe provides a path toward scalable quantum processor architectures using standard CMOS-compatible platforms.

title: Predicting Enhancer DNA Utilizing $k$-mer Features and Multi-Headed CNN
authors: Priya Barua, Faisal Ahmed, Muhammad Anwarul Azim, Mohammad Khairul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Enhancers are non-coding DNA elements that play a critical role in regulating gene expression. Accurately predicting DNA enhancers is essential for understanding genetic regulatory mechanisms and their implications for health and disease. However, enhancer identification remains challenging due to the heterogeneous and complex nature of sequence motifs. To address this, we propose a novel DNA sequence encoding technique based on multi-k-mer features and a multi-headed Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture that processes multi-scale features simultaneously with four parallel branches to capture complementary local and global sequence patterns for enhancer prediction. Empirical results on a test set show the superiority of our method, achieving 77.25% accuracy, 80% sensitivity, 74.5% specificity, AUC of 0.84, and MCC of 0.55. These findings indicate the effectiveness of multi-scale k-mer feature representations and a CNN architecture for DNA enhancer prediction.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Methods with Enhanced Feature Extraction to Detect Deepfake Images
authors: Fahim Foysal Abak, M. Morsedur Rahman, S. M. Mahedy Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid advancement of generative models has accelerated the spread of deepfake technology, enabling the creation of highly realistic synthetic images that threaten digital content security, privacy, and trust. While deep learning (DL) approaches have shown promise in deepfake detection, conventional methods often fall short in robustness and accuracy. This study presents a comparative analysis of eight convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, such as EfficientNetB4 (EB4), EfficientNetB7 (EB7), DenseNet121 (DN121), DenseNet169 (DN169), Xception (XP), InceptionV3 (IV3), MesoNet (MN), and NasNet (NN) for deepfake image detection. A large-scale Kaggle dataset derived from the OpenForensics benchmark is utilized for training and evaluation. To enhance discriminative feature learning, preprocessing techniques such as Local Binary Patterns (LBP) and Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) are applied, alongside a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for adaptive attention. Experimental results demonstrate that EfficientNetB7 achieves the highest performance and outperforms other CNN architectures, with 93.99 % accuracy and an AUC of 0.9812. These findings highlight that integrating advanced preprocessing and attention mechanisms with CNN architectures significantly improves the reliability of deepfake detection.

title: Trustworthy Smishing Detection in Code-Mixed Bangla-English SMS: Dual-View XLM-R With Calibration, Selective Classification, and Open-Set Detection
authors: Ahammad Hossen Eazaz, Md Intishar Ahmed, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rise of mobile phishing via SMS (smishing) has become a pressing cybersecurity concern, particularly in multilingual regions like South Asia where messages frequently appear in Bangla, English, or their noisy code-mixed forms. Traditional models often deliver high accuracy in controlled settings but fail when confronted with real-world challenges such as spelling variations, transliteration, distributional shifts, or out-of-distribution (OOD) attacks. In this work, we present a dualview XLM-R framework that jointly learns from both original and romanized Bangla SMS, enforcing prediction consistency across views to build resilience against orthographic variation. To enhance trustworthiness, we incorporate probability calibration through temperature scaling, evaluate selective classification with risk-coverage analysis, and benchmark multiple OOD detection strategies including Energy, MSP, MC-Dropout, and Mahalanobis distance. Experiments on a newly curated Bangla-English code-mixed dataset show that our model achieves over 99.06 % accuracy, near-perfect calibration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E C E \approx 0.005$</tex>), and reliable selective abstention. While Energy and MSP struggle under OOD conditions, Mahalanobis distance reaches AUROC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.996$</tex>, offering strong open-set robustness. These findings highlight not only superior performance over strong baselines but also improved reliability and transparency, positioning our approach as a practical and trustworthy solution for smishing defense in low-resource, multilingual contexts.

title: URes2Net: U-Net Architecture with Res2Net for Skin Lesion Segmentation
authors: S¸Eyma Karagozoglu, Fırat Hardalac
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: In this paper, we present URes2Net, a novel and efficient architecture developed for medical image segmentation. The architecture of our model is based on a U-shaped network, in which both the encoder and the decoder part are improved by a proposed residual block, called Res2Block. Each Res2Block consists of a one-stage encoder-decoder pair to capture multiscale contextual information. Furthermore, the backbone of each Res2Block is built upon Res2Net, which enables hierarchical residual connections within the block. This design allows the network to effectively capture local and global contextual information while extracting features at different scales. URes2Net was evaluated on the ISIC-2017 skin lesion segmentation dataset. Quantitative results demonstrate that our model achieves a Dice loss of 0.819, MAE of 0.084, and a maximum F-measure of 0.897, which clearly outperforms comparable U-shaped architectures. Qualitative results show that the predicted probability maps align closely with ground truth masks across lesions of varying sizes. Thus, the network preserves fine details while accurately segmenting complex structures. By integrating multi-scale contextual information through hierarchical residual connections, the network demonstrates good results in detecting lesions with diverse shapes and sizes. The proposed model can also be extended to other biomedical image segmentation tasks, making it a promising solution for real-world clinical applications. The source code is available: https://github.com/sym-codes/URes2Net.

title: Code Smell Detection Framework Using Self Attention CNN: An Explainable Feature Selection Approach
authors: Fayzul Noor Tabeeb, Kazi Nusrat Jahan Suchi, Dipto Das, Nazneen Akhter
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Code smell detection is essential for preserving software quality and ensuring long-term maintainability. However, most existing approaches either struggle with high computational costs or fail to achieve both accuracy and interpretability simultaneously which leaves a gap in building efficient and transparent models. To address this gap, this study introduces a hybrid model that combines feature selection with an attention-based convolutional neural network (CNN) to enhance the accuracy, efficiency, and interpretability of multiple code smell classification. The feature selection module filters out irrelevant and redundant attributes that leads to a substantial reduction in computational time; for instance, the average detection time for God Class reduced from 4 m 26.9 s to 2 m 42.8 s, and for Long Method from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 m} 21 ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> to 2 m 56.4 s. The attention mechanism further strengthens the model's ability to prioritize significant patterns within the code representations, boosting key metrics such as precision, recall, and F -measure, which reached up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.975,0.990$</tex>, and 0.983, respectively for Long Method Code Smell Detection. In addition to performance gains, the model incorporates LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) for visualizing feature importance which provides transparent insights into the internal decision-making processes of the classifier. The proposed approach demonstrates promising potential for real-world integration, and future work may extend this framework to multi-label smell detection, support for multiple programming languages, and real-time deployment in intelligent development environments for proactive, context-aware feedback.

title: Transformer-Assisted Recommendation System with Apriori-Enhanced Behavior Mining in Decision Models for Engineering
authors: Mariyam Ahmed, Jainish Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Recommender systems still have a long way to go before they can accurately and consistently predict users’ future preferences. To address these challenges, this study proposes a Transformer-Assisted Recommendation System based on Association and Enhanced Behavior Modeling (TARSAEBM). The core objective of this approach is to integrate deep learning transformers with association rule mining to better capture and interpret complex user behavior patterns and dynamic preferences. By leveraging the sequential modeling power of transformers and the relational insights of association rules, TARS-AEBM enhances the understanding of contextual dependencies and item correlations. Experimental results demonstrate that TARS-AEBM significantly outperforms traditional recommendation methods in terms of F1-score, recall, and accuracy, proving its effectiveness in providing more personalized, adaptive, and precise recommendations across diverse user environments in decision model.

title: Physics–AI-Driven Design of GaN P-MISFETs with InGaN/AlN/AlGaN Heterostructures
authors: Linkun Sun, Zhehao Hu, Qiyi Guo, Bowen Li, Kain Lu Low, Wen Liu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid physics–AI framework for the design of CMOS-compatible p-type GaN MISFETs based on a polarization-engineered InGaN/AlN/AlGaN/AlN/GaN heterostructure. The workflow combines TCAD simulations, design of experiments (DOE), clustering analysis, and SHAP-informed feature evaluation to identify key design parameters and trade-offs. Results reveal that recess depth is the dominant factor, improving subthreshold swing (SS) and boosting I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> but shifting V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> negatively. In contrast, an AlN interlayer and higher Al/In compositions raise V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> via stronger polarization but degrade transport through reduced hole velocity and screening. SHAP analysis confirms these trade-offs, underscoring the need for co-optimization of recess depth, material composition, and interlayer design to balance I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>, SS, and V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> in GaN CMOS technology.

title: Enhanced Visual Retrieval and Decision Support
authors: Abdelkrim Saouabe, Said Tkatek, Doha Banoui, Khalid Tizra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The exponential growth of visual data across digital platforms, industrial monitoring, and healthcare applications has intensified the demand for intelligent and interpretable image retrieval systems. Traditional Content-Based Image Retrieval (CBIR) methods, based on handcrafted or convolutional descriptors, have laid the foundation for visual search but remain limited by low semantic interpretability, domain dependency, and restricted adaptability. In contrast, the rise of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) has introduced a new paradigm that redefines image retrieval as a process of semantic reasoning, synthesis, and decision support rather than simple feature matching. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of recent advances in generative AI–driven retrieval architectures, emphasizing their ability to bridge the semantic gap, enhance multimodal understanding, and provide explainable results. The review demonstrates that generative approaches consistently improve retrieval accuracy, robustness to domain variations, and interpretability of results, outperforming traditional CBIR in multiple application scenarios. Beyond performance evaluation, this work contributes to the conceptual unification of generative retrieval systems and positions them as intelligent decision-support tools for applications in medical imaging, industrial inspection, smart surveillance, and event understanding. The findings highlight a decisive shift from perception-driven to reasoning-driven retrieval systems, underscoring the potential for adaptive, transparent, and explainable visual search frameworks that can support complex decision-making across domains.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Grid-Connected Microgrid with PV and Battery Storage
authors: Tonmoy Roy Akash, Md. Sabbir Alam, Md. Naiemur Rahman, Bivas Sarkar, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis and simulation of a grid-connected PV microgrid system considering real constraints like environmental conditions and consumer load consumption. As demand for renewable energy systems continues to grow, grid stability and reliability are significant challenges. The grid-connected PV microgrid system and storage are considered as a continuous power supply. The developed microgrid system has sufficient capacity to meet the load requirements without interruption. The model also follows the defector industry-standard real and reactive power injection to the grid. The paper presents a detailed design of the system's components, including passive components, the controller, PV panel sizing, and battery storage. Several case studies were presented to demonstrate the system's control functionalities in various scenarios. Simulation results show that a 50 kW PV array with a 268 kWh battery bank can reliably supply the community load, maintaining grid frequency within 59.8-60.2 Hz and stable three-phase voltages. The proposed model provides a scalable and sustainable solution for communityscale renewable integration, highlighting both its technical feasibility and its practical deployment potential.

title: Design of an Ultra-Wideband Plane Wave Generator with High Aperture Efficiency for 1–10 GHz
authors: Xiaolong Wang, Weimin Wang, Le Yu, Zhangzhang Jiang, Wei Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of highfrequency beam narrowing and strong mutual coupling at low frequencies in a 1–10 GHz ultra-wideband plane wave generator (PWG) system with Vivaldi antennas. An array integration method based on the orthogonal arrangement of high- and lowfrequency tapered slot antenna (TSA) units is proposed. The method employs low-frequency (1–4 GHz) and high-frequency (4–10 GHz) TSA elements to cover different sub-bands, and combines CST full-wave simulation to extract the transfer function under actual coupling conditions for excitation optimization. This approach achieves wide beam radiation and high-quality quiet zone synthesis across the entire frequency range. The final prototype demonstrates that the PWG achieves a quiet zone utilization efficiency of 80% (a quiet zone (QZ) of 720 mm from a 900 mm aperture), with field perturbations limited to less than 1 dB in amplitude and 10° in phase.

title: A Multi-View Feature Fusion Approach with XLM-Roberta for Spam Email Detection
authors: Bipasha Majumder, Antu Chowdhury, A R M Mahamudul Hasan, Didarul Islam Didar, Bashirul Islam, Shekh Maruf
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Spam and phishing emails remain significant problems in email security and consumer privacy. This article proposes a spam classification model using XLM RoBERTa transformer and a Multi-View Feature Fusion approach to combine CLS token, mean-pooled, and attention-weighted embeddings to enhance the classification performance. Training and evaluation of the model were conducted using the Enron-Spam dataset and achieved its highest accuracy at 99.54%, with precision, recall, and F1-score all above 99%. This performance beats the previously reported performance on the same dataset. Data preprocessing included duplicates removal, cleaning, and tokenization using the XLM-RoBERTa tokenizer. The model underwent training for 10 epochs, incorporating hyperparameter optimization and early stopping techniques to mitigate overfitting. These results demonstrate the validity and suitability of the model for actual spam detection tasks. Future extensions include deploying the model on other datasets as well as deploying it for low-resource languages such as Bangla.

title: An Adaptive Confidence-Weighted Transformer Ensemble Framework for Tomato Leaf Disease Recognition
authors: Md. Fatin Nibbrash Nakib, Md. Shahid Uz Zaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Global economic growth relies on agricultural output, as it ensures food security, raises incomes, and supports employment. However, crop diseases can drastically reduce yields, disrupt supply chains, and strain financial resources, especially in developing countries. Tomato leaf infections are among the most serious threats to the agricultural industry and local farmers. This study proposes a deep learning-based method for the early and precise identification of tomato leaf diseases, covering 10 distinct classes using 18,160 images from the PlantVillage dataset. Two pre-trained transformer-based architectures: Vision Transformer (ViT) and Data-efficient Image Transformer (DeiT) were used as feature extractors, followed by evaluation with multiple classifiers, including logistic regression, XGBoost, and a custom dense layer. Experimental results show that the custom dense layer achieved the highest classification performance among all classifiers. The outputs of the ViT and DeiT models were further combined using a confidence-based weighted ensemble, enabling dynamic prioritization of predictions with higher certainty. The proposed framework achieved 99.95 % classification accuracy with an exceptionally low error rate of 0.0005, outperforming individual models. These results highlight the effectiveness of transformer-based feature extraction combined with ensemble learning for reliable, scalable, and accurate plant disease detection, offering valuable support for sustainable agriculture and global food security.

title: Discovering Academic Pathways of Course Enrollment with Unsupervised Clustering and Rule Mining
authors: Mustakim Ahmed, Sourav Datto, Mohaimen-Bin-Noor
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Student course enrollment data often contains hidden patterns that can provide insights into academic behavior and curricular structures. However, detecting meaningful associations and clusters from such data remains challenging due to the overlap of courses across semesters and specializations. This study presents a combined framework of association rule mining and clustering for analyzing student enrollment records. A binary transaction matrix was created from the most frequent courses and analyzed using both pairwise co-occurrence measures and Apriori rules to capture complementary associations. For segmentation, K-Means, Mean Shift, and Gaussian Mixture Models were applied, with evaluation through silhouette score, Davies Bouldin index, and Dunn index. Results showed that Mean Shift achieved the highest cluster separation but produced too many clusters, while Gaussian Mixture provided fewer interpretable clusters aligned with curricular tracks. Pairwise methods identified strong but rare associations, while Apriori captured more frequent rules. Together these findings demonstrate that student enrollments are not random, but form organized trajectories based on semester timing and subject specialization. The framework supports curriculum design, targeted advising, and identification of academic pathways, providing a practical tool for institutions to enhance program management and student guidance.

title: Modeling of small-signal source and drain charges in ASM-HEMT model
authors: Shrayan Mukhopadhyay, Mohit Shukla, Amit Kumar Behera, Avirup Dasgupta
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper is an attempt to start inclusion of the effect of small signal perturbations at any of the 3 HEMT device terminals specified in the ASM HEMT model. We propose formulae for calculation of small signal charge density at source and drain terminals in terms of device parameters and applied voltages. The proposed model is able to capture the non-quasi static perturbations more accurately and shows good agreement with the TCAD data.

title: GAF-DeepGCN: Enhanced Autism Spectrum Disorder Classification via Gramian Angular Field Augmented Deep Graph Convolutional Networks
authors: Sadman Israk Sporsho, Yuki Bhowmik, Naima Siddika Toha, Riasat Khan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) classification remains a significant challenge due to the disorder's heterogeneity and complex neurological underpinnings. This work introduces GAF-DeepGCN, a novel framework integrating Gramian Angular Field (GAF) encoded temporal dynamics with Deep Graph Convolutional Networks (DeepGCN) to enhance ASD classification performance. By transforming resting-state fMRI (rs-fMRI) time series into GAF images, we capture temporal dependencies and leverage the representational power of ResNet50 to extract salient features. Combined with functional connectivity matrices, these features form the node attributes in a population graph, which a DeepGCN subsequently processes. Our experiments on the Autism Brain Imaging Data Exchange (ABIDE) dataset demonstrate that GAF-DeepGCN achieves 83.65% accuracy and an AUC of 0.9116, surpassing existing methodologies. The results highlight the efficacy of integrating temporal dynamics with graph-based deep learning for improved ASD diagnosis.

title: Employing Machine Learning Algorithms for Intrusion Attack Detection
authors: Nadia Attiya Algaddar, Melad Mohamed Al-Daeef, Alhadi A. Klaib
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: As computer networks grow increasingly, complex and robust security mechanisms are essential and critically needed. This research is an attempt to evaluate four machine learning algorithms they are, Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbor, and Support Vector Machine for real-time intrusion detection using the publicly available CICIDS2017 dataset. Based on performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, Decision Tree and SVM are expected to achieve the highest accuracy, demonstrating strong efficiency and reliability for real-time intrusion detection.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Controller Techniques for Wireless Power Transfer in Electric Vehicles Charging Applications
authors: M. Saranraj, Saravanan Kaliyaperumal, Ali Elrashidi, Kareem M. AboRas, R Narayanamoorthi
abstract: Wireless power transfer technology has emerged as a key enabler for efficient and convenient charging of electric vehicles, addressing the limitations of conventional plug-in charging systems. However, WPT systems face significant challenges, including power control, resonance frequency tuning, load regulation, and efficiency optimization. To overcome these challenges, various control strategies have been developed to enhance the stability, safety, and performance of these systems. This review provides a comprehensive analysis of different controller techniques, including proportional-integral controllers, sliding mode controllers, model predictive controllers, and adaptive control methods. It also explores optimization algorithms and hybrid approaches that aim to improve the dynamic response, robustness, and energy efficiency. By comparing the effectiveness of these control strategies, this study offers guidance to researchers and engineers in selecting the most suitable controller for specific applications in wireless charging of electric vehicles. The review concludes by highlighting future research directions and potential advancements in intelligent control strategies for next-generation wireless charging systems.

title: A Time-updated Fusion of Fast-varying and Slow-varying Signals for Health Assessment of Liquid Aerospace Engines
authors: Zhenyi Chen, Wenhao Zhang, Jianshu Yin, Jin Chen, Yanyang Zi, Hongxing Jia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Many time-varying state monitoring methods, such as auto-regressive moving average (ARMA) and adaptive threshold algorithm (ATA), often analyze these data streams independently, lacking a joint analysis of the multi-physics signals. Furthermore, liquid aerospace engines (LAEs) operate under multi-source loads and complex conditions, leading to difficulties in system-level assessment throughout the entire working stage. To solve these issues, this paper proposes a dynamic fusion method for fast-varying signals (FVS) (e.g., vibration and pulsating pressure) and slow-varying ones (SVS) (e.g., flow, pressure) to comprehensively assess the system-level health state. First, the concerned instantaneous frequencies of FVS are obtained by second-order synchrosqueezing transform, providing fusion indexes of FVS. Second, a time-varying upper confidence limit algorithm and a confidence interval algorithm are proposed to map structural and functional health indicators onto a unified scale for system-level, whole-stage evaluation. Finally, a dynamic fusion algorithm, where the confidence boundaries of health indicators evolve with time rather than remaining fixed, is proposed for customized nonlinear time-tracking health indexes to translate the extracted features of FVS and key features of SVS into a standardized scale, quantifying dynamic deviation for each feature from its healthy baseline, and subsequently fusing these multi-source indicators into a comprehensive health index to achieve a holistic system-level assessment. To validate its performance, two ground-running tests of LAEs are conducted. The results show that the proposed method can not only cover the whole working stage for health assessment, but is also capable of assessing structural health, functional health, and system-level health, providing an effective tool for health monitoring and assessment for LAEs.

title: A Differential Resonant Ultrasound Pulser in 180 nm BCD: Fundamental Limits and Design Considerations
authors: Yaohua Zhang, Andreas Demosthenous
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This article presents the design of a differential resonant pulser implemented in 180 nm BCD process for PMUT-based ultrasound applications. By employing a precisely timed switching scheme together with differential resonant operation, a novel voltage step-up methodology is developed. A key contribution of this work is the first comprehensive, phase-by-phase analytical treatment of the pulser operation. This analysis reveals critical inter-phase coupling mechanisms that are not captured by conventional simulation-based approaches. It identifies two previously overlooked design limitations: 1) ac current leakage inherent to back-to-back DMOS configurations; and 2) an unavoidable timing offset caused by finite on-resistance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R}_{\mathrm {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) between the inductor current peak and the capacitor voltage zero-crossing during discharge. These effects establish fundamental performance boundaries for resonant pulsers. Measurement results validate the proposed design, achieving a 17.7 V output from a 5 V supply (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.5\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> voltage step-up) and a 71.7% reduction in power consumption. The measured performance represents state-of-the-art and shows excellent agreement with both theory and simulation.

title: When Future Communications Shift Toward Narrow Beams: A Forward-Looking Survey on Pointing Errors and Alignment Limits
authors: Meysam Ghanbari, Mohammad Taghi Dabiri, Osamah S. Badarneh, Mazen Hasna, Yazan H. Al-Badarneh, Mustafa K. Alshawaqfeh, Khalid A. Qaraqe
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Directional links in free-space optical (FSO), millimeter-wave (mmWave), and terahertz (THz) systems are a cornerstone of emerging 6G networks, yet their reliability is fundamentally limited by pointing errors and misalignment. Existing studies address this impairment using technology-specific definitions, models, and mitigation approaches, which hinders cross-domain comparison and transferable design insight. This survey provides a unified treatment of pointing errors across optical and high-frequency wireless communications. We establish consistent terminology and a cross-technology taxonomy of pointing errors, review angular misalignment and statistical distribution models, and analyze their impact on system performance. Mitigation techniques are systematically surveyed with emphasis on optical systems and their connection to underlying pointing-error models. The survey further provides a detailed examination of pointing-error effects in orbital angular momentum (OAM) links and quantum optical communications, and surveys the corresponding mitigation approaches tailored to mode-dependent impairments and quantum measurement constraints. The survey also outlines open challenges and future research directions. By consolidating fragmented literature into a coherent framework, this work supports consistent analysis and robust design of next-generation directional communication systems.

title: Efficient Approximation of Goldbach Partition Function Using Machine Learning Models
authors: Cheng-Zhe Wu, Shyong Jian Shyu
abstract: Goldbach’s conjecture, still unproven, posits that every even integer greater than 2 can be expressed as the sum of two primes. The Goldbach partition function G(n) counts the number of such prime pairs for even integer n > 2. While several analytic approximations exist, recent studies have explored machine learning approaches to estimate G(n). Building on prior architectures, we propose refined models that integrate Euler’s totient function φ(n) as a primary feature, combined with nonlinear logarithmic-exponential transformations, data exchange, residual learning, and deeper layers with increasing unit sizes. These innovations enable more accurate and stable approximations. Experimental results confirm that our models consistently outperform existing ones by achieving lower mean squared errors and Error rates. Our best-performing model not only delivers reliable accuracy but also demonstrates strong robustness across various test ranges.

title: A Super Low Frequency Transmitter for Ground-transparent Communication Across Four-layer Medium
authors: Xie He, Jianya Zhang, Qiang Zhou, Changfeng Liang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Rotating magnet based mechanical antenna (RMBMA) is a novel super low frequency electromagnetic transmitting technology. It uses the mechanical rotary motion of permanent magnet to directly excite electromagnetic waves and is expected to achieve the miniaturization of the super low frequency ground-transparent transmitting system. To reveal the ground-transparent propagation characteristics of the RMBMA, this paper establishes a four-layer medium model of air - reinforced concrete - air - underground rock layer. By using the Helmholtz equations and the Sommerfeld integral, the analytical expression of the RMBMA in the four-layer medium is given. Based on the electromagnetic field simulation software FEKO, the correctness of the proposed four-layer medium propagation model is verified, and the directional characteristics of electromagnetic wave propagation are analyzed. Moreover, the effects of the reinforced concrete layer conductivity and operating frequency on the propagation characteristics of the RMBMA are investigated, and a method for extending the horizontal transmission distance is provided. A prototype of the RMBMA is set up to conduct ground-transparent propagation and communication experiments. This verifies the feasibility of the scheme and lays a foundation for the realization of efficient and miniaturized ground-transparent communication.

title: ilLog: Incremental Learning Based Anomaly Detection from Evolving System Logs
authors: Jiyu Tian, Mingchu Li, Zumin Wang, Liming Chen, Jing Qin, Jianyuan Gan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Log anomaly detection (LAD) is of paramount importance to enhance the reliability and stability of software systems. Current state-of-the-art LAD suffers a significant performance degradation when dealing with consistently evolving log events caused by system updates. To build a reliable LAD model under the context of log data evolution, we propose an incremental learning-based method for LAD, namely ilLog, to avoid catastrophic forgetting of previously learned knowledge while continuously updating the model for better detection when processing the evolving log events. In particular, we design a novel entropy-driven sorting algorithm for real log sample replay, which enables the preservation of old knowledge via storing representative samples with discrete sequence features from previous tasks. Additionally, we introduce a Halton-based low discrepancy sequence to better approximate the sliced Cram´ er distance between the probability distributions of two models, thus enhancing the model learning capability. Based on a standard incremental learning protocol setting, we evaluate the newly proposed ilLog method on three publicly available datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves the best performance compared to SOTA LAD methods and models by applying existing IL-based methods in evolving software systems.

title: Vibration Signal Generation for Straddle-Monorail Gearboxes Using a Physics-Conditioned VQ-VAE Model
authors: Ling Zhao, Hangjun Wu, Na Zhang, Gang Wu
abstract: Accurate fault diagnosis of straddle-monorail gearboxes is essential for operational safety. However, data-driven methods are often limited by the scarcity of labeled fault samples. Existing generative approaches, such as generative adversarial networks and diffusion models, tend to produce kinematically inconsistent signals and require substantial computational resources. To address these issues, this article proposes a physics-conditioned vector-quantized variational autoencoder. The model incorporates gearbox dynamics into the generation process via a lightweight, differentiable physical conditioner. This module generates time-aligned physical sequences, including meshing stiffness and fault-induced impulses, which are injected into the latent space via a dual-path conditioning mechanism. This design ensures spectral–temporal consistency between generated signals and underlying physical processes. Experimental results on both self-collected and public datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves improved generation quality and computational efficiency. Specifically, the model achieves a root-mean-square error of 0.0497, a peak signal-to-noise ratio of 26.17 dB, and a frequency spectrum cosine similarity of 0.98 or higher. The method also shows strong generalization across multiple datasets, including Southeast University Gearbox and Case Western Reserve University. The proposed approach provides an effective and practical solution for data augmentation in industrial fault diagnosis.

title:
P-GaN-Gate GaN-on-Si HEMTs with BV>1200 V and Excellent Dynamic Performance Realized Through Low Power NH
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Plasma Acceptor Re-Passivation
authors: Zhaofeng Wang, Zhihong Liu, Weichuan Xing, Weihang Zhang, Shenglei Zhao, Xiangdong Li, Shuzhen You, Jincheng Zhang, Yue Hao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this letter, we report p-GaN gate enhancement-mode (E-mode) GaN-on-Si high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) fabricated using an Mg acceptor re-passivation technique (ARP HEMT) via NH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf> plasma treatment. The gate-to-source and gate-to-drain access regions were subjected to hydrogen ion implantation through NH₃ plasma treatment in a plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) system, forming a high-resistance cap layer. The fabricated device demonstrates a high threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>) of 2.1 V, a low gate reverse leakage current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gr</inf>) of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">⁻¹¹</sup> A/mm, and a high ON/OFF current ratio (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>) of up to 3×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">⁹</sup>. The dynamic on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,d</inf>) measured under a quiescent drain voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds,q</inf>) of 600 V with a stress time of 1200 s is only 1.03 times the static on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,s</inf>) at 25°C. A breakdown voltage (BV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>) of 1298 V and a specific on-resistance of 0.85 mΩ•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> were achieved in a device with Lgd=6 μm after additional SiN passivation. The developed fabrication process shows significant potential for application in GaN-based power transistors.

title: Fake News Detection in Bangla: A Transformer Ensemble Framework for Robust Classification
authors: Shubail Haque Turza, Akif Mahdi, Md. Ibne Shihab Shad, M. Hasnat Kabir, Ariful Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The exponential growth of fake news over digital media has seriously disrupted information and public trust, as well as threatened social stability. These challenges are even more severe for low-resource, morphologically rich languages like Bangla, where the scarcity of NLP resources and a high amount of code-mixed content make the automated detection even more challenging. To address this gap, the research proposes a transformer-based ensemble model for Bangla fake news detection, which combines BanglaBERT, IndicBERT, and XLM-RoBERTa via soft voting, along with a morphology-aware preprocessing pipeline. On the balanced IBFND benchmark, BanglaBERT achieved the best single-model result with 99.03% accuracy, and the ensemble achieved 98.93 % accuracy with more stable performance on evaluation metrics. Cross-dataset validation on the BanglaFakeNews corpus provided additional evidence of the approach's robustness but also demonstrated the effect of imbalance and domain coverage on evaluation. Overall, the results have shown that language-aware preprocessing combined with ensemble learning sets a high and consistent baseline for Bangla fake news detection, and offers a promising direction for under-resourced languages with complex linguistic structures as well.

title: Hybrid Classical-Learned Residual Lossless Image Compression with Affine Translation Augmentation and Entropy-Fidelity Tradeoff Analysis
authors: Sumaya Ishrat Moyeen, Md. Sadiqur Rahman Mabud, Md. Shahid Uz Zaman, Md. Al Mamun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid residual compression framework that integrates classical spatial prediction with a convolutional autoencoder to improve lossless image coding. Established predictors—PAETH, MED and a simplified CALIC-GAP—are first applied to generate low-entropy residuals, with integer-pixel translation augmentation used during training to enhance predictor robustness. The residuals are then normalized and encoded by a lightweight autoencoder trained for minimal distortion. Evaluations on MNIST, CIFAR-10 and CLIC datasets under both teacher-forced and predictor-driven decompression demonstrate consistent entropy reduction with high reconstruction fidelity. Comparative analysis highlights that PAETH predictor consistently achieves the best balance between compression efficiency and reconstruction fidelity, yielding the highest SSIM/PSNR and lowest MSE across all the datasets, while also maintaining competitive entropy levels; CALIC minimizes true entropy but inflates reconstructed entropy; and MED yields the lowest reconstructed entropy at the cost of significant quality loss. These results confirm that combining classical prediction with learned residual modeling provides an efficient and practical pathway toward deployable lossless image compression.

title: LoRA-Adapted Vision Transformers for Efficient and Explainable Colorectal Cancer Classification
authors: Nafisa Binte Ghulam Kibria, Hafsa Binte Kibria, Md. Nahiduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths, where an early and accurate diagnosis is vital for effective treatment. Conventional histopathological analysis is manual, time-consuming, and subject to inter-observer variability. That is why automated diagnostic tools come in handy. Deep learning has shown great potential to address these challenges by enabling fast and reproducible image-based classification. In this study, Vision Transformers (ViTs) are used for the classification of colorectal cancer tissue using the NCT-CRC-HE100 K dataset. A baseline ViT demonstrates strong performance but requires high computational resources. To enhance efficiency, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method, is introduced. By updating only 3.25% of the total parameters, LoRA improves classification performance, achieving 0.9949 accuracy and a 0.9949 weighted F1-score. Gradientweighted Class Activation Mapping (GradCAM) is integrated to provide visual explanations of predictions, supporting clinical interpretability. External validation on the EBHI-Seg dataset further demonstrates generalizability, with over 90% accuracy and F1-score across six tissue classes. Overall, the LoRA-adapted ViT offers an accurate, efficient, and interpretable solution for colorectal cancer diagnosis.

title: An Emotion-Aware Conversational AI with Intensity Scoring, Response Ranking, and Emotional State Visualization
authors: K.M.S.S. Kumarasingha, W.M.C.J.T. Kithulwatta, M.M S.M. Hansana, S.L.C. Hansika, P.A.T.C. Wijerathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Various psychological distresses are leading global healthcare challenge. Therefore, this research study proposes a multi-component, emotion-aware solution for detecting and responding sensitively to users' emotional states during conversations. This study includes emotional intensity scoring, emotion classification, & emotional response generation. Firstly, analyzing user input using a BERT-based model fine-tuned with the Go-Emotions dataset to determine emotional intensity via the NRC emotional intensity dictionary was done. The user message is also processed through a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The system uses 5 possible responses from the model for ranking their suitability The most suitable response selection collects emotional metadata about intensity and category and stores it for visualization of user-model relationship patterns. The study indicates that the responses yielded a RAG relevance score of above 0.70 in each instance with a mean value of 0.77 and emotional intensity scores of between 0.0168 and 0.1344. It means that there is a controlled emotional expressiveness variation. Centered visualization shows a gradual increase in the emotional valence of the user between -0.4 and about 0.8 in all interactions, which is a positive emotional regulation. The study observed for a positive relationship between user emotional status and system responses. This research projects practical applications for mental health support, emotion recognition and socially intelligent Artificial Intelligence tools, which needed for conversation-based adaptive agents.

title: Accelerating Distributed GNN Training by Attention Based Two-Level Dynamic Feature Caching
authors: Md Golam Morshed, Md. Murad Kabir Nipun, Raton Kumar Nondi, F A Sabbir Ahamed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The effectiveness of deep learning approaches on datasets such as images and texts has been extended to more complicated graph-structured data by evolving graph neural networks (GNNs). The current frameworks integrate mini-batch and sampling to train models efficiently on big graphs using GPU accelerators. Distributed GNN training has become more common as several concurrent workers learn on separate parts of a big graph, driven by the rapid increase in both the size of graphs and the dimensions of features. However, research has shown that the collection of graph features across servers takes up the majority of each worker's training time, which poses a serious problem for the training of distributed GNN. This paper presents a new and fast memory-aware, two-level attention-based dynamic feature caching method to accelerate GNN training. Our suggested solution effectively minimizes the time required for obtaining features by using the available GPU and CPU resources to store frequently accessed graph data in a cache. Additionally, it incorporates an efficient dynamic caching strategy that considers both the attention score and the structural information of the graph, as well as the data access patterns during the training of a sampling-based GNN. We found that it could achieve a performance speedup of up to 5x over the state-of-the-art baselines, all while eliminating the feature retrieval time from the distributed GNN training pipeline. We used six publicly available large graph datasets to show the efficacy of our method.

title: A Comparative Study of FPGA Debouncing Architectures for Emergency Stop Safety Channels
authors: Md Towhidul Islam, Shanjida Islam, Isha Das, Chaitaly Nandi, Monjurul Islam, Tasnim Jerin Labonno
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: When a human hits an emergency stop, the logic must not chatter. Mechanical contacts exhibit millisecondscale bounce that can produce multiple spurious transitions. For emergency-stop (E-Stop) channels, debouncing must provide explicit guarantees on latency and single-count interpretation. This paper implements and compares two FPGA debouncers on a Cyclone IV device at 5 0 MHz: (i) a window-qualified finite-state machine (FSM) that accepts a change only after a fixed stability interval, and (ii) a cyclelevel FSM that accepts the first stable edge and enforces a lockout to bound subsequent toggles. Both designs employ two- stage synchronization and close timing with large margin. We characterize resource cost, timing, power, latency distributions, and accepted-rate envelopes under shared bounce stimuli. The windowed design offers a simple audit trail tied to an explicit qualification window; the lockout design delivers near-instantaneous response with a clear throughput contract. Results indicate that either approach can supply deterministic, auditable behavior at negligible area and power, enabling straightforward integration into certified E-Stop functions.
