publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Air quality has significant impacts on human health, ecosystems, and wildlife, making its monitoring and forecasting a critical concern. Bangladesh frequently experiences high levels of air pollution that exceed safe thresholds. This paper presents a harmonized Air Quality Index (AQI) dataset for Bangladesh (2012-2024), covering eight cities and ten monitoring stations, and a systematic comparison of static and forecasting deep learning (DL) models for AQI prediction. Building on this dataset, two complementary modeling frameworks were developed: (1) a forecasting pipeline employing residual learning with temporal lags, and (2) a static pipeline utilizing concurrent pollutant and station data. DL architectures including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) were systematically evaluated under static and forecasting scenarios, with ensemble methods applied to enhance prediction accuracy. Stacking ensembles consistently outperformed individual models, achieving mean absolute error (MAE) of approximately 2.33, mean squared error (MSE) of 40.56, and coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.98 in static evaluation, and improving from MAE of 4.1 to 1.99 in forecasting with extended temporal windows. CNN and TCN demonstrated strong performance for longer horizons, whereas recurrent models and MLP were less effective. These findings can inform more accurate AQI forecasting and support evidence-based air quality management in Bangladesh.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Pea Leaf Diseases Using EfficientNetB7: A Scalable Approach for Agricultural Diagnostics
authors: Srikanta Kumar Mohapatra, K. K. Manivannan, Dipannita Ray, A. Parvathi Priya, Kochumol Abraham, Disha Garg
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Pea leaf diseases affect the yield of crops and thus require a proper and efficient identification system for management. This research leverages the EfficientNetB7 architecture to classify pea leaf samples into five categories: FRESH_LEAF, DOWNY_MILDEW_LEAF, LEAFMINER_LEAF, POWDER_MILDEW_LEAF, and OTHER. The data used in this work encompasses 1,432 images from the ghetto-d dataset; the images were pre-processed by resizing, normalizing, and applying augmentation. The dataset was split into training, validation, and test subsets in an 80:10:10 ratio to ensure balanced evaluation. The proposed model achieved 85% accuracy and a high level of precision, recall, and F1 score for all classes, thus confirming the model’s good generalization capabilities. Some methods, such as dropout layers, early stopping, and learning rate scheduling, were used to reduce overfitting to improve the performance. The confusion matrix and classification report provided the model’s effectiveness, and four minor classification errors identified further optimization opportunities. This work shows that EfficientNetB7 is capable of automating disease classification and offers a scalable approach for agricultural applications. Possible further studies could enhance the present dataset by including wider datasets or applying more effective feature learning systems, such as Vision Transformers. Such developments may also enhance the automatic control of sustainable agriculture to manage crops.

title: Covert Communication for UAV-Assisted Systems with an Active Warden
authors: Mingjie Yang, Linying Tian, Chan Gao, Bingbing Xiao
abstract: To address the inherent openness of wireless communication environments, covert communication has emerged as a promising approach to protect legitimate transmissions from unauthorized detection. This paper investigates a UAVassisted wireless covert communication system, where channel uncertainty is exploited to enable covert information transmission in the presence of active wardens acting simultaneously as jamming sources. We first model the indirect communication strategy between legitimate parties and characterize the detection behavior of an active warden. To achieve both covertness and communication efficiency, the transmit power of the devices and the hovering positions of UAVs are jointly optimized, subject to the dual constraints of maintaining a sufficient covert communication rate and ensuring a high detection error probability at the warden. Closed-form expressions for the wardens detection error probability and the achievable covert communication rate are derived. Furthermore, numerical optimization is employed to maximize the achievable rate under covertness constraints. Simulation results corroborate the theoretical analysis and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in enabling covert communication in jamming-assisted active warden scenarios.

title: Female Leadership in HR Decision Models for Employee Satisfaction in a Cement Company
authors: Nanda Ayudhea, Delsa Arrani, Vilena Nadianty Gunawan, Rini Kurnia Sari, Aisyah Qanita, Baghas Budi Wicaksono
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Employee satisfaction has long been acknowledged as a key determinant of organizational performance, productivity, and sustainability. Employee satisfaction is affected by multiple factors, such as female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline by multiple determinants, such as female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline, which collectively shape employees’ motivation levels, accountability, and engagement among employees. The purpose of this study is to explore how female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline directly affect employee satisfaction in an engineering-based context, with PT XYZ in the cement industry as the case example. A quantitative research design was applied, utilizing purposive sampling involving 100 participants. The data collected were processed through multiple regression analysis using SPSS version 22. The results of the validity and reliability testing confirmed that all measurement items were both valid and reliable, while classical assumption tests demonstrated that the regression model satisfied all statistical requirements. The results indicate that female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline each exert a positive and significant effect on employee satisfaction. Among these, female leadership demonstrated the strongest effect, underscoring the importance of inclusive and transformational leadership in motivating employees. These results highlight the strategic importance of leadership, culture, and discipline in fostering a motivated, productive, and competitive workforce within industrial organizations.

title: Collaborative Semiconductor Production Economic: Mobile Demand Fulfillment via Leakage Predictive Model and Device Technology Optimization
authors: Rami Salem, Songtao Wo, Ting Chen, Ahmed Mohamed, Huy Do, Anand Subramaniam, Tsung-Han Lee
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper addresses critical challenges in meeting mobile unit demand while minimizing production costs through an advanced thermal design power predictive model and concurrent device process optimization. The integrated approach increased mobile unit allocation from 15% to 70%, thereby making the mass production more cost effective while meeting customer specifications, demonstrating effective collaboration in semiconductor yield enhancement.

title: Toward Safer Workplaces: A Review of Digital Twin-Enabled Safety Training
authors: Shahed Chowdhury Omi, Musfiq Alam Chowdhury, Sabiha Alam Chowdhury, Mehedi Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Rapid technological advancements in Industry 4.0 are reshaping industrial environments, introducing new operational risks alongside productivity gains. Traditional safety training methods often fail to adequately prepare workers for the complex, dynamic challenges of modern workplaces. This paper presents a comprehensive review of Digital Twin (DT) technology as a transformative solution for industrial safety training. By creating dynamic, high-fidelity virtual replicas of physical systems that are updated with real-time data, DTs offer immersive, risk-free and data-rich training environments. This review explores the limitations of conventional training, examines the role of enabling technologies such as the Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Extended Reality (XR) and outlines the significant benefits of DT-enabled training, including enhanced realism, scenario repeatability and datadriven performance feedback. It also tackles the main obstacles to adoption, including infrastructure expenses, data standardization and legacy system integration. This paper makes the case for DT-based methods' potential to greatly increase workforce competency and lower workplace incidents by contrasting them with other digital training solutions and highlighting their distinct advantages in developing flexible and contextually relevant safety protocols.

title: Polarization Insensitive Metasurfaces Absorber for Visible Wavelengths
authors: Hayatul Islam Navin, Abu Ismail Siddique, Makshudur Rahman, Sharaf Tasnim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Metasurface-based plasmonic absorbers leverage engineered metallic surfaces to enhance light absorption at specific wavelengths, which have opened new applications in nanophotonics. However, many existing plasmonic absorbers suffer from narrow bandwidths or reduced efficiency in the visible regime, which limits their applicability in practical photonic devices. This work presents a plasmonic light absorber (PLA) using metasurfaces to achieve a high absorption in the visible spectrum of light. The metasurface consists of an infinite array of unit cell with a cylinder surrounded by a ring, where the cylinder and the ring are made of aluminum oxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex>) and titanium (Ti), respectively. Simulation results reveal an average absorption <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>97 \%$</tex> covering the visible wavelengths of 380 to 700 nm and a maximum absorption around unity from 380 to 500 nm. Additionally, the metasurface is polarization-insensitive due to its structural symmetry, hence independent of incident light polarization and giving same absorption efficiency. The proposed metasurface absorber can be used for applications requiring broadband, polarization-independent absorption in the visible spectrum.

title: SBEX: Noise-Robust Speech Bandwidth Expansion Through Mamba-Inspired Residual Time-Frequency Learning
authors: Sayeda Rahman Ananna, Taieba Athay, Nursadul Mamun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Robust speech super-resolution aims to reconstruct high-frequency cues from low-bandwidth speech while preserving intelligibility in real-world acoustic conditions. Although state-of-the-art methods perform well in clean scenarios, they often fail to recover high-frequency components under noisy environments. To address this challenge, SBEX is proposed as a noise-robust framework for speech super-resolution. Unlike conventional approaches that degrade under acoustic distortions, SBEX leverages a state-space-inspired core to capture both fine spectral details and long-range temporal dependencies. In addition, a noise-aware encoder is integrated to ensure stable and accurate reconstruction of high-frequency harmonics. Experimental results demonstrate that SBEX produces more natural and intelligible speech across diverse noisy conditions, highlighting its potential for deployment in telecommunications, speech recognition, and assistive hearing technologies.

title: CPS-Guard: Defensive Architectures for Securing Large Language Models in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Md. Mazharul Islam, Mubasshir Ahmed, Niaz Ashraf Khan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Cyber-physical systems (CPS) are increasingly adopting large language models (LLMs) for log analysis, operator support, and anomaly triage. While LLMs enable powerful reasoning and flexible natural-language interaction, their probabilistic and opaque behavior introduces new attack surfaces, including prompt injection, adversarial manipulation, and privacy leakage. Existing CPS defenses largely assume deterministic models and do not account for LLM-specific threats, whereas current LLM safety work rarely targets real-time, safety-critical control environments. To bridge this gap, we propose CPS-Guard, a defense-in-depth architecture for LLM-enabled CPS that operates under advisory-only semantics. CPS-Guard combines secure prompt handling, explicit threat modeling, a deterministic safety validator with policy and bounds checking, and anomaly-based veto over plant telemetry. We implement CPS-Guard as middleware between CPS controllers and an aligned LLM, and evaluate it on 300 synthetically generated adversarial inputs spanning prompt injection, adversarial perturbations, and poisoning-style triggers. Compared to an unprotected LLM advisory baseline, CPS-Guard reduces failure rates by more than 85 % while incurring only 10 18 % additional latency across small to large CPS workloads. These results indicate that system-level hardening can make LLM-assisted CPS substantially more robust without violating real-time constraints.

title: Salary Prediction in Thailand’s IT Sector Using Supervised Learning Models
authors: Arocha Saengwirojanapat, Jirachaya Nongbualang, Natthida Theepakornsukkasem, Nuntanut Bhooanusas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Salary determination remains a growing concern in global job markets, as many job seekers lack accurate selfevaluation skills and often overestimate their qualifications. Consequently, salary recommendation systems have been increasingly introduced across various platforms. However, most existing models rely on candidate-centric data, which rarely reflect employers’ actual requirements. This paper proposes a salary prediction framework for Thailand’s IT industry using employeroriented job postings collected from real-world portals. As ITrelated positions in Thailand continue to expand due to digital transformation, our study also provides data-driven insights into emerging job trends. A key challenge in data collection is the rarity of explicit salary offers. To address this, reference salaries from the ISM-Tech Salary Report 2024 were adopted, and controlled noise was introduced via an exponential distribution to improve model generalization. The proposed method achieved promising performance, with a median percentage error of 16% overall and only 8% for software development roles using the default XGBoost model.

title: Trajectory-centric Probing of Frozen Chest CT Foundation Embeddings for Multi-endpoint Lung Cancer Phenotyping
authors: Kuan-Ting Wu, Hsu-Ching Huang, Wei-Kai Lee, Wei-An Chen, Chien-Ying Huang, Heng-Sheng Chao, Yung-Hung Luo, Jing-Yao Weng, Yi-Chen Yeh, Yuh-Min Chen, Yu-Te Wu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Serial chest CT is routinely acquired during the preoperative workup of lung cancer, yet most imaging AI studies treat each scan as an isolated snapshot. We study whether frozen chest CT foundation embeddings can support clinically meaningful two-timepoint reasoning without end-toend fine-tuning [1]. Using a paired preoperative cohort with two CT exams per patient (prior exam $t_{S}$ and the preoperative exam $t_{0}$), we construct three longitudinal feature types from each encoder [2], [3]: the single-scan embedding $e\left(t_{S}\right)$, the change vector $\Delta e=e\left(t_{0}\right)-e\left(t_{S}\right)$, and the annualized velocity $v=\Delta e / \Delta t_{\text {years }}$. To mitigate acquisition/protocol heterogeneity across timepoints, we apply fold-wise CORAL alignment [4] and L2 normalization prior to probing. We evaluate a nonlinear probe (RBF-SVM) [5], [6] under 5-fold stratified crossvalidation with class-balanced training, and quantify uncertainty by bootstrap confidence intervals computed from out-of-fold predictions [7]. Across six binary endpoints spanning reportderived progression and pathology/molecular phenotypes, our framework demonstrates that longitudinal information provides statistically significant additive signal beyond single-timepoint embeddings, particularly for progression phenotypes, even without task-specific fine-tuning.

title: GhostNet-Based Peach Tree Disease Detection with Class-Aware Augmentation
authors: Kalikiri Niranjan, Seeniappan Kaliappan, R. Priyadharshini, Ritika Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper evaluates the performance of GhostNet, a lightweight CNN, for four-class peach health prediction using a curated dataset of 4,307 images (Healthy: 3,569; Anarsia lineatella: 370; Grapholita molesta: 304; Dead Trees: 64). Images were rescaled to 224x224 px and normalized using ImageNet statistics. A stratified random split of 70/15/15 was used; augmentation (which was class-aware, and limited to minority classes) and a balanced sampler (targeting B = 360 samples per class per epoch) were applied (using RandomResizedCrop, color jitter, RandomPerspective, CLAHE, MixUp, and CutMix). GhostNet (ImageNet-pretrained) was end-to-end fine-tuned using AdamW and cosine-annealed learning rates. On the held-out test set (n = 644), a top-1 accuracy of 92%, and macro precision/recall/F1 of 0.70/0.90/0.76 was obtained. Recall is good for Anarsia lineatella and Grapholita molesta, and there is residual loss in precision only for the rare class of Dead-Trees. Results suggest that GhostNet provides an effective accuracy vs. efficiency trade-off for field-deployable peach disease monitoring.

title: An Inverse-Time Hybrid Characteristic Design for Communicationless Overcurrent Relay Coordination in Microgrids
authors: Mehran Abdi, Reza Mohammadi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: Microgrids present considerable challenges for conventional protection systems due to the significant variations in fault current levels between grid-connected and islanded modes. To overcome this issue, this paper introduces an innovative inverse-time characteristic that leverages both current and voltage signals. The core of this characteristic lies in the integration of an exponential term, which depends on the fault current, and a logarithmic term, which depends on the voltage. This combination significantly enhances the relay's sensitivity to various fault conditions. It allows for faster and more accurate fault detection without relying on costly communication infrastructure or the need for dynamic setting updates. The optimal parameters for this characteristic are determined using an efficient hybrid approach combining Genetic Algorithm and Linear Programming. This characteristic effectively achieves two opposing objectives: minimizing relay operating time while maintaining full coordination between all primary and backup relays, regardless of the microgrids operating mode (grid-connected or islanded). Simulations conducted on a real 18-bus microgrid show that this method significantly reduces the total relay operating time when compared to the standard IEC characteristic, while ensuring complete coordination between primary and backup relays across all operational scenarios.

title: Enhancing Wind Farm Operational Efficiency Through Power Output Classification Using MLP and Ensemble Models
authors: Md Fujael Ahmed, Rayhan Ahmed Opi, Rashedul Albab, Syed Nadim Mehdi, Abdul Hadee Tahsin, Md. Shahid Iqbal
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurately forecasting wind turbine power output is challenging due to the complex and non-linear patterns present in wind farm operational data. Conventional approaches largely adopt regression-based forecasting, which, while effective in predicting continuous values, often lacks the interpretability required for direct operational decision-making. In this work, we reframe the problem as a multi-class classification task, categorizing power output into discrete levels, low, medium, and high, to provide actionable insights for identifying underperforming turbines and optimizing maintenance schedules. A curated Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) dataset was processed through a rigorous pipeline, including the handling of physically impossible negative power values, outlier capping, feature normalization, and label encoding. Five Machine Learning (ML) models, including Logistic Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting (GB), XGBoost, and a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), were trained and optimized using Grid Search hyperparameter tuning. The MLP model obtained the highest classification accuracy of 93.51%, marginally outperforming the top-performing ensemble models. Feature importance analysis revealed that while tree-based models relied heavily on a limited set of features, the MLP distributed its attention more evenly across all inputs. The proposed classification-based framework offers a robust and interpretable alternative to regression-based approaches, providing direct operational benefits for the renewable energy sector.

title: Slack-Jawed and Breathless
authors: Shawn Powers
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: In this invited position paper, the author explores the interrelationship between aesthetic experience, consciousness, and intelligence in the age of artificial intelligence (AI). Grounded in philosophical traditions as well as personal observation, the author argues that intense aesthetic experiences provoke cognitive engagement and embodied awareness, serving as a foundation for human intelligence. It is noted that language doesn't serve how we think about AI systems creating a default to anthropomorphizing algorithms. The author asserts that human intelligence is not congruent with AI as it is rooted instead in lived experience, cultural contexts, and the capacity for wonder.

title: Bridging Educational Gaps: Predicting Retakes Among Bangladeshi Undergraduates with Machine Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Nusrat Zahan Nila, Md. Ali Mahmud Pritom, Fatema Tuz Johora, Aditya Rajbongshi, Md. Salauddin Sarker, Md. Ashrafuzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In higher education, course retakes present a significant challenge, disrupting academic progression, increasing financial burdens and psychological distress among students. This study presents a novel approach for early prediction of students' course retakes in the context of Bangladesh. The dataset was meticulously collected from different university students, consisting of 474 instances. Initially, the dataset was prepared using various preprocessing techniques, including data cleaning, transformation, handling missing values, one-hot encoding, column renaming, as well as feature scaling technique. Experimentation with several Machine Learning (ML) models revealed that the proposed Soft Voting classifier outperformed the baseline classifiers Logistic Regression (LR), Gaussian Naive Bayes (GNB), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Classifier (SVC), Perceptron, and K-nearest Neighbors (KNN) with an accuracy of 94.74%. To enhance the interpretability of ML model's two eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) algorithms were integrated to identify the key factors influencing the model's predictions. This approach provided a reliable and equitable prediction of course retakes or failures utilizing real datasets, contributing to enhanced student academic success and improved institutional efficiency.

title: CAL-Log: Calibration-Aware Logarithmic Cost Modeling for Active Learning in Low-Resource NLP
authors: Vihanga Supasan Kariyakaranage, Banuka Athuraliya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Building robust NLP systems for low-resource languages remains constrained by the high cost of expert annotation rather than model architecture availability. This paper proposes CAL-Log, a cost-calibrated active learning strategy that jointly accounts for (i) human annotation time and (ii) model miscalibration in early rounds, extending our prior framework for costaware active learning [23] to genuinely low-resource multilingual settings. CAL-Log introduces a logarithmic annotation-cost model to avoid the “cost-collapse” behavior of linear utility-pertoken heuristics, coupled with a calibration-aware uncertainty signal. We evaluate on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> low-resource text classification datasets spanning 8 languages (Sinhala, Tamil, Hindi, Telugu, Yoruba, Amharic, Swahili, Xhosa) over 3 independent random seeds. Results show that CAL-Log achieves an Efficiency Score of 0.281 F1 per 1,000 minutes (mean ± std: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.432 \pm 0.217$</tex> F1 at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,535 ~{\min}$</tex>), maintaining robust performance across all datasets. Multi-seed ablation confirms that removing the cost model raises annotation cost by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 9 \%}(+597 ~{\min})$</tex> and lowers efficiency to 0.180, while removing temperature calibration yields a less stable learning trajectory. Our stability analysis demonstrates that CAL-Log prevents the seed-dependent performance collapse observed in linear cost models (cross-seed std up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.296$</tex> on MasakhaNEWS-sw for CAL-Linear) and achieves superior consistency on morphologically complex tasks such as Sinhala Sentiment <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.547 \pm 0.033$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.422 \pm 0.061)$</tex>. To our knowledge, this is the first active learning framework to jointly formalize annotation cost collapse and integrate model calibration error into the acquisition function under realistic human-time constraints for low-resource multilingual NLP.

title: Reliability Enhancement of 40nm RRAM Chip by Bottom Electrode Engineering
authors: Kaimeng Liu, Chengxiang Ma, Wei Chang, Jinyi Liu, Ruofei Hu, Didi Zhu, Yue Xi, Dong Wu, Bin Gao, He Qian, Jianshi Tang, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work proposes an electrode engineering optimization strategy to enhance the comprehensive performance and reliability of resistive random-access memory (RRAM) chip on a commercial 40nm CMOS platform. By adopting a post-deposition sequential treatment (PDST) with plasma-induced surface activation and thermal oxidation on the TiN bottom electrode (BE), the 40nm RRAM array can achieve a high endurance exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> cycles, long retention over 10 years at 85℃, and excellent uniformity in the initial state within the 16kb array. The physical mechanism underlying the impact of TiN BE on the reliability in terms of TiN layer thickness and interface treatment is clarified through comprehensive electrical tests and material characterizations. These findings provide key insights into the reliability improvement of RRAM and could facilitate the commercialization of RRAM technology.

title: Risk-Aware Hybrid CNN-LSTM for Stock Forecasting with Macro-Technical Integration
authors: Ravi Bandara, Maninda Edirisooriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Stock markets exhibit non-linear dynamics influenced by both technical patterns and macroeconomic regimes. While Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks capture temporal dependencies effectively, they often neglect macroeconomic drivers that account for 15 to 20 percent of price variance. This paper proposes a hybrid CNN-LSTM framework integrating technical indicators with Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) macroeconomic variables for short-horizon S&P 500 price forecasting. A Principal Component Analysis (PCA) preprocessing step orthogonalizes 45 engineered features into 14 principal components (95 % variance retention), mitigating multicollinearity. Bayesian hyperparameter optimization tunes a dual-output architecture that simultaneously optimizes price regression (primary task) and direction classification (auxiliary regularization task). Evaluated on 10 years of daily data (20152024) via walk-forward validation, the model achieves Rsquared <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.86$</tex>, Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) = 3.98%, and 18% Mean Absolute Error (MAE) reduction versus LSTM baselines. Direction prediction accuracy of 45.44 % reflects inherent unpredictability of short-term movements. Risk-aware outputs including confidence scores and volatility bands support practical decision-making. Results validate that systematic macroeconomic integration substantially enhances forecasting accuracy beyond technical indicators alone.

title: A Novel African Vulture Optimization Algorithm for Maximum Power Extraction from Different PV Array Arrangements
authors: Rabbiul Hasan Ankon, Munshi Jawad Ibne Azad, Md. Al Imran Fahim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: With the increasing demand for the world's renewable energy, photovoltaic (PV) systems are an emerging and popular choice for producing sustainable electricity. However, they are strongly impacted by environmental factors such as temperature and sunlight. A plurality of local maxima (LM) can exist in the PV characteristic curve during partial shading (PS), and tracking of global maxima (GM) instead of LM is a big challenge. In this regard, the present work utilized a novel African Vulture Optimization Algorithm (AVOA) for GM tracking to overcome these issues. The AVOA-based maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm is evaluated for multiple PV array configurations, and also, a comparison is made with the Horse Herd Optimization Algorithm (HHOA). Simulation results in MATLAB/Simulink are presented to show the effectiveness of the AVOA, and it is found that it is greater than that of the HHOA in tracking time, settling time, and tracking accuracy. Interestingly, AVOA achieves a 99.98% average tracking efficiency, with an average tracking time 70 ms faster and an average convergence time 60 ms faster. Along with that, AVOA consistently outperforms HHOA in error metrics, ensuring more dependable MPPT performance. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of the AVOA-based MPPT approach to increasing the performance of PV systems under different PV array arrangements.

title: BanglaFeel: Enhanced Bangla Back-Transliteration
authors: Masfiq Mahmud, Sumaiya Akter Iqra, Muhammad Anas, Mohammed Ashikur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Banglish, a Romanized form of Bangla, poses challenges for NLP models, especially sentiment analysis. The aim of this study is to develop an effective back-transliteration model that converts Banglish into native Bangla script, thereby enhancing the accuracy of downstream NLP tasks. Our model integrates a multi-encoder architecture, combining characterlevel CNN (CharCNN) and word-level LSTM (WordLSTM) encoders with a pre-trained T5 transformer decoder. This design helps capture both fine-grained spelling variations and broader contextual relationships. In the context of noisy Banglish, this multi-encoder design is necessary rather than arbitrary: the CharCNN encoder focuses on local character n-grams to model inconsistent and non-standard spellings, the WordLSTM encoder captures sequential word dependencies in often ungrammatical sentences, and the T5 encoder provides a strong semantic prior through transfer learning from large-scale pretraining, allowing the system to map noisy surface forms onto stable meaning representations. Trained on the BanglaTLit dataset, the model achieves a BLEU score of 68.03%, ROUGE-1 F1 of 83.34%, and a BERTScore F1 of 96.38%, demonstrating its superior ability to handle the variability inherent in Banglish. This approach offers a robust solution to the transliteration problem, improving performance across various Bangla NLP applications.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning for Litchi Leaf Analysis: Health Monitoring and Growth Stage Detection
authors: Bishwa Das, Md. Asiful Islam, Md Assaduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Proper evaluation of the health and growth phases of the litchi leaves is crucial to proper management of the crop and prevention of diseases, and optimization of yield. The traditional practices are based on manual inspection, which is time-consuming and error-prone. We address these constraints by suggesting a CNN-ViT hybrid model, which integrates MobileNetV2 with an efficient local feature extractor and a Vision Transformer (ViT) as a global context extractor. The model was trained and tested on a selected set of leaf images of litchi with various health conditions and levels of development, achieving a peak accuracy of 99.74 % with high precision, recall, and F1-scores across all five classes. The hybrid architecture was shown to be better in generalization and robustness as compared to ResNet121, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2. Ablation experiments also demonstrated that batch size 16 is optimal and provides the highest generalization. The results demonstrate the potential that the proposed framework has to automated monitoring of the leaves of the litchi tree and its relevance in the real-life context of precision agriculture systems.

title: Beam-Reconfigurable Quadrifilar Helix Antenna Based on a Bi-Stable Origami Metamaterial
authors: Dongxu Chen, Min Li, Chenye Xu, Wanchen Yang, Weixu Yang, Mengzhe Song, Qiubo Ye, Dian Wang, Wenquan Che
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents a novel beam-reconfigurable quadrifilar helix antenna (QHA) integrated with a bi-stable origami metamaterial structure. The design leverages the mechanical bistability of the origami structure to switch between two distinct radiation states without requiring continuous external power. The antenna radiates with righthand circular polarization (RHCP) in both states. In the unfolded state (State I), the antenna achieves a wide half-power beamwidth (HPBW) of 182° at 1.57 GHz, suitable for broad coverage. In the folded state (State II), it exhibits a narrow HPBW of 109° at 1.22 GHz, ideal for directional tracking. This provides a beamwidth reconfiguration range of 73°. Both simulation and measurement results validate that the design is potential for 6G adaptive communication scenarios.

title: Joint Uplink and Downlink Optimization for Multi-UAV Assisted Emergency Communication Networks: Access Control and Trajectory Planning
authors: Zhe Wang, Haijun Wang, Jiao Zhang, Xinfeng Deng, Haitao Zhao, Li Zhou, Jibo Wei, Kuo Cao, Haijun Zhang
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) acting as aerial base stations are regarded as an effective solution for emergency communication, due to their high mobility and low cost. In this paper, we consider the differentiated communication requirements in disaster relief scenarios, where the uplink demands high throughput and the downlink requires low latency and high reliability. To simultaneously capture the timeliness and reliability requirements of downlink transmissions, we propose a novel QoS evaluation metric based on finite blocklength theory. Building on this, we formulate a system utility maximization problem and solve it by jointly optimizing UAV trajectory and ground node access control. To address this problem, we propose a novel algorithm named PW-QMIX, which integrates a priority-based heuristic access control policy with a QMIX-based trajectory optimization scheme, effectively tackling the challenges posed by the high-dimensional joint action space. Furthermore, to tackle the challenge of dynamic observation caused by UAV movement and sensing limitations, we design a weight generation network (WGN) and incorporate it into the input layer of QMIX. The WGN dynamically generates weight matrices based on observed nodes, enhancing UAV’s adaptability to local observation variations. Simulation results validate the significance of jointly optimizing uplink and downlink communications. Moreover, compared with state-of-the-art algorithms, the proposed PW-QMIX demonstrates significant advantages in convergence and scalability.

title: Reliability Assessment of Generalized Hypercube Networks under a Probabilistic Fault Model
authors: Mengjie Lv, Sixiao Di, Fu Xiao, Weibei Fan, Lei Han, Sun-Yuan Hsieh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the widespread adoption of parallel computing and large-scale distributed systems, reliability has become a fundamental requirement for efficient and stable operation. In large-scale multiprocessor systems, a central challenge is to effectively quantify reliability in the presence of failures, which can be characterized by the ability of subsystems to remain fault free when failures occur in the network. This paper focuses on the subsystem reliability and mean time to failure (MTTF) of generalized hypercube networks (GHNs) under the probabilistic fault model. Firstly, we establish upper and lower bounds along with accurate approximations for subsystem reliability, and develop an efficient simulation procedure algorithm to evaluate subsystem reliability at scale. Secondly, we derive the MTTF and compare fixed partition and liberal partition strategies to characterize how partition flexibility influences system lifetime. Experimental results show that analytical approximation closely matches the simulated reliability, indicating that the precise subsystem reliability can be effectively represented by the approximation while enabling substantially more efficient evaluation, even for sufficiently large network sizes. Moreover, compared with fixed partitioning, liberal partitioning leads to a longer MTTF, which offers a more effective characterization of the reliability and lifetime properties of networks.

title: Sensor fault detection and isolation for asynchronous switched systems using a bank of sliding mode observers
authors: Shafqat Ali, Hao Luo, Zain ul Aabidin Lodhi, Faizan Shahid, Muhammad Taskeen Raza
abstract: This paper studies sensor fault detection and isolation in continuous-time switched systems subject to unknown but bounded output or measurement disturbances. The proposed approach utilizes the asynchronous switching phenomenon, in which there is a lag between subsystem and observer switching. The aim is to design a bank of sliding mode observers for fault isolation, with robustness to residual provided by H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</sub> performance. For this purpose, we first transform the sensor faults into pseudo-actuator faults using a coordinate transformation with a low-pass filter, making the design of the observer framework more convenient. Then, a series of sliding mode observers is designed for effective fault isolation such that each residual signal is affected by its corresponding sensor fault. Next, to provide an optimal solution in the sense that the effect of measurement disturbances on the residual signal is minimized, the H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</sub> optimization index is utilized for the problem of asynchronous switched systems. To ensure stability, a piecewise Lyapunov function along with an average dwell time switching constraint is utilized for both matched and mismatched periods of asynchronous switching. The conditions for a feasible solution lead to sufficient conditions that are formulated in terms of linear matrix inequalities. Moreover, the evaluation of the residual signal and threshold is computed to enhance fault detection and isolation capability. Simulations of a three-tank system for multiple sensor faults are utilized to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Design of a High-Uniformity Illumination Optical System for Metasurfaces
authors: Jiguo Li, Xin Zhang, Qingbin Jiao, Huansong Huang, Minghui Gu, Chao Liu, Sijia Jiang, Ding Ma, Mingyu Yang, Liang Xu, Xin Tan
abstract: To enable high-sensitivity and high-stability detection with Fano resonant metasurfaces, the illumination light field must satisfy stringent specifications in three critical aspects: angular sensitivity, wavefront fidelity, and spatial spot matching with the metasurface sensing region. Conventional metasurface illumination systems suffer from several interrelated limitations—including geometric mismatch between the illumination spot and the active sensing area, poor irradiance uniformity, insufficient control over incident angle distribution, wavefront aberrations, and low optical integration density. To address these challenges, this work introduces a purpose-built, high-uniformity illumination optical system tailored specifically for efficient excitation of Fano resonant metasurfaces. Grounded in the principle of optical étendue conservation, the system performs secondary light redistribution of a halogen source using an ellipsoidal reflector. A square-shaped light integrator rod ensures highly uniform spatial intensity distribution, while a custom-designed relay lens group simultaneously accomplishes beam compression, wavefront correction, and strict confinement of the marginal ray exit angles. Experimental validation confirms that the system achieves >95.65% illumination uniformity across the target area, maintains marginal ray angles within ±11°, yields a root-mean-square (RMS) wavefront error of <λ/6, and produces a precisely sized 1.2 mm² square illumination spot aligned to the chip footprint. All output parameters fully comply with the physical excitation requirements of Fano resonant metasurfaces. By combining miniaturization, cost-effectiveness, and high optical performance, this illumination strategy provides a practical and scalable foundation for next-generation metasurface-based biochemical detection platforms.

title: The Sound of Water: Inferring Physical Properties from Pouring Liquids
authors: Piyush Bagad, Makarand Tapaswi, Cees G. M. Snoek, Andrew Zisserman
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: We study the connection between audio observations and the underlying physics of a mundane yet intriguing everyday activity: pouring liquids. Given only the sound of liquid pouring into a container, our objective is to automatically infer physical properties such as the liquid level, the shape and size of the container, the pouring rate and the time to fill. To this end, we: (i) show in theory that these properties can be determined from the fundamental frequency (pitch); (ii) train a pitch detection model with supervision from simulated data and visual data with a physics-inspired objective; (iii) introduce a new large dataset of real pouring videos for a systematic study; (iv) show that the trained model can indeed infer these physical properties for real data; and finally, (v) we demonstrate strong generalization to various container shapes, other datasets, and in-the-wild YouTube videos. Our work presents a keen understanding of a narrow yet rich problem at the intersection of acoustics, physics, and learning. It opens up applications to enhance multisensory perception in robotic pouring.

title: Fast Robustness Analysis of DC Power Distribution Systems Against Transient Disturbances
authors: Michael Gerten, Stephan Frei
abstract: Highly reliable DC power distribution systems are required in a variety of areas, such as industrial grids and vehicle on-board power supply systems. A safe and robust operation shall be ensured even in the case of a fault within the system, for example, a short circuit. Such faults require the fast disconnection of the faulty branches. However, transient voltage or current peaks caused by the switching event can trigger additional failures and adversely affect the system’s overall stability. Additionally, periodically switching components may create ripple currents, resulting in degradation of voltage stabilizing capacitors. To prevent such critical disturbances, stability issues must be identified during the development process. Electric network simulations are necessary. As accurate transient simulations of the many possible fault scenarios in large power systems can be very time-consuming, more efficient computation methods are needed. In this work, a frequency-domain method is proposed to analyze transient disturbances in DC supply systems and identify potentially critical scenarios. As an extension of known modal analysis approaches used for AC grids, transient voltages and currents are described by transfer functions of the respective coupling paths. For these transfer functions, low-degree approximations are then determined that enable an analytic calculation of the resulting transients. The method is demonstrated based on an exemplary distribution system and validated by accurate time-domain simulations and laboratory measurements. The proposed method is shown to be successful in identifying critical scenarios. Compared to extensive time-domain simulations, the total simulation time could be reduced by more than 96%.

title: Learning Context-Adaptive Motion Priors for Masked Motion Diffusion Models with Efficient Kinematic Attention Aggregation_supp1-3684730.mp4
authors: Jie Chen
abstract: Vision-based motion capture solutions often struggle with occlusions, which result in the loss of critical joint information and hinder accurate 3D motion reconstruction. Other wearable alternatives also suffer from noisy or unstable data, often requiring extensive manual cleaning and correction to achieve reliable results. To address these challenges, we introduce the Masked Motion Diffusion Model (MMDM), a diffusion-based generative reconstruction framework that enhances incomplete or low-confidence motion data using partially available high-quality reconstructions within a Masked Autoencoder architecture. Central to our design is the Kinematic Attention Aggregation (KAA) mechanism, which enables efficient, deep, and iterative encoding of both joint-level and pose-level features, capturing structural and temporal motion patterns essential for task-specific reconstruction. We focus on learning context-adaptive motion priors-specialized structural and temporal features extracted by the same reusable architecture, where each learned prior emphasizes different aspects of motion dynamics and is specifically efficient for its corresponding task. This enables the architecture to adaptively specialize without altering its structure. Such versatility allows MMDM to efficiently learn motion priors tailored to scenarios such as motion refinement, completion, and in-betweening. Extensive evaluations on public benchmarks demonstrate that MMDM achieves strong performance across diverse masking strategies and task settings. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/xxx/xxx</uri>.

title: Long-Term Life Prediction of Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cells Based on Dynamic Mode Decomposition Optimization Model
authors: Tong Niu, Caizhi Zhang, Kejun Deng, Ziliang Zhao, Krisztián Kun, Tao Zeng, Gang Lei, Dmitry Spasov, Sergey A. Grigoriev
abstract: The life of proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFCs) is the key factor limiting its commercialization. Accurate prediction of remaining useful life is very important for prolonging cell life and ensuring operational reliability. This article proposes an LSM-TD-DMD optimization model, which combines time delay (TD), the least squares method (LSM), and dynamic mode decomposition (DMD) to improve the accuracy of PEMFC long-term life prediction. The experimental results show that the LSM-TD-DMD model significantly reduces the prediction error. For FC1 and FC2 stacks, the RUL prediction errors of the fixed training set market are 10 and 24 h, respectively, and the errors of different training set lengths are 4 and 2 h, respectively. In the long-term prediction range, the prediction errors of Module 1 and Module 2 were 12.556% and 11.307%, respectively. LSM-TD-DMD model provides a robust and effective framework for PEMFC residual life medicine and provides valuable insight for maintenance strategies and cell life extension.

title: A Bipolar Short-Pulsed Power Supply With Charge Feedback for a DBD Excimer Lamp
authors: Xiongmin Tang, Juxiang Zhong, Chenghui Li, Jie Li, Xuecong Li, Miao Zhang
abstract: Generating an optimal excitation voltage for a dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) load with a simple structure remains a significant challenge. To address this issue, this article presents improvements to a high-voltage high-frequency converter and proposes a novel bipolar short-pulsed power supply integrated with charge feedback. Specifically, by replacing the two resistance–capacitance (RC) snubber circuits in the original converter with two charge feedback circuits, the proposed power supply achieves three key enhancements: a sharp increase in the efficiency of the original converter, further suppression of excitation voltage oscillations, and a notable improvement in the rising rate of the excitation voltage. Additionally, the proposed power supply inherits the inherent advantages of the original converter, including the stable generation of bipolar excitation voltage, smooth adjustability of the excitation voltage idle time, and strong adaptability to different DBD excimer lamps. Experimental validation was conducted on a DBD excimer lamp. Under operating conditions of 100kHz, 17kV, and 90W, the power supply achieved an efficiency of 93%. The average voltage rise rate reached $10.638\times 10^{9}$ V/s, representing a 30% improvement over the original converter.

title: Flexible Optoelectronic InSnZnO Synaptic Devices for Neuromorphic Computing
authors: Xiaocheng Wang, Xiangcheng Liu, Lin Xu, Haiyu Xie, Rongsheng Chen
abstract: As an important part of neuromorphic computing, photoelectric synapses are expected to break through the limitations of traditional architectures. In this letter, flexible and memory-modulated indium tin zinc oxide (InSnZnO) synaptic transistors were fabricated under a low-temperature annealing condition of 300 °C. Even under severe mechanical deformation conditions, the devices exhibit remarkable stability and synaptic plasticity. Based on these synaptic properties, the device successfully achieved image edge detection with Gaussian high-pass filter, as well as the ultraviolet imaging process of instant film. More importantly, the ITZO synaptic device after bending 10,000 times also showed a high accuracy of more than 91% on the handwritten digit recognition dataset, which marks significant progress in flexible wearable and neuromorphic computing.

title: Spoof Surface Plasmon Polariton-Based Shadow Region Illumination for Wireless Networks
authors: Praneet Jain, Saptarshi Ghosh, Simon Cotton, Okan Yurduseven
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This work presents a Spoof Surface Plasmon Polariton (SSPP)-based waveguide that functions as a low-pass filter below 6.3 GHz. A co-planar waveguide (CPW) with flared ground planes is employed to excite the unit cells. Rectangular inserts are incorporated to ensure momentum matching between the CPW and the SSPP unit cells. After validating the performance of the planar configuration, the waveguide is bent by 90° to create a conformal architecture, exhibiting similar transmission characteristics to the original planar design. The conformal SSPP design is then employed in an example sub-6 GHz wireless blind spot illumination scenario, showing its potential as an enabling technology for improving coverage in wireless networks. The proposed structure provides a simple and cost-effective approach for feeding antenna array systems aimed at illuminating shadow regions caused by dense urban buildings.

title:
Electrically Tunable Strain Orientation in MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    for Reconfigurable Logic Circuits
authors: Xinchuan Du, Yang Wang, Yi Wan
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, we present an electrically reconfigurable logic design based on monolayer MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, in which an oriented in-plane electric field directly modulates the strain distribution and directional transport. By integrating orthogonal lateral gates and a suspended channel architecture, we enable deterministic control of charge transport through anisotropic electromechanical coupling. When the in-plane electric field is aligned with the zigzag axis of monolayer MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, the induced strain is maximized, leading to a tunable transition from linear to rectifying conduction. Building on this effect, a compact dual-device logic unit is realized, enabling flexible switching between AND and OR functionalities via electrically tunable bias, which achieves sub-100 μs switching time and operates above 100 kHz. This approach highlights field-direction programming as a scalable route toward high-density and adaptive 2D logic circuits.

title: Arab Public Opinion Perceptions on Artificial Intelligence Ethics: Digital Sovereignty, National Security, and Individual Rights
authors: Rashed Alrasheed, Ayad Mohamed Samir, Nour Naim, Dhari Alghanam, Dalal Alshammari, Dana Showaiter
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study examines Arab public opinion on the ethical implications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in national security, focusing on digital sovereignty, civil liberties, and governance. As AI technologies rapidly integrate into security sectors, concerns arise over transparency, foreign dependence, and cultural relevance. The research aims to assess perceptions of AI’s impact on individual rights, trust in government cybersecurity, and the adequacy of current legal frameworks. Using a descriptive quantitative method, data from 572 respondents across Arab countries were analyzed. Findings reveal strong support for culturally grounded AI ethics and skepticism about foreign AI reliance. The study advocates for inclusive, culturally sensitive AI policies that prioritize transparency, and regional representation.

title: Facilitating Continuous Professional Development: A Machine Learning Approach to Personalized Learning Paths
authors: Cristian-Valentin Valea, Ovidiu Aritoni
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: In the era of rapid digital transformation, continuous professional development and skill enhancement have become imperative. This paper presents a novel software prototype designed to support certification exam preparation through personalized learning pathways powered by advanced Machine Learning techniques. The system comprises a comprehensive multi-platform ecosystem (web, mobile, and desktop applications) enabling users to complete competency assessments via adaptive multiple-choice questionnaires. The primary contribution of this work lies in the development of an automated question classification system coupled with an intelligent adaptive test generation module, implemented within a scalable and resilient software architecture. The AI-powered classifier, developed in Python, employs a multi-provider aggregation strategy that synthesizes outputs from multiple Large Language Model services-OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Cohere-augmented by dedicated APIs and conventional Natural Language Processing techniques (TF-IDF, spaCy). This hybrid approach ensures robust item analysis and precise categorization aligned with educational learning objectives. The system incorporates sophisticated fallback mechanisms, Redis-based caching, and comprehensive cost monitoring capabilities. The software architecture features a Java-based backend implementing RESTful services interfaced with a MySQL relational database. To optimize the learning experience, an adaptive test generation algorithm dynamically constructs assessments comprising 70-80 questions while respecting multiple constraints including difficulty progression, topic distribution, and examination duration. The algorithm continuously monitors individual learner performance, iteratively adjusting content delivery by detecting learning plateaus, identifying knowledge gaps, and dynamically calibrating question difficulty to maximize pedagogical effectiveness and accelerate competency acquisition.

title: Performance Modeling of Data Rebalancing in Sharded MongoDB Clusters
authors: Georgios Paterakis, Efstratios Ntallaris, Kostas Magoutis, Ioannis Moscholios, Georgios Bouloukakis
abstract: Scalable storage systems typically provide mechanisms for re-distributing data over time to ensure a balanced storage allocation. Such mechanisms have traditionally been considered as background activities meant to run at low priority, to avoid penalizing applications accessing storage. In this work, we focus on an alternative design point where data re-distribution is run as a high-priority activity, meant to re-balance data at the full speed allowed by the newly added resources. Such rapid redistribution has become viable in recent years due to the rise of low-overhead technologies in the networking and disk storage space. A challenge in such a scenario is to schedule data transfer flows to newly added nodes in a way that fully and efficiently utilizes network, CPU, and disk resources in the new nodes, while avoiding overload. To understand the impact of different parameters of the rebalance process, such as the number of simultaneous senders, we develop a queueing network model of the process used in MongoDB, and describe a preliminary evaluation of performance prediction via simulations.

title: Typing Under Pressure: Estimating Cognitive Load from Keystroke Dynamics and Textual Patterns
authors: Mahmudur Rahman, Pranto Goswamee, Maria Sultana, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Keystroke dynamics can indicate the cognitive load, enabling real-time support without relying on intrusive sensors. Most existing methods in this area depend heavily on labeled data and tend to miss session-level patterns, such as pauses, error corrections, and overall typing speed. To address this limitation, this study focuses on engineering session-level features, such as pause-ratios, rhythm irregularity, backspace and efficiency. Data were recorded from 100 participants of 20 nationalities, who performed two distinct tasks: password-retype and transcription tasks. Using unsupervised clustering, hidden cognitive-load states were discovered, and the generated labels were later applied to train a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier. The proposed pipeline achieved an ROC-AUC of 99% and F1 of 0.900 for distinguishing the cognitive-load states. Combining unsupervised label discovery with a SVM classifier on interpretable keystroke features yields an accurate, scalable and sensor-free framework for cognitive-load detection.

title: A Comparison Between 82.16 Mhz Pseudo and Pass Transistor Logic Based 8: 1 Multiplexer in 45 Nm Process Node
authors: Mustaq Mahmud Tafhim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: As technology advances into the deep submicron regime, efficient multiplexer (MUX) design becomes increasingly important within modern VLSI systems. This paper compares two logic design approaches for implementing an 8:1 MUX in a 45 nm CMOS process: Pass Transistor Logic (PTL) and Pseudo CMOS Logic. In order to achieve important performance metrics like propagation delay, rise/fall time, power consumption, slew rate, transistor count, layout area, and robustness across process-voltage-temperature (PVT) variations, the designs are created, simulated, and verified using Cadence Virtuoso. The 73 transistors pseudo-CMOS logicbased MUX performs well in terms of speed and signal integrity. Strong voltage swing across outputs, an exceptional slew rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$556.7 \text{GV} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, and a propagation delay of 15.39ps are all attained. While the post-layout area is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$194.5 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>, the power consumption is still moderate at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$242 \mu W$</tex>. Strong statistical reliability and appropriateness for high-frequency, timing-critical applications like digital signal processors and embedded systems are further demonstrated by this design's stability under PVT variation and Monte Carlo simulations. On the other hand, the PTL-based MUX provides outstanding power efficiency and area optimization with just 20 transistors. It achieves an extremely low dynamic power consumption of only 389.1nW, a faster delay of 13.48ps, and an exceptionally small layout area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30.38 \mu m^{2}$</tex>. However, it is more appropriate in ultra-low-power settings where area and energy efficiency are more important than speed because of the decreased voltage swing and weaker noise margins. The layout-versus-schematic (LVS), electrical rule check (ERC), and design rule check (DRC) requirements are all met by both designs. The implementations, which run at 82.16 MHz, show that they are feasible for use in sophisticated SoC design. The study offers insightful information for upcoming low-power and high-performance digital systems by highlighting significant trade-offs between speed, power, area, and reliability when choosing MUX architectures.

title: Deep Learning Architectures for Real-Time Monkeypox Detection: A Comparative Study of CNNs, Transformers, and Siamese Networks
authors: Sakib Azmain Sahil, Badhan Rani Das, Hrithik Majumdar Shibu, Aisha Hayder Chowdhury
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Monkeypox (MPOX) is a zoonotic viral disease transmitted from animal hosts to humans through direct contact with the bodily fluids of infected individuals. Since its resurgence in 2022, the global outbreak has intensified, necessitating an early and accurate diagnosis along with taking tangible measures for timely intervention and containment. Clinical diagnosis is challenging due to the overlap of symptoms with other viral infections. Although the Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) test is currently construed as the gold standard for the detection of MPOX, it comes with a lot of downsides; it is expensive, time-consuming, and depends on specialized equipment and trained personnel. This paper presents a comparative study combining deep learning and vision transformers for MPOX diagnosis based on skin lesion images, facilitating recent advances in computer vision. We investigated and compared the performance of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Vision Transformers (ViTs), and a novel and latest Siamese Neural Network-based model on both binary classification (monkeypox vs. non-monkeypox) and multi-class classification (monkeypox, chickenpox, measles, cowpox, hand-foot-and-mouth disease, and healthy). All models were fine-tuned and evaluated using standard performance metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that CNNs provide fast and reliable baseline performance, with the ConvNeXt model scoring 99% accuracy on both types of datasets. ViTs consistently achieve the highest classification accuracy (95% and 98%), and Siamese Networks offer robust similarity-based detection capabilities with the accuracy of 99% and 98% for binary and multi-class datasets, respectively. The proposed framework demonstrates promising potential for clinical deployment, facilitating rapid, accurate, and accessible MPOX diagnosis, particularly in resource-limited settings.

title: Correlation-Aware Feature Attribution Based Explainable AI
authors: Poushali Sengupta, Yan Zhang, Frank Eliassen, Sabita Maharjan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Explainable AI (XAI) is increasingly essential as modern models become more complex and high-stakes applications demand transparency, trust, and regulatory compliance. Existing global attribution methods often incur high computational costs, lack stability under correlated inputs, and fail to scale efficiently to large or heterogeneous datasets. We address these gaps with ExCIR (Explainability through Correlation Impact Ratio), a correlationaware attribution score equipped with a lightweight transfer protocol that reproduces full-model rankings using only a fraction of the data. ExCIR quantifies sign-aligned co-movement between features and model outputs after robust centering (subtracting a robust location estimate, e.g., median or mid-mean, from features and outputs). We further introduce BlockCIR, a groupwise extension of ExCIR that scores sets of correlated features as a single unit. By aggregating the same signed-co-movement numerators and magnitudes over predefined or data-driven groups, BlOCKCIR mitigates double-counting in collinear clusters (e.g., synonyms or duplicated sensors) and yields smoother, more stable rankings when strong dependencies are present. Across diverse text, tabular, signal, and image datasets, ExCIR shows trustworthy agreement with established global baselines and the full model, delivers consistent top- k rankings across settings, and reduces runtime via lightweight evaluation on a subset of rows. Overall, ExCIR provides computationally efficient, consistent, and scalable explainability for real-world deployment.

title: Microclimate Control Strategies and Their Implications for Interior Environments: A Systematic Literature Review (2010–2025)
authors: Nader Badawy Ahmed Sonpol, Ayman M. Zakaria Eraqi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: According to the PRISMA 2020 protocol, the evolution, technologies, and implications of microclimate control for interior environments between 2010 and 2025 are systematically reviewed through 295 peer-reviewed studies conducted in 46 countries. The literature identifies intersections among thermal comfort, indoor air quality (IAQ), humidity, and particulate matter management with human health, energy efficiency, and design performance. Even though people are becoming more aware of the importance of health, there is still a significant gap between saving energy and ensuring the well-being of those inside a building. This suggests that there is a growing need for systems that incorporate comfort and safety measures while also being eco-friendly. Recent technological progress includes the development of hybrid heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems, architectural design that uses natural methods and minimizes energy consumption, and building management that utilizes data analysis. Moreover, the information available has become increasingly intricate, with improvements to simulation and monitoring procedures, yet continues to suffer from uncertainty, unequal access, and a lack of consideration for user experience. Research on controlling warmth and humidity in buildings must move towards adaptive systems that are fair and make use of artificial intelligence, so that the environmental, physical, and social aspects of designs for the indoors can be balanced. This information offers essential direction for architects, engineers, and those in government working to create healthier buildings and communities that can withstand the changing climate.

title: FUSE: Unifying Spectral and Semantic Cues for Robust AI-Generated Image Detection
authors: Md. Zahid Hossain, Most. Sharmin Sultana Samu, Md. Kamrozzaman Bhuiyan, Farhad Uz Zaman, Md. Rakibul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The fast evolution of generative models has heightened the demand for reliable detection of AI-generated images. To tackle this challenge, we introduce FUSE, a hybrid system that combines spectral features extracted through Fast Fourier Transform with semantic features obtained from the CLIP's Vision encoder. The features are fused into a joint representation and trained progressively in two stages. Evaluations on GenImage, WildFake, DiTFake, GPT-ImgEval and Chameleon datasets demonstrate strong generalization across multiple generators. Our FUSE (Stage 1) model demonstrates state-of-the-art results on the Chameleon benchmark. It also attains 91.36 % mean accuracy on the GenImage dataset, 88.71 % accuracy across all tested generators, and a mean Average Precision of 94.96 %. Stage 2 training further improves performance for most generators. Unlike existing methods, which often perform poorly on high-fidelity images in Chameleon, our approach maintains robustness across diverse generators. These findings highlight the benefits of integrating spectral and semantic features for generalized detection of images generated by AI.

title: Evaluating the Role of AI, Technology Used, and Technology Skill Proficiency in Onboarding: A Quantitative Study of Developer Productivity and Scalability in Sri Lanka
authors: Shenara Lelwala, Malsika Munigoda, Nimthara Ashinshana, Thisuni Tantirige, Krishantha Wisenthige, Colinie Wickramaarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Given the rapid evolution of business application development in modern-day organizations and industries, an enhanced, scalable solution is the primary characteristic. In this scenario, comprehending the ways in which effective onboarding activities drive the successful implementation of scalable business applications in the long-term is included. This study answers the research questions of how factors such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Technology Used (TU), and Technology Skill Proficiency (TSP) influence the Developer Productivity (DP), while improving the ability to execute scalable business applications (SBAs). Through a quantitative approach, this research aims at medium and large-scale IT organizations of Sri Lanka, employing a non-random sampling technique. 104 responses were gathered through a five-point Likert scale questionnaire, which was analyzed with Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM) using SmartPLS software to investigate the relationships between our key factors of AI, TU, and TSP with their direct connection to DP, and how it ultimately improves SBAs. Conducting this research disclosed the significance of a formal onboarding process on the productivity of software developers in enhancing the SBAs. To conclude, the findings demonstrate that the objectives of the study were fulfilled, the research questions were addressed, and the hypotheses were likewise validated. Our study has shown the importance of proper onboarding practices, which have a significant impact on improving DP and the development of SBAs.

title: Quantifying the Impacts of Social Media Engagement in E-Commerce Product Performance Using Decision Modelling
authors: R. Joshiga, Sharanya Jegadeesan, Kawmadi Jayasinghe, D.P. Jayathunga, Murugaiya Ramashini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The growth of e-commerce has made social media engagement a critical factor in shaping consumer trust and product visibility. Metrics such as likes, shares, comments, and views provide signals of consumer interest, but their measurable effect on product performance is not well established. This study examines how social media engagement influences product outcomes on Amazon’s clothing, shoes and jewellery category by integrating product ratings and review data with Facebook engagement metrics. Using Lasso regression, the analysis identifies the most influential engagement variables and evaluates their predictive power through error-based performance metrics. Results show that total reactions and shares are the strongest predictors of ratings and review counts, while comments and views contribute minimally. Among the strong predictors total reaction gave negative impact on rating while shares matric has given positive impact. The regression models achieved low prediction errors (MSE = 0.1936, RMSE = 0.44 for ratings; MSE = 0.1722, RMSE = 0.41 for review counts), demonstrating strong predictive capacity. In contrast, the binary classification model achieved balanced accuracy (82.9%) but weak discriminative power (ROC AUC = 0.51). The study contributes by integrating cross-platform datasets, applying an interpretable modelling approach, and demonstrating that social engagement can be transformed into reliable decision signals. These insights enrich both the theoretical understanding of social influence and the practical approaches used in data-driven marketing.

title: PruneFedAD: Federated Learning with Structured Pruning for Low-Resource Alzheimer’s Disease Detection
authors: Mizbaul Haque Maruf, Farhana Tazrin, Pronob Saha, Aritra Das, M. F. Mridha, Jungpil Shin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a brain disorder that slowly damages memory and thinking abilities, making early identification essential for better treatment results and slowing down the disease. This work includes centralized training with k-fold cross-validation, structured pruning of MobileNetV3-Large architecture for computational efficiency, and federated learning for privacy-preserving Alzheimer’s disease classification from MRI scans. MobileNetV3-Large model was used for centralized training with 5-fold cross-validation, achieving 99.99% accuracy with minimal variance across folds. Structured pruning of the baseline model reduced model parameters by 44.2% and computational operations by 42.1% while maintaining 99.95% accuracy. The pruned model required only 5 to 7 epochs for convergence during fine-tuning, indicating preservation of learned features. Federated learning implementation using Federated Averaging algorithm across four clients achieved 76.62% accuracy on cross-dataset evaluation, representing a 60.44% improvement over centralized training tested on external data. The integration of structured pruning with federated learning demonstrates that efficient, privacy-preserving artificial intelligence systems for medical diagnosis are achievable without compromising diagnostic accuracy.

title: Blockchain for Healthcare: Efficient Appointment Scheduling and EMR Management
authors: Urmi Biswas, Tutul Dhar, Humaira Nur Tisha, Pranab Kumar Dhar, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The expansion of inefficient and insecure Electronic Medical Record (EMR) management systems addresses several risks to the integrity, accessibility, and security of healthcare data. Traditional systems are prone to unauthorized access, often lack transparency, and suffer from inefficiencies in the case of data sharing and storage. In addition, patients are still needed to wait in long queues to secure appointments with doctors and also face discrimination due to not having contacts with doctors, resulting in unnecessary delays and unfair access to healthcare. This study proposes a novel blockchain-based healthcare framework that integrates EMR management with online appointment scheduling. This hybrid architecture uses Ethereum smart contracts, the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) for decentralized storage, and Bloom filters to enhance efficiency during EMR sharing. Django is used to implement back-end frameworks and cryptographic algorithms such as ECDH and HKDF to ensure secure data encryption and sharing. The findings of the proposed system illustrate that the solution ensures security, transparency, and scalability. By combining decentralized storage, efficient appointment scheduling, and smart contract automation, the system fosters trust and transparency in healthcare while ensuring both data integrity and patient accessibility.

title: Optimizing Deep Learning-Based Bushfire Detection from Sentinel-2 Satellite Data via Channel Selection
authors: Md. Farhan Shakib, Md. Shahid Uz Zaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Bushfires are destructive natural hazards that cause extensive environmental, economic, and health impacts. Timely detection of fire-affected areas using satellite imagery is crucial for mitigating their consequences. Existing methods for bushfire detection include pixel- and object-based approaches, machine learning, and deep learning techniques. Pixel- and object-based methods often rely on fixed thresholds or segmentation quality, limiting their generalization. While many machine learning and deep learning approaches depend on single-source datasets or preselected spectral channels, which can overlook informative features and require high computational resources.
