To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN) for multiclass bushfire detection by integrating the EuroSAT and Sen2Fire datasets, providing a diverse representation of fire and non-fire land covers. We employ a gradient-based channel ranking method to systematically identify the most informative spectral channels, and train the CNN using incremental channel combinations to determine the optimal subset. Our approach achieves a peak classification accuracy of 95.0% using only the top five ranked channels, demonstrating that a small, carefully selected set of spectral channels is sufficient for accurate detection. These results highlight the effectiveness of gradient-based channel selection and dataset integration in improving both accuracy and deployability of bushfire detection models, offering a practical solution for real-world monitoring and early warning applications.

title: Automated Tomato Leaf Disease Detection Using EfficientNetB3: A Deep Learning Approach for Agricultural Diagnostics
authors: Gyana Ranjana Panigrahi, G.Susan Shiny, Gaurav Vishnu Londhe, Shivani Sharma, Alok Misra, Divya Bhardwaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study presents a deep learning-based approach using the EfficientNetB3 model to classify tomato leaf conditions into four categories: Tomato___Bacterial_spot, Tomato___Early_blight, Tomato___Late_blight, and Tomato___healthy. The dataset used in this work consisted of 9,248 high-resolution images obtained from Kaggle, and the image preprocessing techniques that included resizing, normalizing, and data augmentation were applied to improve the model’s ability to generalize. The fine-tuned transfer learning model, EfficientNetB3, had an overall validation accuracy of 94%. The performance was fairly balanced across all classes, as shown in the classification report and the confusion matrix using precision, recall, and F1-score. In order to prevent overfitting, important techniques like dropout, batch normalization, and early stopping were utilized to train the model effectively. This work shows that the model can be useful in a real-world application in agriculture for disease diagnosis for farmers through real-time and automated analysis. Further work may include the collection of larger datasets for improved generalization of the results, and also the implementation of the proposed solutions in real-life settings through mobile or IoT apps for better access and convenience. This work provides a good starting point for understanding how deep learning can be used to improve agricultural yield and efficiency.

title: Real-Time IoT System for Air Quality Monitoring and Booster Fan Predictive Maintenance
authors: Kaliffathullah. S, Harish Gnanasambanthan G
abstract: This paper addresses air-quality issues in underground tunnels and industrial area. It introduces an Internet of Things (IoT) Air-Quality Booster Fan (AQBF) system that includes real-time sensing, control, and maintenance. The system tracks particulate matter (PM2.5, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{g} \mathrm{m}^{-3}$</tex>), carbon dioxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{C O}_{2}$</tex>, ppm), temperature (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>), and vibration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{m} ~ {s}^{- 2}$</tex>). It uses an ESP32-based controller that can operate in manual, semi-automatic, and fully automatic modes. In automatic mode, an adaptive PID plus AI algorithm adjusts the fan speed (revolutions per minute, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{r} \text{m i n}^{-1}$</tex>) based on the level of pollutants and occupancy. A web-based dashboard allows for live visualization, remote monitoring, and maintenance alerts. Experimental validation showed that pollutant recovery happened within 1.2 seconds. It achieved steady-state accuracy within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2 \%$</tex>, and overshoot remained below 5%. Electrical energy use dropped by about 25%, and the power factor improved from 0.81 to 0.94. Analytical evaluation confirmed that the measured responses matched the mass-balance and power models. This verifies reliable air-quality compliance with increased efficiency, dependability, and sustainability.

title: Bridging AI and Sustainability: The Role of Explainable AI in Climate Policy and Ethical Decision-Making
authors: Alfredo L. Calimbo, Angelo C. Arguson, Ronel F. Ramos, Joseph G. Gonzales
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as an integral part of modern-day environmental governance. It has been used as a supporter or facilitator in various domains like climate modeling, energy demand simulation, and monitoring of biodiversity. However, despite this progress, there have been various challenges arising from the lack of understanding of complex models of machine learning. The said challenge has become critical in the domain of sustainable scenarios because of their longer-term implications on nature and society. This has led to the advent of an important concept called Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), which has made it possible to understand these models and even point out factors influencing them and defining certain levels of uncertainties. The said literature has been predominantly technical and lacks pervasion into decisionsupport systems and governance of scenarios like climate change. This particular study has made an attempt to compile literature from 2023-2025 and presents a conceptual understanding of its applicability in decision-support systems of climates. To fill such gaps, this paper proposes the Policy Aligned Explainability Framework (PAEF), which aligns explanation techniques with requirements from the governance perspective, offering rolespecific explanation results, guidelines for evaluation, and compliance-friendly artifacts such as model cards, explanation reports, and monitoring logs. By proposing such a framework, the paper illustrates its application through two examples, which are demand response forecasting for the energy domain and early warning for forest deforestation through remote sensing. It also sees environmental sustainability and explainability through the perspective of governance.

title: Polarization-Sensitive Photodetectors Based on 2D GeSe with Enhanced Low-Temperature Response
authors: Kexin He, Yali Yu, Yue-Yang Liu, Liyuan Liu, Zhongming Wei, Shuangming Yu, Ziqi Zhou
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Low-temperature photodetection demonstrates extensive applicability in deep space exploration, signal monitoring of low-temperature quantum computing, and polar region environmental remote sensing. In the low-temperature detection system, polarization-sensitive photodetectors can significantly improve target recognition accuracy by analyzing the polarization state. However, the development of device miniaturization has limited the integration of low-temperature and polarization photodetection. GeSe, an in-plane anisotropic two-dimensional (2D) material, enables polarization detection without additional integrated polarization structures. Herein, we conducted photoelectric performance tests on GeSe under temperature gradients, and the results show that at room temperature, the responsivity is 1.1 A/W, and the polarization ratio (PR) is 1.95. When the temperature decreases to 70 K, the polarization ratio increases to 2.27. This work explores the feasibility of anisotropic materials for low-temperature photodetection, offering valuable insights for developing low-cost, miniaturized low-temperature and polarization-sensitive photodetectors.

title: Impulse Buying in the Indonesian E-Commerce Market: The Role of AI-Driven Personalization
authors: Roozbeh Babolian Hendijani, Kathrin Jaszus
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: This research investigates the impact of AI-driven personalization on impulsive buying in e-commerce platforms in Indonesia, highlighting the mediating roles of perceived enjoyment and trust. A total of 200 questionnaires were collected in Greater Jakarta and were analyzed by SMART-PLS. Findings show a significant relation between AI-driven personalization, perceived enjoyment, and trust. Findings also show there is no significant relationship between AI-driven personalization and impulsive buying. Both trust and perceived enjoyment mediate the relationship between AI-driven personalization and impulsive buying. The results of this study support the S-O-R model, underpinning the significance of organism items in shaping consumer reactions. E-commerce platforms must design approaches that increase enjoyment via engaging elements and reinforce trust by transparent data policies to motivate impulsive buying and encourage customer loyalty in the long term.

title: A Novel Programmable Diode Using 0.18μm Standard CMOS Process for High-density One-Time-Programmable Application
authors: Tianci Wang, Xibo Chen, Junru Qu, Wenhao Liu, Hongrui Zhang, Bing Chen, Yan Liu, Genquan Han, Bo Li
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, we proposed a novel programmable diode fabricated with a standard 0.18μm CMOS process through an N-well and additional N+ region process design to enhance its programmability for the high-density one-time-programmable (OTP). The anti-fused state and fused state can be achieved by employing program voltage under the condition of current compliance, respectively. This device features a large storage window about 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup>, which can effectively prevent the incorrect programming, and a simple structure friendly for CMOS circuit integration. The write and read of the 2×2 memory array and characteristics of the large-scale array constructed using this device are also analyzed in detail. Experimental results demonstrate its feasibility for the application field of high-density OTP memory arrays.

title: Evaluating E-Government Web Quality: A Large-Scale Empirical Assessment of 152 Public Portals in Sri Lanka Across Accessibility, Performance, and Security
authors: Shakira Nisamdeen, Erandika Lakmali
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: As digital governance expands; government websites serve as the primary interface for public service delivery. The effectiveness of these platforms is contingent upon the quality of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), specifically regarding accessibility, performance, and security. Despite Sri Lanka's implementation of national guidelines to support citizen-centered digital services, there is limited empirical evidence regarding the actual compliance of these platforms with international best practices. This study quantitatively evaluates the extent to which Sri Lankan government websites adhere to global engineering benchmarks. A proportionate stratified random sampling method was employed to select 152 active websites, which were assessed using automated diagnostic tools—including Google PageSpeed Insights, WAVE, and Internet.nl—supplemented by structured manual verification. The evaluation framework was derived from WCAG 2.1, Core Web Vitals, OWASP/SLCERT security guidelines, and ICTA standards. The results indicate that the majority of websites fall into the “Very Poor” or “Poor” categories across all three dimensions. While accessibility and security metrics demonstrate compliance with basic structural requirements, there is a critical lack of advanced user-centered features and robust protective mechanisms. Performance emerged as the weakest dimension, characterized by widespread deficiencies in loading speed, resource efficiency, and mobile optimization. The findings suggest that while a foundational digital presence exists, significant technical interventions are required to render these systems truly inclusive, efficient, and secure. To achieve this, the study recommends stronger centralized oversight, continuous automated monitoring, and enhanced technical capacity building across government agencies.

title: Federated and Fairness-Aware Learning for Rural Healthcare Risk Prediction Under Data Scarcity
authors: Dhruti A, Saksham Garg, Panchadip Bhattacharjee, Somyajeet Arukh, Nishanth Shet, Gururaj H L
abstract: Centralized machine learning is difficult to implement and susceptible to demographic bias in rural healthcare risk prediction due to fragmented data, small sample sizes, and stringent privacy regulations. In order to facilitate collaborative model training across dispersed rural healthcare sites without exchanging sensitive data, this paper suggests a federated and fairness-aware learning framework. Dirichlet-based partitioning is used to simulate heterogeneous clients using National Family Health Survey data from 707 Indian districts with 14 socioeconomic and healthcare indicators in order to model realistic non-IID conditions. To jointly balance accuracy, equity, and privacy, the suggested federated ensemble integrates gradient-boosted tree models with optimized weighting, empirical differential privacy-inspired noise mechanisms. While maintaining approximately 0.98 of centralized AUC performance (AUC: 0.865 vs. 0.879), the framework reduces socioeconomic disparity gaps and achieves an AUC of 0.865 and an F1-score of 0.705. The introduction of a federated evaluation suite includes uncertainty estimation, feature stability, client contribution attribution, and heterogeneity measurement. Explainability analysis identifies infrastructure access, public healthcare use, and demographic makeup as important risk factors. The findings show a workable approach to fair, comprehensible, and privacy-aware AI risk prediction in simulated federated settings in rural healthcare settings with limited resources.

title: Performance Comparison of Spatial Filters for Denoising Rayleigh, Rician, and Gamma Noise Models in Ultrasound Images
authors: Monir Hossain, Abdul Jalil Tamjid, Mohammed Ibrahim Hussain, Mohammad Motiur Rahman, A.H.M Shahariar Parvez, Muhammad Minoar Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Ultrasound imaging is a common way to diagnose medical problems because it is safe, non-invasive, and cheap. However, noise like Rayleigh, Rician, and Gamma noise frequently deteriorates its quality and obfuscates crucial diagnostic information. These sounds could be caused by environmental interference, device limitations, or tissue characteristics. The spatial filters (average, Gaussian, bilateral, median, min, and max) for denoising ultrasound images are assessed in this study. Rayleigh and Gamma noise are handled as multiplicative, whereas Rician noise is modeled as additive. SNR, PSNR, MSE, SSIM, and EPF are used to evaluate the performance of the filter. Our experiments show that Gaussian filtering yields the highest SNR/PSNR under low-moderate multiplicative noise, whereas median filtering performs best at high noise levels. Bilateral filtering most effectively preserves SSIM in additive (Rician) noise. These results guide the choice of denoising filters for different ultrasound noise conditions.

title: The Effect of Layout Design on Hot Carrier Degradation in N-type STI-LDMOS
authors: Jia Qiu, Qianwen Guo, Dawei Gao, Ran Tao, Junkang Li, Rui Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, the effect of gate length L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</inf> and gate width W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</inf> on hot carrier degradation (HCD) in shallow trench isolation n-type lateral double diffusion MOS (STI-NLDMOS) fabricated by 55 nm CMOS standard process is investigated. As W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</inf> decreases, the degradation of R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> in short-channel devices initially falls and then increase rapidly. TCAD simulations indicate that severe self-heating in wide-channel devices suppresses impact ionization rate. At the same time, hot hole injection becomes the dominant degradation mechanism, resulting in a reduction of R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> as the channel width increases. The results suggest that employing a wider device layout can mitigate HCD in short channel devices.

title: Real-Time Bolt Length Measurement with Adaptive Calibration
authors: Emre Güçlü, Oğuz Ergin, İlhan Aydın, Erhan Akın, Yunus Emre Uyanık
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Bolts, specifically bolts, are essential in industry settings, where dimensional accuracy of parts is crucial for the reliable and safe operation of machines. This necessitates manufacturers to consistently employ quality control of produced products. Yet, the current methodologies for checking fasteners involving directly observing them by eye and using calipers to check dimensions is slow and does not scale for mass production for both accuracy and efficiency. This demonstrates the need for an innovative fast measurement system. In this paper, a low-cost method for real-time automatic hardware measurement using vision systems is described and evaluated for use as a contending measurement procedure for the fast measurement of bolt lengths. The vision system is constructed of a monochrome industrial camera with an LED lighting setup attached to a motorized rotating platform driven by an Arduino microcontroller, capturing images of the rotating bolted hardware. An adaptive Region of Interest (ROI) was employed which removes excess noise and reduces computation time, while the bolt edges were evaluated using the Canny edge detection algorithm. A Hough Transform was employed to straighten the image for appropriate alignment and increased measurement accuracy, while an adaptive calibration procedure to convert pixels to length, based on previous measurements, was utilized. Trials focused on the use of bolts from 3–6 cm in length and showed that accuracy was more than 0.02% Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) at low to medium speeds and varying accuracy was observed at high test speeds due to motion blur.

title: RNN-Based MADRL for Distributed Inter-Satellite Routing in LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Siyuan You, Honghao Fu, Mingjie Feng
abstract: With the growing demand for global connectivity, Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication networks have become a vital approach to provide Internet access to users in remote or underserved areas. An inevitable trend of satellite communications is constructing a space-based core network, in which multi-hop links need to be established to support data transmission between satellites. As a result, inter-satellite routing is a key design factor that impacts the data transmission performance. However, large scale deployment of satellites and the rapidly changing states of inter-satellite links, the design of inter-satellite routing strategies faces multi-fold challenges. To this end, we propose MA-DRQN, a routing algorithm that combines Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) with Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL). Specifically, RNN is utilized to capture the temporal patterns in traffic and state of various inter-satellite links while MADRL enables each satellite to learn environment-adaptive routing policies in a decentralized pattern. Simulation results under both stable and disrupted link scenarios show that the proposed MA-DRQN algorithm achieves significantly lower packet loss and delay and higher throughput compared to benchmark algorithms.

title: Resource Efficiency in Containerized Distributed Microservices Through Advanced Orchestration
authors: Sachin Telalwar
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Container-driven microservice platforms have achieved near-ubiquitous adoption, yet mainstream orchestrators still leave 30%-50% of CPU capacity idle and waste comparable fractions of memory because they rely on threshold heuristics oblivious to workload semantics. We introduce AROMA—an Adaptive Resource-Oriented Microservice Arranger that couples time-series forecasting with a duelling deep- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex> network and a sidecar-aware placement heuristic. Mathematical modelling defines a composite efficiency score that balances utilization, latency and energy, while a reinforcement-learning agent continuously maximises that score under service-level constraints. Experiments on a 40-node hybrid Kubernetes test-bed running the TPC-C benchmark demonstrate that AROMA lifts mean CPU utilisation from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$55.3 \pm 3.8 {\%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$74.1 \pm 3.2$</tex> % and trims 95-th-percentile latency from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$182 \pm 21 \text{ms}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$119 \pm 15$</tex> ms compared with the vanilla scheduler. Energy per successful transaction falls by 19.4% and availability under single-node failures rises from 99.41% to 99.79%. These results indicate that intelligent orchestration can reconcile performance, sustainability and resilience in mission-critical microservice fabrics.

title: Investigation of Plasma Processing Conditions for Low-damage Multi-Vt Process
authors: Hidefumi Hirabayashi, Satoshi Sakai, Kiyohiko Sato, Kofuji Naoyuki, Miyako Matsui, Taiga Kasai, Makoto Miura
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Multi-Vt process in advanced logic devices become more demanding in terms of scaling and Vt controllability. Recently, plasma-induced damage on gate structure during dry etching gather attention. In this paper, plasma-induced damage on TiN/HfO2/SiO2 stacked structure during multi-Vt process is investigated under various process conditions, such as pressure, etc. The results show an increase in HfO2 wet etch rate, interlayer oxide regrowth, and an increase in charge trap density can be mitigated by lowering process pressure. It was found out ions with lower energy play a key role in suppressing plasma-induced damage.

title: Heterogenous integration of III-V materials on Silicon
authors: Gauri Karve
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: III-V semiconductors are central to numerous applications such as telecom, datacom, automotive radar, high speed RF, IR sensing and imaging<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>. The demand for some of the applications like short range datacom with co-packaged optics is expected to dramatically increase with the push for fast and energy efficient AI systems<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Properties such as direct band gap, extended absorption range beyond visible wavelength, and high carrier mobilities make III-V materials ideal candidates for high performance lasers, LEDs, RF devices, and imagers. The corresponding drivers and digital logic processing in these systems remain silicon-based due to the performance and cost benefits. Therefore, co-integration of III-V materials with Silicon has been a topic of interest for several decades. Heterogenous integration of III-V on silicon has been explored using methods such as micro-bumping, micro-transfer printing, direct epitaxial growth of III-V on Silicon and wafer reconstitution – each at a different technology readiness level<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3,4</sup>. This talk will provide a comparative analysis of these heterogenous integration strategies, highlighting their maturity levels and applicability across different applications.IMEC has a long standing history of integrating dissimilar components, including III-V materials on a die or a wafer<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5,6</sup>. Processes such as die singulation, die-to-wafer and wafer-to-wafer bonding, temporary bonding and de-bonding, dielectric gap fill, grinding and planarization techniques have been developed and refined based on application-specific requirements. We will present the current status of several critical heterogenous integration processes using examples from three applications – GaN on silicon micro-LED displays, short wave infrared InP-based imagers and InP-based O-band lasers. Finally, we will discuss the main challenges that remain and outline potential pathways for their mitigation.

title: Gate Stack Engineering of 3D Oxide Channel FeNOR Memory with High-Speed and Reliabilitity
authors: Yuejia Zhou, Ru Huang, Kechao Tang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, we systematically engineer the gate stack (MFS, MFIS, MIFS, and MIFIS) in 3D vertical oxidechannel FeNORs to optimize memory characteristics. The interlayer-free MFS device is demonstrated to provide the optimal overall performance, achieving the fastest operation speed of 0.25 μs and highest endurance of >10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>, while maintaining a practical 1V memory window and robust retention. Characterizations of interface and oxide traps reveal that suppressed charge trapping in MFS is the critical mechanism for enhanced reliability. Our work presents a viable strategy for realizing fast and reliable 3D FE memory.

title: Improving Colorectal Cancer Classification and Detection: Leveraging VGG16 and Explainable AI for High-Accuracy Classification
authors: Al Arian Ahmad, Md. Abu Johab, Nakib Aman, Durjoy Kumar Dutta, Salehin Ahmad, Md. Abdul Wadud
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer (CRC) remains a leading cause of cancer-related morbidity and mortality worldwide and early, accurate detection is crucial for improving patient survival. Recent advances in deep learning have shown exceptional promise for automated classification and diagnosis in medical imaging. In this study, we propose a multi-model deep learning framework for CRC classification and detection across 13 clinically relevant categories, including adenocarcinomas, polyps, ulcerative colitis and anal carcinomas. A curated dataset of 30,766 images was utilized to train and evaluate six state-of-the-art architectures: EfficientNetB0 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.59 \%)$</tex>, MobileNetV2 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.42 \%)$</tex>, ConvNeXtV2 (99.85%), AlexNet (99.81%), InceptionV3 (100%) and VGG16 (99.90%), with VGG16 delivering the most consistent performance. To enhance clinical reliability, the models were further integrated with Explainable AI (XAI) methods such as Grad-CAM and LIME, enabling interpretable predictions by highlighting key pathological regions influencing classification outcomes. The high accuracy achieved across multiple architectures demonstrates the robustness of the proposed system, while the integration of XAI ensures transparency and fosters trust in AI-driven diagnostics. Our framework underscores the potential of combining highperforming deep learning models with explainability to support medical professionals in timely, confident, and interpretable detection of colorectal cancer.

title: RL-Optimized Natural Language Prompts for Enabling User-Centric Computational Thinking via Small-Scale LLMs
authors: Shubham S. Bhatt, Michael S. Hsiao
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: We introduce a reinforcement learning (RL)-based framework to optimize discrete natural language prompts for enhancing both the accuracy and clarity in sentence simplification. Using a lightweight PPO policy, our method learns to guide a frozen small-scale LLaMA-3.2B model toward effective simplification for supporting user-centric computational thinking tasks. Results show that our RL-optimized prompts significantly surpass manual baselines in semantic fidelity, logical coherence, and instructional quality. Moreover, the proposed RL-optimized prompting approach enables a much smaller LLM to achieve results that are comparable in clarity and instructional value to those produced by a much larger LLaMA-3.3 70B model.

title: High Quality Malay Corpus via LLM-Aided Validation
authors: Nur Amalina Baharom, Wandeep Kaur Ratan Singh, Muhammad Ajrul Amin Mohd Zaidi, Masnizah Mohd, Abdul Hadi Abd Rahman, Lailatul Qadri Zakaria, Shazwan Hakim Muhamad, Afrina Rosa Mohamad Sattar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: High quality training data is an essential first step for developing reliable large language models (LLMs), particularly for low resource languages such as Malay. While existing data preparation workflows often focus on cleaning, normalization, and deduplication, these steps are insufficient and do not guarantee dataset quality or suitability required for LLM training. To address this limitation, this paper presents an LLM-aided validation framework that introduces sentence level evaluation as a final quality control stage for Malay corpora. The proposed approach employs a locally deployed Gemma-2 9B model as a linguistic evaluator aligned with the Malay Language Standard Framework (KSBM) defined by Kementerian Pendidikan Malaysia (KPM). Each sentence is evaluated based on three specific metrics: content clarity, grammatical accuracy, and the appropriateness of both discourse coherence and register. The resulting scores are aggregated to stratify sentences into six levels from TP1 to TP6, providing a clear pathway for the systematic filtering, correction, and selection of high-quality training data. To ensure reproducibility, privacy preservation, and compliance with institutional data governance requirements, all validation processes are executed locally without reliance on external APIs. Experimental results on large scale Malay text collections demonstrate that the proposed validation layer effectively identifies low quality and inconsistent sentences that are not reliably detected through rule-based preprocessing alone. The findings highlight the importance of LLM-aided validation as a scalable and practical approach for improving data quality in low resource language LLM development.

title: Financial Development, Green Growth and Sustainable Development in OECD Countries
authors: Hala Jalali, Foday Joof, Ala Fathi Assi, Aliya Isiksal, Bayan Mohammed, Talent Daisy Chamboko
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study aims to investigate the association between air pollution exposure (APE) represented by PM2.5, the development of sustainable energy technologies, green growth, financial expansion, and renewable energy. The study analyzes the 26 OECD countries rich in fossil fuels and their renewable energy investments with employing Pooled Mean Group and Autoregressive Distributed Lag analysis by using data from 2010 to 2020. PMG-ARDL results show that one unit rise in renewable energy, clean energy technology, green growth and financial development occur a unit decrease in pollution. Consequently, findings indicate that renewable energy can replace traditional fuels, while sustainable technologies can serve as proactive and efficient measures for improving air quality and reducing pollution. Moreover, economic development can motivate institutions to offer greener loans and investments, leading to a shift in the loan portfolio towards green business operations. Which then forces companies to undertake green activity in OECD countries.

title: Robust Audio Representation Learning with Differential Privacy via Self-Supervised Contrastive Pre-Training
authors: Indrojit Sarkar, Anjan Kumar Bagchi, Shaikh Anowarul Fattah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The growing use of machine learning to analyze sensitive biomedical audio signals, like coughs and breathing patterns, requires strong privacy-preserving methods. However, training models with Differential Privacy (DP) often reduces performance significantly, which limits real-world use. To tackle this privacy-utility issue, we propose a two-stage framework that separates representation learning from classification. First, we pre-train a VGG-based feature encoder on audio spectrograms using a self-supervised contrastive objective along with Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD). The resulting frozen, private encoder is then used to train a linear classifier for the main task. Experiments on the ESC-50 benchmark show that our method achieves a classification accuracy of 74.2 % with a privacy budget of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon=5.0$</tex>. This significantly outperforms a standard end-to-end supervised DP baseline by 12.7 percentage points. This work shows that self-supervised pre-training is a highly effective approach for creating accurate and private models for sensitive audio-based health applications.

title: Hybrid STL-CNN Transformer and BiLSTM-Attention Architectures for Short-Term Server Power Prediction
authors: Maria Hasan, Shamima Sharmin Ananna, Amit Mandal
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate short-term forecasting of server-level power consumption is essential for optimizing energy efficiency, preventing overloads, and ensuring sustainable operation in modern data centers, which account for nearly 2% of global electricity demand. Prior studies using statistical and standard machine learning methods often fail to capture complex temporal dependencies. In this work, we evaluate classical models (Random Forest, XGBoost) and deep learning baselines (univariate LSTM), observing only moderate accuracy. To overcome these limitations, we propose two hybrid architectures that integrate seasonal-trend decomposition using Loess (STL) with advanced sequence modeling: STL-CNN-Transformer, which combines convolutional embeddings with a Transformer encoder, and STL-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention, which unites convolutional feature extraction, bidirectional LSTM layers, and an attention mechanism. Experiments on a real-world dataset of over 184,426 server records show that the STL-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model achieves the best performance (RMSE 0.8992, MAE 0.4804), consistently outperforming classical and deep learning evaluated baselines. These results highlight the effectiveness of residualbased hybrid architectures with attention for robust power consumption forecasting in data centers.

title: Hy-GeneNet: A Hybrid Ensemble of N-Gram and Attention-Based LSTM for Reliable and Interpretable DNA Gene Sequence Prediction
authors: Touhid Alam, Sayedur Rahman, Abdullah Al Jubair, Md. Jakir Hossen
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid growth of genomic data from highthroughput sequencing calls for accurate DNA sequence classification. While traditional machine learning methods like N gram models excel at capturing local statistical patterns, deep learning architectures like Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks are adept at learning long-range dependencies. We propose Hy-GeneNet, a novel hybrid ensemble model that synergistically combines the strengths of both models. Hy-GeneNet integrates a feature-based N-gram model with a Random Forest classifier and a Bidirectional LSTM enhanced with a multihead attention mechanism. Evaluated on a challenging multiclass DNA sequence dataset, Hy-GeneNet achieves a state-of-theart accuracy of 98.26 %, and a perfect ROC AUC score of 1.00, significantly outperforming its individual N-gram (98.01 %) and LSTM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(97.85 \%)$</tex> components. Moreover, through Explainable AI (XAI) techniques, we validate that the model's high performance is grounded in interpretable and biologically relevant sequence features, establishing Hy-GeneNet as a robust and trustworthy tool for genomic analysis.

title: Properties of p-type SnO Thin Films via Oxygen Flow Control during Sputtering Deposition
authors: Tomoki Otani, Yuta Ito, Tappei Nishihara, Atsushi Ogura
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The effects of varying oxygen partial pressures during SnO deposition were investigated using a combination of Hall effect measurement, X-ray diffraction, Raman spectroscopy, and X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy. We clarified that low oxygen partial pressure during SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> deposition exhibits p-type behavior with the crystalline state, while high oxygen partial pressure results in n-type behavior with the amorphous state, demonstrating a correlation between crystalline and electrical properties. The investigation of the valence band maximum (VBM) revealed that defect levels may influence p-type electrical characteristics. In addition, depthprofiling using X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) and hard X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (HAXPES) revealed that the surface of the SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> films is covered with an oxygen-rich layer.

title: Performance Management System's Role in Boosting Employee Performance at an Engineering Consulting Firm
authors: Rini Kurnia Sari, Tasya Padilah, Diah Astrivianti, Visy Yulanda Mutiara
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The construction industry plays a pivotal role in driving Indonesia’s economic growth, contributing 10.44% to the national GDP in 2022. However, the sector continues to face structural challenges such as fluctuating material costs, shortages of skilled labor, and increasingly stringent safety requirements, which significantly affect organizational performance. These challenges are also evident in engineering consulting firms, including PT ABC (pseudonym), where recent performance records indicate declining employee performance across several departments. In this context, the effectiveness of the Performance Management System (PMS) becomes essential in maintaining service quality and operational resilience. Using multiple regression analysis, the results show that performance planning, feedback, performance assessment, training, and appreciation significantly and positively affect employee performance. Adjusted R-squared value of 0.534 indicates that 53.4% of the variation in employee performance is explained by these factors. The findings reinforce the theoretical importance of integrated Human Resource (HR) practices and offer managerial insights for strengthening PMS implementation in project-based organizations, particularly in engineering consulting firm.

title:
Temperature-dependent analysis of Ge
                    <sub>1-x</sub>
                    Sn
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    -based single-photon avalanche diodes for cryogenic applications
authors: Harshvardhan Kumar, Jyoti Tater
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work presents the first proof of the temperature-dependent analysis of Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.93</inf>Sn<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.07</inf>-based single-photon avalanche diodes (SPADs) designed for cryogenic applications. The results indicate that the operating temperature significantly impacts the performance of the proposed SPAD. Notably, both DCR and SPDE exhibit an increase as temperature rises. Nonetheless, the temperature has a minimal impact on the responsivity. Furthermore, the thickness of the GeSn active layer positively influences the SPDE value. The performance of the proposed SPAD is outstanding across cryogenic temperatures, room temperature, and elevated temperatures. Moreover, the comparison with previously reported SPADs suggests that the proposed GeSn SPAD design could be a viable candidate for future developments in quantum computing and communication technologies.

title: The Impact of Digital Transformation on HRM Effectiveness in Government Institutions
authors: Mohammed Yousif Abo Keir, Dauji Ali Khalifa Al-Noaimi, Ahmad Saleh Ali Shatat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study examines the role of digital transformation in enhancing human resource management (HRM) performance in government institutions. Using the Office of the Deputy Prime Minister in Bahrain as a case study, three key dimensions of digital transformation technological environment, electronic management, and organizational communication were analyzed. A descriptive-analytical approach and survey of 120 employees revealed that electronic management and communication significantly improved HRM performance, while the technological environment showed no significant effect. The study highlights the importance of investing in digital infrastructure and communication tools to improve HRM efficiency in the public sector.

title: Wideband Massive Characteristic-Mode Tracking of Metasurfaces Using Single-Layer Near-Field Global-Correlation (NFGC) Method
authors: Xi Chong You, Feng Han Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: In characteristic mode analysis (CMA), wideband massivemode tracking of metasurfaces (MTSs) remains challenging due to mode swapping and mode missing. In prior works, the method of near-field global-correlation (NFGC) has been found effective in resolving the problems for up to 20 characteristic modes (CMs) by using dual-surface sampling. This work attempts to extend the method to track 30 modes over a larger bandwidth without losing accuracy by using a simplified singlesurface sampling method. With the proposed single-layer NFGC method, 30 modes of two patch-type MTSs are tracked over 40%, showing significantly enhanced density and bandwidth of mode tracking over commercial solvers.

title:
ALD Grown Local Single-Crystal Ferroelectric Hf
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    Zr
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    for FeFET Gate Oxide
authors: Liang Chen, Runteng Zhu, Kechao Tang, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, for the first time, we propose and demonstrate a novel local single-crystal ferroelectric Hf0.5Zr0.5O2 (HZO) film by Atomic Layer Deposition (ALD) process suitable for use as the gate dielectric in ferroelectric field-effect transistors (FeFETs). The trench array in dielectric HfO₂ provides lateral size confinement for HZO grains, suppressing monoclinic (m) phase formation. Ultimately, the HZO within the trenches attains a single-crystal ferroelectric state. The novel ferroelectric oxide enables faster polarization switching for improved FeFET program/erase (PRG/ERS) speed, reduces device-to-device (D2D) variation through controlled phase distribution, and enhances endurance via tunable polarization. These attributes make our local single-crystal ferroelectric HZO a promising gate dielectric candidate for next-generation FeFET devices.

title: Digital Twin-Enabled Predictive Maintenance for Conveyor Belt Systems
authors: Muhammad Khalid, Amaliia Kolmykova, Muhammad Zakir Shaikh, Enrique Nava Baro, Bhawani Shankar Chowdhry, Ritu Raj Lamsal
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Predictive maintenance in an industrial system has become more convenient with the support of emerging technologies like digital twins, smart sensing, and the Internet of Things as a backbone. Many industries use conveyor belt systems for production. The problems likely to lead to failures in conveyor belt systems are misalignment of the belts, wear, motor overload, overheating, and speed fluctuations. These parameters should be monitored closely, as deviations from the normal can result in production delays and financial losses. In contrast to most currently available digital twin applications that rely on proprietary cloud infrastructure and expensive sensor networks, this paper proposes a low-cost, scalable, sensor-based predictive maintenance framework and a lightweight digital twin framework for real-time predictive maintenance of conveyor belt systems. The system consists of sensors such as load cells, current sensors, temperature sensors, and rotary encoders to measure changes in load, motor performance, thermal performance, and belt movement. Sensor data are acquired and analyzed against preset thresholds to detect anomalies in current consumption, overheating, abnormal load conditions, and speed. The maintenance score is updated in real time. When it reaches a critical level, it generates alerts and stores them in a database for historical analysis. Real-time sensor data processing and mapping to a 3D digital twin model provide real-time visualization of system performance and early anomaly detection. The effectiveness of the sensing and prediction pipeline was confirmed by experimental testing at different load and speed conditions. The abnormalities indicated by the system included a 1822 % increase in current, a 810° C rise in temperature, and a 12 % deviation in belt slip, which are indicative of dependable operation under various operating conditions. The suggested system is affordable and can be used in small- to medium-sized industrial systems, enabling real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance, and allowing visualization of digital twins on virtual and real-world displays.

title: Evaluation of Total Sleep Time Using Dominant Breathing Frequency from Nasal Airflow Signals
authors: Maysha Rahman Trisha, Effat Mahajabin Ahona, Afifa Halim Prapti, Md. Bashir Uddin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The precise measurement of total sleep time (TST) is a key to diagnose and treat sleep disorders. Traditional methods to measure TST depend on the use of multi-channel polysomnography (PSG) that is expensive and time-consuming. This study suggested a simple rule-based algorithm that is used to estimate TST using a single-channel nasal airflow signal. Eighty-eight PSG records were collected from the sleep heart health study and nasal airflow signals were extracted. A bandpass filter (0.1-1.5 Hz) was used to eliminate noise and unwanted frequency components. The airflow signals were divided into 30 -second epochs and the dominant respiratory frequency was identified with the help of the Fourier transform. The dominant frequency was then transformed to the breathing rate or breaths per minute (BPM). Breathing rates within the range 14-22 BPM were categorized as sleep, otherwise categorized as wake. The findings revealed that the predicted TST (5.96 ± 1.84 hours) was in close proximity with annotated TST (5.46 ± 1.36 hours), with a mean bias of −0.51 hours and 95% limits of agreement of −3.92 to 2.90 hours. Though there were certain deviations in some records due to the noisy airflow signal, the algorithm attained a good agreement with annotation. This simple automated approach can be applied to sleep testing devices that is especially designed to detect sleeprelated problems.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Nail Diseases Using ResNet101: A Study on Healthy Nails, Onychomycosis, and Psoriasis
authors: Madhamanchi Sreevani, Seeniappan Kaliappan, M.Muthukannan, Divya Bhardwaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study examines a ResNet101 model to classify nail diseases: Healthy Nails, Onychomycosis, and Psoriasis. Data, obtained from Kaggle, consists of 1466 images from the three classes with varying levels of nail conditions to enhance diversity and model generalizability. Data preprocessing, including resizing, normalization, and grayscale transformation, was performed to prepare the data for effective use in the ResNet101 architecture. Training was performed using 1165 images, and testing was performed using 301 images, yielding a total accuracy of 82%. Parameters like precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix reported good performance from the model, with certain strengths in classifying Onychomycosis with a 0.87 precision. There was difficulty classifying Healthy Nails from diseased classes, with occasional misclassifications. This paper shows promise in applying deep learning to nail disease classification, providing a good starting point for enhancing model accuracy and generalizability in the future, with scope for further research in data augmentation and multimodal learning.

title: Dynamic Multi-Depot Mobile Charging Vehicle Routing Problem with Recharging Services and Heterogeneous fleet
authors: Imane Benchelih, Fouad Maliki, Abdelghani Bekrar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Nowadays, adopting electric vehicles (EVs) in logistics represents a promising strategy to reduce transportation related emissions. With growing environmental concerns, stricter regulations, and increasing demand for sustainable solutions, EVs offer significant benefits. However, despite these advantages, their adoption faces critical challenges, particularly insufficient public charging infrastructure and the need for efficient recharging strategies. To address these issues, recent research has focused on smart energy strategies, especially Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) technologies. This study focuses on the Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) approach which is the sharing of energy between EVs. Our paper focus on finding the optimal routing and charging decisions of an heterogeneous fleet of Mobile Charging Vehicles (MCVs) which will recharge EVs that are unable to complete their routes due to battery depletion considering energy demand variation, and recharging constraints. In each period, the routing and charging of MCVs can change dynamically to better respond to spatial demand fluctuations. To address the problem we proposed A mixed-integer linear programming mathematical model which aims to minimize total travel and charging costs while satisfying the charging needs of electric vehicles and ensuring service continuity. To solve this complex problem, we propose two methods: an exact approach using the CPLEX solver and a metaheuristic approach based on the Artificial Bee Colony (ABC) algorithm. A case study using realistic data illustrates the effectiveness of both approaches.

title: Evaluating Large Language Models for Security Bug Report Prediction
authors: Farnaz Soltaniani, Shoaib Razzaq, Mohammad Ghafari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Early detection of security bug reports (SBRs) is critical for timely vulnerability mitigation. We present an evaluation of prompt-based engineering and fine-tuning approaches for predicting SBRs using Large Language Models (LLMs). Our findings reveal a distinct trade-off between the two approaches. Prompted proprietary models demonstrate the highest sensitivity to SBRs, achieving a G-measure of 77 % and a recall of 74 % on average across all the datasets, albeit at the cost of a higher false-positive rate, resulting in an average precision of only 22 %. Fine-tuned models, by contrast, exhibit the opposite behavior, attaining a lower overall G -measure of 51 % but substantially higher precision of 75 % at the cost of reduced recall of 36 %. Though a one-time investment in building fine-tuned models is necessary, the inference on the largest dataset is up to 50 times faster than that of proprietary models. These findings suggest that further investigations to harness the power of LLMs for SBR prediction are necessary.

title: Impact of Dust Accumulation on Photovoltaic Panel Performance in Semi-Arid Climates*
authors: Mustapha Ezzini, Mohammed Ennejjar, Raja Mouachi, Abdelali El Gourari, Mohammed Boukendil, Mustapha Raoufi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Dust accumulation, or soiling, is a critical factor affecting the performance of photovoltaic (PV) panels in arid climates. This study investigates the impact of natural dust deposition on polycrystalline PV modules in Marrakech, Morocco. Experimental measurements were conducted on clean and soiled panels to quantify the reduction in short-circuit current and energy output. Dust levels of 5%, 10%, and 15% caused average current reductions of 3.8%, 7.9%, and 12.6%, respectively, with full recovery after cleaning, confirming the reversibility of the soiling effect. Soiling loss analysis revealed that Dust Type 2 resulted in slightly higher losses (up to 27.5%) compared to Dust Type 1 (up to 26.0%). The study highlights the nearly linear relationship between dust load and energy loss, emphasizing the importance of regular cleaning to maintain optimal PV performance. These findings provide insights for maintenance strategies and performance prediction in arid regions.

title: Lemon Quality Detection Using MobileNetV3-Large for Automated Classification
authors: T. Roseline Velankanni, M.Muthukannan, Alok Misra, Dipannita Ray, Yashasvi Saini, Manish Kumar Singla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research investigates the application of MobileNetV3-Large for detecting the quality of lemons, specifically to differentiate Good Quality lemons from Bad Quality lemons. There are 12,456 images in the dataset, which were created from an initial 2,076 image set that represents Good Quality with 1,125 images and Bad Quality with 951 images. Data augmentation methods during the 15-epoch training phase enhanced model generalization. The system attained 94% accuracy with 0.95 precision in the case of good-quality lemons and 0.94 precision and 0.92 recall in the case of bad-quality lemons. The model can be validated in real-world scenarios since the confusion matrix indicates low misclassification errors. The MobileNetV3-Large system demonstrates great potential in automated quality control because it combines a lightweight structure with strong performance capabilities in the agriculture and food production sectors. Research needs to investigate bigger datasets and multi-class classification approaches, together with transfer learning methods aimed at increasing detection precision.

title: Ensemble Machine Learning for High-Fidelty Techno-Economic Forecasting of Grid-Scale Hybrid Renewable Energy
authors: Syed Taimoor Ali, Ubaida Fatima
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: This research develops an AI-based predictive techno-economic modeling framework for hybrid solarwind systems in resource-rich Sindh region of Pakistan. For this study, we created a synthetic large-sized dataset of 5000 observations that included meteorological, operational, and economic variables and built and evaluated ensemble machine learning models which were Random Forest (RF) and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). These models were then trained to forecast critical performance indicators such as total energy generation, Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE), and CO2 emission intensity. The dataset includes Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI), wind speed, temperature, humidity and capital cost data obtained partially from NASA-Power analogs. With the XGBoost regressor yielding coefficients of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}\right)$</tex> of 0.9978 for energy prediction and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 9 3 0}$</tex> for LCOE prediction.

title: Techniques of Attribution in Cyberspace Based on Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Ahmed Hussein Ali, Alaa Hussein Al-Hamami, Zeki Saeed Tawfeaq, Mohammad Alaa Al-Hamami
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Cyber attribution is the process of locating, identifying, and attributing blame to the person who carried out a cyberattack or other hacking attack. Cybercrimes must be held accountable in order to reduce crime rates and to ensure that the necessary degree of response is ready. The pattern has been spurred by the increasing use of internet in our daily activities and the high degree of secrecy that cyberattacks afford their perpetrators. Because of this, cybercrimes have the capacity to seriously interfere with our daily lives while preserving the identity of the perpetrator.In order to help enterprises properly assess cyber threat attribution, this article presents a paradigm. A company's finances; credibility, compliance, and public relations can all suffer from cyberattacks. In this study, we developed a five-step approach to attribution: (1) Identifying the specific cyberattack that was utilized; (2) Identifying the cyber attack's source; (3) Target Value; (4) Target Position; (5) and (6) finally determination of the actual attacker; Attribution techniques based on Artificial Intelligence are evolving rapidly, offering more accurate, faster, and scalable methods to identify and trace cyber threats.These techniques are crucial for enhancing cybersecurity measures and mitigating the risks posed by malicious actors in cyberspace. The Triangle Model gets its name from the fact that it determines attribution using three high-fidelity indications. The sector, tools, and methods, as well as processes (TTPs) are the triangle model's vertices. Given that it is difficult for a Threat Actor to switch tools and even more difficult to alter behavior, the Triangle Analysis Model views tools and TTPs as high-fidelity indicators.

title: Improving Aspect-Category-Sentiment Detection through a Weighted Attentional Graph Framework
authors: Neda Keivandarian, Marco Carvalho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) aims to extract fine-grained opinions associated with specific aspects. However, existing models often struggle with implicit aspects, conflicting sentiments, and long-range dependencies. This paper presents the Weighted Attention Graph for Aspect-CategorySentiment Detection (WAG-ACSD), a framework that integrates contextual and structural cues through a Graph Attention Convolutional Network (GACN). The proposed model introduces a similarity-based attention bias that combines contextual similarity and syntactic distance, enabling it to emphasize opinion terms relevant to each aspect category while reducing contextual noise. Experimental results on the SemEval-2015 and SemEval-2016 benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvements over previous methods in end-to-end aspect-category-sentiment detection. Qualitative analysis further confirms WAG-ACSD’s capability to capture complex sentence structures and correctly associate abstract categories with their textual evidence. Overall, the framework offers an interpretable and effective solution for finegrained sentiment analysis without relying on external knowledge or data augmentation.

title: Impact of Heat Transfer Fluid Velocity on Sustainable Multi-Stage Latent Thermal Energy Storage Performance in CSP Systems
authors: Ahmad W. Omari, Basil Ibrahim, Hamdy M. Mohamed, Hend ElZefzafy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study presents a computational investigation of a multi-stage latent heat thermal energy storage (LHTES) system integrated with cascaded organic phase change materials (PCMs) for application in concentrated solar power (CSP) plants. The research focuses on quantifying the influence of heat transfer fluid (HTF) inlet velocity on melting dynamics, charging efficiency, and overall thermal response of the storage unit. A three-stage PCM configuration—employing Octadecanoic Acid, RT58, and Dodecanoic Acid—was modeled to exploit sequential melting across distinct temperature ranges, enhancing system stability and thermal utilization. Using computational fluid dynamics (CFD) in ANSYS Fluent with the SIMPLE algorithm, simulations were carried out under fixed HTF temperature (100°C) while systematically varying inlet velocity from 35 m/s to 70 m/s. The results demonstrate that increased HTF velocity accelerates convective heat transfer and reduces melting duration, though improvements remain incremental compared to temperature-driven effects. Additionally, higher velocities introduce greater heat loss to the aluminum shell, limiting net efficiency gains. This work provides key insights into velocity optimization as a secondary performance parameter in LHTES design, complementing temperature control strategies to maximize charging efficiency, minimize thermal losses, and enhance CSP plant reliability and sustainability.

title:
Microscopic Mechanism of Imprint Suppression in Hf
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    Zr
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -based Memory Devices: The Role of AlO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Interlayer and Wake-Up Schemes
authors: Wen Sun, Bowen Shen, Yuyan Wang, Mingcheng Shi, Yi Ding, Benjamin Yang, Jian Yuan, Bin Gao, Jianshi Tang, He Qian, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study addresses the imprint effect in HZO ferroelectric memory by introducing an AlO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> interlayer and four electric-field operation schemes. The bottom-electrode driving with increasing step voltage ramping effectively suppresses oxygen vacancy accumulation at critical interfaces, minimizing voltage shift (0.09 V) and enhancing imprint immunity (retained 2P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> = 28.3 µC/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> after thermal stress). Through atomic-resolution EELS and DPC characterization, we directly reveal the relationship between the oxygen vacancy distribution and defect fields under different biasing conditions. A comprehensive mechanism linking electric-field configurations, oxygen vacancy dynamics, and macroscopic performance is established, providing critical insights for designing reliable HZO-based memory devices.

title: Early-Stage Weed Detection in Sorghum Using Instance Segmentation for Precision Agriculture
authors: Jonel R. Macalisang, Alvin Sarraga Alon, Michelle C. Reyes, Ryan Reyes, Sammy V. Militante, Aimee G. Acoba
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Weed infestation is a major challenge in sorghum cultivation, as it competes with crops for nutrients, water, and sunlight, leading to significant yield losses if not managed effectively. Traditional manual weed detection and removal methods are time-consuming, labor-intensive, and prone to human error. To address this issue, this study presents a novel instance segmentation–based approach for automated weed identification during the early crop development stages in sorghum fields. Utilizing deep learning techniques, particularly the Mask R-CNN framework, the model is capable of accurately detecting and segmenting weed occurrences among sorghum plants. The dataset used in this study comprises pixel-level annotated images containing broad-leaf weeds, grasses, and sorghum samples. Through careful training and validation, the model achieved a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 87.4%, demonstrating strong detection accuracy across multiple classes. The inference results further confirm the model’s ability to reliably identify diverse weed types under varying field conditions. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed approach to enhance precision agriculture, enabling more efficient weed management that promotes sustainability and higher crop productivity in sorghum farming.

title:
10
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    × Improvement in Read Disturbance by Inserting Oxide Barrier Layer for 40nm RRAM Chip
authors: Ruofei Hu, Siyu Chen, Jianshi Tang, Chengxiang Ma, Kaimeng Liu, Jinyi Liu, Jiale Cao, Didi Zhu, Yue Xi, Bin Gao, He Qian, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work proposes the insertion of an oxide barrier layer (OBL) next to the resistive switching layer (RSL) as an effective method to enhance the performance for HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based resistive random-access memory (RRAM) on 40nm CMOS platform. Compared to the baseline film stack, RRAM with OBL achieves 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>× improvement in the read disturbance and 20% larger on/off ratio without sacrificing other key performance metrics including the forming voltage, endurance or retention. Furthermore, the mechanism underlying OBL insertion is thoroughly investigated. This work provides a promising solution for further integration of RRAM towards more advanced technology nodes.

title: Sleep Stages Detection from Multimodal Polysomnographic Sleep Recordings
authors: Md Ashraful Alam Tamim, Fairoj Tasnim Ruhi, Syed Chinaul Islam, Tanzeem Tahmeed Reza, Md. Taisirul Muktadi, Md. Fazlul Karim Khandoker
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate sleep stage classification is important in diagnosis and monitoring of numerous sleep disorders. In this paper, we present a research proposal, which is a multimodal structure of sleep stage detection using automated methods with the Sleep-EDF Expanded data. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Fpz}-\text{Cz}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Pz}-\text{Oz}$</tex> EEG signals were converted to three complementary time-frequency representations Spectrograms through Short Time Fourier Transform (STFT), Continuous Wavelet Transforms (CWT) and HilbertHuang Transforms (HHT). All the representations were processed by a hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture that incorporated the spatial feature extraction properties of CNN with the properties of the Transformer to predict the temporal relationships. Also, a multi-branch network is constructed to be able to process different input representations simultaneously. The model combines these properties into a composite predictor which is much better in terms of generalisation and robustness. The experimental findings prove better results on six stages of sleep (Wake, N1, N2, N3, N4 and REM) with spectrogram, CWT, HHT, and multi-input models accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$92.49 \%, 97.00 \%, 97.30 \%$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 7 9 \%}$</tex>, respectively. These results highlight the effectiveness of multimodal fusion and hybrid designs in the capture of complementary spectral characteristics which facilitate efficient and interpretable identification of sleep stages.

title: Attention-Guided Blood Cell Analysis: A Swin Transformer Framework for Automated Hematologic Cancer Detection
authors: Ila Singh, Shagun Gehlot, Ritu Singh, Rashi Tanwar, Nitika Verma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study presents a Swin Transformer–based framework enhanced with GAN augmentation for robust, interpretable blood-cell classification. Using the public Blood Cell Images for Cancer Detection dataset (5,000 RGB images across five leukocyte classes), the training corpus was expanded to 7,500 images via StyleGAN2-ADA, retaining realism with FID ≤ 30 and improving intra-class variability. A Swin-Tiny backbone with hierarchical, window-based self-attention captures local nuclear/cytoplasmic cues and global morphology. To emphasize diagnostically salient regions and handle hard examples, the pipeline integrates a dual-branch attention fusion module and class-balanced focal loss. The model was trained for 44 epochs; the best checkpoint (epoch 25) achieved a validation loss of 0.10. On a held-out test set of 1,125 images (225/class), the method attained 92.17% overall accuracy, with class-wise precision/recall ≈ 0.90–0.93 and macro/weighted F1 = 0.922, indicating balanced generalization across all classes.

title: Performance Enhancement of Bilayer Threshold Switching Memristors by Modulating Conductive Filament Formation
authors: Zhejia Zhang, Pengtao Li, Qi Luo, Xuemeng Fan, Guobin Zhang, Zijian Wang, Kun Ren, Yishu Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Due to the random growth of metallic conductive filaments, key electrical parameters of metal cation-based threshold switching (TS) memristors fluctuate randomly, greatly affecting their practical applications. Here, we propose a bilayer Ag/ZnO/HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Pt structure. Introducing a thin HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer between the dielectric and inert electrode effectively inhibits Ag excessive injection, thus enabling effective control of filament growth. Compared with the single-layer Ag/ZnO/Pt, the bilayer device has lower off-state current and smaller coefficient of variation (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">V</inf>) of threshold voltage. This work offers new insights for realizing high-uniformity TS devices.

title: A Transformer-Based Framework for Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction with Region-Aware Representation and Temporal Encoding
authors: Spandan Basu, Barshon Sen, Tanvir Anjom Siddique, Kanchon Gharami
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) are a promising substitute for conventional human-driven systems and represent a new paradigm in transportation. Trajectory prediction stands out as a crucial element for guaranteeing safe and effective navigation among the many difficulties in AV development. Many of the methods that have been suggested involve considerable computational cost and complexity. This research work focuses on the motion forecasting benchmark and uses the Argoverse 1.1 dataset to provide a straightforward yet efficient motion forecasting algorithm. The proposed method lowers complexity without sacrificing efficiency by introducing a number of significant advancements. To improve contextual knowledge, a region-based modeling approach has been imposed instead of forecasting individual vehicle trajectories. Secondly, the complexity of training and inference is reduced by using a single transformer-based architecture rather than multiple models. Thirdly, a positional encoding approach that is both efficient and lightweight is provided, successfully representing the spatial linkages and interactions between the agent and nearby cars. Unlike state-of-the-art approaches that struggle with high complexity and limited generalization, our proposed model effectively overcomes these limitations. Using common measures such as Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Average Displacement Error (ADE), and Final Displacement Error (FDE), the proposed methodology is compared to the most advanced techniques. According to experimental data, a much reduced model complexity is maintained while competitive or superior performance is achieved across all criteria.

title: Simulation and measurement framework for bridging QE and PDP in UV–VIS FSI SPADs
authors: Mathieu Vandwalle, Anton Greve, Daniel Gaebler, Mirjam Skadell, Hannes Schmidt, Alexander Zimmer, Vinoothini AP Jeya Kumar, Ong Yi Ling
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work presents a simulation and measurement methodology for modeling the Photon Detection Probability (PDP) of Front-Side Illuminated (FSI) CMOS Single-Photon Avalanche Diodes (SPADs) across the ultraviolet-visible (UV–VIS) range, achieving over 40% PDP between 375 nm and 465 nm with 2 V of excess bias above avalanche breakdown. Accurate PDP predictions across temperatures, voltages, and process variations have been achieved by combining modeled absorption profiles, measured Quantum Efficiency (QE), and avalanche breakdown simulations without relying on fitting parameters.

title: Quantum Computing Applications to Enhance Cryptographic Techniques for Cybersecurity Decision Systems
authors: Deepak Dasaratha Rao, Reghunath K, Senduru Srinivasulu, Monalisa Panda, Ramkumar Krishnamoorthy, Anshul Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Quantum computing has introduced both new and intriguing possibilities and challenges to the cybersecurity industry. Quantum algorithms pose a threat to conventional encryption methods since they can optimize search queries (Grover's algorithm) or factor integers (Shor's algorithm), two formerly difficult operations. Quantum technologies also offer ways to secure data and communications, making them impenetrable to prying eyes. In this study, we look at quantum computing's possibilities in cybersecurity, first as a substitute for traditional cryptographic techniques and second as a way to speed up the development of solutions that are resistant to or even better than quantum-level attacks. Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) further strengthens encryption by automatically detecting eavesdropping and using keys generated by Quantum Random Number Generation (QRNG). Organizations construct future-proof infrastructures that improve threat detection, secure data transfer, and safeguard sensitive information by integrating these cryptographic technologies based on quantum theory into their cybersecurity decision systems. Intentionally adjusting cybersecurity frameworks to integrate quantum technologies while lowering new dangers is emphasized in the study.

title: Exploring Efficient Circuits and Systems for Acceleration of TinyML Applications
authors: Enas Alzoubi, Mahmoud Masadeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) devices continue to drive the development of Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML)12. This is a way to produce advanced artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities on minimal hardware edge products. This work provides a systematic examination of the critical constraints and co-optimization strategies necessary for AI acceleration in TinyML applications. The work focuses particularly on the basic characteristics as well as the strict restrictions of TinyML, e.g., sever memory shortages, low computing power, and tight battery operations with no margin for error. This work systematically analyzes a spectrum of hardware architectures, including the Microcontroller Units (MCUs), Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs), Application Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs), Graphic Processing Units (GPUs) and Neural Processing Units (NPUs), comparing their advantages, disadvantages, and energy efficiency-related indicators.In addition, we studied a variety of energy-efficient techniques, ranging from compression (quantization, pruning, distillation, sharing parameters) and lightweight design principles such as Neural Architecture Search to depth-separable convolutions. To provide a deeper understanding of the trade-offs inherent in TinyML system design, this paper proposes a novel metric: the Power-Constrained Efficiency (PCE) Score, to objectively benchmark hardware-model co-design suitability. A key theme is the critical interaction and co-optimization strategies between Al models and hardware designs, highlighting the trend toward holistic development paradigms. Our quantitative analysis, based on a 1 mW power-constrained benchmark, demonstrates that Neural Processing Units (NPUs) achieve up to an 8.16× higher PCE score compared to a general-purpose MCU reference model when utilizing aggressive 4-bit quantization. We also review some of the main real-life applications that have benefited from TinyML acceleration, including speech and vision systems, health monitoring, and industrial automation. Finally, it raises critical concerns, such as the heterogeneity of hardware and software and the lack of uniform standards.
