This system enhances outcomes for both patients and healthcare professionals-patients benefit from improved adherence and better health, while healthcare providers can remotely monitor compliance, enabling early intervention and reducing complications from non-adherence. This article explores the technology behind smart pharmaceutical packaging, its implications for healthcare, and the challenges and opportunities in its implementation.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Mango Diseases Using InceptionResNetV2: A Robust Approach for Agricultural Diagnostics
authors: Chaitanya Singla, T. Roseline Velankanni, K Chokkanathan, S. Dhivya, Samridhi Sharma, Manik Rakhra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In this study, the InceptionResNetV2 deep learning model is applied for the classification of mango diseases to improve agricultural practices by accurately identifying diseases. A dataset of 862 images was utilized, encompassing five classes: Black Mould Rot, Alternaria, Anthracnose, Healthy, and Stem End Rot. Results show that the model resulted in an overall accuracy of 94%, successfully identifying healthy and diseased mangoes. A training and validation process showed a large drop in loss, indicating successful learning, and a confusion matrix was used to find which labels were misclassified. High Precision and recall rates were noted in the classification report, especially for the Healthy class. Deep learning techniques are shown to be a promising tool for agricultural diagnostics, with the presented research demonstrating their potential to reliably identify mango diseases for farmers and researchers. This future work can be carried on by integrating other data sources to improve model robustness and also by investigating more advanced architectures for better classification accuracy over the varied datasets.

title: Resilient Load Frequency Control Against DoS Attacks Using Hybrid LSTM-PID Defense Scheme
authors: Md. Faiyaj Ahmed Limon, Ifte Khairul Amin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The increasing reliance of modern power systems on cyber-physical infrastructures makes Load Frequency Control (LFC) particularly vulnerable to cyberattacks such as Denial of Service (DoS). This paper presents a novel hybrid defense methodology that synergizes a Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)-tuned Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based anomaly detector to enhance resilience under adversarial conditions. A comprehensive dataset generated from a two-area interconnected power system under sequential load disturbances was used to train the LSTM network for robust ACE signal estimation. The proposed architecture employs an intelligent switch mechanism that replaces compromised ACE signals with LSTM-predicted values during DoS attacks, ensuring continuous and stable control action. Simulation results demonstrate that while DoS attacks cause severe frequency deviations (up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.25$</tex> p.u. in Area 1 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-0.16$</tex> p.u. in Area 2) and tie-line power oscillations (up to 0.035 p.u.) in conventional systems, the hybrid framework achieves superior performance in mitigating these effects. The LSTM-based detector successfully estimates missing ACE signals, reducing frequency fluctuations and maintaining system stability, confirming the effectiveness of integrating intelligent anomaly detection with traditional control strategies for improving the robustness and security of cyber-physical power systems.

title: Simple Controller Designs for Bio-Medical Applications
authors: Petros Tolis, Julia Nako, Costas Psychalinos
abstract: Simple designs of controllers which are suitable for biomedical applications of glucose level and dose of irinotecan control are presented in this work. Both integer and non-integer-order proportional-integral controllers are considered with the last ones approximated by an appropriate curve-fitting based method. The resulting rational integer-order impedance function is realized using a Foster type-I RC network. The behavior of the resulting schemes is evaluated through the employment of the OrCAD PSpice software.

title: Hybrid ML/DL Framework for Arthritis Risk and Severity Prediction
authors: Sanjeewa Sandeepani, Shehan Amarasooriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Arthritis is a common chronic disease with significant consequences for the quality of life among older populations. Early diagnosis of arthritis can help in preserving positive health outcomes through mitigating the progression of the disease. In this study, a hybrid predictive framework was introduced that takes individual lifestyle parameters along with the radiographic imaging of the patient as parameters that have not been studied in the Sri Lankan context before. Data were collected from Gampaha Wickramarachchi Ayurveda Hospital, and the data were split into two main data sets: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 0 0}$</tex> records of lifestyle factors and clinical manifestations, and 598 diagnostic X-radiography images of knee joints. Supervised Machine Learning algorithms have been applied to the textual dataset, and the best validation accuracy of 96 % for the prediction of the likelihood of Arthritis by employing the Random Forest (RF) classifier. Patients at high risk were graded into three groups using deep learning architectures (ResNet-50 and a convolutional neural network). CNN validation accuracy was 95 % and 97 % for training accuracy. The outcomes of this research have been operationalized through a web application that integrates the two models, enabling arthritis risk prediction based on lifestyle and symptom data, followed by severity classification through X-ray analysis when risk is detected.

title: Hybrid Open-Switch Fault Diagnosis in 3-Phase T-Type Inverter
authors: Jeevan N D, Niraj Kumar Dewangan, Krishna Kumar Gupta, Sunil K Singla, Pallavee Bhatnagar, Karthik B M
abstract: Multilevel inverters (MLIs) are extensively used in renewable energy systems, grid-connected converters, and industrial drives due to their superior power quality and reduced harmonic distortion. However, as the number of semiconductor switches increases, the probability of switch failures increases drastically. This may lead to a reduction in the reliability of the system. This paper presents a hybrid fault diagnosis approach for open circuit switch faults in a three-phase five-level T-type MLI based on ensemble empirical mode decomposition. The normalized output voltage signals are decomposed into intrinsic mode functions, from which kurtosis and energy entropy features are extracted to capture fault-related characteristics. For fault classification, four machine learning techniques: Decision Tree, Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting, and Gaussian Naive Bayes are used and evaluated. Among them, the RF classifier achieves the highest accuracy of 99.61% and is employed for the online localization of individual switch OCFs. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method for practical power conversion applications.

title: Performance Evaluation of an Erlang Loss System with Server Failures and a Single Repair Facility
authors: Konstantinos Lolis, Marinos Vlasakis, Ioannis Moscholios, Irene Keramidi, Dimitris Uzunidis, Georgios Bouloukakis, Nikolaos Tselikas, Michael Logothetis
abstract: Loss models of fixed capacity constitute a fundamental tool in teletraffic theory, with the classical Erlang loss model being widely used for dimensioning purposes. However, practical communication systems often experience time-varying capacity due to server failures and repairs, which significantly affects Call Blocking Probabilities (CBP). This paper studies an Erlang loss system where busy servers may fail. Failed servers are repaired by a single shared repair facility, while in-service calls are lost upon server failure. Three different approaches for determining CBP are examined. The first one provides exact results through the solution of a 2D Markov chain but becomes computationally demanding for large systems. The second one, known as the performability method, offers a simple approximation but allows failures of idle servers. The third approach employs state aggregation and restricts failures to busy servers. Through analytical comparisons, we evaluate the accuracy of the two approximate methods against the exact solution. Results show that the third approach consistently outperforms the performability method.

title: Real-Time Performance Prediction Using Tiny Machine Learning in Advanced Catalytic Systems
authors: Hakan Karasu, Veysi Adın, Rashid Ali, Fredrik Sikström
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Electrochemical CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> reduction requires real-time monitoring to assess catalyst efficiency and detect degradation patterns, yet current approaches rely on resource-intensive computing infrastructure. Here we present a lightweight machine learning model deployed on resource-constrained microcontrollers for real-time performance prediction. We developed a one-dimensional convolutional neural network with $\mathbf{1 1, 9 2 3}$ parameters that simultaneously predicts total current, CO Faradaic efficiency and $\mathbf{H}_{\mathbf{2}}$ Faradaic efficiency from linear sweep voltammetry data, achieving mean absolute errors of $0.038 \mathrm{~A}, 1.97 \%$ and 1.97%, respectively. Systematic comparison with a traditional convolutional neural network architecture and an attentionenhanced variant demonstrates that the proposed model achieves comparable prediction accuracy with significantly reduced model complexity. Post-training quantization compressed the model to 22.31 kB, enabling deployment on ESP32-S3 and Arduino Nano 33 BLE microcontrollers. Our work establishes the feasibility of accurate, real-time electrochemical performance prediction for autonomous experimental monitoring in resource-limited environments.

title: Voting-Based Feature Selection on Hybrid Feature Representations for Bangla Sentiment Analysis
authors: Anjan Debnath, Al-Mahmud
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Understanding public opinion on social media and other digital platforms has made sentiment analysis of Bangla text increasingly crucial. However, challenges, including highdimensional feature spaces, data sparsity, and language complexity, continue to restrict performance in this field. This study presents a hybrid approach that captures the semantic and statistical properties of Bangla text by combining BanglaBERT embeddings and TF-IDF features. To overcome dimensionality and redundancy, high-rank features are identified using a votingbased feature selection method that integrates mutual information, ANOVA F-test, and XGBoost feature importance. Principal component analysis (PCA) is also assessed for comparison. Then, this study applies several classifiers, including KNN, DT, RF, and LR, LightGBM, and MLP, to evaluate performance. Among them, the MLP classifier achieved the maximum accuracy of 83.16% and F1-score of 0.83. According to experimental findings on a benchmark dataset of Bangla drama and TV series reviews, the proposed method outperforms a recent existing study. To this end, this study demonstrates that constructing high-rank features from hybrid representations under the direction of voting-based selection offers a solid foundation for sentiment analysis in Bangla.

title: Safety-Critical IoMT Security: A Lightweight, Cost-Aware Approach at the Network Edge
authors: Md. Asraful Islam Khan, Md Mahinur Alam, Md Sakibul Islam, Kanita Jerin Tanha, Sadia Salma, Mohammad Mofasserul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Hospital networks require intrusion detection systems that are both practical and accurate, especially in situations where computational resources are scarce. For the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), this study suggests an inexpensive and lightweight IDS. The technique uses Random Forest for top-K feature selection, then a compact CNN-LSTM architecture. A contextual-bandit Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm is used in a final fine-tuning stage. It is guided by a reward matrix that penalizes missed attacks more severely than false alarms. System operators can adjust detection priorities based on clinical risk levels thanks to this design. The suggested method achieved 96.79 % accuracy, a macro-F1 score of 0.9242, a Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC) of 0.9557, and an expected calibration error (ECE) of 0.0336 when tested on the CIC-IoMT 2024 dataset. With an average CPU latency of 0.71 ms per network flow, the model is only 0.147 MB in size. The percentage of attacks incorrectly classified as benign decreased to 1.67 % when the penalty for missed detections was increased. With a latency of 0.74 ms and a model size of 0.146 MB, the system achieved 97.95 % accuracy, 0.9524 macro-F1, 0.9056 MCC, and 0.0140 ECE in binary classification. The false-positive rate was 5.13 %, and the false-negative rate was 1.64 %. These findings show that, without increasing model size or computation time, cost-conscious training techniques can significantly lower missed detections. The system is appropriate for edge deployment and clinical evaluation due to its small size and well-calibrated outputs.

title: Dual Band Polarization Insensitive Metamaterialbased Em Absorber for 5G EMI Shielding
authors: Nishan Das, M. H. Mahmud Sajib, Md. Mirajul Islam, Touhidul Alam, Mohammad Tariqul Islam, Rezaul Azim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This article proposes a dual-band metamaterialbased absorber (MMA) for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> Sub-6 GHz electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding, demonstrating absorption at 3.5 GHz and 4.6 GHz. Designed on a low-cost FR4 substrate with electrical dimensions of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.25 \lambda \times 0.25 \lambda \times 0.013 \lambda$</tex> (where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda$</tex> is calculated at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 5 G H z}$</tex>), the MMA achieves maximum absorptions of 90 % and 99.5 % through a resonating patch consisting of two concentric circular copper rings. Simulations were performed using CST Microwave Studio 2025, revealing angular stability of 0 to 30 degrees in both TE and TM modes. The MMA exhibits negative permittivity over the frequency ranges <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3}. \mathbf{4}-\mathbf{3}. \mathbf{6} \text{GHz}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}. \mathbf{5}-\mathbf{4}. 7 \text{GHz}$</tex>, along with corresponding near-zero permeability, ensuring its single-negative (SN) characteristics. This MMA can be integrated into various electronic devices or systems for effective EMI shielding against 3.5 GHz and 4.6 GHz signals.

title: Federated Contrastive Learning for Privacy-Preserving ECG Arrhythmia Classification
authors: Md. Labib, Md Foysal Rahaman, Ahammed Ekrak Utsha, Shadman Sakib Faruki, Sharfuddin Mahmood
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate detection of ECG arrhythmias plays a critical role in early diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases, yet traditional methods that rely on centralized data aggregation raise concerns about patient privacy and data sharing regulations. This study proposes a novel framework that integrates federated learning and contrastive learning to address these challenges in a decentralized setting. The proposed approach enables multiple healthcare clients to collaboratively train deep models without sharing sensitive data, while simultaneously leveraging contrastive learning to enhance the quality of ECG feature representations. Experiments are conducted on a public ECG arrhythmia classification dataset using five non-IID data partitions reflecting various class imbalance scenarios. The results demonstrate that the federated contrastive learning framework achieves competitive classification performance, with an F1-score of 0.9789 in the fully balanced setting and improved recall in imbalanced scenarios, highlighting its robustness in detecting minority classes. Key techniques such as client-specific augmentations, mixup regularization, and federated batch normalization contribute to improved generalization under data heterogeneity. This work provides empirical insights into the convergence behavior, representational quality, and privacy-preserving benefits of contrastive objectives in federated environments. The proposed solution shows strong potential for scalable and secure deployment in realworld clinical settings where data diversity and privacy are paramount.

title: Exploring Compute-in-Memory Architecture with Various Peripheral Circuits and Neural Networks
authors: Jonghyun Ko, Jiseong Im, Jong-Ho Lee
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Compute-in-memory (CIM) stores weights in nonvolatile memory to perform vector–matrix multiplications (VMMs) via Ohm’s and Kirchhoff’s laws, reducing energy and latency. We present a comprehensive and comparative study of CIM architectures employing different peripheral circuits and neural network schemes. Fixing the nonvolatile memory array (AND-type flash) and technology node (45 nm), we evaluate four CIM architectures on a 9-layer conventional DNN (CIFAR-10) and a spiking DNN. By replacing ADCs with PWMs in only the first two layers, we reduce dynamic energy by 18.5 % and area by 19.8 % with no throughput loss. Substituting LIF neurons yields a 14.9 % energy saving at the cost of a small area overhead. ADC-based baseline achieves 87.05 % accuracy on the DNN and 81.85 % on the spiking DNN, while hybrid schemes reduce quantization loss.

title: Integrating Country Clustering with Hybrid Graph-Spectral Forecasting of Agri-Food $\text{CO}_{2}$ Emissions: A Bangladesh Case Study
authors: Md. Mamun Hossain, Nusrat Kaniz Khan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: We propose a unified framework that integrates advanced hybrid forecasting with country-level clustering to model and interpret global agri-food <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions. At its core is a novel Graph Laplacian-Short-Time Fourier Transform-Conformal Prediction (GL-STFT-CP) model, which leverages graph-based signal processing to capture cross-country interdependencies and STFT-derived spectral embeddings to represent complex temporal patterns. Compared to ARIMA and LSTM baselines, GL-STFT-CP achieves superior predictive accuracy and delivers calibrated multi-step uncertainty intervals, addressing key challenges of nonlinearity, spatial heterogeneity, and forecast reliability. As an illustrative case, we present 10-year forecasts for Bangladesh, where GL-STFT-CP provides tighter, calibrated prediction intervals and more accurate trends than ARIMA. Exploratory analysis also contrasts Bangladesh with the South Asia region, contextualizing national trajectories within broader dynamics. Building on the global forecasts, we apply hierarchical clustering to multi-decade emission trajectories, revealing two coherent groups: a high-emission, rapidly growing cluster dominated by emerging economies, and a larger group of moderate- or low-growth countries, including many OECD members. Silhouette analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(s \approx 0.91)$</tex> and bootstrap stability tests (ARI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1.0)$</tex> confirm the robustness of this partitioning. Finally, we derive differentiated policy implications: high-growth countries need efficiency and low-carbon practices, while mature economies can focus on technology diffusion and refining existing practices. By linking advanced forecasting with clustering-based diagnostics, we demonstrate how an integrated pipeline yields data-driven, cluster-specific insights for climate policy and sustainable agriculture planning.

title: Exploring Gemini AI’s Text-to-Image Capabilities for Adaptive Learning in Education
authors: Ronel F. Ramos, Roman M. De Angel, Juan Paulo H. Magcuyao, Alfredo L. Calimbo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping education by enabling adaptive and personalized experiences. This study explores the integration of Gemini AI’s text-to-image generation into an adaptive learning environment for undergraduate BSIT students. Using a mixed-method developmental design with 100 participants, we analyzed learning gains through pre/post testing, motivation via the IMMS survey, interaction telemetry, and focus group discussions (FGDs). Quantitative analyses revealed substantial improvement from pre-test to post-test, with a significant paired t-test and an ANOVA indicating differences across performance subgroups. Post-hoc comparisons (Tukey HSD) suggested that lower-performing learners benefited most, pointing to the potential of visual scaffolds to narrow achievement gaps. Qualitative findings supported these results, highlighting enhanced conceptual understanding, stronger motivation through co-creation, and increased teacher trust when explainability accompanied AI-generated visuals. Benchmarking showed a trade-off: Gemini provided higher-fidelity visuals suited to complex concepts, while a lightweight variant (NanoBanana) offered lower latency and broader device accessibility. A hybrid routing approach is conceptually recommended to balance fidelity and speed; however, hybrid evaluation was proposed but not executed and is reserved for future empirical testing. Overall, the findings support text-to-image generation as a practical means to strengthen adaptive learning and advance inclusive, equitable quality education aligned with SDG4.

title: Predicting Wildfire Burn Severity from Pre-Fire SAR Signatures: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Kris Mannino, Deb Zani Deb, Karen An
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Climate-driven increases in wildfire frequency and severity necessitate improved prediction capabilities. Existing models rely on real-time atmospheric data and often overfit to specific conditions, while pre-fire landscape assessment remains underdeveloped. This study addresses whether static landscape features captured before ignition can predict burn severity patterns. We developed a deep learning framework using L-band polarimetric SAR data from NASA’s UAVSAR to predict four-class burn severity from pre-fire conditions alone. Using the 2020 Bobcat Fire (115,997 acres, Southern California), we integrated six features: HH, HV, and VV polarizations, HH/HV ratio, Alpha 1 Angle from Cloude-Pottier decomposition, and terrain slope. A U-Net convolutional neural network with patch-based semantic segmentation achieved $51 \%$ accuracy (F1-macro: 0.48, ROC-AUC: 0.74-0.82), representing $2.04 \times$ improvement over random baseline and substantially outperforming pixel-wise methods (Random Forest: $\mathbf{0 . 3 6}$, XGBoost: $\mathbf{0 . 3 7}$). The model achieves $\mathbf{6 4 \%}$ recall for high-severity areas, prioritizing detection sensitivity over precision for risk screening applications. Results establish that pre-fire SAR features encode meaningful information about potential burn severity, providing a foundation for integration with real-time fire weather models. The July 2025 launch of NASA-ISRO SAR (NISAR), offering systematic 12-day global L-band coverage, makes this approach particularly timely for operational wildfire management.

title: A novel, multistep neural network approach to improve anomaly detection in online operational status
authors: Aditya Singla, Vamanie Perumal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: With the instances of service disruptions from large-scale cloud systems such as IBM, the user experience is negatively impacted, and consequences are increased without accurately and quickly identifying these anomalous instances. The integration of automated screening processes can aid in the accuracy and time taken to predict these anomalies and become aware of the issue at hand. Our study aims to provide a robust solution to anomaly detection in cloud infrastructure systems. We used a multistep optimization and filtering pipeline to improve upon preliminary results from Islam et. al, who provided the dataset we used in our anomaly detection process. We were able to achieve significantly greater results because we managed to avoid weaknesses presented by a single neural network structure, presenting an argument for a more widespread use of such layered approaches. We successfully predicted $\mathbf{1 6 / 2 5}$ ground truth anomalies labeled in the dataset, improving upon the initial 6/25 anomalies predicted in the initial research; we also attained a greater ratio of true positives to false positives in our final results. Cloud-based systems can be positively impacted by the incorporation of the kind of deep learning approach presented in this research, as it can help with preliminary anomaly detection so that operation teams can be informed of suspicious time windows.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning and the IID Assumption
authors: Fatih Özgan, Herman Engelbrecht, Gregor Schiele
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning algorithms dominate the reinforcement learning research now for more than a decade. Various algorithms based on different theoretical foundations, such as action-value methods, stochastic policy gradient methods and deterministic policy gradient methods address different issues arising in reinforcement learning tasks. Some of these issues are inherent to reinforcement learning and part of its nature, whereas some are induced or amplified by the application of deep learning as a function approximator. This work provides an intuitive example on the importance of independent and identically distributed data in the application of stochastic gradient descent and the consequences of correlation and non-stationarity. This work draws a distinct line between the issues induced by deep learning into reinforcement learning and problems inherent to reinforcement learning.

title: Design and Testing of Internet of Things-Based Monitoring System for a Boiler Prototype
authors: Sumantri Sri Nugroho, Mujiyono Mujiyono, Isana Supiah Yosephine Louise, Khakam Ma’Ruf, Rizal Justian Setiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The Boiler system is an important component in industrial operations, requiring continuous monitoring to ensure both efficiency and safety. Recent advancements in the Internet of Things (IoT) provide innovative opportunities for enhancing conventional boiler monitoring practices. This study seeks to design and develop a prototype IoT-based monitoring system capable of evaluating critical boiler parameters, including water temperature monitoring, boiling process characterization, and evaporation rate measurement, using distilled water as the test medium. The research employs a Research and Development (R&D) approach comprising five stages: IoT monitoring system design, prototype fabrication, hardware–software integration, controlled experimental testing, and data analysis. The system architecture integrates an ESP32 microcontroller, a DS18B20 thermocouple temperature sensor, a load cell sensor, and employs the MQTT protocol to enable real-time data transmission to a monitoring dashboard. Experimental results demonstrate that the system successfully monitors temperature variations from 27.5 °C to 103.4 °C with high precision, records a water mass reduction of 71 grams during an 11-minute boiling process, and consumes 0.5 kWh of energy. The findings indicate that the developed IoT-based monitoring system exhibits stability and accuracy, making it suitable for implementation at the micro, small, and medium enterprise (MSME) scale.

title: EquiTea: An IoT–Machine Learning–Blockchain Integrated System for Transparent Wage Payments in the Tea Industry of Bangladesh
authors: Nishat Tarannum Mim, Tasmia Islam, Md Yak Safu
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The tea sector in Bangladesh has systemic labor violations, late payments, and questionable wage practices that create poor living conditions and financial vulnerability for laborers. This paper proposes EquiTea, an integrated IoT, Machine Learning, and Blockchain-based wage automation system that utilizes facial recognition to identify workers, IoT-based weight measurement of harvested leaves, and smart contracts instantiated on the Ethereum blockchain to calculate wages following transparent and immutable procedures. Payments are directly provided to the workers' digital wallets, offering real-time tracking of wages while resolving disputes, delays, and exploitation. The proposed architecture, which integrates multiple technologies, aims to remediate widespread issues with existing manual systems, including verification, payment delays, and wage misallocation and ensure that the system builds in auditability and security, and will scale for large labor-intensive operations.

title: The Formulation of Rice Straw Nanocellulose-Based Bioplastic Combined with PVA Using the Taguchi
authors: Ahmad Wahyu Setiawan, Aswan Munang, Dina Rachmawaty
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Bioplastic is a type of natural polyester material that can decompose more quickly than petroleum-based polyester. One of the raw materials used to produce bioplastics is plant-derived nanocellulose fiber. Nanocellulose can be extracted from rice straw through a series of processes, including extraction and acid hydrolysis, and then combined with polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) as the main matrix. The plastic solvents used are glycerol, sorbitol, and polyethylene glycol (PEG). The experimental design employed an L9 (3<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup>) Orthogonal Array, involving four key factors: nanocellulose concentration (3%, 5%, 7%), PVA content (2%, 4%, 6%), type of plasticizer (glycerol, sorbitol, PEG), and drying temperatures (40°C, 50°C, 60°C). The results obtained were analyzed using Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) and Signal-to-Noise Ratio (S/N). Analysis to identify the dominant factors and determine the optimal composition for the physical and thermal mechanical properties of the bioplastics. Overall, the application of the Taguchi method proved efficient, as it reduced the number of required experiments from 81 to only 9 combinations without compromising result accuracy. The findings confirmed that rice straw-derived nanocellulose has significant potential as a natural reinforcing agent in the production of high-performance, environmentally friendly bioplastics.

title: Adaptive Density-aware Spectral-Fuzzy Clustering: A Robust Hybrid Framework for High-Dimensional and Non-Convex Data
authors: Anil Kumar Singh, Abhishek Singh, Manik Chandra, Manish Gaur, Shiv Prakash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Clustering remains a fundamental problem in unsupervised learning with persistent challenges in handling noisy data, unknown cluster shapes, and high dimensionality. Building on taxonomies and critical reviews of clustering methods, we propose Adaptive Density-aware Spectral-Fuzzy (ADSF) clustering: a hybrid algorithm that combines local density estimation, spectral embedding, and fuzzy membership refinement to improve robustness on non-convex and noisy datasets. ADSF constructs a density-weighted similarity graph using adaptive neighbourhood radii inferred from local density; computes a spectral embedding that respects the density-weighted graph; and performs fuzzy membership optimization in the embedded space to allow soft cluster assignments and better handling of boundary points. We provide algorithmic details, complexity analysis, and an experimental protocol comparing ADSF to standard baselines on synthetic and benchmark datasets. Results demonstrate improved clustering quality in variable-density and non-convex settings while maintaining competitive runtime. Code and datasets will be released upon publication.

title: Fine-Tuning and Semantic Prompt Enrichment for LLM-Based Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
authors: Francesco Salzano, Marco Guglielmi, Simone Scalabrino, Rocco Oliveto, Remo Pareschi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: This study examines the combined effect of fine-tuning and semantic prompt enrichment on Large Language Model-based vulnerability detection in Solidity smart contracts. We fine-tune ChatGPT-4o through a two-phase process aligned with the DASP Top 10 taxonomy—first to internalize theoretical vulnerability knowledge, then to specialize on labeled Solidity functions. We further enhance the fine-tuned model with automatically generated and human-validated code summaries as semantic enrichments to its prompts. The resulting model achieves an average F1-score of 0.58, a 66% improvement over the baseline (0.35), with the largest gains in Access Control (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+146 \%$</tex>), Denial of Service (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+353 \%$</tex>), and Reentrancy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+35 \%$</tex>) detection. These results show that domain-aligned fine-tuning and semantic prompt enrichment jointly improve the precision and recall of LLM-based smart-contract auditing, offering a practical path toward AI-assisted security analysis.

title: The Role of Community Adaptive Capacity and Policy Flexibility in Rice-Based Food Security: A Systematic Review
authors: Annisa Indah Pratiwi, Elisa Kusrini, Winda Nurcahyo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study systematically reviews the existing body of literature on community adaptive capacity and policy flexibility within the broader framework of rice supply chain resilience. Despite growing scholarly attention to food system sustainability, current studies have yet to propose a comprehensive analytical framework capable of evaluating how government policies respond to dynamic risks and uncertainties across the supply chain. Furthermore, empirical indicators that measure the social endurance and adaptive capacity of institutional agencies in managing challenges such as land-use conversion, climate variability, and supply–demand fluctuations remain limited. To address these gaps, a Systematic Literature Mapping (SLM) coupled with K-diagram analysis was conducted using leading academic databases Scopus, ScienceDirect, and Google Scholar covering publications from 2020 to 2025. This combined approach was designed to identify emerging knowledge trends, thematic gaps, and methodological orientations across relevant peer-reviewed studies. The findings highlight the critical need to formulate adaptive and resilient intervention strategies that incorporate six essential dimensions of food security—availability, access, utilization, stability, agency, and sustainability while accounting for the inherent complexities and uncertainties of contemporary food supply chain systems.

title: 3D Simulations for Fin Geometry Effects on the Linearity and On-Resistance of AlGaN/GaN Multi-Channel Fin-HEMTs
authors: Hui Jiao, Peiran Wang, Xiaowei Ma, Wenchuan Tao, Qing Wang, Hongyu Yu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, the effects of fin geometry on the linearity and on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>) of AlGaN/GaN multi-channel Fin-HEMTs are systematically investigated using three-dimensional technology computer aided design (3D TCAD) simulations. Compared with baseline planar HEMTs (BSL HEMTs), the multichannel Fin-HEMTs exhibit significantly improved linearity and conduction performance. The simulation results further reveal distinct trends in multi-channel Fin-HEMTs characteristics associated with variable fin geometry. Specifically, a larger fin length increases the carrier path leading to increased R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> and degraded linearity. In contrast, narrower fins leads to higher R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> and better linearity, which is attributed to the stronger channel confinement. Alternatively, increasing fin height reduces R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> and better linearity. Finally, the duty ratio (α) strongly influences inter-fin coupling. These findings provide valuable physical insights and practical design guidelines for optimizing the design of GaN-based multi-channel Fin-HEMTs.

title: A SPICE-Compatible Compact Model for Ferroelectric-Gated GaN-HEMTs
authors: Abhishek Kumar, Dariskhem Pyngrope, Avirup Dasgupta
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: We present a compact model for ferroelectric-gated GaN high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs). The model consists of a ferroelectric capacitor model combined with an industry-standard advanced SPICE model for HEMTs to capture the behavior of ferroelectric-gated HEMTs. The proposed model is based on the nucleation-limited switching approach, which captures the statistical multidomain dynamics of ferroelectric materials. We have validated the model with measured data from ferroelectric-gated HEMT devices. The model accurately captures the device behavior.

title: EndoVision: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Endoscopic Image Classification
authors: Prottoy Kundu, Arnob Das Shacha, Sabbir Hossain Durjoy, S.M. Abdullah Al Muhib, Rejowan Arifin Nayeem, Md. Emon Shikder, M. Humayet Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The gastrointestinal (GI) system is an important part of the human body that breaks down food into necessary nutrients and removes the unused portion from the body as feces. This system includes the mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine, liver, gallbladder, and pancreas. If any organ malfunctions, it negatively affects the entire body. Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases are diagnosed correctly at an early stage, and the chances of treatment are greatly increased. These diseases are usually diagnosed using endoscopy technology, but this process is dependent on the experience of the specialist. As a result, small lesions or symptoms are often not detected due to differences in observation, which delays early diagnosis and complicates treatment. Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases such as infections, ulcers, and tumors can be lifethreatening. Although endoscopy is effective in detecting these diseases, it is time-consuming and experience-dependent, as only a few of the thousands of frames contain disease symptoms. Deep learning-based artificial intelligence plays a special role in addressing this challenge. The hybrid stacking ensemble model proposed in this study has shown high accuracy and sensitivity in detecting GI diseases, which helps in unbiased and effective diagnosis. The best performance in our experiments came from the proposed Hybrid model of CNN, LSTM and ViT, which attained 97.67% accuracy. These outputs are considerably higher than those of single models, including ViT (accuracy of 93.97%), CNN (90.37%), and LSTM (80.07%), and other networks, including DenseNet121, GAT, GCN, InceptionV3, and MobileNet. This high performance indicates that our method is very reliable for detecting gastrointestinal diseases based on endoscopic imaging, facilitating less demand for manual detection and higher possibility of earliest diagnosis.

title: A Novel S-FLOD-CD-FDTD Method for Simulating Stochastic Media
authors: Zhi Wang, Guilin Hou, Guoda Xie, Xingang Ren, Gang Wang, Zhixiang Huang, Xianliang Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A novel and efficient stochastic fundamental locally one-dimensional finite-difference time-domain method with a complying divergence property (S-FLOD-CD-FDTD) is proposed for simulating the electromagnetic (EM) behavior of complex stochastic media. In this approach, based on stochastic field theory, key material parameters—such as relative permittivity and conductivity—are treated as stochastic variables with prescribed probability distributions and integrated into the discretized FLOD-FDTD framework. This leads to the development of a numerical iteration scheme tailored for the S-FLOD-FDTD method. To ensure consistency with Gauss’s law during the numerical discretization of Maxwell’s equation, an explicit complying divergence output strategy is introduced, completing the formulation of the S-FLOD-CD-FDTD computational model. The unconditional stability and complying divergence properties of the proposed method are systematically analyzed, providing theoretical support for its feasibility. Finally, numerical experiments involving various stochastic media are conducted to validate the accuracy and efficiency of the proposed approach in comparison with the traditional S-FDTD method.

title: A Deformation-Aware Structure-Integrated Registration Method for Floating Macroalgae Images from Multi-Source Satellite Sensors
authors: Guodong Ding, Jinhe Hu, Wei Xue, Dongxue Li, Peisong Jia
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: High-precision monitoring of floating macroalgae blooms is of great ecological and economic significance, and accurate multi-source satellite image registration of floating macroalgae serves as a critical technical prerequisite for achieving such monitoring. However, due to the floating-induced deformation of floating macroalgae itself, coupled with the differences arising from the varying resolutions of different satellite sensors, existing registration methods fail to achieve accurate registration. To address this challenge, we propose an innovative registration method that integrates rigid and non-rigid registration modules. First, we perform a simple and efficient rigid registration for floating macroalgae based on their Difference Vegetation Index (DVI). On this basis, to achieve more accurate non-rigid registration of floating macroalgae images, we designed a Deformation-Aware Structure (DAS) block that can efficiently identify deformations. Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our method across various scales, including (10m, 16m), (10m, 30m) and (10m, 50m). Compared with the other existing methods, our approach demonstrates higher accuracy and better adaptability.

title: Plug-and-Play Architecture for Non-Identical Dual-Parallel PMSMs: A Hybrid Learning Control Approach
authors: Tianyi Liu, Ziyao Wang, Rongfu Lin, Ziqi Jia, Haichen Li, Xiaoyan Ma
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: The mono-inverter dual-parallel permanent magnet synchronous motor (MIDP) drive system is a cost-effective solution for compact multi-motor applications, but existing control schemes suffer from strict constraints of identical motor parameters and precise prior parameter knowledge, limiting their practical implementation. To address this challenge, a novel plug and-play control architecture integrated with hybrid learning and adaptive control is proposed for non-identical MIDP systems in this paper. First, a strict theoretical proof based on the Unknown Input Observer (UIO) error dynamic model is presented, verifying that the reconstructed signals of the supplementary motor are independent of its inductance and flux linkage under steady-state conditions, which decouples signal reconstruction and parameter identification. On this basis, a two-stage hybrid parameter learning mechanism is designed to circumvent the rank-deficiency problem such that the stator resistance is identified at standstill, and the inductance and rotor flux linkage are estimated online by the Recursive Least Squares (RLS) with a forgetting factor. Furthermore, the non-master-slave control strategy is extended with a newly derived stability criterion to accommodate non identical motors. Comprehensive experiments covering start-up, sudden speed fluctuations, and extreme unbalanced load torques are conducted on a hardware platform with two non-identical PMSMs. Experimental results show that the proposed architecture enables plug-and-play operation of the significantly improving the flexibility and practicality of the MIDP drive system.

title: Eco-Friendly and Explainable Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Sustainable Crop and Fertilizer Recommendation System_supp1-3688560.pdf
authors: Abhirup Paria
abstract: This work proposes a sustainable crop and fertilizer recommendation system using eco-friendly hybrid machine learning (EHML) and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) models. The integration of EHML with XAI is introduced for the first time in the crop and fertilizer recommendation system. This work focuses on crop recommendations solely based on the micro and macro nutrients present in the soil, an aspect that has been largely overlooked till date. Based on various hybrid machine learning (HML) models such as CatBoost and Extreme Gradient Boosting, optimized with Sparrow Search Algorithm, Phasor Particle Swarm Optimization, and Grey Wolf Optimization, crops are recommended with accuracy ranging from 99.64% to 99.87%. The state-of-the-art work is also compared with hybrid models optimized with standard optimizers, such as Genetic Algorithm and Particle Swarm Optimization. To reveal critical drivers of crop selection and enhance the interpretability of the best-performed models, XAI algorithms (Local Interpretable Model Explanation and SHapley Additive exPlanations) are employed. The best-performing eco-friendly model is also recommended based on low <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${CO}_{2}eq$</tex-math></inline-formula> emissions and electricity consumption, helping to preserve natural resources. Additionally, a farmer-friendly web application is developed to provide instant crop and fertilizer recommendations for sustainable agriculture. Finally, the recommended fertilizers are also validated using real-time field data collected via IoT sensors.

title: Frequency Compounding Cross Spectral Matrix Fitting for Passive Cavitation Imaging
authors: Célestine Lachambre, Bruno Gilles, Jean-Christophe Béra, François Varray, Barbara Nicolas, Adrian Basarab
abstract: High-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) can induce cavitation, which requires monitoring for applications such as sonoporation, targeted drug delivery, and histotripsy. Passive acoustic mapping has been proposed as a cavitation monitoring method, but it suffers from limited axial resolution and coherent artifacts, particularly in non-inertial cavitation for the latter, leading to potential mislocalization of the cavitation cloud. Previous work introduced the Cross-Spectral Matrix Fitting (CMF), a regularized inverse problem approach, to improve axial resolution. Since coherence artifact positions vary with frequency, a common strategy is to mitigate them by summing maps from different frequencies. In this study, rather than summing frequency-specific maps, we propose Weighted Frequency Compounding-Cross-Spectral Matrix Fitting (WFC-CMF), which integrates multiple frequencies directly into the inverse problem and applies regularization to the reconstructed solution. To enhance robustness against outliers, we incorporate a Lorentzian M-estimator. Compared to Delay-and-Sum, WFC-CMF improves the Dice score by up to 35 points and outperforms summed-frequency CMF (sCMF) up to 14 points.

title: PLUM: A Prompt-Guided Lightweight Unified Model for Enhancement of Multidegraded Underwater Image
authors: Alik Pramanick, Akshay Daydar, Sonal Kumar, Arijit Sur, V. Vijaya Saradhi
abstract: Degradation in underwater images is commonly caused by haze, low contrast, blur, and color distortion, which can hinder the effectiveness of many computer vision tasks. Most of the existing image enhancement works treat these degradations separately, thus limiting their applicability. To handle this problem, we propose a unified model to learn multiple degradations concurrently, improving resilience to practical conditions. First, we generate various degraded versions (haze, blur, low-contrast, and color distortion) of the input degraded image through domain translation. Next, original degraded and translated images are fed into a novel multidegraded enhancement network, which employs multiple encoders with an intermediate feature-swapping mechanism and a single decoder. Each encoder block enhances the network’s ability to characterize internal relationships in spatial and frequency domains by incorporating the fast Fourier transform. Intermediate feature swapping encourages the model to learn more robust representations. The decoder block contains the proposed prompt-guided spatial-frequency cross-attention block to encode degraded visual information effectively. Extensive experiments on several underwater image enhancement data sets demonstrate that our model outperforms the state-of-the-art methods with less computational cost (e.g., 1.20% structural similarity index boost on UIEB data set with 84.1% less parameter).

title: Decoupled Modeling and Direct Power Distribution Control of Multisource Inverter Via Double Synchronous Coordinate Frame
authors: Dehong Zhou, Jiayu Qi, Jianxiao Zou, Zewei Shen, Xin Liu
abstract: The multisource inverter (MSI) is a high-efficiency interface for hybrid electric vehicles due to the removal of intermediate dc-dc converters. However, it is challenging to simultaneously achieve motor control on the ac side and power distribution on the dc side in the MSI, due to the absence of dc-dc converters. Given this, this paper proposes a decoupled modeling and direct power distribution control scheme of MSI via double synchronous coordinate frame (DSCF) to realize power flow control on the dc side and motor drive control on the ac side simultaneously. In the proposed scheme, a decoupled model with DSCF is established, where the MSI is considered as two sub-inverters, each managed by a synchronous coordinate frame. Direct power distribution schemes are developed by employing the extra degree of freedom provided by DSCF, where the motor drive control on the ac side is achieved by controlling the sum of dq-components of synchronous coordinates, and the power distribution control on the dc side is achieved by controlling dq-components of the sub-inverters. With the proposed scheme, both motor drive control on the ac side and power distribution control on the dc side are regulated in direct current quantity, simplifying the MSI control scheme design and enabling rapid dynamic performance. The principles, implementation, and power distribution range of the proposed scheme are analyzed in detail. Finally, the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed schemes are demonstrated by experimental results.

title: Software-Hardware Co-Optimized High-Throughput CNN Acceleration Architecture for on-board Remote Sensing Based on Distribution Difference-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization
authors: Haitao Chen, He Chen, Ning Zhang, Tong Wang, Shuo Ni, Liang Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have demonstrated outstanding performance in various remote sensing intelligent tasks in recent years. However, in scenarios such as on-board emergency response, second-level on-board processing is required to achieve both low latency and high accuracy, while the limited resources and power constraints of on-board platforms make it difficult to process the large-scale parameters and intensive computation of CNNs efficiently on-chip. To address the aforementioned issues, this paper proposes a real-time on-board CNN acceleration architecture based on Distribution Difference-aware Mixed-precision Quantization (DDAMQ), achieving efficient deployment through collaborative hardware and software optimization. At the algorithm level, low-bit quantization is employed to reduce computational and resource overhead. Furthermore, feature distribution difference modeling and outlier suppression are used to adaptively optimize the quantization bit width and pruning threshold, significantly mitigating the accuracy loss caused by low-bit quantization and improving model performance. At the hardware level, the computation and storage structure is optimized to improve resource utilization and reduce overall power consumption, making this architecture more suitable for resource-constrained real-time intelligent processing tasks. A resource consumption evaluation model is constructed to provide guidance for efficient deployment that meets the on-chip resource constraints of FPGAs. Experiments based on four mainstream CNN models on five typical datasets are conducted and validated on an AMD-Xilinx VC709 development board. The results show that, at a similar compression ratio, the proposed DDAMQ strategy achieves superior classification performance; compared with recent state-of-the-art work, the designed CNN acceleration architecture improves throughput and energy efficiency by at least 2.17 and 2.13 times.

title: Sensorless Controlled PM Flux Linkage Estimation of Direct-Drive DTP-SPMSGs Under Variable Speed Control
authors: Peng Wang, Z. Q. Zhu, Zhibin Feng
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Permanent magnet (PM) flux linkage is critical for monitoring the state of permanent magnet in dual three-phase surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous generators (DTP-SPMSGs). However, for direct-drive DTP-SPMSGs under sensorless control where the blade speed is variable and completely depends on the wind speed, this leads to the variation in position error. This makes conventional PM flux linkage estimation methods ineffective, as the position error is no longer constant but dynamically coupled with operating speed. This paper proposes a virtual flux linkage (VFL) injection-based method for online PM flux linkage estimation of DTP-SPMSGs under sensorless variable-speed operation. By injecting opposite virtual flux linkage pulses into two position observers corresponding to two winding sets simultaneously, the response of difference in observed positions between two sets can be utilized to construct a PM flux linkage estimation model, where the unknown terms including time-varying position error, stator resistance, stator inductance, and inverter nonlinearity are totally cancelled. The proposed method has been validated experimentally.

title: Improved Finite Control Set Model-Free Predictive Current Control of PMSM Based on Optimized Scaling Factors
authors: Yongting Deng, Wenjie Li, Chuanlong Zhai, Haiyang Cao, Xiufeng Liu
abstract: In ultra-local-model-based finite-control-set model-free predictive current control (FCS-MFPCC) of permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs), the scaling factor critically affects current prediction accuracy, while its conventional fixed design remains sensitive to inductance mismatch. To address this issue, an improved finite control set modelfree predictive current control (FCS-MFPCC) with identification and adaptive switching system to online identify and apply the accurate scaling factor is proposed. First, the influence of scaling-factor deviation on current-variation estimation is analyzed in terms of z-domain pole-zero distribution and maximum estimation error. Then, instead of relying on lumped-disturbance observation under a fixed scaling factor, a unified framework integrating disturbance estimation and input-gain adaptation is developed. A dynamic mapping from the adaptive scaling factor to the estimated lumped disturbance is established, based on which a gain regulation law and an adaptive switching strategy are designed. Finally, the estimated lumped disturbance and updated scaling factor are used to synchronously generate the current increments of all eight candidate switching states for look-up table (LUT) refreshing, while a two-step prediction scheme is adopted to compensate for digital delay. Simulation and experimental results verify improved current tracking performance, lower total harmonic distortions (THD), and enhanced robustness against parameter mismatch.

title: A Multi-Dataset and Manual Annotation-Driven Framework for Accurate Blood Cell Detection with YOLO and GELAN-E Architectures
authors: Md. Sakib Bin Islam, Shamima Sultana Ridoy, Shahneela Shailee, Rafia Jannat, Akid Ornob
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Manual blood cell counting is labor-intensive, error-prone, and highly dependent on technician expertise, while automated hematology analyzers, although accurate, remain costly and inaccessible in resource-limited settings. To address this gap, we propose a deep learning-based framework for blood cell detection and classification using YOLO and GELAN-E architecture. Our method integrates seven publicly available blood smear datasets with manual annotations, enabling simultaneous classification of eight blood cell types, including red blood cells, platelets, five white blood cell subtypes, and abnormal leukocytes associated with leukemia. Initial training and optimization were performed with YOLOv5 to refine dataset size, preprocessing strategies, and hyperparameters, followed by deployment on YOLOv9c and GELAN-E. The YOLOv9c model achieved a precision of 94.6%, recall of 92.8%, mAP50 of 97.1%, and mAP50-95 of 75.5%, while GELAN-E achieved a precision of 95%, recall of 94%, mAP50 of 97%, and mAP50-95 of 75%, demonstrating superior generalizability across diverse imaging conditions. EigenCAM visualizations confirmed that the models captured critical morphological features for accurate classification. Overall, this framework provides a cost-effective, scalable, and accurate solution for AI-driven hematological analysis with strong potential for integration into mobile and point-of-care diagnostic systems, especially in low-resource healthcare environments.

title:
Enhancing Ferroelectric Performance of Al
                    <sub>0.7</sub>
                    Sc
                    <sub>0.3</sub>
                    N Capacitors through Substrate Engineering
authors: Yuan Fang, Haoji Qian, Xiaoxi Li, Jiawei Li, Cizhe Fang, Xiangyu Zeng, Tao Wu, Chengji Jin, Jiajia Chen, Yi’an Ding, Yan Liu, Genquan Han
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, we investigated the impact of different substrates on the ferroelectric properties of Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.7</inf>Sc<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.3</inf>N capacitors. The Si-based device exhibited superior performance compared with those on Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> and Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> substrates. Specifically, it achieved a remanent polarization (2P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf>) of 366 μC/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> compared with 257 μC/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> for the Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> device, and its coercive field was reduced by 0.12 MV/cm. Endurance measurements further revealed stable switching beyond 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> cycles with low leakage, while the Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>-based device broke down after ~2×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> cycles. These improvements are attributed to the stronger c-axis orientation and smaller lattice constant c of 4.95 Å for the Si-based devices. These results demonstrate that substrate engineering is an effective strategy to lower switching barriers and leakage currents, enabling reliable low-voltage operation of wurtzite ferroelectric devices for next-generation memory and logic applications.

title: Sinhala Speech Recognition System for Speech-Based Autism Intervention in Children Using the NAO Robot
authors: Heshani Bopage, Koliya Pulasinghe, Samantha Rajapaksha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This research focuses on the development of a Sinhala speech recognition engine tailored to identify the language content of conversations with children. The engine leverages machine learning algorithms and natural language processing (NLP) techniques to transcribe and classify speech in Sinhala. Key features include an acoustic model optimized for the nuances of Sinhala phonetics and a language model trained on datasets encompassing texts of child-directed speech. The system evaluates linguistic aspects to assess the appropriateness of content and engagement levels in child-centric dialogues. By addressing challenges such as phoneme variation and informal conversational patterns, the system aims to enhance the understanding and facilitation of Sinhala-based child interactions, promoting effective communication and developmental support.

title: Explainable Predictions of Preterm Birth: A LIME-Supported Ensemble Framework
authors: Mohammad Tarek Aziz, Renzon Daniel Cosme Pecho, Nahida Zakir, Md Saharab Hossain, Akba Ull Hasna Era, Nayeem Uddin Ahmed Khan, Nasrin Akter Srabony, Sevara Sadullayeva, Odamova Ugiljon, Valisher Sapayev Odilbek Uglu, Tanjim Mahmud
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Predicting preterm birth remains a critical medical challenge, as early identification can significantly reduce health risks for both mothers and newborns. In this study, we investigate the effectiveness of machine learning techniques for accurately predicting preterm birth. A comprehensive exploratory data analysis (EDA) was first conducted using correlation analysis, feature distribution visualizations, and pie charts to better understand and preprocess the dataset. After data normalization, several classifiers—including Random Forest, AdaBoost, Decision Tree, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, Neural Network, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost—were applied for prediction. Among the individual models, KNN and Random Forest achieved the highest accuracy of 99%. An ensemble learning approach further improved the performance, reaching a maximum accuracy of 99.14%. To enhance model transparency and clinical interpretability, the Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) technique was employed to explain individual predictions. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach can reliably predict preterm birth and has strong potential to assist healthcare professionals in early risk assessment and decision-making.

title: Multi-Threaded Recast-Based A* Pathfinding for Scalable Navigation in Dynamic Game Environments
authors: Tiroshan Madushanka, Sakuna Madushanka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: While the A* algorithm remains the industry standard for game pathfinding, its integration into dynamic 3D environments faces trade-offs between computational performance and visual realism. This paper proposes a multi-threaded framework that enhances standard A* through Recast-based mesh generation, Bezier-curve trajectory smoothing, and density analysis for crowd coordination. We evaluate our system across ten incremental phases, from 2D mazes to complex multi-level dynamic worlds. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework maintains 350+ FPS with 1000 simultaneous agents and achieves collision-free crowd navigation through density-aware path coordination.

title: Heterogeneous Bit- and Channel-Aware Mapping Framework for Area-Efficient and Robust RRAM-based CIM
authors: Zecheng Pan, Jiyang Cao, Zhoujie Pan, Yuwan Hong, He Tian, Yi Yang, Tianling Ren
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Resistive random-access memory (RRAM)-based computing-in-memory (CIM) offers massive parallelism for neural network acceleration but suffers from severe accuracy degradation and resource inefficiency under device nonidealities. This work presents a heterogeneous bit- and channel-aware hybrid inference framework that simultaneously optimizes precision allocation and array utilization for robust and area-efficient RRAM-CIM. The proposed method introduces a two-level heterogeneous mapping mechanism: (1) bit significance–aware mapping that assigns the most significant bits (MSBs) to robust binary memristor crossbars (BXBs) and the least significant bits (LSBs) to compact multi-level crossbars (MXBs); and (2) a channel-importance–aware allocation that dynamically prioritizes high-impact convolutional channels with more reliable hardware resources. Moreover, a tunable resource–accuracy balancing algorithm enables flexible operation modes—accuracy-prioritized, area-prioritized, and balanced—for diverse deployment scenarios. Comprehensive simulations based on an extended MNSIM platform demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves up to 50% area reduction with less than 0.5% accuracy loss and maintains robust inference under nonlinearity and variation, significantly outperforming conventional homogeneous CIM schemes. This heterogeneous mapping strategy establishes a scalable pathway toward hardware-adaptive and reliability-aware CIM architectures for next-generation edge intelligence.

title: An Efficient Distributed Framework for Top-k Dominating Query Over Uncertain Databases
authors: Md. Safwan Zaher Asif, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Probabilistic top-k dominating (PTD) queries are essential for retrieving the most influential objects from uncertain data, but executing them over large datasets is computationally expensive. This work presents a distributed framework that evaluates PTD queries in a Hadoop environment by combining a filtering stage with a lightweight refinement step. The framework introduces a dynamic candidate pruning strategy that exploits lower and upper bounds to eliminate weak objects early, and an aggregated emission strategy that reduces redundant intermediate data during MapReduce execution. These methods improve query efficiency while preserving exact results. The proposed framework was tested on two datasets, a synthetic smartphone collection and a Bangladesh road dataset derived from OSM geometries, and the results show that the wall clock time was reduced by 34.2% on the synthetic dataset and 24.7% on the real dataset, while the communication cost remained unchanged. Communication cost remained unchanged as output cardinality was kept identical to the baseline for fair comparison. These improvements confirm that the framework works effectively across different uncertain domains, demonstrating that pruning and emission aggregation can cut unnecessary computation and data transfer and making the approach practical for large-scale probabilistic query processing in distributed environments.

title: BnSFD: YOLOv12-Based Detection of Bangladeshi Street Foods for Cultural Heritage
authors: Ahammad Nafiz, Mohammad Saidul Islam, Abu Anas, Moshiur Rahman Sabbir, Abid Hasan, Khushnur Binte Jahangir, Shoib Ahmed Shourav, Md. Tarek Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Current computer vision systems tend to focus on Western foods, leaving many traditional South Asian dishes out of the picture. That means popular Bangladeshi street foods like Singara, Peyaju, and Beguni often go unrecognized by automated tools. To address this gap, we developed a new real-time detection method using an improved version of YOLO (YOLOv12m). Our first step was creating a robust dataset: we gathered and labeled 3,988 high-quality images covering 13 traditional Bangladeshi food categories. In this study, we focused on three especially challenging ones: Singara, Peyaju, and Beguni. After optimizing the model, we achieved an average detection accuracy of 87.7% on these classes, while running smoothly at 151 frames per second. We also added image enhancement and refined the model to handle real-world variations in lighting, background, and food presentation. This work isn't just about better accuracy, it's about making technology more inclusive. By recognizing diverse cuisines, our system can help with nutrition tracking, support cultural preservation, and move toward computer vision that truly sees the richness of global food traditions.

title: AI-Powered Protection of Energy and Flexibility Scheduling in Interconnected Local Energy Networks Across Automation Cyber-Physical System
authors: Abolfazl Nazari, Ali Riki, Amir Abdollahi, Mahdi Ramezani Karim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: The growing reliance on renewable energy resources and digital communication technologies in modern smart grids requires operational strategies that are not only fast and reliable but also robust against cyber threats targeting automation infrastructures. As digitalization increases, both software-based and hardware-based components of cyber-physical automation systems become potential entry points for attacks that can compromise the accuracy of energy and flexibility scheduling. Local energy networks (LENs) are established to integrate distributed energy resources and improve system reliability, yet their dependence on automated sensing, communication, and control devices makes them vulnerable to data manipulation. This study introduces a cyber-secure scheduling platform for interconnected local energy networks (ILENs) that explicitly addresses these vulnerabilities. A bilevel scheduling framework is developed in which the central operator coordinates energy and flexibility exchanges, while local operators optimize their internal operation. To assess and enhance the cybersecurity of the automation system, false data injection (FDI) attacks—representing manipulation of software-based metering processes and potential exploitation of hardware interfaces—are modeled using a Gaussian probability distribution. A machine-learning-based defense mechanism, employing an XGBoost-driven deviation-bound detection and correction scheme, is proposed to protect the automation system from such attacks. The method demonstrates a maximum detection accuracy of 91.67 percent, and its corrective actions restore scheduling performance to conditions closely aligned with pre-attack results. This confirms the approach's ability to reinforce both software and hardware cybersecurity within automated energy management systems.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Sugarcane Leaf Diseases Using ResNet101 for Agricultural Sustainability
authors: Karamala Naveen, M.D.Raj Kamal, S. Kaliappan, Manishka Chitra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research presents a deep learning approach using the ResNet101 model to classify the sugarcane leaf diseases into five classes: Some of the diseases include Healthy, Mosaic, Red Rot, Rust, and Yellow Disease. A dataset of 2,569 images was used for training, validation, and testing, coming from Kaggle and having a stratified split ratio of 70:15:15. To enhance the generalization ability of the model, resizing and normalization of data were performed to preprocess the data, while flipping, rotating, and adjusting the brightness were also done to augment the data. The model achieved an overall accuracy of 86.92% and high precision, recall, and F1 scores for most classes. Nonetheless, some difficulty was encountered in differentiating the Mosaic disease because of the similarity in symptomatology. The study is focused on the use of transfer learning to solve agricultural problems. Possible future work includes the analysis of larger datasets and the inclusion of geographic diversity, multi-label classification, and incorporation into mobile applications for real-time use in the field. From the observations made in this research, there is evidence that deep learning has the capability to revolutionize the management of diseases in sugarcane farming.

title: From Impulse to Introspection: Two-Round Peer-Aware Architectures for Large Language Model Trading Agents
authors: Jung-Hua Liu, Yiqing Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: This study introduces a two-round prediction-execution architecture for large language model (LLM) trading agents, separating prediction and execution to improve decision quality. Tested across four LLMs on cryptocurrency markets, the framework reduced overtrading and exposed systematic overconfidence and herding. Findings highlight implications for consensus design and confidence calibration in AI decision systems.

title: MobileNetV4-Conv-M for Mushroom Safety: Leakage-Free Augmentation and High-Accuracy Edible-vs-Poisonous Classification
authors: Avani Mittal, Sneha, N. Lakshmi, A. Anto Spiritus Kingsly, J. Mahil, Ashish Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Accurate identification of poisonous mushrooms is a safety-critical problem often limited by the lack of labeled data. This study constructs a relatively small, reproducible pipeline centered around MobileNetV4-Conv-M, including leakage-free preprocessing, stratified 85:15 splitting, and train-only augmentation for better generalization on a small Kaggle image set (edible vs. poisonous). Transfer learning is used with AdamW & cosine scheduling, and five-view test-time augmentation is used for inference. The system achieves 95.63% peak validation accuracy (minimum val loss 0.0576) and test accuracy of 92.31%, macro-F1 = 0.923, and balanced accuracy = 92.86%. The confusion matrix shows that the poisoning predictions have no false positives and one false negative, reflecting conservative decisions regarding toxicity with high specificity. The protocol, including duplicate control, class weighting, and thresholdable probabilistic outputs, provides a practical pattern for tiny-sample, risk-sensitive image classification. Findings support the deployment of these systems on resource-constrained devices and advocate expanding them to large, specimen-diverse corpora and to calibrated, risk-aware decision rules.

title: Adaptive Modality Gating: Explainable, Confidence-Guided Fusion for Source-Only Zero-Shot Deepfake Detection
authors: Kalyan Kanti Halder, Joy Debnath, Md Abdul Masud
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deepfakes often keep real speech while altering facial motion (and vice versa), which can fool detectors trained on a single dataset. We propose Adaptive Modality Gating (AMG), a per-clip, confidence-guided fusion module that weights visual, geometric, and optional audio cues. Here, adaptive means clipwise gating, not domain adaptation. We add two general temporal signals: (i) how smoothly landmarks move over time, and (ii) how much the visual embedding changes frame to frame. A lightweight gate decides, per clip, which cues to trust: it boosts audio when manipulation is likely (e.g., voice-conversion artifacts or A/V mismatch) and down-weights audio when it is not informative (e.g., real/flat audio as in DeepfakeTIMIT). We train and select on source datasets only and evaluate zero-shot on targets, using per-clip normalization so no target statistics leak in. AMG also reports per-clip weights, offering a clear explanation of each decision. This simple, confidence-guided, per-clip choice of when to trust audio versus visual cues helps transfer across datasets, while a tiny gate suppresses non-diagnostic streams to improve robustness without target statistics.
