In short, we propose a simple, per-clip, confidence-guided gating scheme that uses generic temporal cues to decide when to trust audio versus visual streams. In our survey, this lightweight pairing of reliability cues with exported fusion weights as the explanation is rarely emphasized, and it improves zero-shot cross-dataset transfer without target statistics.

title: A Feedback-Driven Machine Learning Framework for Teaching Mode Decision Support in Higher Education Using Module Characteristics
authors: P.A. Janendra Chamodi, Dinesh Asanka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The post-pandemic expansion of online and blended learning has intensified the need for systematic decision-support mechanisms in higher education. Universities are increasingly required to determine appropriate teaching modes for academic modules while balancing student engagement, pedagogical effectiveness, and resource constraints. However, delivery-mode decisions are often guided by intuition or retrospective evaluations rather than structured, data-driven analysis. This study presents a feedback-driven machine learning framework designed to support teachingmode decision making in higher education. The framework integrates comparative student feedback with structured module characteristics to provide advisory recommendations on whether a module is more aligned with online or physical delivery. A dataset comprising 245 undergraduate survey responses across 32 academic modules was analyzed, incorporating pedagogical variables such as module type, credit count, interaction level, assessment type, semester, and academic year. Supervised learning models, including Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, and XGBoost, were evaluated. Experimental results indicate that module-level pedagogical characteristics contribute meaningfully to differentiating teaching-mode suitability, with the XGBoost model demonstrating the strongest overall performance (Accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=63.3 \%$</tex>, Macro-F1 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.58$</tex>, AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.63$</tex>). Rather than emphasizing predictive accuracy as a standalone objective, the study positions machine learning as a decision-support tool that complements academic planning processes. The proposed framework enables transparent, feedback-informed, and context-sensitive teaching-mode planning, particularly relevant for resource-constrained higher education systems.

title: Sentiment Analysis in Bangla E-Commerce Reviews: An Aspect Based Approach
authors: Jahidul Islam Rayhan, Ayesha Siddika, Md Nazir Ahmed, Fahim Shakil Tamim, Akibul Islam, Shahinur Alam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: When it comes to learning what consumers actually think about goods and services when they shop online, customer reviews are crucial. Despite being the seventh most spoken language with more than 284 million speakers, Bangla has lagged behind major languages like English and Chinese, which have seen tremendous advancements in sentiment analysis due to abundant data and excellent tools. We developed BEABSA, a brand-new dataset designed especially for Bangla E-commerce Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis, to address this problem. 4,300 carefully chosen Bangla reviews from well-known Bangladeshi websites like Pickaboo and Daraz are included in this collection. Importantly, each review has been skillfully classified by native speakers using sentiment tags that correspond to particular aspects of the product. We tested a variety of analytical techniques on BEABSA to demonstrate the value of this data, ranging from deep learning (CNN) to sophisticated Transformer-based models, and from conventional machine learning (such as LR, KNN, and SVM). Results show that while CNN (68%) and SVM (62 %) produce acceptable results, transformer models show notable gains. Bangla-BERT greatly outperforms all baseline models, achieving an accuracy of 82 % and an F1-score of 81 %. These results highlight transformer architectures' capacity to comprehend contextual significance in Bangla and set a strong benchmark for future BEABSA research.

title: Advances in Developing Metal Structures Suppressing Global Flying Crosstalk in CMOS
authors: Albert Wang, Zijin Pan, Weiquan Hao, Xunyu Li, Runyu Miao, Zijian Yue
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Global flying noises emerge as a dominant crosstalk problem for complex IC chips, particularly when various in-substrate noise-isolation techniques have been routinely used to efficiently block the crosstalk throughout a chip substrate. This paper provides an overview of recent advances in developing novel metal structures in CMOS back end of line (BEOL) stack to efficiently suppress global flying crosstalk on a chip.

title: Digital Financial Literacy and Fintech Adoption in Saudi Arabia: A Decision-Aid Perspective
authors: Basheer M. Al-Ghazali, M. Sadiq Sohail, Mohammed Abdulrazzaq Alaghbari, Adel Ghaleb Haddad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The rapid emergence of financial technologies (fintech) is reshaping the digital economy, fostering new modes of payments, lending, investment, and wealth management. In Saudi Arabia, fintech is a cornerstone of Vision 2030’s financial sector development, with government initiatives aimed at promoting digital financial inclusion and innovation. However, the adoption of fintech depends on multiple determinants that go beyond technical readiness, encompassing behavioral, cultural, and educational dimensions. This study investigates the relationship between digital financial literacy (DFL) and key determinants of fintech adoption in Saudi Arabia. Drawing on computational perspectives of decision support in digital systems, the paper develops and validates a research framework integrating constructs from the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT), and financial literacy literature. The framework positions DFL as a critical enabler and moderator of fintech adoption. A conceptual model is proposed, supported by a review of 30 recent academic studies, and contextualized to Saudi Arabia. Findings offer theoretical and practical implications for policymakers, fintech firms, and engineers developing digital financial solutions.

title: Real-Time Plant Disease Detection in Smart Agriculture Using a YOLO11-Based Deep Learning Model
authors: Md. Mostafuzur Rahaman Nafiz, Md. Asraful Islam, Sharfuddin Mahmood
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Plant diseases are one of the biggest challenges to agriculture. They reduce the quality of the produce, reduce yield, and can lead to economic losses that are substantial. Early and accurate detection of diseases is very important as this helps farmers to respond quickly and not suffer huge losses. Traditional methods are dependent on visual observation, which is prone to being fatiguing, time-consuming, and inconsistent at the scale necessary for agriculture. Deep learning methods are faster and more consistent. Here, we adopted the YOLO11x model for detection of disease in four crucial crops, i.e., eggplant, chili, potato, and tomato. The model is trained and tested on a sizeable data set that includes diverse choices of plant disease images. We implemented data augmentation to make the system more resilient under diverse field conditions such as lighting and overlapping leaves. The model achieved a significant improvement in disease classification, reaching an accuracy of 99%. The results show that YOLO11x can potentially be an efficient plant disease detection system. It can promote precision agriculture and smart agriculture by helping farmers detect potential problems before they arise. The system can reduce damage to crops, optimize food production, and promote sustainable farming.

title: A Blockchain-Based Framework for Secure and Efficient KYC Using Hyperledger Fabric and IPFS
authors: Mohd. Sultan Ahammad, Mahmudul Hasan, Amit Karmaker
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Enterprise applications such as insurance services require data sharing in an anonymous, selective, and tamper-proof manner. Validating the Know Your Customer (KYC) information of clients is one example where customers share personal data. The insurance company verifies customer identity and assesses the risks associated with each client. However, the current KYC process is often redundant, inefficient, and costly for both insurance companies and customers. Blockchain technology offers a suitable solution to ensure secure and transparent transactions between multiple parties, possessing the necessary capabilities to meet enterprise application requirements. Furthermore, clients should retain control over their shared KYC data, and the relationships between customers and their insurance companies should remain hidden from other insurers. In this work, we propose a blockchain-based KYC system that leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) to store KYC documents and employs smart contracts on the Hyperledger Fabric network to enable anonymous sharing of this information among insurance providers. The proposed method also empowers customers to manage access to their data. Additionally, two smart contract protocols are introduced to ensure that documents and consent records cannot be forged. The proposed solution promises significant cost savings, enhanced customer experience, and improved process efficiency.

title: Real-Time Earthquake Magnitude Classification from Initial P-Waves: Models, Dataset, and Comparative Analysis for South Asia
authors: Md Nasiat Hasan Fahim, Md. Abid Ullah Muhib, Rayhanul Amin Tanvir, Abdullah Al Noman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Rapid earthquake magnitude estimation is crucial for effective early warning systems that can save lives and reduce economic damage. In this paper, we present a comprehensive study of magnitude classification using only the vertical component of the initial 7 -second <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P}$</tex>-wave window from a single station. We compare six machine learning approaches that range from traditional models to state-of-the-art deep learning architectures. We also curated a novel dataset of 7,318 earthquake events in South Asia. The dataset was categorized into five Richterscale classes: slight (3.0-3.9), light (4.0-4.9), moderate (5.0-5.9), strong (6.0-6.9) and severe (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 7.0$</tex>). Our experiments show that deep learning models substantially outperform traditional approaches. Our Transformer based architecture achieved 76.23% standard accuracy and 81.56 % adaptive accuracy with 4.8 ms inference latency. The adaptive-accuracy metric is introduced for the inherent uncertainty in magnitude estimation of near class boundaries. These results indicate that the attention mechanisms in Transformers combined with adaptive classification effectively capture the temporal dynamics of seismic signals. The architectural advantage facilitates promising generalization to rare high-magnitude events, despite the inherent data scarcity characteristic of seismic catalogs. The adaptive accuracy provides a more realistic assessment of model performance, and the result suggests viability for real-time deployment.

title: An AI-Driven Approach for User-Personalized Interior Color Matching Using Room Complexity and Visual Context
authors: A.H.T.N. Premachandra, A.N. Warnasooriya, A.M.C.B. Adikari, D.M.I.P.B. Gunasinghe, S.P.D.M.V. Dharmasinghe, K.A.S.H. Kulathilake
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Interior color selection plays a critical role in shaping the aesthetic quality and psychological comfort of indoor spaces. However, most existing interior design tools rely on predefined color palettes or rule-based systems that fail to consider individual user preferences, room characteristics, and existing visual context simultaneously. As a result, color recommendations often lack personalization and practical relevance. This paper proposes an AI-driven framework for user-personalized interior color matching that integrates multiple complementary models into a unified system. The framework integrates a fuzzy logic-based room complexity analysis, user preference clustering, color and texture detection, and a color recommendation strategy to generate context-aware and personalized color palettes. Room complexity is quantified using area-based, entropy-based, and visual clutter metrics, which are fused through a fuzzy logic decision system. User preferences are collected through a structured questionnaire and grouped using K-means clustering to identify dominant preference patterns. In parallel, computer vision techniques are applied to detect wall regions, identify textures, and extract dominant wall colors from interior images. The outputs of these models are integrated within a color recommendation module that applies color harmony principles while adapting color vibrancy based on room complexity and user cluster characteristics. A lightweight real-time visualization approach is employed to allow users to preview recommended colors directly on room images without relying on hardware-intensive augmented reality technologies. Experimental evaluations and user studies confirm improved user satisfaction (84 % realism, 88 % preference) and efficient mobile performance (1.7 s per image) for the proposed personalized color recommendation framework. This research highlights the potential of combining user modeling, environmental analysis, and visual context to advance AI-assisted interior design systems.

title: Predictive Modeling of LED Luminaire Degradation Using Artificial Neural Networks and Environmental Input
authors: Pavol Belany, Zuzana Florkova, Peter Hrabovsky, Roman Budjac
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: One of the key requirements in the design of lighting systems is the long-term reliability of LED luminaires. This aspect is particularly emphasized in smart buildings and infrastructure, where maintenance must be optimized both economically and functionally. Despite the excellent properties of LEDs in terms of energy efficiency and lifetime, their components inevitably degrade over time due to thermal, electrical and environmental stresses. This leads to a gradual decrease in luminous and photometric performance. This paper proposes a hybrid predictive framework to estimate the degradation of LEDs by calculating a wear index based solely on indirect inputs such as daylight intensity, daily energy consumption and operating time. In this work, two multilayer artificial neural network models were developed: one to predict daily energy consumption, the other to estimate the average operating time of luminaires. The outputs of these models are then used to calculate the wear index using a derived formula based on the principles of physical degradation. Experimental validation using real measurement data shows that the proposed models achieve high predictive accuracy with relative errors of around 5% for partial parameters and below 10% for the final estimate of the wear index. The results presented in the paper confirm the suitability of models based on artificial neural networks for predicting the degradation of LED diodes without the need for direct operational monitoring or measurement of operating time. The proposed approach enables the implementation of cost-effective predictive maintenance strategies, which allows for the early identification of luminaires approaching the end of their functional life. This method supports the concept of proactive lighting management in smart environments, ensuring visual comfort, operational continuity and extended system sustainability. This approach eliminates the need for direct luminaire runtime monitoring, significantly reducing system complexity and operational costs.

title: Model Persistence in Distributed Environments: Change-Based Encoding on Blockchain
authors: Marco Scappa, Juri Di Rocco, Alfonso Pierantonio
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Change-Based Persistence (CBP) is a mechanism used in Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) tools to record the evolution of models as sequences of fine-grained change events. CBP models are typically stored as local XML files, making them vulnerable to accidental corruption, loss, or intentional tampering. This paper introduces COBRA, a blockchain-backed persistence approach that ensures integrity, immutability, and long-term auditability of CBP histories. COBRA serializes model change events into blockchain-compatible transactions and stores each modeling session as an immutable block on a permissioned Hyperledger Fabric network. The approach is agnostic to any specific modeling language or notation: any EMF-based model represented as change events can be persisted and reconstructed. COBRA also implements duplicate detection and chunked storage to handle large histories efficiently. We provide a complete encoding/decoding pipeline together with an empirical evaluation measuring execution time and energy consumption under different workloads. Results show that COBRA preserves full recoverability of model histories while providing trustworthy, tamper-resistant storage with acceptable computational overhead.

title: Dual-Branch Attentional Multi-Fusion Network with Uncertainty Quantification and Explainable AI for Skin Cancer Classification
authors: Ankita Saha, Fazlay Rabby, Ayetullah Al Khomane, Nusrat Hasan, Mou Das
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Skin cancer remains one of the most prevalent cancers worldwide, and early and accurate diagnosis is essential for reducing mortality. This study introduces AMF-Net, a novel dual-branch attentional multi-fusion network introducing a three-tier, uncertainty-based decision framework, directly addressing a critical gap in clinical readiness. Our unique framework integrates EfficientNet-B3 and ResNet-50 with enhanced CBAM attention and a new multi-component focal loss. The model was trained on a diverse 12,577-image dataset, including a newly collected hospital dataset, which significantly enhances clinical applicability beyond public benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with 96.47% accuracy and ROC-AUC scores exceeding 0.90 across all seven lesion classes. Calibrated uncertainty quantification via Monte Carlo Dropout enables our three-tier system (automated, expert-assisted, expert-driven), mirroring real-world clinical workflows and ensuring patient safety. Grad-CAM++ visualizations further improve interpretability by highlighting clinically relevant features validated by dermatologists. An ablation study confirms the superiority of our dual-branch fusion design. AMF-Net's unique combination of high accuracy, reliability, and explainability makes it a highly promising and clinically applicable candidate for deployment in real-world dermatology settings.

title: A Generative Bangla Question Answering System Using Large Language Model for University Admission Related Frequently Asked Questions
authors: Srijita Dhar, Hasan Murad, Aninda Roy Dhruba
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate dissemination of information to Bangladeshi candidates for university admission depends on frequently asked questions (FAQs). This endeavour aims to solve frequent questions about admissions to help students by introducing a generative question-answering system. Low-resource languages like Bangla specifically lack sufficient datasets, especially in fields like university admissions. As such, Bangla question-answer pairs related to university admissions lack a comprehensive dataset. The data set consists of 5000 Bangla question-answer pairs derived from university prospectuses, social media groups, and first-year candidates. Three categories define the dataset of general, public, and private universities. We have utilized the exceptional natural language understanding of the unsloth LLama, Qwen, Gemma, Mistral & DeepSeek, and TinyLlama model. Our evaluation using Bert-Score, BERT-based Semantic Metrics, Partial, and Fuzzy Match Performance, MRR, and Hit Rate, and METEOR score shows that the system routinely generates relevant and accurate findings. We prioritized DeepSeek and LLaMA over other models because they consistently delivered better evaluation scores across key metrics. DeepSeek had the edge over LLaMA in most metrics, Mean Reciprocal Rank (0.9870 vs. 0.9850), Hit Rate@1 (0.9741 vs. 0.9701), partial and fuzzy match accuracy, and average METEOR score, and LLaMA, meanwhile, slightly outperformed on BERT-based precision. Basically, we choose DeepSeek for better retrieval, ranking, and semantic understanding, or LLaMA for more comprehensive text generation and precision-oriented tasks. As a result, these two models received higher priority in our experiments and analysis.

title: Decision-Aided Inventory Management in Retail Using Advanced Machine Learning Techniques
authors: K H. Srilakshmi, F. Rosita Kamala, K. Suneetha, R. Joyce, Kriti Kusum
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Decision-aided inventory management in retail is very important for finding the correct balance between having enough products and keeping costs down. Employing advanced machine learning techniques in combination offer a data-driven solution to enhance the accuracy of predictions and determine the optimal methods to replenish. Existing stockroom management systems, however, are generally not able to handle shifting customer demand, slow decisions on whether to resupply, and not being able to adapt to alterations in sales or supply conditions quickly enough. Inventory problems often lead to either excess stock or shortages, negatively affecting profits and customer satisfaction. This research introduces a Predictive Demand Optimization Framework. The framework uses LSTM models to properly estimate demand and RL to change inventory choices in real time. The system determines refilling time and amount based on sales trends, seasonal changes, and supply circumstances. This strategy helps supermarkets predict demand and reduce waste, especially perishable ones. PDOF increases forecasting accuracy, holding and shortage costs, and operational efficiency, according to experiments. This smart inventory management method is flexible, responsive, and profit-driven.

title: Leveraging CNN-ViT Hybrid Model For Enhanced AI Generated Image Detection
authors: Md. Tareq Zaman, Ziyan Shirin Raha, Md. Ashraful Alam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The emergence of AI-generated images, aided by Generative Adversarial Networks and diffusion models, raise concerns about media authenticity and safety. These images, often resembling genuine ones, pose risks like misinformation, digital deception, and identity theft. Advances in generative models necessitate more robust detection strategies. This paper proposes a hybrid approach that merges Convolutional Neural Networks with Vision Transformers (ViTs) to leverage the advantages of both architectural types for enhanced identification of AI-generated images. The paper assesses the performance of various CNN and ViT models, such as EfficientNet, Densenet, ResNet, VGG19, Alexnet, ViT-B/16 and DeiT, through a comprehensive experiment. According to the results, the hybrid CNN-ViT model which combines the best of the CNN models that is EfficientNet and the best of the Vision Transformer models that is DeiT, achieves a maximum accuracy of 96.99% in distinguishing between actual and AI-generated images. Compared to standalone CNN or ViT models, this model performs better in both datasets like RealSynthX and CIFAKE. RealSynthX, a custom made noval dataset that contains both actual and AI-generated images, is used as a dataset for this paper. In RealSynthX, it consists of 1910 real images and 1910 ai-generated images. In addition, it is evaluated using the CIFAKE dataset, demonstrating its resilience to various generative methods. This study provides a hybrid AI model that produces excellent detection accuracy, contributing to ongoing efforts to combat the spread of bogus media. This promotes digital content security and integrity.

title: Artificial Neural Network-Based Assessment of Flood Control and Public Safety in the Philippines
authors: Samantha Krayelle M. Estose, Dana Althea L. Salaysay, Jose Benedict Macado, Van Julian D. De Vera, Maela Madel L. Cahigas, Althea Angel F. Fernando, Sebastian Luis D. Bugayong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: One of the most severe and recurring natural disasters in the Philippines is flooding, causing devastating financial, infrastructure, and environmental losses to individuals’ livelihoods yearly. While existing flood management strategies emphasize physical and hydrological measures, there is a limited understanding of how public perception and decision-making influence the success of these interventions. The study integrates behavioral analysis with Artificial Neural Network (ANN) modeling to examine how risk perception, past flood experiences, institutional trust, and policy effectiveness shape public support for flood control initiatives, leveraging ANNs’ high adaptability and predictive capabilities. A framework was developed using the data collected from 207 respondents across the Philippines to identify nonlinear relationships among behavioral, environmental, and socio-demographic variables. The model was trained over 30 runs and evaluated using Mean Squared Error (MSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), achieving an average MAPE of 25.06%. The bestperforming run achieved an MSE of 0.4419, MAE of 0.5429, and MAPE of 17.81%, indicating strong predictive accuracy. Through normalized feature importance scores, results reveal that institutional trust and perceived policy effectiveness are significant drivers of community participation, while geographical vulnerability and prior flood experience reinforce risk perception and preparedness. Although significant economic and infrastructural factors were not included, bridging behavioral insights and AI-based modeling offers a novel approach to flood risk management, providing policymakers and local governments with data-driven, effective strategies for designing communitycentered, resilient, and adaptive flood control initiatives in the Philippine context, where future work could extend the model to hybrid or time-based models, improving flood management analysis.

title: Adaptive Deep Sequential Networks Empowered by Multi-Model Feature Selection for Effective Phishing URL Identification
authors: Abdullah Al Mamun, Md. Kamal Hosain, Antu Roy Chowdhury, Md Arif Hossen
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Phishing through malicious URLs remains a major cyber risk because attackers disguise links to trick users into revealing confidential information such as login details. This research proposes a sequential deep learning architecture based on Gated Recurrent Units (Custom-GRU) for detecting phishing URLs with high precision. A balanced dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> URLs, containing lexical, host-based, SSL/TLS, and content-related attributes, was sourced from Kaggle for experimentation. Feature selection was performed using a consensus ranking strategy that combined XGBoost, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting, narrowing the space to the 30 most discriminative features. The refined dataset was then used to train four models-CustomGRU, Custom-BiGRU, Custom-LSTM, and Custom-BiLSTMunder identical conditions. Among these, the Custom-GRU model demonstrated superior performance, converging efficiently and achieving 99.39 % training accuracy, 97.23 % precision, 98.40 % recall, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.81 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex>-score. These results indicate that lightweight GRU-based architectures, when enhanced by robust feature selection, can capture complex sequential dependencies in URL data and provide a highly effective solution for real-time phishing detection.

title: Assessing the Suitability of Using Federated Learning in Edge Computing for Providing Real Time Services
authors: Tajul Islam, M.M.A. Hashem
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid development of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and the increasing demand for real-time services in applications such as autonomous vehicles, health care monitoring, smart cities, and smart grids have led to significant interest in combining edge computing with Federated learning (FL). It is an advanced machine learning technology. It is a decentralized learning prototype that integrates with edge computing to enable real-time services, low latency, and privacy-preserving data. A comprehensive analysis is presented through latency, throughput, Jitter, packet loss, error rate, response time calculation, and so on. FL can significantly enhance the responsiveness and adaptability of edge-based services. It also provides an optimized FL infrastructure design to efficiently support real-time edge intelligence. In a Centralized System, providing real-time services is difficult for smart applications. If it is possible to provide a middle layer for storage, the processing would be sufficient to provide real-time services. The implications of the work would contribute to the well-being of real-time application-based services and provide future-generation computerized systems with a fine margin.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Decentralized Vehicle Management System for Secure and Transparent Transactions Using Hyperledger Fabric
authors: Jannatul Ferdous Nekima, Abdullahil Kafi, Nusaiba Tahsin, Faiza Rashid
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Traditional vehicle management systems face challenges such as data tampering, fraud, poor transparency, and ineffective record keeping. The paper introduces a Hyperledger Fabric decentralized vehicle management system that will guarantee secure, tamper-proof, and auditable records throughout the vehicle life cycle, such as vehicle registration, ownership transfers, maintenance records, and so forth. Data integrity is ensured with cryptographic hashing and consensus mechanisms, smart contracts are resistant to frauds that enforce the uniqueness of VIN, authenticate transactions, and disallow unauthorized modifications. The network incorporates the members of the government, dealers and service providers under a single administrative structure that allows accountability and deployment to be realistic. Experimental analysis of the system proves that the system is correct under different loads of transactions, and comparison analysis shows that it has improved trust, traceability, and reliability compared to traditional centralized databases.

title: Efficient Deep Learning Models for Marine Fish Classification Using Transfer Learning and Knowledge Distillation
authors: Vivaan Sharma, Karthikeya Bethu, Rohan Chidri, Prabhu Prasad B M, Pavan Kumar B N, GN Girish
abstract: Marine classification and monitoring are critical for conservation but tend to involve time-consuming manual methods. This paper introduces an efficient deep learning method for marine classification, with a special interest in fish species identification. We apply and compare two methods: transfer learning with ResNet-50 and knowledge distillation for developing lighter models that can be deployed. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first application of knowledge distillation to marine species classification. Experiments use a combined dataset of 23,358 images from FishCLEF and Fish-Pak, covering 21 fish species. Our transfer learning solution achieved 99.37% accuracy on the test set, whereas the knowledge distillation method yielded a much smaller model (92.3 MB) with 98.40% accuracy. The outcomes prove the efficiency of these methods in producing both high-performance and low-resource solutions for marine classification tasks, which can aid conservation efforts by enabling automated, scalable monitoring systems.

title: A Feature-Driven Approach to Diabetic Retinopathy Grading with SMOTE-Based Class Balancing
authors: Shafi Mahamud, Md. Jahidul Hasan Jahid
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Diabetes is becoming more common which shows why it is important to notice and treat its complications, mainly Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), as it can cause vision loss and blindness if left unchecked. Usually, DR detection requires an ophthalmologist to study retinal images manually, creating a process that can be both tiresome for them and prone to error. The rapid growth of AI and DL makes it possible for automation of DR detection to help improve both the reliability and efficiency in making a diagnosis. In this work, we have applied different pre-trained models to get deep features from retinal images and then classify them using a Sequential neural network. A technique called Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique or SMOTE, was used to deal with class imbalance, balancing the training data. By using transfer learning along with data balancing methods, we managed to create an approach that achieved classification accuracy of 89 % and a weighted-average F1-score of 85.2 % for automated DR diagnosis where Inception V3 was used to get deep features and Sequential neural network as the classifier.

title: Persistent Human Feedback, LLMs, and Static Analyzers for Secure Code Generation and Vulnerability Detection
authors: Ehsan Firouzi, Mohammad Ghafari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Existing literature heavily relies on static analysis tools to evaluate LLMs for secure code generation and vulnerability detection. We reviewed 1,080 LLM-generated code samples, built a human-validated ground-truth, and compared the outputs of two widely used static security tools, CodeQL and Semgrep, against this corpus. While 61% of the samples were genuinely secure, Semgrep and CodeQL classified 60% and 80% as secure, respectively. Despite the apparent agreement in aggregate statistics, per-sample analysis reveals substantial discrepancies: only 65% of Semgrep's and 61% of CodeQL's reports correctly matched the ground truth. These results question the reliability of static analysis tools as sole evaluators of code security and underscore the need for expert feedback. Building on this insight, we propose a conceptual framework that persistently stores human feedback in a dynamic retrieval-augmented generation pipeline, enabling LLMs to reuse past feedback for secure code generation and vulnerability detection.

title: Room-temperature, Broadband, and Integrable Graphene Terahertz Detectors enabling Wireless Communications for 6G
authors: Zhuo Dong, Dong Wang, Kang Zhou, Boyu Dong, Keqin Tang, Liu Yang, Junwen Zhang, Hua Li, Kai Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The contradiction between limited spectrum resources and growing data demands in wireless communication is intensifying. Terahertz (THz) band is a promising candidate for next-generation wireless communication technology, and its development requires miniaturized receivers that are high-speed, broadband, and highly sensitive. This study developed a direct and compact THz detector (area＜0.1 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) based on graphene, which achieves a broadband response (W/J band, 2.55 THz). The detector demonstrated a high sensitivity of 6.6 pW/Hz<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1/2</sup> and achieved a data transmission rate exceeding 220 Mbps. This work demonstrates the potential of graphene detectors for wireless communication.

title:
Angle-Dependent Conductivity in MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Field-Effect Transistors with Bi Contacts
authors: Han-Chieh Lo, Tilo H. Yang, Edmayelle Alforja, Ming-Hao Liu, Min-Hung Lee, Yann-Wen Lan
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The directional dependence of single crystalline molybdenum disulfide (MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) critically influences its electronic transport properties. Recent advances in contact engineering have enabled ultralow contact resistance in MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> field-effect transistors (FETs) using semi-metal bismuth (Bi) contacts. In this work, we systematically investigate the angle-dependent conductivity of Bi-contacted MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FETs by varying the relative orientation between the electrodes and the MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> lattice. The conductivity exhibits a continuous angular dependence, reaching a maximum at 0° and minimum at 30°, with nearly a twofold enhancement at the optimal alignment. These results demonstrate that precise control of contact orientation is essential for realizing high-performance two-dimensional devices and provide valuable guidelines for designing next-generation heterogeneous integrated circuits with enhanced energy efficiency and computational speed.

title: Short-Circuit Power Prediction in 20kV Distribution Feeders Using Physics-Guided Gradient Boosting Regression
authors: Zahra Ghavami, Ensiyeh Askari, Mohammad Pourheydari, Fatemeh Kordi, Ehsan Imani, Hakimeh Rezaei
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: Accurate estimation of short-circuit parameters in medium-voltage (MV) distribution feeders is vital for equipment sizing, relay coordination, and system safety. Conventional analytical approaches based on IEC 60909 require complete network data and repetitive numerical calculations, limiting their use for real-time or scenario-based studies. This paper proposes a physics-guided machine learning framework that predicts the initial symmetrical short-circuit current, short-circuit apparent power, and the equivalent Thevenin resistance and reactance along a 20 kV feeder using only two inputs: distance from the substation and conductor type. The training dataset was generated from a real operational feeder modeled in DIgSILENT PowerFactory under IEC 60909 conditions. Each target quantity was learned using a family of monotonic gradient-boosting regressors, and the optimal model was selected by cross-validated error minimization. The proposed model achieved 99% accuracy and mean absolute percentage error below 1% for most outputs, while strictly preserving physical monotonic trends. The results demonstrate that a physics-aware gradient-boosting surrogate can reproduce analytical short-circuit behavior with millisecond inference time, supporting digital-twin and protection-engineering applications.

title: On the Understanding of Aging-Induced Band Tail States Under Cryogenic Temperatures
authors: Chenyang Zhang, Yilin Hu, Guang-Hua Xu, Maokun Wu, Yue-Yang Liu, Pengpeng Ren, Runsheng Wang, Zhigang Ji
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work investigates how bias-temperature-instability (BTI) stress affect the transfer characteristics of FinFETs under cryogenic temperatures. Compared with room-temperature operation, BTI-stressed devices exhibit counterintuitive trends: a reduced subthreshold swing (SS), an increased on-current (Ion), and a higher peak transconductance (Gmmax). We attribute these shifts to the charge trapping of near-interfacial defects of which the thermal emission is strongly suppressed at cryogenic temperature. The resulting persistent occupancy perturbs band-edge states at the interface and reshapes the band tail states distribution—enhancing the localized states while reducing the transport states. The physical simulations quantitatively reproduce these behaviors, supporting the proposed mechanism. In addition, ab-initio calculations implicate oxygen vacancies (Vo) within interfacial layers in PMOS as the dominant traps. These results explain the reasons for the aging phenomenon of cryo-devices and identify an additional origin of band tail states relevant to cryogenic operation.

title: QOLE: A Browser-Native Quantum Circuit Simulator Based on Decision Diagrams
authors: Asimakis Kydros, Konstantinos Prousalis, Vasilis Skarlatos, Nikos Konofaos
abstract: The classical simulation of quantum circuits is inherently constrained by the exponential growth of the quantum state. Strategies based on decision diagrams, such as the Quantum Multiple-valued Decision Diagram structure, exploit regularities in the tensor descriptions of quantum operators and states to mitigate this limitation. This paper presents QOLE, a browser-compatible simulator for quantum circuits, that utilizes these diagrams in its implementation. QOLE offers a simple and intuitive high-level programming interface for building circuits, while its internal diagram-based compiler maps quantum gates and state vectors into compact graphs that enable more efficient simulation. The open implementation of QOLE demonstrates how diagram-based simulation can be made accessible within the constraints of modern web execution environments.

title: An Improved Hybrid 3D U-Net and ResNet101 Framework for Brain Tumor Segmentation and Classification from MRI Scans
authors: Utpal Barua, Nuzhat Tabassum, Steve Austin, Arnab Barua, Shah Nawaz Haider, Sarowar Morshed Shawon
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Brain tumors cause disruption in normal neurological process. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans are used to detect brain tumors. The reports are examined by radiologists that can cause errors and waste time. Aiming to avoid this issue, this research presents a 3D-UNet and ResNet101 a hybrid model from MRI scan for brain tumor segmentation and classification. The model integrates the volumetric structure of 3D U-Net for precise tumor boundary delineation with ResNet101 branch providing deep residual feature extraction. ResNet101 works as the encoder for precise feature extraction with deep residual layers that enable high level representation learning, while the 3D U-Net ensures accurate spatial localization of brain tumors through skip connections. The implementation was done on BRISC 2025 dataset and BraTS 2020 dataset presenting an accuracy of 99.50% with a precision score of 99.50%, a f1-score of 99.5% and 89.04% of accuracy, 89.04% of f1-score, 95% precision score respectively. Unlike single purposed models, this study introduces a hybrid model that ensure both reliable classification with near to accurate segmentation in real world data to help radiologists and low resourced hospitals.

title: TrueNorth: PERMA+4 and Conversational Agentic RAG to Optimize Long-Term STEM Engagement
authors: Katja Crusius, Joan Puteri Zheng, Varsha Shetty, Sonia Baron, Tracy Gaolese, William Wyatt, Anthony Dong, Windtell Souphavong, Yan Li
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: The persistent “leaky pipeline” in STEM fields threatens workforce retention and technological progress. This paper applies Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to overcome limitations of large language models in delivering contextually accurate, evidence-based mentoring for STEM professionals. We present TrueNorth, a dialogue system that integrates knowledge retrieval with the PERMA+4 framework to enhance factual accuracy and mentorship effectiveness. TrueNorth employs an advanced agentic RAG architecture with nine autonomous modules, including optimized indexing, query refinement, semantic-aware chunking, and hallucination detection. The system draws on PERMA+4-aligned, peerreviewed literature and best practices, leveraging Google Gemini for vector search and natural language generation within a PostgreSQL knowledge base. PERMA+4 extends well-being theory to organizational contexts by adding physical health, mindset, environment, and economic security to the traditional model. Evaluated on 116 test scenarios across 12 common STEM workplace challenges, TrueNorth delivered actionable, obtainable guidance, with 11 of 14 evaluation dimensions 79%) showing no significant category-dependent variation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}>0.05$</tex>), indicating robust and reliable performance across a variety of contexts. These results demonstrate TrueNorth's potential to support the multifaceted issue of early-career STEM development while upholding responsible AI principles.

title: Practical Analysis of Various Types of Inrush Current and Implications for Protective Relay Functionality
authors: Nader Jalali Marand, Hasan Abniki, Milad Habibi, Mohammad ali Ataei, Hossein Askarian Abyaneh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: A significant current, known as Inrush Current (IC), is produced on the high-voltage side of the transformer as a result of the saturation of the magnetic core. This occurs either when the circuit breaker is activated to energize the transformer or when the primary voltage of the transformer rises to the nominal value as a result of the correction of external faults. To prevent the malfunction of protective functions, the accurate identification of IC is a critical factor. The Inrush Current Detection (ICD) function is responsible for sending a blocking signal to the transformer protection functions. Various categories of ICs, such as initialize IC, sympathetic IC, and voltage recovery IC, are simulated with PSCAD/EMTDC software. The resulting signals for each scenario are exported in COMTRADE format. The AMT205 testing device injected these signals into the AMR and 7SJ62 relays. These relays differentiate between IC and faults by using the ratio of the second harmonic to the fundamental component.

title: Beyond Artifacts: Semantic Deepfake Detection and Digital Provenance Tracking with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Jabunnesa Jahan Sara, Shahriar Rizvi, Tasfia Jannat Tasfi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deepfakes are man-made media, produced using advanced artificial intelligence, that manipulate videos or images to portray fake but realistic content. General-purpose deepfake detectors have been found highly accurate by detecting low-level digital signals, but perform poorly with emerging manipulation techniques that are not explained. In this paper, we primarily explore the performance of commercial Large Language Models (LLMs), GPT-4o and Gemini Pro, for semantic deepfake detection, capitalizing on their multimodal abilities with Chain-of-Thought prompting. We propose an end-to-end approach that investigates a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6. 4 k}$</tex> real and fake images from the combined FaceForensics++ and Celeb-DF dataset, producing binary labels and textual rationales. When a suspected deepfake is found, the system then goes ahead to evoke a web-traceability module to search for the spread of the image online, providing verifiable evidence. Our experimental results show that domain-specific CNN baseline (XceptionNet) achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1. 3 3 \%}$</tex> accuracy and 91.77 % F1-score over zero-shot LLMs. While Open-source models such as LLaVA, and BLIP-2 entirely failed, resorting to trivial predictions, our web-traceability module achieved an accuracy of 95.12 % in correctly classifying both online and offline images. Our end-to-end detection-traceability pipeline presents opportunities for more sophisticated forensic technologies not only for the detection of fake media, but also for tracing their online origin, providing real-world value to policymakers, researchers, and forensic analysts.

title: An Adaptive Image Enhancement Method through Automated CLAHE with Real-ESRGAN
authors: Rehnuma Tarannum, Md. Mahbubur Rahman, Himel Ahmed, MD. Shameem Ahammed, Anamika Shingha Urme, Md. Shake Farid Uddin, Md Mostafizur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Image enhancement is the key to improving the scale of low-quality, poorly captured images. It works well in many domains such as medical diagnostics, satellite imaging systems, and surveillance etc. Traditional methods including Histogram Equalization (HE) and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C L A H E$</tex>) enhance the image by optimizing contrast. However, they often fail to preserve structural detail and perceptual quality. Deep learning models like Real-Enhanced Super-Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (Real-ESRGAN) work effectively to upgrade image resolution and create visually impressive results. But they may fail when the input is strongly degraded in the absence of contextual pre-processing. In this paper, we propose a hybrid image enhancement framework that adaptively combines automated CLAHE-based preprocessing with Real-ESRGAN superresolution. CLAHE facilitates the dynamic evaluation of image quality parameters namely brightness, contrast, sharpness, and denoising before upsampling. Along with feeding these parameters to Real-ESRGAN as input, we get the intensified output. Experimental assessment shows the effectiveness of our proposed method, where it constantly surpasses both classical and deep learning approaches. Furthermore, it offers a better visual appearance with the increase of Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio(PSNR) (30.15 dB) and the Structural Similarity Index Measure(SSIM) (0.84).

title: Degradation Evaluation of SiC MOSFETs in HTRB test with Various Gate Biases
authors: Fanpeng Zeng, Lingling Lai, Shoulai Gong, Kuan Yew Cheong, Yingxin Cui, Jisheng Han
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Since negative gate bias is widely used for reliable turn-off in SiC MOSFET applications, it is important to examine the effect of gate bias on high temperature reverse bias (HTRB) reliability of SiC MOSFET devices. This work systematically investigates the HTRB reliability of 1200 V/20 mΩ SiC MOSFETs under gate bias of 0 V, -5 V and -10 V. The results show that in the HTRB test under zero gate bias, the electrical characteristics of the SiC MOSFET devices are stable. While, in the HTRB test under negative gate bias, the electrical parameters, including the threshold voltage and on-resistance, of the devices exhibit negative shift slightly. Interestingly, as the negative gate bias increases, the variation occurs in the opposite direction. Furthermore, a substantial increase in switching loss is observed across all test conditions. These results demonstrate that the combination of prolonged high reverse electric field and negative gate bias stress poses a significant reliability risk, resulting in the variation of static electrical parameters and increased switching losses in SiC MOSFETs. This finding is crucial for optimizing switch-off capacity in practical applications.

title: Machine Learning-Assisted Optimization of $\text{Rb}_{2} \text{LiGaI}_{6}$-Based Perovskite Solar Cells: Insights from SCAPS-1D Simulation
authors: Ahnaf Tahmid Abir, Uday Syed, Mohammad Dilwar Ali Alvee, Maruf Kabir Refat, Md. Mahfuzul Haque
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The development of high-efficiency and stable perovskite solar cells (PSCs) requires both suitable material selection and advanced predictive modeling. In this study, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Rb}_{2} \text{LiGaI}_{6}$</tex>, a halide perovskite with a bandgap of 1.13 eV, is explored as the absorber material due to its high absorption coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>10^{5} ~\text{cm}^{-1}$</tex>), low carrier effective masses (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.2-0.4 m_{0}$</tex>), and thermodynamic stability. Device architecture optimization was performed using a machine learning (ML) framework to predict photovoltaic performance metrics, including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J_{s c}, V_{o c}, \text{FF}$</tex>, and PCE, under varying ETL/HTL interface configurations. Among the tested models, Random Forest achieved the best predictive accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.9974 surpassing XGBoost, HistGBR, and KNN. Correlation and SHAP analyses revealed that HTL bandgap, conduction band offset, electron affinity, and hole mobility play critical roles in governing efficiency. Device simulations showed that absorber thickness strongly influences PCE, to a maximum of 22 % at 1000 nm which is very close approximation of the ML predicted PCE of 22.13 % with only a prediction error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 6 \%}$</tex>. Optimization of transport layers demonstrated that a thin ETL (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \mathbf{5 0 ~ n m}$</tex>) avoids parasitic absorption losses, while moderate HTL thickness (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \mathbf{1 0 0 - 1 5 0 ~ n m}$</tex>) enhances <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{o c}$</tex>. Temperaturedependent studies further confirmed that device efficiency drops drastically due to elevated dark current. These findings highlight the potential of combining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Rb}_{2} \text{LiGaI}_{6}$</tex> absorbers with ML-guided optimization to design efficient and stable PSC architectures.

title: TTS-Based Data Augmentation for Low-Resource Malay Vowel Recognition Through Multi-Stage Sample Selection
authors: Shakir A. Mrayyen, Shahrul Azmi M.Y.
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Low-resource languages face a significant challenge in automatic speech recognition due to limited labeled training data. While synthetic data from text-to-speech (TTS) systems offers a promising solution, the lack of automatic quality control often results in noisy samples that degrade model performance. To address this, we introduce a confidence-based filtering framework for TTS-generated synthetic data, using vowel classification confidence as an automated quality measure. Working with Malay CV syllables, we generate 10,500 synthetic samples using the Malaysian Parler TTS model and retain only those where a real-data-trained classifier demonstrates high prediction confidence (>0.7 threshold). This automated selection yields 6,197 samples at 59% retention. Our approach achieves 97.12% accuracy with Multi-Layer Perceptron, representing a 40.9% reduction in vowel error rate compared to using real data alone. Statistical analysis confirms this filtered approach significantly outperforms naive augmentation by 6.63 percentage points (p<0.001), demonstrating that intelligent quality control matters more than simply adding more synthetic data. The approach requires approximately 200-300 real samples for effective filtering, making it suitable for realistic low-resource scenarios.

title: Application of EfficientNetB7 for Accurate Classification of Black Gram Leaf Diseases Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Gyana Ranjana Panigrahi, R.Rajitha Jasmine, Nikhil Mangrulkar, Tanya Gera, Rachit Moudgil, Ashwani Garg
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The purpose of this work is to propose the use of the EfficientNetB7 Deep learning model for the identification of Black gram leaf diseases to improve the diagnostic capabilities in agricultural practices. A total of 1007 high-resolution images of black gram leaves were collected and divided into five classes: healthy, anthracnose, leaf crinkle, powdery mildew, and yellow mosaic. The training was done for 30 epochs, and the overall accuracy was 94%, with good precision and recall in all classes. The classification report showed high precision for the most part, especially for the detection of healthy leaves and powdery mildew. However, some confusions were made between anthracnose and leaf crinkle, which can be considered for future work. The confusion matrix helped to reveal some of the particular misclassification patterns, which called for improved feature extraction methods. This research shows that deep learning models can be applied in agriculture to aid in the early identification of crop diseases and improve crop management systems.

title: A Hybrid IRLS-Adaptive LASSO Algorithm for Logistic Regression in Classification
authors: Tharsika Inparasa, Manikkavasakar Kayanan, Balasubramaniam Yogarajah
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Logistic regression is widely used for binary classification; however, its performance in real-world applications is often hindered by multicollinearity, high-dimensional feature spaces, and imbalanced class distributions. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes a Hybrid IRLS-Adaptive LASSO framework integrated with Least Angle Regression (LARS) for binary logistic regression. The proposed approach employs an adaptive <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\ell_{1}$</tex>-penalty to jointly perform feature selection and coefficient estimation, while Iteratively Reweighted Least Squares (IRLS) provides stable and reliable parameter estimation. The integration of LARS further enhances computational efficiency in high-dimensional settings. The proposed method is evaluated using multiple real-world binary classification datasets obtained from open repositories, including Oil Spill, Ion, Heart Disease, and PC1, which exhibit varying levels of class imbalance. Performance is evaluated using precision, recall, F1-score, accuracy, specificity, and balanced accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves more stable and improved performance on imbalanced datasets, particularly with respect to recall and balanced accuracy, while maintaining competitive performance on balanced data. In addition, the method consistently identifies a compact and informative set of predictors, improving model interpretability. Overall, the proposed framework provides a robust and computationally efficient solution for binary classification tasks in medical diagnosis, remote sensing, and environmental monitoring.

title: Critical Node Identification Using Random Forest Based Weight Learning for Multi-Criteria Decision Making
authors: Abdul Moiz, Ubaida Fatima, Muhammad Zeeshan Ul Haque
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Identifying important nodes within networks has many applications in fields of study including biological networks, wireless sensor networks, optical communication systems and optimizing network reliability. In this research a hybrid method was proposed that utilizes a Random Forest based (RF) algorithm and the VIKOR method to identify the most important nodes within a network utilizing the Escherichia coli protein-protein interaction network as a model of a complex network. A key issue in network optimization is the need to weight the various centrality metrics. Rather than use existing methods in the literature the RF-VIKOR method learns the optimal weights using supervised machine learning techniques based upon ground truth data to reflect the non-linear relationships among the features in networks. Results were obtained through comparative studies with entropy-based Weighting (ET-VIKOR) on the 2,727 nodes with 254 experimentally confirmed important nodes. Network topology analysis indicated that heterogeneous variance patterns existed across all centrality metrics with betweenness centrality having the greatest variance (Coefficient of Variation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=3.38$</tex>). The results also indicated that the RF-VIKOR methodology provided an improvement over ET-VIKOR in identifying important nodes by identifying 120 of the top 600 ranked nodes as true important nodes while ET-VIKOR identified 117 important nodes. Therefore, the enhanced pattern recognition ability of the RF-VIKOR methodology is beneficial for the identification of nodes with complex multi-metric attributes.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Leaf Disease Classification in Indoor Plants with YOLOv11: Stable Diffusion Augmentation Approach
authors: Nishad Mahmud Opu, Md Saiful Islam, Sayed Hanzala Abdullah, Ruma Akter
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Indoor plants such as Money Plant, Snake Plant, and Spider Plant enhance indoor air quality and aesthetics. However, they are highly susceptible to diseases such as Bacterial Wilt, Manganese Toxicity, Anthracnose, Leaf Withering, Fungal Leaf Spot, and Leaf Tip Necrosis, which compromise their health and ornamental value. Early and accurate classification of these diseases is critical for maintaining plant health, yet traditional diagnostic methods rely on expert knowledge that may not be readily accessible to indoor cultivators. This study employed the YOLO11s-cls model for automated disease classification across nine distinct conditions in the selected indoor plant species. Three experimental configurations were evaluated: the original dataset (787 images), traditional augmentation dataset (4,722 images using geometric and photometric transformations), and a diffusionaugmented dataset generated using Stable Diffusion v1.5 with controlled synthetic image generation. Comparative analysis revealed progressive performance improvements: baseline model achieved 96.7% test accuracy, traditional augmentation reached 98.17%, while the diffusion-augmented YOLO11s-cls model achieved exceptional 99.44% test accuracy. This represents an 83.0% error reduction from baseline to diffusion augmentation, with only 4 misclassifications among 711 test samples. The findings demonstrate the superiority of diffusion-based synthetic generation over traditional augmentation methods, establishing a practical framework for deployment-ready indoor plant disease monitoring systems.

title: EcoDetect: Lightweight Real-Time Recyclable Waste Detection Using YOLOv11n Trained on LLM-Guided Synthetic Data
authors: Ahsan Farabi, Md. Abdul Ahad Minhaz, Israt Khandaker, Ibrahim Khalil Shanto, Md. Tanvir Raihan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The improper disposal of recyclable waste creates numerous issues for urban waste management systems and significantly harms the environment. Manual sorting is often ineffective, inconsistent, and economically unfeasible, especially in areas with limited resources. In this study, we present EcoDetect, a lightweight, real-time recyclable waste detection system optimized for edge deployment. To address the shortage of labeled training data, the proposed approach combines prompt-based synthetic data generation with Roboflow-assisted annotation and augmentation. A YOLOv11n-based object detection model is trained to detect three waste categories: plastic, paper, and aluminum. Evaluation on a completely unseen real-world test set demonstrates superior generalization from synthetic to real domains, achieving a precision of 78.9%, recall of 76.5%, and mAP@0.5 of 81.1%. By completely eliminating the need for manual data collection while maintaining high accuracy, EcoDetect delivers a scalable, low-latency solution optimized for real-world deployment and efficient edge operation. The complete dataset, source code, and pretrained weights are publicly available at: https://github.com/TheAhsanFarabi/EcoDetect.

title: An Efficient 3D U-Net with Squeeze-and-Excitation Module for Lung Nodule Segmentation from CT Scans
authors: Md. Sozib Hossain, Md. Rabiul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Early and accurate lung nodule segmentation in 3D computed tomography (CT) images is very useful to radiologists for effective lung cancer diagnosis. Researchers always try to improve the segmentation performance by focusing on the backbone network, which results in a more complex model for 3D segmentation. In this work, an efficient 3D U-Net architecture augmented with a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) module is proposed to enhance channel-wise feature representation and improve nodule segmentation. This end-to-end pipeline begins with raw DICOM and XML data, performing lung volume reconstruction, mask generation, and 3D patch extraction <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(64 \times 64 \times 64$</tex> voxels) to create a high-quality dataset for model training. In addition to conducting extensive experiments on the LIDC-IDRI dataset, we evaluate both the attention-enhanced model and a baseline without SE attention to quantify the impact of channel attention via an ablation study. The proposed model achieves a Dice coefficient of 0.783, IoU of 0.660, precision of 0.761, recall of 0.853 and specificity of 0.997, which demonstrates improved segmentation performance as measured by HD95 and ASD metrics. Compared to the baseline model, the addition of SE modules improves segmentation accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency. The results show the effectiveness of integrating attention mechanisms into 3D U-Net architectures for robust performance. It provides insights for future research in volumetric medical image analysis.

title: Characterization of Ionospheric Scintillation Across South Asia Using Multi-Constellation GNSS Observations
authors: M.F. Shajid, A. Islam, M.G. Mostafa, S.K. Panda
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: From one-year ionospheric scintillation data (July 2024 to June 2025) during the peak solar activity period of Solar Cycle 25, amplitude scintillation index (S4) of two ionospheric scintillation monitoring stations located in South Asia is analyzed. The stations, Dhaka (latitude: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23.81^{\circ} \mathrm{N}$</tex>, longitude: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90.41^{\circ} \mathrm{E})$</tex> and Guntur (latitude: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6. 3 0}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}} \mathbf{N}$</tex>, longitude: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0. 4 6}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}} \mathbf{E})$</tex>, are located near the northern crest of Equatorial Ionospheric Anomaly (EIA) and near the magnetic equator respectively. Analysis shows that the severity of post-sunset scintillation is higher at the EIA region, whereas the occurrence of scintillation is higher at the magnetic equator. Very low occurrence of post-midnight scintillation is found. The sky plots showing the directional distribution of the S4 confirms that severe scintillation predominantly occurs near the equator, whereas at low-latitude, weak scintillation is observed and occurs with higher frequency. At the EIA region, scintillation was higher during the autumnal equinox. However, amongst two equinoctial days, the one occurring closer to the peak of Solar Cycle 25 exhibited stronger scintillation activity. On a day of unusually high scintillation activity observed at both stations, the station located closer to the EIA region exhibited comparatively stronger scintillation. The two-station analysis offers valuable insight into the ionospheric behavior over South Asia, particularly in the region between the EIA and the magnetic equator. Significant spatio-temporal variability in S4 is exhibited. The findings of this study may contribute to the development of effective forecasting and scintillation mitigation models for the low-latitude South Asian region.

title: The Effect of Financial Development on Iraq's Economic Growth
authors: Ala Fathi Assi, Zhiyar Saadi Ali Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The study examines the impact of financial development on Iraq's economic growth from 1991 to 2022, focusing on domestic credit to the private sector, money supply (M2), and inflation. Despite oil's role in Iraq's economy, the importance of financial sector development for diversification and sustainable growth has been emphasized. The Autoregressive Distributed Lag (ARDL) model was used to account for short-run dynamics and long-run cointegration effects. The findings show that domestic credit to the private sector has a significant positive effect on GDP per capita, while M2 has both positive and negative impacts due to inflationary pressures. Inflation is negatively related to growth, confirming the Mundell-Tobin effect, which suggests that high inflation makes it difficult to balance savings investment. These findings highlight the need for specific financial sector reforms in Iraq, such as improved credit allocation mechanisms and inflation-stabilizing policies. The study contributes to the existing literature on the relationship between finance and growth in the Iraqi economy and offers policy implications for other resource-based economies.

title: Under-FET Buried PN Junction T-Sensor for Thermal Management in 22nm FDSOI
authors: Weiquan Hao, Zijin Pan, Xunyu Li, Runyu Miao, Zijian Yue, Zexiao Li, Albert Wang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Modern integrated circuits (IC) suffer from severe self-heating, limiting chip performance and reliability, while complicating systems due to cooling. On-chip thermal management is highly desirable, which however remains impractical due to poor chip-level temperature mapping (T-mapping) limited by existing coplanar temperature sensors (T-sensor). This paper reports the first under-FET T-sensor using buried PN junction structure in commercial CMOS technologies. Demonstrated in a foundry 22nm FDSOI technology, the T-sensor prototype utilizes triple-well structure, native to the FDSOI, to build diode temperature sensors. Simulation and measurement show advantages of new under-FET diode T-sensor over its coplanar counterparts. The prototype under-FET T-sensors achieve high sensitivity that is more than two times higher than coplanar sensors (0.165 K/mW to 0.496 K/mW), in measurement, while saving ~90% of layout area. The new under-FET T-sensor will enable runtime full-chip T-mapping with spatial resolution down to transistor level and potentially revolutionize dynamic thermal management for future chips and systems.

title: Hybrid Relay-Reflecting Intelligent Surface-Assisted Covert Communication for Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Jiayan Li, Yan Liu, Ranran Sun, Weidong Yang, Yulong Shen
abstract: This paper investigates a hybrid relay-reflecting intelligent surface (HR-RIS) assisted covert communication scheme for a satellite-terrestrial network comprising a geostationary Earth orbit (GEO) satellite Alice, a terrestrial legitimate receiver Bob, a terrestrial warden Willie, and an HR-RIS deployed on a building facade. By combining reflective and amplifying elements, the HR-RIS strengthens the legitimate link while suppressing detectability at Willie, which enhances covertness and alleviates severe path loss. Building on channel modeling for satellite-terrestrial and HR-RIS links, we formulate a joint optimization of satellite transmit power and HR-RIS configuration under practical power and covertness constraints, and propose an alternating optimization algorithm to solve it effectively. Simulation results demonstrate that HR-RIS achieves higher covert rates than passive RIS and approaches fully active RIS with reduced power consumption and hardware complexity.

title: First Design Proposal of Capacitor-less DRAM Array with Feedback-enhanced Self-refresh and Crosstalk Suppression
authors: Anzhi Yan, Enyi Zhang, Jingkai Song, Houfang Liu, Yi Yang, Tian-Ling Ren
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The amorphous oxide semiconductor (AOS)-based two-transistor-zero-capacitor (2T0C) dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) has emerged as a promising candidate for next-generation memory technology due to its elimination of trench capacitors, non-destructive readout capability, and potential non-volatility. However, inherent challenges including capacitive coupling and crosstalk have hindered its implementation in array configurations with demonstrated memory functionality. Capacitive coupling induces data disturbances during write/read operations, while crosstalk generates low-resistance pathways between read word lines, triggering sharp current surges, driver overstress, and subsequent readout errors. To address these limitations, this study presents a novel 1 kbit 2T1d DRAM array architecture, integrating complementary cells, rectifier diodes, and feedback-enhanced self-refresh modules. Comprehensive circuit simulation and functional verification demonstrate that both the proposed cell structure and array design successfully execute essential DRAM operations, while also exhibiting scalability for manufacturing implementation. The effective mitigation of capacitive coupling and crosstalk in this array-level realization highlights the viability of 2T1d DRAM technology for embedded memory applications and marks a significant advancement toward practical implementation of capacitor-less DRAM.

title: Towards Smart Healthcare: Custom Transfer Learning Approach to Detect Offline Handwritten Medicine Names in Real-Time
authors: Ariful Islam, Rabeya Begum, Muqit Farhan, Md. Manirul Islam, Md. Mehedi Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Physicians often write in a style intended for the general public, making certain pharmacists difficult to read their prescriptions. This results from doctors' hectic schedules and heavy patient loads, leading them to hastily and sometimes carelessly write prescriptions. Consequently, their handwriting can be extremely challenging to decipher and confusing for both ordinary people and pharmacists. This research used deep learning algorithms and image processing techniques to tackle this problem. The main objective was to develop a system that can accurately identify and predict the names of medicines. A custom CNN architecture based on Transfer Learning addressed the challenge of detecting handwritten medicine names. This technique successfully predicted the medicine names with unseen test data, achieving an average accuracy of 95.80 % with an average 0.55 loss using five different pre-trained models connected with a custom CNN. Moreover, a Python real-time medicine name (RTMN) recognition environment was created to evaluate the model in real-world scenarios. This research investigates the potential of machine vision and artificial intelligence to serve as valuable tools in advanced healthcare systems where handwritten medical prescriptions are still prevalent.

title: BRIGHTER MM+: A Multimodal and EmojiEnriched Emotion Recognition Framework for LowResource Languages
authors: Haseena Bibi Zulfiqar, Touba Ahmed Zulfiqar, Arslan Sher
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Emotion recognition research has historically focused on text data and high-resource languages, overlooking the major role of emojis in digital communication. This research article introduces BRIGHTER MM +, a multimodal extension of the BRIGHTER framework intended for low-resource languages. The collection contains human-annotated, multi-label, intensitybased emotion annotations in seven languages. The experimental results highlight the benefits of emojis on emotion modeling while also revealing limitations in cross-lingual and few-shot scenarios.

title: Al Coated High Birefringence Low Loss Hollow Core Polarization Maintaining Fiber in THz Regime
authors: Kumary Sumi Rani Shaha, Md. Sagor Ali, Mst. Sumaya Akter, Abdul Khaleque, Rosni Sayed, Sakib Sadman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
