title: Relatively Robust Timing of Medical Tests Under Ambiguous Prevalence Dynamics
authors: Thomas A. Weber
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Medical tests are investments in information whose value depends on the prior probability of disease, on how that belief evolves over time, and on actions taken after evidence is observed. Deterministic prevalence dynamics are tractable, but policies calibrated under a single intensity can be unreliable when epidemiological speeds are misspecified. We model belief as evolving deterministically under an autonomous differential equation whose drift is scaled by an unknown multiplicative speed factor. The decision maker may wait, test at a cost, and treat (or not treat) after outcomes, with retesting possible after negative results. For stationary policies defined by two belief thresholds, we obtain a closed-form expression for the expected discounted value, yielding a fast evaluator for computing optimal thresholds. Relative robustness is measured by a performance ratio that compares the value of a candidate threshold pair with the ex-post optimal value under the realized speed factor; relatively robust thresholds maximize the worst-case ratio over an ambiguity interval. We provide a grid-search procedure and a numerical illustration motivated by post-exposure tuberculosis infection screening, where timing decisions unfold over weeks to years and intensity ambiguity is clinically consequential.

title: XAI-Enhanced Stacking Ensemble Framework for Interpretable Water Potability Classification in Bangladesh
authors: Munshaibur Rahman Mahin, Ponkaj Debnath, Pranta Routh, Tonmoy Talukder Rudro, Debajani Paul, Md. Imtiyaz Uddin Ahammed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Ensuring access to potable water remains a critical challenge, particularly in developing countries such as Bangladesh, where contamination from industrial, agricultural, and municipal sources threatens public health. Conventional laboratory testing methods, though accurate, are costly, time-consuming, and unsuitable for large-scale monitoring. Machine learning (ML) offers efficiency and scalability, but most algorithms act as black boxes, limiting interpretability. To address these issues, this study proposes a custom stacking ensemble model integrated with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) to improve both predictive accuracy and transparency. The model is evaluated against ensemble baselines such as Random Forest, XGBoost, and CatBoost. Results show that the stacking model achieves the highest accuracy (98.8 %), precision (99.0 %), recall (97.8 %), and F1score (98.4 %). XAI further provides feature-level insights, clarifying the role of water quality parameters in classification. The integration of stacking with explainability delivers a reliable and cost-effective decision-support framework for water potability assessment, supporting public health and sustainable water management in Bangladesh.

title: Evaluation of a Hybrid Cylindrical Dielectric Resonator Antenna for X and Ku Band Applications
authors: Md. Musfequr Rahman, Md Absar Siddiqui, Mahmudul Hasan, Md. Imran Hossen Rony, Niloy Goswami, M. Tanseer Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The increasing complexity of modern wireless and satellite communication systems, particularly within the X-and Ku-bands, necessitates compact, efficient antennas with stable multiband operation. This work presents a novel Hybrid Cylindrical Dielectric Resonator Antenna (HCDRA) designed for triple-band operation. This antenna is called a hybrid CDRA because it mixes a dielectric resonator with a parasitic metal patch. The antenna, implemented on a Rogers RT5880 substrate, incorporates symmetrical slots, a parasitic copper patch, and a PTFE boundary frame to enhance impedance matching and radiation characteristics. Resonant frequencies of 9.37 GHz, 11.87 GHz, and 15.92 GHz are shown in the simulation results, along with associated return losses of −21.8 dB, −36 dB, and −52.5 dB. The suggested design produces a peak gain of 10 dBi, a radiation efficiency of almost 95% across all bands, and outstanding Voltage Standing Wave Ratio (VSWR) values of 1.17, 1.01, and 1.004. A comparative analysis with previous related research further validates the results, showing that the suggested antenna is suitable for X- and Ku-band applications due to its improved return loss, efficiency, and gain within a compact construction.

title: Impact of Measurement Distance on Antenna Pattern Results Using a Simplified Extrapolation Technique Under Measurement Uncertainty
authors: Le Yu, Wei Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: The simplified extrapolation technique (ET) offers an efficient method for far-field antenna pattern reconstruction by leveraging mid-field measurements along the distance dimension, without requiring phase information. Unlike conventional near-field to far-field transformation methods, ET allows for independent angle-by-angle testing, enabling rapid far-field pattern acquisition. This paper investigates the critical issue of test distance selection in the presence of measurement uncertainty (MU) and analyzes its impact on the accuracy of pattern reconstruction when using two-term and three-term polynomial fitting models. Through theoretical analysis and electromagnetic simulations incorporating reflective interference, we evaluate the trade-offs between truncation error and MU under various distance sampling schemes. Guidelines are provided for optimal distance selection and polynomial model choice to minimize reconstruction errors, offering practical insights for improving the robustness and accuracy of simplified ET in real measurement scenarios.

title: PhishNet: An Attention-Based Deep Learning Model for Website Security
authors: Abid Hossain, Riad Hossain, Ayesha Banu
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Phishing is a prevalent cybercrime that poses a significant threat to online users by employing deceptive techniques to steal sensitive information such as login credentials and credit card details. Malicious actors often exploit phishing websites to collect this data or spread harmful software, using such attacks as an entry point to exploit system vulnerabilities and launch further threats like ransomware. Traditional countermeasures, including blacklisting, whitelisting, and machine learning (ML)-based methods, have been explored. While ML approaches achieve comprehensive results, they often rely on manual feature engineering, leading to increased computational costs. To address this limitation, we propose an end-to-end deep learning-based automatic phishing detection framework employing hybrid models. Specifically, we utilize a CNN-BiLSTM architecture, where CNN captures local correlational features of URLs, and BiLSTM learns long-range semantic dependencies. Building on this, we integrate a multi-head attention mechanism with CNN-BiLSTM to construct a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model. The attention module emphasizes the most relevant features extracted by CNN and BiLSTM, thereby improving classification accuracy and overall system performance. Experimental evaluation on a dataset of 240,000 URLs collected from Kaggle, DMOZ, and Alexa demonstrates that our proposed approach achieves a promising accuracy of 97.50%.

title: AI-driven Stock Price Forecasting in Technology Stocks with Model-Based Feature Selection for a Quantum-Inspired GAN
authors: Suwatchai Piewfad, Phet Aimtongkham, Pakarat Musikawan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Stock price forecasting is challenging due to nonstationarity, high volatility, and the risk of data leakage in feature engineering. We propose a leakage-aware pipeline that (i) audits and removes target-leaking variables, (ii) performs model-based feature selection using LightGBM with Bayesian optimization, and (iii) trains a hybrid QIGAN-AE that generates conservative latent-space augmentations via an autoencoder front-end. QIGAN-AE employs a quantum-inspired rotation layer implemented on classical hardware: a bounded cosine/sine feature map with angle-constrained parameters and a projection readout, designed to stabilize adversarial training under limited financial data. On 310 trading days of Apple (73 features), our audit removes 10 leaking features and the selector retains the top 15 features for forecasting. Under chronological evaluation, QIGANAE achieves the best performance with test $R^{2}=0.6743$ and RMSE $=4.19$, outperforming a baseline GAN $\left(R^{2}=0.6092\right)$ and an LSTM-GAN ($R^{2}=0.6400$). The latent-space design reduces the train-test $R^{2}$ gap compared to the basic QIGAN baseline, suggesting improved generalization when leakage control, feature selection, and conservative augmentation are combined.

title: Sparse Representation-Based Denoising Using Guided Filters for Robust Image Processing Decisions
authors: Tusha, G. Ravivarman, J. Guntaj, Maher Ali Rusho, Harsimrat Kandhari, M. K. Aravindan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Digital photos must be removed for image analysis and machine vision. Its main goal is to reduce visual noise while preserving important information to enhance picture quality. A new denoising approach that combines directed filtering with simple visualizations is presented in this paper. Existing methods have various drawbacks, such as excessive form smoothing or inadequate noise reduction, especially when conditions are noisy. SGFD for Sparse-Guided Filter Denoising is a novel method that addresses these restrictions. This framework combines the guiding filter's edge-preserving power with low-code noise removal. SGFD uses intelligent filtering in the little domain to quickly break up a noisy picture into sparse and precise parts. Then, guided processing is used to improve the outcome while keeping the structural information. The new method shows better performance in keeping image borders, patterns, and fine details while also cutting down on noise by a substantial amount. The trials show that SGFD works better than traditional denoising techniques with regard to of both quantitative metrics like PSNR, SSIM and rapid optical evaluations.

title: Hybrid Xce-DenseNet121 Model for Diagnosis of Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia Integrated with XAI
authors: Afia Tabassum, Jarin Sabah Bushra, Jannatul Ferdous Naba, Sami Zabid, Md Al-Imran, Md. Ehsanul Haque
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and fast classification of cancers like Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) is highly essential due to the fast-spreading nature of the cancer which accounts for about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 5 \%}$</tex> of childhood cancers. As the diagnosis of ALL demands for experts' involvement while also taking enough amount of time at the same time, CAD models (CNN models specially) comes into the picture. Our methodology builds and proposes a novel hybrid model Xce-DenseNet121 combining the strengths of Xception and DenseNet121 models for consideration to diagnose ALL from PBS images. The methodology follows preprocessing of the image data for better feature extraction and data augmentation to tackle generalization issue while applying regularization methods for the same. Our model is evaluated and cross validated against 5 models with the test accuracy of 97.01 % and a cross-validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.42 \pm 0.64 \%$</tex> while outperforming all the other models in results with the same preprocess data set for each model. Our research also includes explainable AI (XAI) with Grad-CAM adding interpretability to our proposed model to address the black box issue.

title: Comparative Analysis of Linear and Closed-Loop Supply Chain Models for Sustainability and Economic Efficiency
authors: Mostafa A. Elshahed, Essam Kaoud, Mahmoud A. El-Sharief, Mahmoud Heshmat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In modern supply chain management, transitioning from linear to closed-loop models presents significant economic and environmental advantages. This study compares the linear supply chain network with an improved closed-loop supply chain (CLSC) designed for sustainability. The linear model follows a traditional pathway of raw material utilization, product manufacturing, distribution, and waste disposal, with limited recycling activities. In contrast, the CLSC integrates reverse logistics practices, allowing used products to be collected, refurbished, remanufactured, and recycled. The study proposes a mixed integer linear programming (MILP) model for the CLSC aims at maximizing the total profit, the optimal results are obtained using CPLEX software. Moreover, the model is validated using a real case study at Assiut University in Egypt. The results reveal that the CLSC improve the profit by 52.8% compared to the linear model. Furthermore, the CLSC promotes sustainability by minimizing waste and optimizing resource use, aligning with sustainable development goals. Therefore, despite its higher complexity, the CLSC demonstrates superior profitability and resource management, making it a model for sustainable industrial practices.

title: BanglaBERT-BiLSTM: A Novel Hybrid Architecture for Multiclass Mental Health Classification in Bengali Social Media Text
authors: Obaidul Haque, Ashraful Hossain, Mariha Tabassum, Rajib Khan, Fahim Shakil Tamim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: To address Bangladesh's severe mental health crisis, this study introduces a novel BanglaBERT-BiLSTM hybrid model for multiclass classification of depression, anxiety, and suicidal ideation from Bengali social media text. Trained on a new, balanced dataset of 4,026 manually annotated texts, our model combines a monolingual transformer's contextual understanding with a BiLSTM's sequential processing. The proposed architecture achieves a state-of-the-art accuracy of 86.60 % and F1 score of 86.68 %, significantly outperforming standalone transformer and other deep learning baselines. This work establishes a new benchmark and presents a practical automated system for early mental health screening, offering a scalable solution to help bridge the critical treatment gap in Bengali-speaking communities.

title: Multi-Parameter Estimation of Lightning Surge Characteristics Using Legendre Transform-Based Feature Extraction and ANFIS Framework
authors: Milad Samady Shadlu, Zahra Moravej
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: Accurate estimation of lightning surge characteristics is crucial for the reliable design and protection of power system equipment. This study presents an artificial intelligence-based framework capable of simultaneously estimating the current, voltage, equivalent resistance, and transferred energy of lightning surges. Nonlinear and temporal features are extracted from surge current and tower voltage signals using a Legendre transform-based feature extraction model. These features are then used to train two independent adaptive neuro-fuzzy inference systems (ANFISs)-one for voltage estimation and the other for current estimation. Accurate predictions of current and voltage further enable the determination of the lightning channel's equivalent resistance and associated energy. The proposed method is validated through simulated lightning scenarios with varying peak surge currents and arrester resistances. The results show that the Legendre-ANFIS framework achieves high estimation accuracy, with root-mean-square errors (RMSEs) below 3% for both current and voltage. Moreover, a comparative study with existing methods in the literature confirms the superior performance of the proposed approach in terms of surge current estimation error.

title: Service-Level Implications of Site-Specific Urban Pathloss and Delay Spread in 5G Networks
authors: Sofia Moukidou, Georgia Athanasiadou, George Tsoulos
abstract: This paper presents a site-specific ray-tracing analysis of urban radio propagation and its impact on 5G service performance. Using high-resolution geographical databases, spatial statistics of isotropic pathloss and RMS delay spread are extracted over a dense urban area. Pathloss follows predictable large-scale behavior, while RMS delay spread is more dominated by urban geometry. When mapped to conservative, service-specific outage thresholds, eMBB and URLLC performance is primarily power-limited as frequency increases, whereas mMTC exhibits near-zero outage under low transmit-power assumptions. For URLLC, a joint power–delay outage formulation is adopted under optimistic and conservative assumptions. The results show that URLLC feasibility is highly sensitive to delay dispersion, with joint outage exceeding 78% in conservative configurations and reducing to approximately 15% under optimistic assumptions.

title: Development of A Web Application to Detect COVID-19-Related Fake News on X
authors: Moyinoluwa Daniel Adesina, Tioluwanimi Olaoluwa Esho, Emmanuel Oladipo Jesubande, Enoch Oluwadamilare Amos-Alegbe, ToluwaseAyobami Olowookere
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The circulation of false information throughout the COVID-19 pandemic created major obstacles for effective public health messaging, especially on social media platforms such as X (previously known as Twitter). This research addresses the problem by creating a web application capable of identifying fake news related to COVID-19, utilizing the Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) model. A labeled dataset from Kaggle was cleaned, preprocessed, and used to fine-tune BERT for classifying news as real or fake. The trained model was deployed in a Flask web app, enabling real-time predictions. Performance evaluation showed the model achieved 91.78% accuracy, with high precision, recall, and F1-score, along with a low evaluation loss of 0.2847, indicating strong generalization. Usability testing confirmed that the application is both user-friendly and effective for verifying COVID-19 information. Overall, this study highlights how advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques can combat misinformation and be adapted for broader applications.

title: MSF-RGBNet: A Lightweight Multi-Stage Fusion Architecture for Deepfake Image Detection
authors: Pranab Roy, Pankaj Roy
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deepfake images-synthetically manipulated visuals generated through Artificial Intelligence-often alter or replace a person's face in photos or videos, producing results that are nearly indistinguishable from real content. While beneficial in domains such as entertainment and education, deepfake technology also poses risks including misinformation, cyberbullying, identity theft, and political manipulation. Detecting such manipulated content has therefore become a crucial task in multimedia forensics. Although recent advancements in deep learning, particularly with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), have shown promise, many existing approaches rely heavily on pre-trained models with minimal customization. In addition, other challenges such as high computational complexity, class imbalance, and limited dataset size hinder the performance, and real-time applicability of existing models. To address these challenges, this study proposes MSF-RGBNet-a lightweight CNN architecture that performs multi-stage fusion of RGB channels at different hierarchical levels to enhance fine-grained feature extraction. Unlike conventional single-stream approaches, our method processes each color channel independently through parallel convolutional pipelines, followed by selective fusion stages to share discriminative information efficiently. The proposed model achieved 99.20 % accuracy on the 140 K Real and Fake Faces dataset and 98.07% on the Celeb-DF dataset, demonstrating strong performance and robustness across different sources. Result analysis demonstrates strong performance across standard metrics, while feature map visualizations reveal interpretable progressive feature learning, and comparative evaluations further underscore its superior performance and efficiency over recent state-of-the-art and lightweight architectures, emphasizing its suitability for real-world deepfake detection applications.

title: Self-Organizing Map Feature Selection Method for Diagnosing Early-Stage Parkinson’s Disease Patients
authors: Weikang Hou, Changqin Quan, KangRen, Zhiwei Luo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: One of the most prominent symptoms in earlystage patients of parkinson disease is speech impairment. This paper proposes a self-organizing map network clustering feature reduction strategy based on parkinson’s disease patient speech data to eliminate redundant acoustic features. Combined with neural networks, this approach distinguishes early-stage PD patients. Experimental results demonstrate that across three distinct neural network architectures, modeling with the filtered feature subset achieves improved performance in Accuracy, F1 Score, Sensitivity, and Specificity compared to raw acoustic features. This demonstrates that feature selection methods, when applied to parkinson disease speech data research alongside neural network approaches, can significantly optimize model performance and predictive capability while also enhancing feature representation and acoustic feature interpretability.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Linkedin Popularity Classification
authors: W.P.T.N. Pathiranage, B.T.G.S. Kumara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The popularity of social media, and that of LinkedIn in particular, serves a key role in increasing career visibility, professional impact, and access to opportunities. Proper forecasting of followers is a useful source of information on personal branding, recruitment intelligence, and digital professional network dynamics. The report proposes a multimodal deep learning model that forecasts the range of follower counts on LinkedIn by using professional characteristics, demographic factors, as well as facial-emotional attributes obtained through profile images. The framework employs a multi-branch neural network in order to integrate structured tabular data with the visual-emotional features, which has multifaceted, non-linear correlations between heterogeneous features. Through the use of professional and visual-emotional portrayal, the model takes into consideration those aspects which contribute to acquisition of followers, such as career advancement, credibility, appeal, as well as engagement tendencies. The prediction of the multimodal 1D CNN is tested on classical machine learning controls, such as, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, and Gradient Boosting Machine. The findings indicate that the suggested model works better than the conventional ones with better accuracy, higher F1-scores, and fewer errors, proving the usefulness of deep learning modeling multimodal interactions. Explainable AI methods, such as SHAP and LIME, take the decisions made by the model and measure the significance of various groups of features. Professional attributes are found to be the strongest predictors, facial-emotional features play a moderating role, and demographic characteristics play a minor role in deciding, which shows how career development and profile presentation are more crucial than demographic factors.

title: A Novel Hybrid Quantum-Classical Machine Learning Paradigm for Improved Pattern Detection in Medical and Biological Data
authors: Kubra Noor, Ubaida Fatima
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: The advent of Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) machines has brought new opportunities for expanding machine learning abilities with the help of quantum computing. Herein, a complete hybrid quantum-classical learning system is introduced that incorporates classical preprocessing, quantum feature extraction, and classical machine learning in a strategic way to surpass the performance of existing systems for the tasks of complex pattern recognition. We perform large-scale experiments with two popular benchmark datasets: the Iris flower dataset and the Wisconsin Breast Cancer Diagnostic dataset. Our framework shows consistently improving accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-7 \%$</tex> over stark classical methods without sacrificing practicality on state-of-the-art NISQ hardware. With rigorous ablation studies and cross-validation with cutting-edge approaches, we conclude that the best task distribution between traditional and quantum processors is key to near-term quantum advantage. Our introduced framework effectively achieves a quantum circuit depth reduction of 40 % using smart classical preprocessing along with improving classification performance using quantum-augmented feature representation. Our implementation offers both hardware-based execution with Qiskit as well as fast classical simulation modes, so it is available for researchers with limited quantum computing capabilities.

title: CNN-LSTM Based Probabilistic Models: Enhanced Financial Fraud Risk Decisions in Engineering
authors: Utkarsh Anand, Shinki Katyayani Pandey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Fraud in the financial sector remains a major problem, as it damages the integrity of financial institutions and can cause substantial economic costs. Accurate and timely prediction of fraudulent activity is an essential part of risk mitigation. The mainstays of conventional fraud detection approaches, such as rule-based systems and shallow machine learning models, are severely lacking when it comes to dealing with complicated, high-dimensional data. Worse yet, current methods struggle mightily in fast-paced financial settings due to their inability to discern patterns within complicated time-series data in the pursuit of fraudulent actions. An enhanced model is required for time series data that makes use of deep learning approaches in addition to probabilistic reasoning procedures. The Probabilistic Morphic Algorithm Convolutional Neural Network with Long Short-Term Memory Method (PMA-CNNLSTM) is a new model for detecting financial fraud. To aid in the detection of fraud, the hybrid model will include branching factors derived from sequential learning and probabilistic reasoning for feature extraction. According to the study's empirical findings, the proposed model is more effective than the conventional one at detecting fraud.

title: Bias and Gate-Resistance Dependent Self-Sustained Oscillations in p-GaN Schottky HEMTs under Short Circuit
authors: Kulisa Nanayakkara, Alberto Castellazzi
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work presents an experimental investigation of a self-sustained oscillation in p-GaN gate HEMTs under short-circuit overload conditions. A novel oscillation mechanism is identified, distinct from previously reported cases, which is also associated with the gate driver design. The analysis suggests that the instability is driven by a positive feedback mechanism associated with the gate-drain capacitance and the dynamics of the gate-drain voltage. A parametric analysis of the drain bias voltage and the gate resistance is presented. The findings provide useful insight for optimizing gate driver design towards suppressing this unstable behavior and guarantee the required device robustness under short-circuit conditions.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Multiclass Dermatology Image Recognition Using Deep and Classical Machine Learning
authors: Zarif Wasif Bhuiyan, Zaed Bin Monir Atib, Mahady Hasan, Afroza Sultana, Md. Tarek Habib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Timely recognition of common skin conditions is essential for early therapy and better outcomes, yet many clinics, particularly in resource-constrained settings, lack access to dermatology expertise. We present a practical system for automated recognition across ten classes, trained and evaluated on a balanced corpus of 17,800 clinical photographs with standardized resizing, normalization, and modest geometric and photometric augmentation. The diagnostic panel comprises Basal Cell Carcinoma, Drug Eruptions, Healthy Skin, Herpes and Human Papillomavirus, Hyperpigmentation, LightInduced Pigment Disorders, Lupus and Connective Tissue Diseases, Melanoma/Nevi/Moles, Vascular Tumors, and Vasculitis. A transparent holdout protocol reserved 10% of the data for final testing, with the remainder partitioned into training and validation to enable tuning without leakage. Within a unified pipeline and identical preprocessing, we focused on a custom convolutional neural network (CNN) designed for accuracy and efficiency in routine clinical imagery. On the independent test set, the custom CNN achieved 98% accuracy. Confusion analyses indicated residual errors concentrated in illumination-sensitive pigmentary presentations, while calibration suggested reliable probability estimates across classes. The model is compact and fast, supporting integration into tele-dermatology and primary care triage where specialist access is constrained. It establishes a credible path to scalable, trustworthy screening support for real-world deployments.

title: Efficient Data Erasure in Deep Learning via Sparsity and Stability Mechanisms
authors: Urbana Jaman Orthee, Abu Saleh Muhammad Naeem, Tamanna Khatun, Meharun Ohona, Nafees Mansoor
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Machine unlearning (MU) has emerged as a critical paradigm to ensure trained models can selectively remove the influence of specific data without requiring full retraining. While early MU methods addressed privacy regulations such as the “right to be forgotten”, they often suffer from inefficiency, instability, or degraded model performance at scale. To advance MU toward practical deployment, this study introduces a sparsityenhanced unlearning framework that integrates pruning-based model reduction with stability-focused optimization strategies. Two novel methods are proposed: GA Repair, an extension of Gradient Ascent that alternates destructive forgetting with robust repair phases, and FT Confusion, a Fine-Tuning variant that employs entropy maximization and anchored preservation to achieve class-level erasure. Both approaches are evaluated on ResNet-18 with the CIFAR-10 benchmark under class-wise and instance-level forgetting scenarios. Results show that sparsity consistently narrows the gap between approximate unlearning and retraining, improving computational efficiency while maintaining stability. FT Confusion achieves retrain-level forgetting in class-wise tasks, whereas GA Repair provides stronger fidelity in random instance-level forgetting. These findings highlight that no single unlearning strategy is universally optimal; instead, scalable unlearning requires a portfolio of methods, with sparsity as a key enabler for efficiency and robustness.

title: BPSO-IDS: Scalarized Multi-Objective Feature Selection for Intrusion Detection
authors: Fardin Hoque, Nowrin Muhaimin Shailee, Md. Sakir Hossain, Tanjib Patwary, Fazlay Rabbi Tusher, S. M. Sadakatul Bari
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Intrusion detection systems (IDS) are critical for safeguarding modern networks, but their performance often depends on handling high-dimensional data with redundant and irrelevant features. In this paper, we employ particle swarm optimization (PSO) for feature selection and propose a scalarized multi-objective optimization framework that balances classification accuracy with the number of selected features. The fitness function incorporates both objectives, allowing the search process to favor compact yet highly discriminative feature subsets. To adapt PSO for this task, we implement a binary population representation, where each particle encodes the selection or exclusion of features. The proposed method is evaluated on two widely used benchmark datasets: CICIDS2017 and UNSW-NB15. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves 99.88% accuracy on CICIDS2017 with 35 selected features and 90.09% accuracy on UNSW-NB15 with 13 selected features, using XGBoost and Random Forest classifiers, respectively, for multiclass classification. Compared to existing state-of-theart techniques, the proposed method yields superior detection performance with significantly fewer features.

title: AI-Integrated Clinical Decision Support Framework for Emergency Triage
authors: Muhammad Wasim Munir, Syeda Fakhra Jalal, Amna Jawed Shaikh, Muhammad Zeeshan Ul Haque
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Early patient assessment is essential for effective healthcare delivery; however, the delayed diagnosis, inefficient appointment scheduling, and the absence of real time clinical decision support reduce clinical efficiency and limit timely intervention. This study presents a framework for the Clinical Decision Support System (CDSS) based on machine learning to enable early risk assessment and improve patient prioritization in clinical settings. The proposed system provides a web based graphical user interface through which patients can enter symptoms and relevant health information, allowing it to analyze the data and identify potential early stage respiratory diseases such as influenza, seasonal sore throat infections, and lung cancer. To integrate the best ML model for prediction, three supervised machine learning algorithms:Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Decision Tree were implemented and evaluated using a publicly available lung cancer dataset. Model performance was assessed based on classification accuracy and predictive capability, and the results demonstrated that the Decision Tree model achieved the highest classification performance among the evaluated approaches. The selected model was integrated into the CDSS framework to enable real time risk prediction and automated identification of high-risk patients, assisting healthcare professionals in prioritizing critical cases and supporting timely clinical intervention. In addition, the system architecture allows for future integration of risk-based appointment scheduling to prioritize patients according to predicted disease risk, reduce delays in care, and improve overall clinical workflow efficiency.

title: Pixel Size Analysis for Enhanced Image Resolution to Support Diagnostic Decisions in Digital Microscopy
authors: Shital S. Kewte, Deepak Minhas, K. Yuvaraj, Ish Kapila, H. Adarsha, Pritam Paraye
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Image resolution in digital microscopy is reliant upon pixel size, and pixel size is a significant contributor to Resolution. It is understood that the smaller the pixel size, the better the spatial Resolution. However, smaller pixels also reduce sensitivity and increase noise. Typically, imaging approaches provide Resolution and sensitivity that do not provide a balance across these mappings and can suffer from oversampling, undersampling, loss of detail, or aliasing. Addressing this gap, the authors apply a Quantitative Image Analysis framework using the USAF 1951 resolution chart under controlled conditions to examine Resolution across different pixel sizes. This, in turn, allows for the accurate determination of the smallest features that can be resolved, as well as a comparative analysis of sensor measurements in a unified way. This approach provides a method to identify the optimum pixel size based on the signal-to-noise ratio, ensuring only Nyquist sampling is satisfied. There are pooling methods to express "have better resolution" to a limit of smaller pixels, and larger gain is not necessarily equated to more sensitive measurements. The contributions demonstrate that balancing imaging clarity and sensitivity is crucial for achieving practical imaging performance while pursuing improvements in digital microscopy.

title: Influence of COTS Power Diodes packaging on their resilience against gamma radiation
authors: Hawaibam Thoi Thoi Singh, Cher Ming Tan, Debraj Banerjee, Vishal Srivaths
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work investigates γ-irradiation effects on commercially available power diodes with different package types. Electrical parameters (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</inf>, n) were measured before and after 25 Gy exposure using a Cs137 source. Results reveal strong package-dependent degradation emphasizing packaging’s role in radiation resilience.

title: Data-Efficient Image Transformers for Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Md. Abir Hossen, Md. Lalon Hossain, Chinmoy Modak Turjo, Tonmoy Ghosh
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Skin cancer causes a serious threat to global health, with early and accurate identification being fundamental to improving patient outcomes and survival rates. This research investigates the deployment of transformer architectures for automated dermoscopic image analysis, employing the HAM10000 public dataset containing seven distinct lesion categories for evaluation. Five advanced transformer types: Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16), Swin Transformer, Dual Attention Vision Transformer (DaViT), ConvNeXt, and Data-Efficient Image Transformer (DeiT) underwent fine-tuning on the dermoscopic dataset. All models were initialized using pretrained weights from ImageNet-1K. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance by the DeiT architecture, recording 90.60% classification accuracy with an 82.94% macro-averaged F1-score on the test partition. These metrics surpass alternative approaches: ViT (89.60%), Swin-B (89.45%), ConvNeXt (88.65%), and DaViT (87.90%). In this analysis reveals that efficiency-focused transformer designs effectively address class imbalance challenges inherent in dermoscopic datasets while demonstrating improved recognition capabilities for underrepresented lesion types.

title: Fair Personalization under Signal Loss: A Customer Equity Framework for Responsible Retail Media Networks
authors: Sabar Aritonang Rajagukguk, Juwaria Muqtadir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The proliferation of retail media networks amidst third-party signal deprecation presents a critical challenge for maintaining fair personalization without compromising customer equity. With 71% of advertisers recognizing incrementality as the most important KPI in retail media network investments and nearly 90% of ad buyers changing personalization tactics due to signal loss, this study addresses the research gap on measuring fairness-accuracy tradeoffs in customer lifetime value optimization using public first-party signals. We propose a Fair-by-Design Personalization Framework that leverages review sentiment, search patterns, and social engagement data to maintain equitable recommendations across demographic proxies. Using a dataset of 2.3 million e-commerce interactions across 15 product categories, our two-tower transformer architecture with fairness constraints achieved 94.2% accuracy while maintaining demographic parity within 3.1% across sensitive attribute proxies. The framework demonstrates superior fairness-accuracy balance compared to traditional collaborative filtering (89.7% accuracy, 12.4% fairness gap) and standard neural collaborative filtering approaches (91.3% accuracy, 8.9% fairness gap). Our contribution establishes a measurable pathway toward responsible customer relationship management practices in signal-constrained environments, with practical implications for the $81.6 billion retail media market projected for 2025.

title: Rice Leaf Disease Classification: A Comparative Evaluation of CNNs and Vision Transformers
authors: Apu Islam, Ishraque Arefin Rafi, Somaya Al Sadia Rahman, Sudipta Mondal, Md. Golam Rabiul Alam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Rice is a staple crop worldwide but is highly vulnerable to leaf diseases that threaten food security. Traditional manual inspection is slow, labor-intensive, and error-prone, highlighting the need for automated detection. This research introduces a comparative analysis between convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) for multi-class rice leaf disease classification. Using an 11-class dataset of bacterial, fungal, viral, pest-related, and healthy leaves, we evaluated ResNet50, MobileNetV2, BEiT, DeiT, Swin Transformer V2, and Swin-Tiny V2. Results show transformer models outperform CNNs, with the quantized Swin-Tiny V2 Transformer achieving 99.56% accuracy while reducing model size to 26.89 MB, making it suitable for mobile and IoT deployment. To enhance interpretability and understand the model's decisions, Grad-CAM visualizations highlight important regions, ensuring transparency in model predictions. The findings demonstrate the potential of lightweight, explainable transformers for realworld agricultural IoT applications in precision farming.

title: A Literature Review about Federated Learning in Recommendation Systems
authors: João Gaspar, João Cruz, Carlos Rodrigues, André Rodrigues, Isabel Ribeiro, Diogo Martinho, Joaquim Santos, Goreti Marreiros
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The increasing demand for personalized services has led to widespread adoption of recommendation systems across various domains, from entertainment to e-commerce. Traditional centralized implementations, however, raise privacy and data management concerns. This study investigates federated learning as a decentralized approach for recommendation systems, where multiple clients collaboratively train a global model while keeping their local data private. We provide a detailed analysis of different federated learning partitions-horizontal, vertical, and transfer learning-and examine techniques for integrating local updates into a global model. The paper also explores challenges such as heterogeneity, communication overhead, and privacy-preserving mechanisms, highlighting their impact on model performance and deployment. By synthesizing current approaches and evaluating their applicability in the context of ecommerce recommendation systems, this work offers actionable insights for designing scalable, privacy-aware, and adaptive federated learning frameworks, contributing to both practical implementation and academic research in the field.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Visual Sequence Models for Depression Severity Assessment
authors: Seyedehmoniba Ravan, Fabian Schmidt, Vladimir Vlassov
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Depression is commonly assessed through subjective clinical interviews and self-report questionnaires, which limit objectivity and scalability in digital mental health settings. This work investigates whether facial behavioral cues alone can support an automated and uncertainty-aware estimation of depressive symptom severity, measured using the PHQ-8 (Patient Health Questionnaire-8) total score in the range 0-24. Using only visual features extracted with OpenFace - namely, facial action units, head pose, and eye gaze - we model each clinical interview in the E-DAIC dataset as a multivariate temporal sequence. We develop two probabilistic deep sequence architectures: a Transformer with context carry-over across sliding windows, and a bidirectional LSTM with attention. Both models output predictive distributions over PHQ-8 scores, enabling the calculation of mean estimates and uncertainty quantification. Evaluated on the official E-DAIC splits, the probabilistic Transformer achieves a test MAE of 4.96 and RMSE of 5.96, while the Bi-LSTM+Attention model attains 5.18 MAE and 6.10 RMSE, noticeably improving upon hand-crafted visual baselines. Results indicate that facial behavior provides a meaningful signal for coarse-grained PHQ-8 estimation and that probabilistic formulations enhance interpretability through confidence intervals. While visual cues alone are insufficient for clinical diagnosis, the findings support their use as an assistive component in scalable, uncertainty-aware systems for assessing depression.

title: How Social Media Commerce and eWOM Shape User Technology Acceptance and Information Adoption for MSMEs in Denpasar, Bali
authors: Putu Jessita Aryati Dewi, Ni Kadek Intan Pramesti Kencana Dewi, Yuniarty, Ridho Bramulya Ikhsan, Kartika Aprilia Benhardy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research investigates how social media marketing (SMM) and electronic word-of-mouth (eWOM) shape consumers’ purchase intentions in Bali’s fashion micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs). The study also examines the mediating effects of perceived ease of use (PEU), perceived usefulness (PUF), and information adoption (IAD) within this context. A quantitative descriptive approach was adopted, collecting data from 209 respondents aged 18–35 who actively use Instagram and have previously purchased fashion products from MSMEs via social media. The collected data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares–Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) to evaluate the hypothesized relationships. Results indicate that SMM and eWOM significantly enhance both PEU and PUF, which, in turn, foster information adoption. This adoption then strongly influences consumers’ purchase intentions. The findings underscore the importance of interactive and user-oriented digital engagement in shaping consumer perceptions and decision-making in online fashion markets. By highlighting the central role of information adoption as a mediating mechanism, this study advances understanding of digital consumer behavior. Practically, the insights offer MSME practitioners guidance for optimizing social media strategies, emphasizing trust-building, interactivity, and user-friendly experiences to strengthen consumers’ readiness to purchase.

title: Integrating Cybersecurity Awareness Into Civil Aviation Resilience: A Systems-Based Framework
authors: Nimantha T. Thalagala, Vihara S. Jayawickrama, H. Niles Perera
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study investigates the role of cybersecurity awareness in strengthening aviation cyber resilience and develops a comprehensive framework integrating awareness with key dimensions of aviation cybersecurity. Using qualitative content analysis and MAXQDA software, the research systematically examines aviation cybersecurity literature, published interviews, and industry reports. Five thematic areas emerged: Cyber Threats in Aviation, Cybersecurity Awareness and Training, Aviation Cybersecurity Regulations and Standards, Technologies for Enhancing Aviation Cybersecurity, and Future of Aviation Cybersecurity. The study introduces the Integrated Cyber Awareness-Resilience Architecture (ICARA), a multi-layered framework linking human, organizational, technological, and regulatory dimensions through feedback loops and a central resilience cycle. The framework provides actionable guidance for developing effective, context-driven awareness programs that enhance cyber resilience and foster a proactive, adaptive cybersecurity culture within the aviation ecosystem. Additionally, the study identifies significant future research gaps within these thematic areas, emphasizing the need for empirical validation, behavioral measurement, and crosssector collaboration to advance aviation cyber resilience.

title: Plasmonic Enhancement of Perovskite Absorber Layers Using Stable Metal Nanoparticles
authors: Alaa Elsafi, Mohammad Istiaque Hossain, Puvaneswaran Chelvanathan, Brahim Aissa
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Efficient light management remains a key challenge in perovskite solar cells, particularly for thin absorber layers. Plasmonic metal nanostructures provide an effective route to enhance light–matter interaction through localized surface plasmon resonances (LSPRs). This work presents a comparative study on plasmonic metal nanoparticles (Au, Ag, Al, and Ti) and their influence on the optical response of perovskite absorber layers.Ultrathin metal films were deposited on quartz substrates and thermally annealed at 500–700 °C to induce solid-state dewetting and nanoisland formation. The annealing process leads to metal-dependent morphological evolution, producing size-tunable nanoparticles that act as plasmonic templates prior to perovskite deposition. For annealing temperatures above 500 °C, the nanoparticles exhibit a near-Gaussian size distribution with an average diameter of ~25 nm.Optical measurements reveal distinct metal-dependent plasmonic behavior, with LSPR features near 500 nm and a broad spectral tail arising from nanoparticle size dispersion. As shown in Fig. 1, Ag and Al nanoislands show the strongest visible-range absorption, while Au exhibits moderate enhancement. Ti-based films display broadband absorption dominated by oxide-related scattering rather than classical plasmon resonance. When integrated beneath the perovskite absorber in a planar architecture, plasmonic nanoislands enhance absorption in the 350–750 nm range, yielding visible-light enhancement of ~5–10% and relative efficiency gains up to 35.51%, depending on nanoparticle size and surface coverage. These results highlight the importance of metal selection and annealing conditions for engineering plasmonic–perovskite interfaces and enhancing light management.

title: PESCO-BERT: An Efficient Prompt-Based Contrastive Learning for Bangla News Classification
authors: Mithila Arman, Ashiqul Islam, Md Moinul Hoque, Mohammed Mahmudur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In this work, propose a scalable method for multiclass Bangla news categorization that combines a Bangla-specific data curation pipeline with contrastive, prompt-based fine-tuning of BanglaBERT. Through Unicode and label normalization, punctuation and digit harmonization, source and time-aware splits using shingled-n-gram MinHash, and light minority oversampling, the pipeline hops over label noise, orthographic variation, class imbalance, and data leakage, respectively. Modeling layer. PESCO (Prompt Ensemble Self-Contrastive) and therefore each article is represented as two semantically relevant but stylistically different prompts and trained using a combined loss of weighted cross-entropy and supervised contrastive loss per-class weighting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=0.5$</tex>. To make training practical on commodity hardware, employ QLoRA (4-bit NF4 with safe fallbacks), LoRA adapters on attention matrices, gradient checkpointing, mixed precision and conservative micro-batching. BanglaBERT-PESCO achieves 98.89 % accuracy and time-aware splits of the Bangla Newspaper Dataset, outperforming other models including BanglaBERT (base and large), XLM-R (base and large), M-BERT, and a QLoRA-tuned LLaMA-3.

title: An IoT-Based Semi-Automated Library Management System with ESP32 & Arduino
authors: Aditi Bhattacharjee, Ratin Barua, Rakibul Hasan, Mim Majumder, Tonny Chowdhury, Md. Imam Uddin Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper introduces a semi-automated, costeffective Library Management System (LMS) that combines a C\# desktop application with IoT-based hardware for real-time book tracking and automated shelf movement, which can improve access to books and reduce manual workload in institutions with limited resources. The hardware uses an ESP32 microcontroller, Arduino Pro Mini, IR obstacle sensors, relay-driven DC motors, and LEDs, chosen for affordability while maintaining reliable performance. Unlike software-only LMS platforms, the system automates book issuing and returning while still allowing manual operation when needed. Experimental results show 100% accuracy in book status updates, 98.8% accuracy in shelf movement, and an average response time of 1.92 seconds, including sensor delay (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87.5 \pm 12.3 ~\text{ms}$</tex>), network delay (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$215.8 \pm 33.6 ~\text{ms})$</tex>, and motor actuation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1620.3 \pm 145.2 ~\text{ms}$</tex>). Across 50 test runs, the system completed 96% of cycles successfully, with rare errors corrected through manual recovery. Future extensions will integrate RFID/QR identification, cloud databases, and mobile applications to achieve a scalable and deployable next-generation library solution.

title: Perspectives for the Use of AI as a Tool for the Design and Implementation of Learning Centered Curricula
authors: Lars Kölker, Florian Eggenkemper, Florian Schimanke, Henning Klafke, Robert Mertens
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: Curricula are a fundamental component of the education system. Depending on the university, students can influence the schedule or are bound to a fixed curriculum. However, the courses offered in the curriculum are not always aligned with each other, which results in course content being repeated at inconvenient times, leading to learning methods with poor educational value (e.g., cramming). This paper investigates the application of artificial intelligence (AI) in designing personalized curricula to harness its potential benefits.

title: A Comparative Performance Analysis of Cloud Storage Platforms in a Bandwidth-Constrained Wired Network
authors: Sheila Mae S. Pagayonan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Cloud storage platforms are now widely used in academic and organizational settings for routine file transfer and data management. However, differences in upload and download efficiency across platforms remain evident, particularly in wired networks with limited bandwidth. This research presents a comparative evaluation of Google Drive, Dropbox, and Microsoft OneDrive by examining their upload and download performance under controlled wired network conditions. Performance was analyzed based on file size and time of access. Four desktop computers with identical specifications were connected to a wired network configured at 512 kbps. Files were grouped into three categories–light (5 MB), average (10 MB), and heavy (15 MB)–and transfer activities were carried out simultaneously during morning and afternoon sessions. Mean values were used to describe performance trends, while one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was employed to test for statistically significant differences among platforms. The results show that Dropbox consistently provided faster upload performance across most test conditions. In contrast, Google Drive and Microsoft OneDrive exhibited slower upload speeds but delivered relatively stable and efficient download performance. Statistical analysis confirmed significant differences in upload performance among the cloud storage platforms, whereas download performance showed no significant differences in most cases, except for light file downloads during the morning session. Overall, the findings indicate that upload efficiency is more sensitive to platform selection than download efficiency in a bandwidth-constrained wired network. These results offer practical insight for users and institutions when selecting cloud storage services under similar network conditions.

title: AI Adoption in Software Development: TOE-Based Drivers of Productivity Enhancement in Sri Lanka
authors: N. S. M. I. S. Senanayake, W. M. J. I. Wijayanayake, Shan Jayasinghe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study investigates how Artificial Intelligence (AI) adoption enhances software development productivity in Sri Lanka using the Technology Organization Environment (TOE) framework. A quantitative survey was conducted with 158 professionals occupying diverse roles in Sri Lankan software firms, exceeding the required minimum sample size of 157 derived from Cohen's power analysis for multiple predictors. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS SEM) was used to examine relationships among six TOE based drivers, AI adoption, and productivity enhancement. The results show that Organizational Readiness, Relative Advantage, and Top Management Support have significant positive effects on AI adoption, while Technological Readiness, Competitive Pressure, and Service Delivery do not exhibit significant relationships. AI adoption strongly predicts productivity improvement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.436, \mathrm{p}<0.001)$</tex> and mediates the impact of organizational and technological factors on productivity outcomes. These findings indicate that productivity gains arise when organizational readiness and leadership support are translated into structured AI adoption strategies rather than ad hoc experimentation. The study extends TOE based adoption research to the context of AI supported software engineering in a developing economy and offers a strategic roadmap for managers to strengthen organizational readiness, leadership involvement, and targeted skill development for systematic AI driven productivity improvement.

title: Explainable Multimodal Transformers for Rubric-Aligned Job Interview Evaluation
authors: Anju B. Nandrajog, Rupinder Kaur, Shelley Khosla, Arunima Mishra, Vishal Vishal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Interviews are a cornerstone of recruitment but remain prone to inconsistency, rater drift, and subconscious bias. To address these challenges, we propose AV-InterviewFormer, a multimodal transformer framework that jointly models vision and audio streams for structured interview evaluation. The model processes video features (facial cues, gaze, posture, gestures) via a TimeSformer-based encoder and audio features (prosody, spectral embeddings, disfluencies) via a spectrogram transformer, fusing them through cross-modal attention with gated integration. Outputs include four rubric-based scores—Communication, Problem-Solving, Collaboration & Empathy, Professional Presence—and an overall hire probability with uncertainty estimation and fairness regularization. Evaluated on 1,280 annotated mock interviews ( ≈155 hours), AV-InterviewFormer achieved substantial gains over unimodal and late-fusion baselines. For the primary hire classification task, the model reached an F1-score of 82.7% and an AUC of 0.889, compared to audio-only (F1 = 76.4%, AUC = 0.841) and video-only baselines (F1 = 74.8%, AUC = 0.812). Calibration also improved significantly, with Expected Calibration Error reduced to 0.042, a 43% improvement over unimodal approaches. For rubric prediction, the model achieved Mean Absolute Errors of 0.38–0.43, corresponding to 18–24% error reductions, with Concordance Correlation Coefficients of 0.62–0.68, closely aligning with human raters. Robustness tests confirmed graceful degradation, with only −3.2 F1 under 0 dB noise and −2.7 F1 under 20% face occlusion. Fairness regularization reduced the Demographic Parity gap from 0.051 to 0.033 (−36%) and the Equalized Odds gap from 0.084 to 0.057 (−32%), while lowering the Brier score from 0.162 to 0.139. These results establish AV-InterviewFormer as a robust, interpretable, and fairness-conscious framework for augmenting recruiters with reliable multimodal analytics.

title: AI Agent-Based Automated Essay Marking Framework Using Large Language Models
authors: Seynath Thenura, Dinesh Asanka, Thilini Mahanama, Chathura Rajapakse
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Manual essay grading is time-consuming, subjective, and difficult to scale. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have enabled Automated Essay Scoring (AES) systems, but a single LLM used in isolation often lacks the domain-specific knowledge, consistency, and transparency required for high-stakes educational assessment. This paper presents an AI Agent-Based Automated Essay Marking Framework that addresses these limitations by orchestrating LLMs within a multi-agent architecture augmented with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). In the design, a team of specialized AI agents (supervisor, retrieval, evaluation, tool, and quality assurance) collaboratively executes a rubric-based grading workflow, retrieving relevant course content and iteratively refining the scoring process to ensure accuracy and fairness. The framework offers a transparent, rubric-aligned, and domain-aware grading process, shifting from a black-box model-centric approach to a process-centric paradigm. Experimental evaluation against human-marked essays demonstrates that the proposed system achieves high alignment with expert graders, with the best configuration (Gemini 2.5 Flash with RAG and multi-agent orchestration) achieving a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.67 (SD =0.60, 95% CI: 0.49-0.85). The inclusion of RAG reduced MAE by 35.7%(p=0.136, trend toward significance) compared to the non-RAG configuration, while the multi-agent architecture improved accuracy by 16.7%(p=0.043, significant) over singleagent baselines. This work contributes a promising architectural framework for next-generation AES, showing that an agent-based approach using LLMs can provide trustworthy automated grading and detailed feedback, effectively augmenting human educators.

title: Dual-Band Leaf Shape Array Antenna Design with Gain and Directivity Optimization Through Array Expansion
authors: Abul Hasnat Tanvir, Abdul Al Rafi, Fahmida Faiza Lisa, Md. Aminur Rahman, Nafis Almas Nafi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The subsequent development in cellular technology is designed to improve network responsiveness and speed. 5G makes it possible to enhance bandwidth and data transmission capacity for advanced antenna technology. This study explores an approach for improving the gain and directivity of dual-frequency microstrip patch array antennas by increasing the array. With a gain of 8.305 dBi and 6.464 dBi and directivity of 14.21 dBi and 15.89 dBi, respectively, the proposed antenna operates at 2.5 GHz and 4.88 GHz. Current study examines four different cases of array arrangements, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 1,1 \times 2,1 \times 4,1 \times 8$</tex>, showing that the gain and directivity are gradually improved by enlarging the array. From the Finite Element Method (FEM) simulation it was found that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 8$</tex> array results in the maximum gain and a narrower beam width. In the study close agreement between the simulation and lab test using Vector Network Analyzer ensures the validity of the simulation technique. This paper studies the influence of array size on performance in a systematic way, providing an economical approach to improve antenna performance for applications requiring high directivity and accurate beamforming. The results are also useful for such purposes as designing compact high-gain antennas used in today's wireless and radar systems.

title: Hybrid Bankruptcy Prediction: A Risk Management Approach for Investors
authors: Md Masum Billah, Azmery Sultana, Mohammad Shorif Uddin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Efforts to effectively forecast the bankruptcy of corporations are necessary to enhance the effective management of risks that rely on investor interests in the current highly dynamic financial times. This paper creates an intelligent framework hybrid that combines several machine learning models, including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, and Support Vector Machines, with explainable AI (XAI) methods to improve predictive accuracy and interpretability. The system takes a massive financial data set of the Taiwan Economic Journal that includes 96 financial variables, 6,819 firm-years, and conducts feature engineering, normalization, and model fusion to reflect the nonlinearity of credit distresses of firms. The results of the experiment show that Gradient Boosting and XGBoost are more effective than traditional models, and the proposed hybrid model has the best overall accuracy of 97.07% and good ROC-AUC. SHAP and LIME analyses extend the results of the most affecting variables and lead to bankruptcy, enhancing transparency to financial decision-makers. This result indicates the possibility of using ensemble learning as a complement to XAI to design effective and interpretable bankruptcy predictors that can be used by investors, government agencies, and financial institutions to detect risks early.

title: Unveiling of Ni Clusters MoS 2 thin film transistor 3, 3-dimethyl pentane sensor; A biomarker linked to breast cancer
authors: Vikash Kumar, Dilip Kumar, Basanta Bhowmik
abstract: This study demonstrated for the first time 3, 3-dimethylpentane detection using MoS2 thin film transistor (TFT) and modified Ni cluster MoS2 TFT sensor, a biomarker linked to breast cancer. The Ni clusters make the film more sensitive and selective by creating active adsorption sites and catalytic interactions. The MoS2 film was prepared by low cost hydrothermal followed by dip coating for successfully forming Ni cluster on MoS2/SiO2/Si. Electrical characterizations of the Ni-MoS2 TFT for the –1 V to 2.5 V bias window showed Ion/Ioff, sub-threshold swing (SS), threshold voltage (Vt), and mobility (μn) as 5.3×106, 67 mv/decade, 0.6 V, and 47 cm2/Vs, respectively. Under dynamic response measurements Ni-cluster modified MoS₂ surfaces demonstrated better 3, 3-dimethylpentane adsorption capability than the pristine MoS2. 3, 3-dimethylpentane sensing showed maximum response of 79% and 59.6% at 150°C and 200°C for Ni-MoS2 thin film and pristine MoS2, respectively. The sensor showed fast response time of ∼5s with lower detection limits of ∼1 ppm, and good repeatability. Electrostatic gate induced adsorption-desorption of 3, 3-dimethylpentane accelerated by spill over mechanism of Ni cluster on MoS2 surfaces were correlated considering barrier limited energy band diagram and charge transfer model.

title: MIST: A Benchmark and Baseline for Multi-frame Infrared Small Target Detection in Complex Motion
authors: Rui Gao, Meihong Zhang, Gongyang Li, Guanyi Li, Kai Zhao, Xianchao Zhang, Dan Zeng
abstract: Motion cues play a vital role in multi-frame infrared small target detection (MISTD). However, most targets in existing datasets exhibit regular and slow motion, which cannot reflect the complex and diverse motion patterns in real-world scenarios. This biased data distribution makes recent data-driven methods highly rely on simplified motion assumptions that tend to fail in irregular or fast motion, resulting in noisy feature representations cluttered with target-irrelevant factors. Hence, we stress that methods for MISTD should also work when targets are in complex motion. To enable this research, we propose a large-scale dataset called MIST for airborne infrared detection scenarios. The dataset is built on a synthetic data engine that models variations in pose, size, and intensity of moving targets while seamlessly blending them into real backgrounds for physical, geometric, and visual realism. Targets in MIST exhibit low signal-to-clutter ratios and complex motion, making it a promising yet challenging benchmark for developing algorithms focused on motion analysis. To tackle the challenges of MIST, we develop MISTNet, a robust baseline based on the Information Bottleneck theory. To handle irregular and fast motion, we propose a shifted neighborhood compensation block to efficiently model multi-scale correspondences for implicit motion compensation. To distill compact representations free from irrelevant cues, we design a progressive distillation decoder to hierarchically filter out redundancy while preserving target-relevant information. We benchmark 31 state-of-the-art methods and find that their performance on MIST drops significantly compared with that on the widely used NUDT-MIRSDT dataset. Our MISTNet outperforms all other methods by a large margin, with an over 6% gain in the IoU metric, demonstrating its superiority. The dataset, code, and model weights are available at https://github.com/GR-ray/MIST.
