title: Online Inter-Turn Short Circuit Fault Diagnosis in Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors by Estimating Secondary Saliency Parameter Using Extended Kalman Filter
authors: Seongyun Kim, Hyeyun Jeong, Sang Woo Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper proposes a novel online inter-turn short circuit (ITSC) fault diagnosis method for permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) based on estimating the secondary saliency inductance parameter (Lδ2) using an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF). Conventional ITSC fault diagnosis methods require either additional hardware such as high-frequency signal injection circuitry, extensive training datasets, or exhibit significant performance degradation under low-load and low-speed conditions. The proposed method addresses these limitations by employing the secondary saliency inductance, which originates from the intrinsic rotor geometry and can be estimated online using only the voltage and current signals available in standard PMSM drives. A comprehensive PMSM model incorporating secondary saliency inductance is developed, and an EKF is employed to estimate Lδ2 as a fault indicator. Experimental validation using an IPMSM test bench under various operating conditions and fault severities demonstrates that the proposed method enables reliable ITSC fault diagnosis across a wide operational range, including zero-load and speeds down to 1000 r/min (22% of rated speed), without requiring additional hardware.

title: CLEAR-Diff: Cloud-Context- and Scene Feature-Guided Diffusion Model for Cloud Removal_supp1-3690610.pdf
authors: Hanfa Xing
abstract: Cloud occlusion severely restricts the utility of optical remote sensing imagery, particularly when thick clouds obscure the underlying surfaces. Inspired by advancements in diffusion models, this study proposes a CLoud-context- and scene fEAture-guided adaptive cloud Removal method via a residual Diffusion model (CLEAR-Diff), designed for effective single-temporal cloud removal. CLEAR-Diff utilizes cloud residuals as the reconstruction target within a diffusion framework to capture cloud-context characteristics accurately. A cloud-context prompt generator is designed to dynamically generate prompt information by learning the morphological features of cloud residuals, enabling adaptive processing of various cloud-context patterns, especially opaque clouds, and improving denoising performance. Additionally, a scene feature generation branch, built on a denoising network, extracts latent ground information beneath cloud-covered regions, providing reliable scene priors to enhance structural detail recovery during image reconstruction. Experimental results demonstrate that CLEAR-Diff significantly improves reconstruction quality in single-temporal cloud removal tasks, particularly under thick cloud conditions. The model excels in thin cloud scenarios and enhances structural detail recovery and fidelity in thick cloud regions. Compared with the state-of-the-art (SOTA) single-temporal approaches, CLEAR-Diff improves PSNR by 4.92 dB on RICE1 and 3.26 dB on RICE2, achieving 37.303 dB and 36.821 dB, respectively. The source code is available at https://github.com/oTvTog/CLEAR-Diff.

title: A Probabilistic Model for Vehicle-to-Vehicle Communication with Autonomous Resource Selection
authors: Oleg Shmonin, Vitaliy Kuptsov, Sergey Trushkov, Georgij Serebryakov, Lin Li
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper presents a probabilistic analytical model for the fifth generation New Radio (5G NR) Vehicle-to-Vehicle communication under autonomous resource selection, also known as Sensing-Based Semi-Persistent Scheduling. The highway scenario specified by the third partnership project (3GPP) consortium is studied. The model introduces the joint probability distribution function of allocated time-frequency resources and the spacing between vehicles using the same time-frequency resource, enabling analytical descriptions of interference, sensing zone size, and packet reception ratio. Half-duplex, hidden-node, and resource reselection collisions are explicitly characterized. The analysis also reveals a distance-dependent behavior of half-duplex collisions, which has not been reported in prior studies. The model provides an upper-bound estimate of performance of Sensing-Based Semi-Persistent Scheduling while offering insights into aspects that are difficult to capture through simulations. Applications include performance evaluation and optimization of New Radio Vehicle-to-Vehicle systems in diverse deployment scenarios. The model may also assist in the design of more efficient resource allocation strategies.

title: Frame Scoring-Based Soft-Integration Approach for High-Resolution ISAR Imaging Under 3D Rotational Motion
authors: In-Hyeok Lee, Kyung-Tae Kim
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: High-resolution inverse synthetic aperture radar (ISAR) imaging is indispensable for target classification but is often compromised by complex 3D rotational motion (RM). Conventional frame selection schemes identify 2D-RM dominant intervals and discard the remaining data, which limits the coherent integration time and significantly degrades Doppler resolution. To overcome this limitation, this letter proposes a soft-integration framework that adaptively incorporates all available data. The proposed method assigns frame-level reliability weights via joint time-frequency analysis to quantify the stability of each interval. By utilizing these weights in a robust parameter estimation process, the framework preserves latent 2D-RM information from 3D-RM dominant intervals while suppressing disruptive out-of-plane effects. Experimental results with simulated and measured datasets demonstrate significant improvements in Doppler resolution and image quality compared to traditional hard-selection methods.

title: Nodal Shape Functions for High-Order Pyramids in Adaptive Finite Element Methods
authors: Damiano Franzò, Roberto D. Graglia, Andrew F. Peterson
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A unified methodology has been proposed for generating complete hierarchical higher-order curl- and divergence-conforming bases for tetrahedral, hexahedral, and prismatic cells by multiplying the lowest-order vector bases with hierarchical scalar functions based on Jacobi polynomials. This methodology was recently extended to pyramid elements, enabling fully conforming h-adaptive refinement on hybrid meshes. Sufficient accuracy for curved geometries usually requires curved cells, and high-order curved pyramid elements were not available within this framework until very recently. This paper introduces high-order nodal pyramid shape functions that allow accurate modeling of curved domains under adaptive h- or p-refinement, with numerical results validating their correctness and performance.

title: SADJSCC: Semantic-Aware DJSCC with Attribute Fidelity Evaluation for Channel-Adaptive Image Transmission
authors: Man Xu, Chan-Tong Lam, YuanHui Liang, Benjamin Ng, Haijian Lai, Bowen Liu, Xiaochen Yuan, Sio-Kei Im
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Conventional deep joint source-channel coding (DJSCC) methods exhibit semantic misalignment in noisy environments due to their reliance on pixel-level metrics and inability to preserve interpretable semantic attributes, particularly under varying low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. Semantic misalignment poses significant risks for safety-critical applications such as telemedicine and autonomous systems, where semantic misinterpretations may result in operational failures. To address these challenges, we propose a semantic-aware deep joint source-channel coding with attribute fidelity evaluation for channel-adaptive image transmission (SADJSCC), a unified framework to address semantic misalignment: semantic feature distortion, latent space displacement, and semantic undetectability. Our SADJSCC introduces a Gaussian discriminant analysis-based semantic-aware module (SAM) that generates coordinated semantic labels and semantic latent vectors through expectation-maximization optimization, resolving latent space displacement while enhancing feature dispersion. The semantic labels guide both the encoder and decoder to achieve semantic alignment. We further incorporate an attention fusion module (AFM) employing multi-head attention to dynamically weight semantic and feature latent spaces, thereby mitigating noise-induced distortions during transmission. To enable quantifiable semantic assessment independent of pixel corruption, we further develop a Semantic Attribute Fidelity Evaluation (SAFE) module that leverages Bayesian counterfactual mechanisms. Moreover, the framework integrates an SNR adaptive module (SNRAM) that dynamically recalibrates features in response to channel fluctuations. Experimental validation across CelebA, CIFAR-10, and MNIST datasets demonstrates consistent performance gains for SADJSCC. On CelebA, SADJSCC achieves an 8.4 dB peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) improvement over conventional DJSCC at SNR=-4 dB, with structural similarity index measure (SSIM) enhanced by 0.16 and learned perceptual image patch similarity (LPIPS) reduced by 46%, while maintaining high computational efficiency of 12.45 dB/G. Visualizations confirm the ability to preserve semantic coherence independent of pixel-level distortions, validating its utility for safety-sensitive applications.

title: Contextual Wireless Video Semantic Communication in MIMO-OFDM Systems
authors: Bingyan Xie, Cong Zhou, Yuxuan Shi, Biqian Feng, Yongpeng Wu, Wenjun Zhang
abstract: This paper proposes a MIMO-OFDM-based context video semantic transmission framework, namely M-CVST, for robust video communication over multi-path multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) channels. It introduces a context-subcarrier correlation map that aligns video feature context with groups of MIMO subcarriers. To leverage the time-correlated nature of multi-path channels, a recursive subcarrier sampling method paired with time-correlated reference embedding is designed, enabling the use of previously sampled MIMO subcarrier CSI to enhance channel state awareness in the entropy coding model. Numerical results verify the superiority of proposed M-CVST over MIMO multi-path channels compared to other semantic schemes and traditional separated schemes.

title: Orchestration of sentiment-aware AI agent for war-related discourse in parallel to Emotion Analysis, and Dual Topic Modeling.
authors: Piyush Vyas, George Mangalaraj
abstract: Investigation of public perceptions regarding warfare constitutes a crucial realm of research. This work harnesses the power of technology to ascertain public perceptions about war by using social media data and Artificial Intelligence (AI). The X platform (formerly Twitter) has emerged as a prominent conduit for public discourse on various societal issues, and the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war (RUW) is no exception. We present a multi-stage process that not only allows for the extraction of sentiment from tweets, but also leads to the creation of a sentiment-aware AI agent that could detect sentiment in tweets, thereby allowing for the contemporaneous gauging of public opinion that is immensely useful for rapidly evolving events or crises where public opinion may shift rapidly. In addition to sentiment extraction, we conduct exploratory emotion analysis and dual topic modeling to capture the multidimensional societal perspectives reflected in RUW‑related tweets. Our analysis identifies three sentiment categories (positive, neutral, and negative) and five emotions (anger, fear, sadness, happiness, and surprise). The bidirectional encoder representations from the transformers (BERT) model achieved a sentiment‑classification accuracy of 94% and were subsequently integrated into the AI agent. We observe that 46% of RUW‑related tweets express negative sentiment, and 30% convey fear. Topic modeling further reveals key themes related to geopolitics, territorial disputes, misinformation, economic concerns, and energy issues. Overall, this study advances research on public perceptions of war by demonstrating the value of combining social media analytics with AI‑driven sentiment classification to generate a holistic understanding of public opinion.

title: Benchmarking ML Models for Multi-Label Mental Health Detection in Sinhala Facebook Posts
authors: K.S.N. Vitharana, P.G.P. Kumara, M.A.S.D. Jayasundara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Digital footprints represent an opportunity to detect mental health distress at an early stage, which is considered a critical opportunity, specifically in high-stigma, low-resource linguistic environments. The study tackles the nonexistence of a tool to be used in such contexts by developing and confirming an automated classifier for Sinhala-language social media content. A multi-category framework was utilized in that posts were either indicative of anxiety, depression, suicidal ideation, or irrelevance. An expert-annotated dataset was used to compare the model architectures in terms of their attributes. The findings showed an apparent performance hierarchy. Neural and traditional baselines were found to be weak, with a Random Forest model achieving a macro F1-score of 0.60 and a Recurrent Neural Network not able to detect suicidal content (F1: 0.31). A Convolutional Neural Network (macro F1: 0.76) was found to improve it significantly. Nevertheless, transformer-based models produced a revolutionary progression. The BERT model had gained a high macro F1-score of 0.83, whereas the RoBERTa model had a new state-of-the-art with a macro F1-score of 0.86. The proposed RoBERTa model showed high per-class results, with F1-scores of 0.88 in both cases of anxiety and depression, a critical 0.85 in suicidal ideation, and 0.83 in irrelevant content. The ability to obtain various, subtle mental health conditions in a low-resource language with a high level of accuracy is the key innovation of the identified work. Therefore, we conclusively provide sensitivity and automated mental health surveillance among the linguistically underserved population with ease. This background tool offers a real, evidence-based avenue of early intervention that avoids societal stigma.

title: An Efficient Framework for Phishing Websites Detection using Machine Learning
authors: Priyanshu Sinha, Dinesh Sahu, Nidhi, Shiv Prakash, Tarun Kumar Gupta, Sudhakar Singh, Vinay Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Phishing is a cybercrime where the attacker tries to extract sensitive information from his or her victim by use of a fraudulent email, message, or website. Of these, phishing websites have gained more popularity, whereby they pretend to be similar websites, but the users are fooled. Recent studies have been looking into using machine learning methods to detect phishing websites, with the problem being cast as a classification problem. In the presented paper, the benchmark dataset consisting of more than 11,000 instances is introduced, and several types of classification algorithms, such as Decision Tree, KNN, Ensemble methods, and SVM, are compared with a proposed wide Artificial Neural Network (ANN) model. Experimental outcomes indicate that the proposed wide ANN has the highest accuracy of 96.14%, which is better than the state-of-the-art baseline models like KNN and Ensemble by 0.3-0.4%. Besides being more accurate, the model is cheaper to compute and has a higher generalization potential, which makes it a powerful and efficient tool to detect phishing websites. The proposed wide ANN model will be optimized by tuning the hyperparameter and feature engineering to improve phishing detector classification and computational efficiency.

title: System-Level Integration of a Chemiresistive IDE Sensor with Wireless Readout for Multi-Ions Heavy Metal Detection
authors: Anju Gupta, Dinesh Rotake, Anand Darji
abstract: This work presents the design and simulation of a novel interdigitated electrode (IDE) sensor system for the simultaneous detection of cadmium ions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{C d}^{\mathbf{2 +}}$</tex>), lead ions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{P b}^{\mathbf{2 +}}$</tex>), and mercury ions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{Hg}^{2+}\right)$</tex>. The IDE array integrates three independent sensing units on a single substrate, each functionalized with a distinct selective layer to ensure specific ion recognition. A graphene oxide-based resistive framework converts the IDEs into chemiresistors, enabling resistance modulation upon ion binding. Simulation studies validate the proposed sensor's structural design, electric distribution, and sensitivity, confirming its capability for reliable multi-ion detection. A conceptual system-level integration with wireless readout and microfluidic interfacing is illustrated to extend the design toward practical applicability. While microfluidic channel simulations are reserved for future work, this integration highlights the potential for real-time, portable, and miniaturized monitoring. The proposed platform, therefore, combines structural novelty with wireless-enabled system integration, offering a pathway toward compact, selective, and efficient heavy metal ions detection.

title: Multitask multiplexed infrared detection
authors: Shikun Duan
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The multifunctionality and integration of detection systems have consistently been a primary focus in the development of optoelectronic devices. The structural design of functionally stacked devices facilitates the acquisition of information for complex and diverse detection tasks, significantly enhancing effective information density. However, challenges such as thermal management issues arising from high integration density and signal crosstalk between different tasks pose substantial obstacles for multifunctional detection structures. In this work, we propose a low-power, low-crosstalk, switchable multifunctional detection scheme. The effective reuse of multifunctionality is attributed to the self-isolation and low energy consumption of the three-terminal optional Schottky junction device. The built-in electric field of the Schottky junction effectively blocks majority carriers while facilitating the transport of minority carriers. This enables the device to achieve a noise current of 4.2 × 10-27 A2 Hz-1 without requiring external bias. We demonstrate the system’s real-time edge imaging capability for letter masks and target vehicles, as well as its capacity to identify flammable hydrocarbons at concentrations as low as parts per million (ppm). This low-power, low-crosstalk multifunctional architecture presents a novel approach for the next generation of multifunctional integrated detection systems.

title: Explainable Wildfire Classification with PVT and Dragonfly-Optimized Decision Trees
authors: Sadman Samir Rafith, Saif Mostasir Rohan, Md. Sayem Kabir, Tasnim Sultana Sintheia, Nure Saba Binte Alam, Kazi Tanvir
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Wildfires pose a growing global threat, driven by climate change, prolonged droughts, and shifting land-use patterns, with devastating impacts on ecosystems, human health, and economies. Early detection is critical for containment, yet conventional monitoring methods such as satellites and ground surveillance often suffer from limited coverage, delays, or subjectivity. Recent advances in deep learning offer promising alternatives, but challenges such as dataset imbalance, environmental variability, and lack of interpretability limit their practical effectiveness. This study introduces a novel hybrid framework, integrating the Pyramid Vision Transformer (PVT) for hierarchical feature extraction with a Decision Tree classifier optimized via the Dragonfly Algorithm (DA). A stacked preprocessing pipeline-consisting of gamma correction, sharpening, and CLAHE-was employed alongside balanced augmentation to enhance discriminative features and mitigate class imbalance. Evaluated on the Flame-3 dataset, the proposed PVT-DA model achieved state-of-the-art performance, with 99.56 % accuracy and a Cohen's Kappa of 0.9911, outperforming baseline CNNs and tree-based classifiers. Explainable AI techniques, including GradCAM, Grad-CAM++, and LIME, further enhanced transparency by highlighting fire-relevant regions, supporting expert validation. These results position the PVT-DA hybrid as a highly accurate, interpretable, and resource-efficient solution for realtime wildfire detection, offering significant potential to strengthen early response systems and mitigate wildfire risks in vulnerable regions.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Architecture Combining Audio Spectrogram Transformer and ResNet-18 Head for Efficient Music Genre Classification
authors: Jannatul Ferdous, Kh. Taqi Tahmid Sudipta, Adib Ahsan, Tasfia Tabassum, Md. Nazmul Abdal
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Classification of music genres is a major task of Music Information Retrieval (MIR) that finds applications in recommendation systems, playlist generation and organization of audio libraries. The classical machine learning methods have difficulty in capturing the complex time-frequency content of contemporary music. The proposed model portrays a hybrid deep learning architecture of a late-fusion model with a combination of an Audio Spectrogram Transformer (AST) and a ResNet-18 Head in this paper. AST and ResNet-18 represent global and local time-frequency dependencies on spectrograms, respectively. Evaluations of the GTZAN dataset indicate that the hybrid model proposed is more accurate and generalizes well than single model baselines. The accuracy of the test is determined at 90.82 % with a macro F 1 -score of 90.78 %. Such findings reflect the potential of the hybrid transformer-CNN models to successfully and scalably detect genres in music.

title: BanEmoHate: Building a Multilevel Dataset for Bangla Text Integrating Emotion and Hate Speech
authors: Md. Masudur Rahman, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: We describe a multilevel Bangla dataset, BanEmoHate, a hate speech-aided emotion dataset that integrates both hate speech annotation and emotion labeling within the same corpus. The dataset improves the understanding of abusive content on social media or natural Bangla text. Hate speech and emotion recognition are emerging research fields for Natural Language Processing (NLP) task. Hate speech in Bangla often carries strong emotional undertones, making it important to study the two phenomena together. Although data sets for hate speech or emotion detection exist separately, very few resources integrate both dimensions in Bangla. Therefore, it is necessary to combine both fields in the same umbrella. With this motivation, a multilevel dataset for natural Bangla text is proposed, integrating Bangla hate speech and emotion recognition. The dataset contains 16,408 statements collected from natural comments and categorized into two labels. Firstly, it is categorized as either hate or non-hate. Then each category is divided into three emotions: happy, sad, and angry. The dataset is validated using Cohen's Kappa and Fleiss' Kappa measures, which yield good scores. The experimental result proves that the dataset can improve hate speech detection models by providing emotional context.

title: Job Characteristics in Engineering Industrial Sector and its impact on Affective Commitment: Evidence from Bahrain
authors: Abdel Nasser Taha Ibrahim Mohamed, Horiya M. A.. Al Deeb, Abdul Rahman Sobh Al-Aoun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The aim of this research is to identify the impact of Job Characteristics (Skill Variety, Task Identity, Task significance, Autonomy, Feedback,) on Affective Commitment at Gulf Petrochemical Manufacturing Company (GPIC) in the Kingdom of Bahrain. (218) employees were surveyed through a random sample. The present analysis is derived from the results of a structured questionnaire administered to a statistically representative cohort of employees at the target organization. The investigators posited a hypothesis asserting that core job dimensions—namely Skill Variety, Task Identity, Task Significance, Autonomy, and Feedback—exert a significant influence on Affective Commitment within Gulf Petrochemical Manufacturing Company (GPIC), located in the Kingdom of Bahrain. Empirical evidence supported the proposed hypothesis. The data indicated a statistically significant and positive relationship between each job characteristic and affective commitment. These empirical outcomes were critically examined, leading to the formulation of multiple conclusions and practical implications, alongside the provision of targeted recommendations and directions for subsequent research endeavors.

title: A Parametric Study of a Medium Rated Squirrel Cage Induction Motor Slots Design for Performance Improvement
authors: M.U. Munsif Ahamed, A.M. Fathima Ishara, A.I.S. Juhaniya, M.G.N.D. Weerarathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Squirrel Cage Induction Motors (SCIMs) have dominated the market in the industrial sector mainly because they are economical, reliable, and require very low maintenance. However, these machines also have certain drawbacks such as low efficiency, high starting current, and limited torque. The present study focuses on the effects of stator and rotor slot geometry on several performance parameters, including: starting torque, breakdown torque, rated torque, starting current, efficiency at rated speed and power factor of a medium-rated motor. In addition, the air-gap length is also taken into account. A baseline motor was simulated in MATLAB, and its results were validated with ANSYS RMxprt and Maxwell 2D to ensure the motor has acceptable distributions of the flux, current, and losses. The Parametric study results revealed that the changing of the stator and rotor slot parameters within certain limits can lead to an increase in the starting torque by 65.5%, breakdown torque by 13.27%, rated torque by 0.92%, efficiency by 0.255%, and power factor by 4.56% and reducing the starting current by 15.63%. The graphical methods were used to analyze the performance dependencies.

title: Sub-100-μV Cryogenic Biasing with Low-Noise Multilevel Memristors: Pt-Interface Tuning for Scalable Qubit Control
authors: Erbing Hua, Stijn Heemskerk, Ryoichi Ishihara
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: We demonstrate Pt/HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Pt/Ti/Pt memristors enabling sub-100 μV cryogenic biasing for scalable qubit control. Compared with pure Pt-based stacks, the Pt-interface-engineered lowers the effective barrier by approximately 0.3 eV, achieving bipolar switching, read-noise ≤0.32% at approximately 4.0 K. Monte-Carlo analysis confirms bias resolution < 100 μV in a six-device programmable-gain amplifier (PGA), validating that interface-engineer can path the way for cryogenic memristors-based wiring bottleneck compared with Au/Pt-based memristors for cryo-compatible analog memory applications.

title: Adaptive Subcarrier-Index Modulation for OFDM Transmissions
authors: Kaiwen Zheng, Xiaoli Ma
abstract: With the great development of wireless communications, Index Modulation (IM) has attracted significant attention due to its energy and spectrum efficiencies gained from utilizing additional transmission dimensions. In this paper, we propose a novel index modulation (IM) system, called adaptive subcarrier-index modulation (ASIM), which simultaneously enhances spectrum efficiency and error reliability over (enhanced) SIM systems through the introduced adaptive techniques. Moreover, its carefully designed adaptive framework enables flexible and efficient deployment across diverse Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) environments and performance requirements. An advanced index subcarrier indicating methodology is proposed to distinguish between the index and regular subcarriers with high power efficiency. To better utilize channel state information (CSI) feedback, advanced adaptive power allocation methods, together with adaptive modulation, are incorporated into the ASIM system to significantly enhance performance. The proposed ASIM system is further developed with an adaptive framework and parameter selection to accommodate diverse transmission conditions and performance demands. Simulation results show that our ASIM OFDM system outperforms the existing OFDM and OFDM-SIM systems with lower error rates and higher transmission rates under various transmission conditions. Furthermore, simulations show that varying demands and priorities can be met through the introduced parameter adaptation, validating the adaptability of the proposed ASIM technique.

title: Design and Analysis of an Ultra-Wideband Elliptical Shaped Metasurface for Linear-to-Linear Reflective Cross-Polarization Conversion
authors: Rudro Saha, Zubayer Bin Rahman, Redwanur Rashid Rayan, Md. Musfiqul Alam, Nafis Almas Nafi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The paper presents a high-performance reflective cross-polarization converter based on a metasurface with an innovative elliptical shape. The converter is designed and optimized for ultra-wideband operation in the frequency range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-21 \mathrm{GHz}$</tex>, thus fully encompassing the whole Ku-band (12-18 GHz) and K-band (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18-27 \text{GHz}$</tex>) and being very well adapted for satellite communications and radar applications. The optimization process and design were carried out to achieve the best bandwidth and Polarization Conversion Ratio (PCR), in CST Microwave Studio. The values obtained numerically guarantee that the final design, which has been realized on Rogers RT5880 substrate with copper patches, displays nearly ideal polarization conversion (PCR = <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96-99 \%)$</tex> within the desired band, with ideal conversion (PCR = <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \%$</tex>) achieved exactly [e.g. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.16-16.71 \mathrm{GHz}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$19.51-19.70 \text{GHz}$</tex>]. The optimized system's robustness is confirmed by this remarkable performance, which is highly efficient for a wide range of frequencies. This robustness is of vital significance in applications reliant on close polarization control, and this paves the way for future innovations in communication technologies and sensor devices. The axial ratios for the structure are exceptionally high—49.11 dB at 16.10 GHz and 52.03 dB at 19.61 GHz—indicating that the reflected wave is strongly linearly polarized at these resonant frequencies, well above the 3 dB threshold for circular polarization. The converter is also excellently angularly robust with high efficiency up to 30° incident angles. The proposed metasurface reveals an efficient and powerful method of forward-looking polarization manipulation in next-generation communication systems.

title: A Novel Deep Learning Framework for Gestational Age Estimation from Fetal Brain MRI Data
authors: Sumaiya Khatun, Md. Omaer Faruq Goni, Abdullah Al Bayozid
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The goal of this study is to estimate the gestational age (GA) from fetal brain MRI, which is important for enhancing prenatal care coordination that includes appropriate follow-up and clinical responses. In order to efficiently extract complex features from multi-planar MRI scans, the proposed method presents a novel deep learning model based on EfficientNet-B0 and improved with a unique multi-head attention (MHA) mechanism. Unlike previous methods that typically relied on single-view images, this model combines axial, coronal, and sagittal MRI slices, providing a more complete and detailed view of the fetal brain. Because the model was trained on a dataset of 52,900 multi-planar images from 741 patients, it was able to pick up reliable features and perform well in different cases. Furthermore, incorporating Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) boosts the model's stability and reliability, making it better suited for real-world clinical applications. The proposed model outperforms the conventional and state-of-theart models and achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) of 1.82 days, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 99.08% and Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 99.55%. The results of this research are not only key for the innovation in medical AI but also a new state of art for future development in diagnostics imaging and maternal-fetal health technology at academic and clinical deployment.

title: Semi-Supervised YOLO Framework for Real-Time Wildfire Smoke Detection from UAV Imagery
authors: Maria Hasan, Md. Tafsiruzzaman, Pritilata Dea, Shamima Sharmin Ananna, Mohammad Rifat Ahmmad Rashid, Raihan Ul Islam, Rashedul Amin Tuhin, M. Saddam Hossain Khan, Md. Nasim Adnan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Rapid and accurate detection of wildfire smoke is critical for minimizing ecological and societal damage. Conventional monitoring approaches, including satellite observations and ground-based surveys, often face delays and incomplete coverage, particularly under dense vegetation or dynamic fire conditions. This study introduces a label-efficient framework for UAV-based smoke detection that combines state-of-the-art YOLO object detectors with semi-supervised learning (SSL) to overcome data scarcity. A labeled UAV dataset of 738 images was used to establish baseline performance, while 1,887 unlabeled images from a distinct wildfire dataset were incorporated to generate pseudo labels through three SSL strategies: FixMatch, FlexMatch, and Mean Teacher. The augmented training set enabled improved model generalization across heterogeneous fire scenarios. Among the configurations tested, YOLOv8n integrated with FlexMatch achieved superior performance, attaining mAP@50 = 0.869 and mAP@50-95 = 0.614. EigenCAM-based interpretability analysis verified that the model consistently focused on smoke-affected regions, reinforcing reliability and operational trust. These findings demonstrate that integrating high-capacity YOLO architectures with semi-supervised learning produces a robust, scalable, and interpretable solution for realtime wildfire smoke monitoring from UAV platforms, offering practical utility for rapid environmental assessment and disaster response.

title: Influence of Authentic Leadership on Employees and Sustainable Performance in Selected Construction Firms: Does Work Engagement Matter?
authors: Banji Rildwan Olaleye, Ntseliseng Khumalo, Joseph Nembo Lekunze, Everistus Zeluwa Orji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Authentic leadership has become increasingly recognized as a crucial factor in organizational performance across various industries. Nevertheless, its effects on employee and sustainable performance in the construction sector, particularly in the unique context of the southwest region of Nigeria, remain underexplored, especially when psychological intervening factors, such as work engagement, are considered. This study examines the relationship between authentic leadership (ATL), employee performance, and sustainable performance, and investigates the role of work engagement as a potential dual influence on these relationships. 490 data were gathered from employees of selected construction companies in this region, via an online Google form. They were subsequently analyzed using the SMARTPLS software. Grounded in authentic leadership theory and the job demand-resources (JD-R) model, data from construction firms indicate that authentic leadership and work engagement are positively related to employee and sustainable performance. However, work engagement, while independently associated with employee performance, significantly mediates the leader-performance connection; the moderating role in relation to sustainable performance remains insignificant. Notably, the traits of authentic leadership help employees develop key skills for top performance, inspiring a culture of continuous improvement and engagement.

title: Hybrid Solar Panel Design Using Multi-Material Configurations: An ANSYS-Based Cost-Efficiency Trade-Off Study
authors: Humira Aznan, Sawrab Basak Joy, Sadia Afrin Heera Monika, Md Samin Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Solar power has become one of the most vital renewable energy sources. This fulfil the growing global energy demand that reduces carbon emissions. However, the availability of different solar panel technologies with different efficiencies, material compositions, and price points create dilemma for users. High-efficiency panels, though desirable, are often expensive and low-cost options suffer from reduced performance. This paper shows the challenge by offering a hybrid solar panel design that accumulates multiple materials within a single module to establish a trade-off between cost and efficiency. Using ANSYS-based thermal simulations, panels composed of single-material and hybrid configurations are modeled to compute temperature distribution and heat flux under standard irradiance. By combining high-cost, high-efficiency materials with lower-cost alternatives, the proposed hybrid approach offers a flexible cost-efficiency spectrum for end-users. The results highlight the capabilities of hybrid designs to serve as a practical solution for diverse consumers. Ranging from low-power household applications to high-performance requirements.

title: Frequent Pattern Mining in Retail Transaction Data to Enhance Cross-Selling Strategies
authors: Alamgir Hossain, Arpita Barua, Tanvinur Rahman Siam, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Frequent pattern mining plays a significant role in understanding customer purchasing behavior, especially in the retail industry. This study focuses on identifying frequent itemsets and generating association rules using the Apriori and FP-Growth algorithms on a real-world dataset obtained from Kaggle. The goal is to uncover hidden relationships among products that can inform effective cross-selling strategies. After preprocessing the transaction data, both algorithms were applied to extract rules based on support, confidence, and lift values. The results revealed strong product associations that can help improve product placement and promotional planning. The study also compares the efficiency and interpretability of the two algorithms, offering practical insights for retail data mining. The findings suggest that integrating frequent pattern mining into retail analytics can support more personalized and strategic decision-making.

title: BELLEVUE: An Automated Product Detection And Inventory Tracking System Using Deep Learning
authors: Aliya Jadik, Muhammad Abdurrahman, Amr Derhem, Seba Alarab, Vinod Pangracious
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: This paper presents an automated product identification and monitoring system for retail environments designed to address the limitations of manual stock checking, which often lead to stock-outs and revenue loss. The proposed solution uses a deep learning-based real-time object detection pipeline to track products of a specific brand on retail shelves, combining computer vision with edge computing architecture to improve inventory visibility and restocking efficiency. The proposed system functions at accuracy of sub- 200 ms inference latency and 85% mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5). Stock-out and low-stock events are logged in PostgreSQL, while backend-triggered email alerts and a prototype dashboard support operational monitoring and future integration with external systems. The system incorporates OpenCV-based image pre-processing, a flexible notification framework, and a hybrid processing strategy for scalability. Practical challenges, including dataset annotation effort, lighting variability, and real-time video processing, are mitigated through adjustable camera mounts, supplemental lighting, and continuous model updates. To ensure GDPR compliance, the system explicitly avoids facial recognition and customer tracking. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed architecture provides a scalable and privacy-aware blueprint for AI-driven retail automation.

title: Comparative Study of LLMs and Transformers for Bangla Healthcare Paraphrasing
authors: Shifat Islam, MD. Shorif Uddin, Rayhan Uddin Bhuiyan, Azizul Hakim Fayaz, Faika Fairuj Preotee, Shahmidul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Paraphrase generation plays a vital role in natural language processing (NLP), particularly in specialized domains such as healthcare where semantic accuracy is critical. Despite Bangla being one of the most widely spoken languages globally, it remains a low-resource language in NLP, with limited datasets and models for paraphrasing tasks. This study addresses this gap by evaluating the performance of both generalpurpose Large Language Models (LLMs) and domain-specific transformers on the bangla-health-related-paraphrased-dataset, comprising 200,000 sentence pairs. We compare five state-of-theart LLMs-Deepseek R1, Llama 3.3, Meta-Llama 4 Maverick, GPT-OSS 120B, and GPT-OSS 20B-with BanglaT5-base and BERT-base models. Results show that GPT-OSS 120B achieves the highest zero-shot performance (ROUGE-1: 0.69, ROUGE2: 0.40), while BanglaT5 demonstrates strong results among fine-tuned models (ROUGE-1: 0.5258, ROUGE-2: 0.2675). Our analysis highlights that multilingual LLMs can effectively handle Bangla healthcare paraphrasing without task-specific training, yet domain-specific models remain valuable for resourceconstrained applications. These findings provide benchmark results for Bangla paraphrase generation and open pathways for developing practical healthcare NLP tools in low-resource language settings.

title: Saving Lives at Sea with Drones: Adaptive Swimmer Detection for Safer Rescue
authors: Sk Muktadir Hossain, Mohammad Saiedur Rahaman, Jahan Hassan, Biplob Ray
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Drowning remains a leading cause of accidental death worldwide, highlighting the need for scalable and technology-assisted rescue systems. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) integrated with deep learning offer promising solutions for real-time swimmer detection, however, cross-dataset generalization and domain adaptation remain largely unexplored. This paper proposes a lightweight YOLO-based model optimized for maritime rescue, and evaluates it using two benchmark datasets: MOBDrone and SeaDronesSee. The evaluation includes self-evaluation, cross-dataset testing, pooled training, and fine-tuning with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 - 2 0 \%}$</tex> of the target dataset. Results show that while conventional YOLOv12s achieve near-perfect accuracy on MOBDrone (mAP@ 5098.47 %, precision 99.12%, recall 98.37%), performance drops significantly under cross-dataset testing (mAP@ 5067.16 %). In contrast, the proposed lightweight model maintains high accuracy on MOBDrone (mAP@50 99.32%) and achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{7 8 - 7 9 \%}$</tex> mAP@50 under cross-dataset fine-tuning with only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 - 2 0 \%}$</tex> of the target data. These findings demonstrate that architectural design combined with minimal data adaptation can balance accuracy, efficiency, and robustness for UAV-based maritime rescue.

title: Enhancing Crop Recommendation with Conformal Prediction and Calibrated Confidence
authors: Sourav Datto, Rafiul Hasan Efti, Md. Abdullah Al Mamun Saykat, Mohaimen-Bin Noor
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The dynamic nature of the global population is increasing, and significant demand for sustainable farming requires the creation of smart decision support-systems in crop recommendation. A comparative summary of eleven supervised machine learning models, that include Extra Trees (ET), Random Forest (RF), Naive Bayes (NB), CatBoost (CB), XGBoost (XGB), LightGBM (LGBM), Gradient Boosting (GB), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree (DT), K- Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Logistic Regression (LR), was done in this research about the multiclass crop prediction. The publicly providing Crop Recommendation Dataset is used in the study that includes soil nutrient (N, P, K), and environmental (temperature, humidity, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, rainfall) parameters that are considered crucial. Accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score were used as measures of model performance, and 5-fold cross-validation was used in evaluation of model stability. The Extra Trees classifier achieved the highest accuracy of 99.72%, outperforming other models in predictive power and generalization. Feature importance analysis highlighted soil nutrients and rainfall as dominant factors, with interpretability enhanced through LIME. The proposed framework integrates optional PCA-based dimensionality reduction for compact modeling, ensemble classifiers for high accuracy, and LIME-based explanations in the original feature space to ensure transparent decision support.

title: CMOS-Compatible 200 mm 0.25 µm GaN-on-Si HEMT Process for FR3 Application
authors: Qingyun Xie, Yuan Gao, Hsien-Shun Wu, Yi Heng Leong, Hanlin Xie, Haorui Luo, Hui Teng Tan, Zhan Jiang Quek, Wee Leng Ong, Lakshmi Kanta Bera, Navab Singh, Geok Ing Ng
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper reports a CMOS-compatible 200 mm 0.25 µm GaN-on-Si high electron mobility transistor (HEMT) process suitable for FR3 application. Competitive RF power performance was achieved across a range of V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS,Q</inf>, including maximum output power (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sat</inf>) of 2.3 W/mm, and a peak power-added efficiency (PAE) of 53%. The results were achieved by a GaN-on-Si process which uses commercially sourced epitaxial wafers, Al-based interconnects, and a subtractive process, making the reported process friendly for transfer to CMOS foundries.

title: Lightweight Deep Learning Framework for Brain Tumor Classification Using Feature Refinement
authors: Shovon Sarker, Md. Mazharul Islam, Kanchon Gharami, Md. Azmain Yakin Srizon
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and efficient classification of brain tumors from MRI scans is critical for early diagnosis and treatment planning. While deep learning methods, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformers, achieve high accuracy, they often demand substantial computational resources, limiting their use in real-world clinical settings. This study proposes two lightweight models for multiclass brain tumor classification: a MobileNetV2-based model with a custom feature refinement head (MobileNetV2+RH) and a lightweight variant derived from MobileNetV2 with the same refinement head (CustomMobileV2+RH). Evaluated on a dataset of 7,023 MRI images across four classes-glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and no tumor-both models achieved over 98 % accuracy while using far fewer parameters than heavier state-of-the-art architectures. These results highlight that combining lightweight backbones with a refinement head yields high-performance, computationally efficient, and deployable solutions for MRI-based brain tumor classification.

title: RRAM-Based Accelerator for HMM Forward Algorithm in a Hybrid CNN-HMM Framework
authors: Han Lu, Dejiang Mu, Zifei Cai, Keqin Li, Xiangshui Miao, Xingsheng Wang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Hidden Markov Models (HMMs) are widely used in pattern and speech recognition for their probabilistic sequence modeling capability, but are often limited by high computational and storage requirements. We present the first RRAM-based hardware accelerator for the HMM forward algorithm, integrated into a hybrid CNN-HMM framework for MNIST digit recognition. Input images are processed by a lightweight CNN to generate feature sequences, which are then classified by HMMs. Core forward algorithm computations, including matrix–vector multiplications and Hadamard products, are executed directly in RRAM arrays. Experiments show a 25× speedup over software implementation with only 3-4% accuracy loss. The hybrid CNN-HMM architecture also reduces hardware resource usage by 3.1×~185× while maintaining robust performance under limited training data.

title: Explainable Writer Verification with Autoencoders, Feature Fusion, and LLM-Based Explanations
authors: Haritha Hasathcharu, Rashmi Sandamini, Sagini Navaratnam, Gamage Upeksha Ganegoda, Shalini Upeksha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Handwriting-based writer verification remains challenging due to style variations and lack of explainable models for real-world use. This work presents an explainable, writer-dependent framework for offline, text-independent handwriting verification balancing accuracy, efficiency, and interpretability. We propose a lightweight autoencoder, fusing line-level hand-crafted features (pressure, direction) with character-level features from the anchor letter ‘e ’. A denoising autoencoder is trained per writer, with outputs explained using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) attributions interpreted through a Large Language Model (LLM) for human-readable explanations. The framework achieves 91.8% accuracy and 6.3% Equal Error Rate (EER) on a private ruled-paper dataset with normal and fast-speed samples, running in minutes on consumer CPUs. Results demonstrate that combining handcrafted features with autoencoders enables effective explainable verification. Integration of SHAP with LLM-generated interpretations enhances transparency and forensic applicability, providing a scalable solution for practical handwriting verification.

title:
Monolayer MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Negative Capacitance CFETs for Energy-Efficient Ultra-Scaled Logic Applications
authors: Md. Abdul Muiz Ayon, Junaid Ibn Faruq, Nazmul Hasan Naime, Afsana Anjum Akhi, Mainul Hossain
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The continued downscaling of complementary metal oxide semiconductor (CMOS) technology is increasingly constrained by short-channel effects, excessive leakage, and power density limitations. The complementary field-effect transistor (CFET) architecture, comprising vertically stacked n- and p-type devices, offers a three-dimensional (3D) pathway for footprint reduction and improved electrostatic control. In this work, we investigate the performance of a metal–ferroelectric–insulator–semiconductor (MFIS) negative capacitance CFET (NC-CFET) using technology computer-aided design (TCAD simulations. Monolayer MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> are employed as NMOS and PMOS channels, respectively, to enable ultra-scaled operation. Incorporation of a ferroelectric Hf<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Zr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> (HZO) layer within the gate stack achieves sub-60 mV/dec subthreshold swing (SS) and reduced supply voltage, enhancing energy efficiency. The proposed NC-CFET demonstrates superior ON/OFF current ratio and significantly suppressed leakage compared to conventional CFETs. The synergistic integration of atomically thin 2D semiconductors with negative capacitance effect combines scaling with performance benefits and presents a viable route to extend Moore’s law towards energy-efficient nanoelectronic systems.

title: Examining the Potential of Reservoir-Based Floating Solar Farms and Their Impact on Aquatic Ecosystems
authors: Muhammad Ismail, Marium Jalal, Awais Yasin, Muhammad Faizyab Ikram
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Floating photovoltaics FPV systems are a renewable energy source that has been proposed as a potential solution, especially in areas with limited land and rising energy consumption. This review analyses the international opportunities for reservoir-based floating solar farms and measures their effects on aquatic ecosystems in recent literature (2018-2025) and in global FPV datasets. FPV systems have cooling effect of water which makes them 5% to 10% more efficient than land based solar systems. The evaporation losses can be reduced by covering less than 15% to 20% of the total reservoirs areas without interfering with the ecological conditions. However, insufficient covering can change thermal stratification and light penetration, dissolved oxygen dynamics, and nutrient cycling, which can affect aquatic biodiversity. Key gaps in ecological monitoring, material sustainability, and policy frameworks are found in developing regions. The results highlight the importance of standardizing environmental assessment procedures, combined with techno-environmental planning and policy development, to achieve the sustainable implementation of FPV systems. In general, reservoir-based FPV is an option that opens a new opportunity to increase clean energy production without adversely affecting water resources or their ecosystem reliability.

title: Fitness Risk Analysis in Gym Training: Evaluating Normal and Abnormal Postures Across Weighted and Non-Weighted Exercises
authors: Mahfujur Rahman, Fairoze Fatema Alam, Md. Istiaque Mahamud, Md. Tarek Hasan, Shoib Ahmed Shourav, Nahid Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Gym training, while essential for physical fitness, often involves risks due to improper techniques or incorrect postures, particularly when weights are incorporated. This research investigates the classification and risk analysis of normal and abnormal positions during gym exercises using YOLOv12, a cutting-edge object detection framework. By training YOLOv12 on a comprehensive dataset of gym activities-including separate datasets for normal postures, abnormal postures, and a merged set-the study achieves exceptional detection performance, with mAP50 scores of 0.967 (normal), 0.841 (abnormal), and 0.988 (merged), demonstrating robustness in identifying deviations from optimal postures. The system evaluates injury risks in real-time, providing instant feedback to gym-goers and trainers to enable timely corrections and mitigate potential injuries. Experimental results highlight YOLOv12's superior accuracy and efficiency in categorizing postures across weighted and nonweighted exercises, with the merged dataset achieving nearperfect detection. This contribution to the field of fitness risk analysis employs the latest deep learning technologies to create a scalable tool capable of making all gym-goers safer and risk-free of injury.

title: Smart Robot for Feeding Fish in Aquaculture Farms
authors: Israa S. AL-Furati
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research paper presents a new idea for feeding fish in private tanks to ensure that all fish at different depths receive the same adequate level of nutrition according to a specific time schedule. The international systems for feeding fish are adopted in programming this idea, where robots replace humans in many aspects of life. The project consists of two parts, one of which is the hardware components and the other is the software. The hardware components consist of: The ESP8266 module, battery lithium, H Bridge, LM2596 DC-DC, Battery holder, servo motor, Actuator, and 2S 7.4V 8A Li-ion 18650 Lithium Battery Charger Protection Board. The software part consists of Remote-control device (RCD), ESP8266, Arduino Uno and the code. To ensure a lightweight boat that works as a robot within a dedicated aquatic environment, the overall hull was printed using a 3D printer to reduce the overall cost of the project. The aim of this project is to reduce feed waste and lower the operating costs for workers in these ponds by reducing human intervention in the fish feeding process by controlling the feeding process within pre-determined times through special programming and according to the type and environment of fish being raised. The project can be developed by linking it to artificial intelligence applications that are managed by mobile phones to automate the system's work in an advanced manner. It is also possible to add an automatic navigation feature that allows the robot to work without the need for remote monitoring, but rather through a single automatic programming.

title: Multitype Bone Fracture Detection and Classification with YOLOv12: A Deep Learning Approach on a Custom X-Ray Dataset and Web Application
authors: Sumit Sikder, Nazmul Hasan, Arpita Howlader, Md Samsuzzaman, Zubayer Ishfar Zeem, Md Ashikur Rahaman Ashik
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Detection and classification of bone fractures in medical imaging greatly help doctors make accurate diagnoses and plan treatments more efficiently. Traditionally, people have depended a lot on manual methods for this, which take up too much time and often lead to mistakes from human oversight. In recent years, advances in deep learning, especially with convolutional neural networks and models like YOLOv12, have changed how we analyze medical images by adding automation to the process. This research presents a model based on YOLOv12 that detects and classifies various types of bone fractures in an accurate and efficient way. The model works on identifying and categorizing bones in areas like the elbow, fingers, forearm, humerus, shoulder, and wrist. It is trained on a custom set of X-ray images, and we evaluate its performance using key metrics such as precision, F1 score, recall, and mean average precision. The results turned out quite strong, showing a precision of 0.946, an mAP at 0.5 of 0.850, and an mAP from 0.9 to 0.95 of 0.600. These numbers show that the model can detect and classify bone fractures with really high accuracy. This kind of outcome supports the idea that the system is reliable and effective for actual use in detecting fractures in real life. On top of that, we created a user-friendly web interface so healthcare professionals can classify fractures right away in real time. Overall, this method gives a solid and efficient way to spot bone fractures, which improves how accurate diagnoses are in medical settings.

title: Adaptive Clustered Personalized Federated Learning for Non-IID Remaining Useful Life Prediction in Edge-Based Industrial Systems
authors: Fardin Kaiser
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: To optimize maintenance schedules, industrial machinery's Remaining Useful Life (RUL) must be accurately predicted. However, centralized learning techniques are hampered by real-world limitations including data privacy and diverse operating environments. This paper examines federated learning (FL) techniques for RUL prediction in non-IID environments using the C-MAPSS FD004 turbofan engine dataset. We compare four FL approaches: Clustered Federated Learning (CFL), FedAvg (a global model), FedPer (a personalized strategy), and our novel Adaptive Clustered Personalized FL (AC-PFL), which groups engines according to degradation patterns and uses tailored training for each group. All methods utilize consistent LSTM architectures and are evaluated across ten randomly selected seeds. FedPer achieved the lowest Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$14.61 \pm 0.98$</tex> cycles, followed by CFL (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15.07 \pm 0.90$</tex>), AC-PFL (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.02 \pm 0.38$</tex>), and FedAvg (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17.61 \pm 2.0$</tex>). While CFL achieves marginally higher accuracy, AC-PFL's superior consistency makes it a more reliable choice for safety-critical applications. These findings underscore the value of hybrid FL strategies that effectively balance global knowledge with local personalization, offering a scalable and privacy-preserving path forward for industrial prognostics.

title: Predicting Endocrine Disruptors: A Deep Learning QSAR Model for Estrogen Receptor Activity
authors: Belaguppa Manjunath Ashwin Desai, Shreyas Murthy, Bhoomika Sridhar, Belaguppa Manjunath Anirudh, Vivien Humtsoe, Pronama Biswas
abstract: Endocrine-disrupting chemicals (EDCs) are compounds that can interfere with hormones and their receptors, affecting growth, reproduction, and other vital functions in humans and wildlife. Since laboratory tests are slow and costly, we created a computational screening method to predict which chemicals are likely to bind the estrogen receptor (ER). This key protein responds to the hormone estrogen. We used a dataset of 224 compounds described by 2,944 molecular descriptors and fingerprints (numerical features that summarize chemical structure and properties). These features were fed into a deep neural network (DNN), a form of machine learning capable of recognizing complex patterns, that included dropout and batch normalization (techniques that reduce overfitting and improve training stability). This QSAR (quantitative structure–activity relationship) model achieved 96.65% accuracy on training data and 91.30% on test data. For the active (ER-binding) class, the model produced a precision of 0.82 (predicted), a recall of 0.88 (found), and an ROC–AUC of 0.81 (overall discrimination ability). To validate predicted active structures, we performed molecular docking using the estrogen receptor structure (PDB ID: 5TOA). Several compounds predicted as active showed docking poses and interactions similar to Estradiol (the natural ligand), including expected hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic contacts. In short, this DNNbased QSAR plus docking workflow provides a fast, costeffective way to detect chemicals that may act as EDCs by targeting the ER, supporting prioritized laboratory testing and improved environmental and biodiversity risk assessment.

title: Novel Gate-injected Ferroelectric FET-based Differential Fully-analog Computing-in-memory with In-situ Charge-domain Activation for Energy-efficient LLMs
authors: Bingrui Song, Shaodi Xu, Runze Han, Jin Luo, Qianqian Huang, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, a novel gate-injected ferroelectric (FE) FET (GI-FeFET) based differential fully-analog computing-in-memory (FA-CIM) architecture with in-situ charge-domain activation is proposed and experimentally demonstrated for the first time, enabling high energy efficiency for large language models (LLMs). Leveraging FE-assisted gate-injection to improve memory window, the fabricated GI-FeFET-based differential cell realizes analog input for charge-domain computing with enhanced sense margin and noise resilience. The differential weight is efficiently written via a row-wise erase and block program scheme. Moreover, an in-situ analog exponential linear unit (ELU) activation is realized by exploiting Q-V relationship integrated from the pre-programmed C-V characteristics in GI-FeFET, reducing hardware overhead. Based on the device-circuit co-design, the proposed GI-FeFET-based FA-CIM achieves energy efficiency of up to 1094 TOPS/W for feed-forward networks, demonstrating its great potential for edge LLM deployments.

title: Citrus Plant Leaf Disease Classification Using MobileNetV3-Large: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Chaitanya Singla, Alok Misra, Firdous Sadaf Mohammad Ismail, Sonali Sharma, Mansi Aggarwal, Shashank Tripathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper investigates using a lightweight deep learning model, MobileNetV3-Large, for classifying diseases in citrus leaves. Five classes have images in the dataset: Black Spot, Canker, Greening, Healthy, and Melanose. The model achieved a total accuracy rate of 86% for a test set of 121 images. High recall and precision for Black Spot and Greening were observed, with lower performance for Healthy and Melanose due to class imbalance and feature overlap. The model was evaluated using a range of measures, including precision, recall, and F1-score, and performance was further analyzed using a confusion matrix. Results show that MobileNetV3-Large can be used for classifying diseases in citrus leaves, but can be enhanced with data imbalance handling and better class differentiation between similar classes. Future research can explore measures to improve model performance, for example, data augmentation or feature addition.

title: GRU-TCN Analytics for Predictive Monitoring: Smart Marketing Decision Models for Industrial Engineering
authors: Shyam Maurya, Parvindar Kaur Chhabda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In industrial systems, thermal anomalies can lead to significant failures, safety issues, and financial losses. For the purpose of capturing the complicated temporal dependencies natural in sensor data, conventional threshold-based monitoring systems are grossly inadequate. This work provides a predictive monitoring system combining Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) architectures to close this gap. The system aims to accurately predict and discover thermal anomalies in an industrial situation. The GRU-TCN model has been able to detect anomalies in systems employed with industrial times series monitored data by utilizing the sequential learning capacity of GRUswith TCNs, which can extract temporability of patterns with a rapid inference time. Industrial thermal sensor data was captured and input into the hybrid neural network (GRU-TCN) for training. Prior to the medium's input, the data was pre-processed to reduce filter while scalzing features for input conformity. The performance of this model was produced against TCN, independent GRU, and previous LSTM method performance. Accuracy, precision, recall, and f1 score metrics were employed to compare model performance. The analysis determined the GRU-TCN model would produce the truest accuracy with rapid early predictions in order, and before anything previously noted. While this predictive monitoring system could create efficiencies around occupational safety, it could also facilitate substantial cost savings by minimizing maintenance efforts and unplanned downtime.

title: Bridging the Gap Between Academia and Industry: An Empirical Study of Active Learning Approaches in a Pre-University Induction Programme
authors: Poorni Fernando, Kushan Bhareti, Banuka Athuraliya, Suresh Peiris, Dhashanthie Gamalatge, Ruvan Weerasinghe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study evaluates an eight-week induction programme redesign aimed at improving graduate employability by shifting from didactic instruction to an active learning model. The intervention integrated Experiential Learning, Problem-Based Learning, and Project-Based Learning within a technology-enhanced environment and structured industry engagement. Using a quasi-experimental comparison of consecutive cohorts within the same institution, placement success was measured as the signing of a mandatory five-year industry contract upon programme completion. Placement rates increased from 61 % under the traditional curriculum to 83 % following the redesign. The paper outlines the curricular design and discusses mechanisms linking active learning to enhanced professional readiness, including contextualized practice, soft-skill development, and formative feedback. Limitations include non-random assignment and potential cohort effects, and directions for future research are proposed.

title: A Binary Insomnia Disease Classification Approach with Feature Optimization and Explainable AI Insights
authors: Abdullah Rahman, Mahbub Hasan, Md. Shohel Babu, Sakib Mahmud
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Insomnia constitutes a substantial public health issue that adversely impacts both mental and physical wellbeing, particularly among adolescents. Conventional diagnostic techniques frequently fail to provide prompt identification due to the complexity and variety of the illness. This study introduces a robust machine learning framework for the binary classification of insomnia, integrating advanced data preprocessing, multicriteria feature selection, and explainable artificial intelligence to enhance predictive accuracy and clinical interpretability. To reduce bias and ensure data quality, we applied a thorough preprocessing workflow that included label encoding, Min-Max scaling, and stratified sampling. Our study utilizes the publicly available “Insomnia in Adolescence” dataset, which contains behavioral, demographic, and psychometric factors. In order to select the best group of predictors, a hybrid strategy that combined ANOVA <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p \leq 0.05)$</tex>, mutual information ranking (top 50%), and variance thresholding (variance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.05$</tex>) was used. Stratified cross-validation was employed to construct and assess various classifiers, including ensemble models, K-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, and Logistic Regression. With an accuracy of 92.85% and a recall of 100%, Random Forest outperformed the others, showcasing a remarkable capacity to reduce false negatives, which is crucial for clinical settings. Despite ensemble methods such as RF+SVM provided competitive performance, they failed to surpass Random Forest. Deep neural networks performed poorly despite evaluation due to the limited dataset size, indicating that traditional machine learning models are better suited for small-sample settings. In order to improve interpretability and clinical application, feature contributions were quantified using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP).

title: CMOS Inverter on a Flexible Plastic Substrate Using Junctionless Double-Gate Poly-Ge Thin-Film Transistors
authors: Akito Kurihara, Tetsuo Okuyama, Toshiyuki Tsuchiya, Akito Hara
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Germanium (Ge) is known to have higher bulk carrier mobility and a lower melting temperature than Si. To fabricate transistors on a flexible plastic substrate, a low-temperature process that does not damage the plastic is necessary, making Ge an ideal material. Generally, thin polycrystalline-Ge (poly-Ge) films have a high hole concentration, at a level of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup>, which allows for the fabrication of junctionless p-ch thin-film transistors (TFTs). In this study, we fabricated a CMOS inverter with a junctionless double-gate (DG) structure for both n-ch and p-ch poly-Ge TFTs on a flexible plastic substrate using a low-temperature (425°C) copper-based metal-induced crystallization (Cu-MIC) process. For the n-ch junctionless DG poly-Ge TFT, phosphorus implantation was performed on an amorphous Ge thin film before Cu-MIC. We successfully operated the CMOS inverter on a flexible plastic substrate with a gain of 7 at V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf> = 5.0 V and evaluated its dynamic performance.

title: Integrating Strategic Planning into Human Resource Decision-Making: A Descriptive and Analytical Approach in the Banking Sector
authors: Ruwaida Hammoud Harj Mahmoud, Bilal Kamel Odeh, Saeed Hameed Aldulaimi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The study aims to identify the role of strategic planning in the re –engineering of human resources in private banks in Iraq. The descriptive analytical approach was adopted for presenting, analyzing and interpreting the collected data. The research was conducted on a sample of (114) individuals within the banks, encompassing bank managers, deputy bank managers, department managers, division managers, and employees, utilizing the comprehensive census method. The questionnaire served as the primary data collection tool. Data were analyzed using a set of statistical methods within the SPSS program. The findings consistent of Strategic planning plays a pivotal role in forecasting future human resource needs. The integration between strategic planning and HR re-engineering represents a fundamental factor in achieving organizational effectiveness. The study emphasizes the importance of employee participation in HR re-engineering processes. The main recommendations that important of adopting the strategic planning methodology within human resource departments and the emphasis on analyzing future gaps in personnel and skills when formulating strategic plans.

title: Theoretical Framework of Engineering Decision-Making in Healthcare under the Influence of Social Media in the UAE
authors: Hagar M. Mohamed, Mohamed Rashad, Mahmoud Khalifa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Healthcare systems in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) are undergoing rapid transformation driven by engineering innovations such as artificial intelligence–powered diagnostics, telemedicine platforms, and robotic surgery. At the same time, social media platforms have become critical arenas for shaping public perceptions of these technologies, amplifying both trust and skepticism. This study develops a theoretical model that explains how social media discourse interacts with engineering decision-making in healthcare and influences technology adoption within the UAE context. Drawing on the Diffusion of Innovations Theory, the Social Amplification of Risk Framework (SARF), Institutional Theory, and trust models, the framework highlights the role of public perception as a mediator between online discourse and engineering choices, while also emphasizing the moderating effect of UAE policy and institutional context. The findings suggest that successful adoption of healthcare technologies requires not only technical excellence but also transparent communication, institutional legitimacy, and alignment with national strategies. By integrating engineering, media, and governance perspectives, this study contributes to advancing theoretical understanding of sociotechnical innovation and offers insights for policymakers, healthcare leaders, and engineers navigating the challenges of digital transformation in the UAE.

title: Metasurface-enabled multi-functional optical devices for bio-imaging and thermal radiation management
authors: Guangwei Hu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Metasurfaces, capable of full-wave manipulation, can be integrated into optical systems to provide new opportunities for customizing multidimensional transfer functions. This work explores this potential through two applications: passive metasurfaces for advanced bioimaging that simultaneously capture and process biological images, and active metasurfaces for thermal camouflage compatible with radiative thermal management.

title:
Sparse Neural Networks via Bi-level ℓ
                    <sub>0</sub>
                    Optimization for Prostate Cancer Prognosis
authors: Maria Frasca, Jianyi Lin, Davide La Torre
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study proposes a bi-level optimization framework integrating ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> regularization into deep neural networks for joint model compression and embedded feature selection.
