The approach employs Hard Concrete distributions to approximate the nonconvex ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> norm, enabling simultaneous learning of binary masks for neurons and input features within a differentiable setting. Applied to a cohort of prostate cancer patients, the framework effectively identifies key clinical and molecular predictors while achieving substantial sparsity. Experimental results show 70% neuron pruning and 60% feature reduction with 90% validation accuracy. The method enhances both computational efficiency and clinical interpretability, providing a scalable foundation for decision-support applications in oncology.

title: Interpretable Machine Learning for Proactive Diabetes Screening: Insights from a Stacking Ensemble on BRFSS Data
authors: Mohammod Ashikur Rahman, Rimon Kumer Roy, Kazi Lutfur Nahar Mithila, Md. Ashiqur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Detection of diabetes is a critical challenge in health care, as detection contributes to the prevention of serious complications and enhances patient outcomes. This study proposes an ensemble model for diabetes and prediabetes detection using the CDC BRFSS 2015 health indicators. After cleaning and balancing the dataset, several machine learning models were compared and the top three performers ExtraTrees, RandomForest, and DecisionTree were selected as base learners. These were combined in a stacking ensemble with CatBoost as the meta learner. The proposed model, a stacking ensemble tuned with RandomizedSearchCV, achieved a test accuracy of 98.87% and a ROC–AUC of 0.9978, outperforming all single models and showing strong, stable results across cross-validation. Explainable AI techniques, including permutation importance, SHAP, and LIME, were used to explain the model's decisions, consistently highlighting BMI, Age, and General Health as the most influential features. This work demonstrates that a carefully tuned ensemble approach, combined with explainable AI, can deliver highly accurate and transparent diabetes prediction for large scale health data.

title: Oxide Semiconductors for Ultralow-Power Sustainable Electronics
authors: Juan Paolo S. Bermundo, Candell Grace P. Quino, Mutsumi Kimura, Radu A. Sporea, Yukiharu Uraoka
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Oxide semiconductors (OS) are essential building blocks in displays, sensors, memory, and emerging neuromorphic applications. As society transitions towards the next-generation, growing demand for these devices will drive the need for more sustainable materials, high throughput processes, and energy efficient performance. Here, we demonstrate how a paradigm shift towards printing process especially when combined with OS carrier concentration control is a more sustainable and high throughput approach. Furthermore, adopting novel contact-controlled device architectures in OS devices will pave the way toward ultralow-power electronics.

title:
Direct Laser Writing of Reconfigurable VO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Electronic and Optical Devices
authors: Hemadri Bandhu, Amit Verma
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Vanadium dioxide (VO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) is a correlated oxide exhibiting a reversible insulator–metal transition (IMT), making it suitable for reconfigurable electronic and optical applications. Conventional VO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> device fabrication typically requires VO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> synthesis in a highly controlled oxygen environment, multiple photolithography steps, film etching, and contact deposition steps. This leads to a significant increase in device development cost due to increased process time and the use of environmentally hazardous chemicals. We present a lithography-free, single-step approach for localized VO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> synthesis via direct laser-induced oxidation of vanadium thin films under ambient conditions. Thermal simulations carried out in COMSOL Multiphysics demonstrate that the temperature distribution strongly depends on the substrate material and aid in identifying suitable laser parameters and optimal conditions to achieve sufficient temperatures to initiate oxidation. Finally, we summarize the application of this technique to fabricate and demonstrate VO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> phase transition-based reconfigurable electronic and optical devices.

title: Few-Shot Facial Emotion Recognition Through South Asian Geographic Transfer Learning
authors: Sayuni Ellepola, Thilakshana Bandaranayake, Anjana Junius Vidanaralage, Harinda Fernando, Naren Selvaratnam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: State-of-the-art emotion recognition systems demonstrate systematic failure across cultural boundaries, with performance degrading from 85 percent on Western benchmarks to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6}$</tex> percent on South Asian populations, a disparity that effectively excludes billions from emotion recognition technology while perpetuating Western-centric bias in affective computing. This work introduces the first systematic geographic transfer learning framework addressing cross-cultural facial emotion recognition under extreme data scarcity by exploiting morphological proximity between culturally similar populations for few-shot adaptation. The framework advances three critical innovations. First, strategic geographic transfer through morphologically proximate intermediate domains from India to Sri Lanka achieves 62.8 percentage point improvement where direct Western transfer catastrophically fails. Second, calibrated 60 percent experience replay integrated with progressive three-stage training prevents catastrophic forgetting while enabling comprehensive six-emotion recognition despite target data covering only four emotions, representing a novel continual learning paradigm unaddressed in existing literature. Third, mobile-efficient architecture ensures practical deployment for resource-constrained populations. Experimental validation demonstrates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 8. 8}$</tex> percent test accuracy with 93 percent sample efficiency relative to conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0, 0 0 0}$</tex>-sample approaches while maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 0}$</tex> percent generalization gap, establishing unprecedented scalability for underrepresented populations. The observed 43.8 percentage point accuracy differential between universal and culturally variable emotions provides empirical validation of dialect theory in computational contexts. This framework establishes a transformative pathway for developing culturally adaptive artificial intelligence systems applicable to 3.8 billion individuals in underserved regions globally, fundamentally advancing equitable technology access beyond Western-centric paradigms.

title: A New Supervised Learning Algorithm Based Upon the Raw and Central Moments of the Underlying Classes
authors: Ahmed Mehedi Nizam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: We propose a new supervised learning algorithm, where each data point in the test set is included into the class for which such inclusion causes minimum displacement in the existing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-th central or raw moment of the respective class under construction. After each such inclusion, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-th central or raw moment, whichever is applicable, of the corresponding class is updated by incremental calculations in constant time, i.e., each class evolves gradually and changes its definition incrementally after the inclusion of every new data point. We then use <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-fold cross-validation techniques to compare the performance of our proposed model with various state-of-the-art supervised learning algorithms including Neural Network (NN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), and Logistic Regression (LR) using the Pima Indian Diabetes (PID) dataset. It has been observed that the performances of different MDEM algorithms involving different orders of moments vary within the range [<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$83.65 \%-95.82 \%$</tex>] of the best algorithm under consideration in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-fold cross-validation.

title:
Using Thermally Oxide Electrode MoO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    to Reduce Read/Write Delay and Enhance Hamming Distance of FeCAM at 85 °C
authors: C.-H Hsu, M.-H Hsiung, H.-M. Chen, S.-T. Huang, R.-Y. Chen, W.-C Meng, H. Yang, W.-N. Chang, C.-C. Lin, Y.-T. Tang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: A FeCAM with a thermally stable MoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> oxide electrode (OE) proposed to enhance thermal stability for searching in-memory unit. The thermally stable oxide electrode enables suppressing MW degradation of 50% after 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">9</sup> cycles at 85°. This capping electrode was also to demonstrated a lower switching barrier (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> = 4.76 MV/cm) to boost faster writing speed. This FeCAM achieves 6 stable states with a maximum Hamming distance (HD) of 4.8 and maintains HD = 2.3 at 85 °C, demonstrating excellent thermal reliability and low-power operation.

title: Distinguishing Between Saint(Sadhu) and Common(Cholit): A Bangla BERT Approach to Bengali Language Classification
authors: MD. Tahidul Islam, Nakib Aman, Md. Abu Johab, Md. Sadikul Islam, Md. Roton Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Bengali is the fifth most spoken language in the world. It displays diglossia in two different registers: formal/classical Sadhu Bhasha and modern/colloquial Cholit Bhasha. Because of their clear grammatical, lexical, and stylistic distinctions, it is still very difficult to distinguish these registers computationally. In order to solve the major difficulties in processing a morphologically complex and historically varied language, this research suggests an automated method for dividing Bengali sentences into Sadhu and Cholit kinds. We use both sophisticated deep learning techniques and automated machine learning (PyCaret). The BanglaBERT model performed better than the others, achieving 95.09% accuracy with AUC(0.9703). The organizing of digital libraries, educational technology, the preservation of cultural heritage, and several Bengali NLP applications like machine translation and sentiment analysis can all benefit from accurate register categorization. By combining cutting-edge computational methods with conventional linguistic insights, this research advances NLP capabilities for low-resource languages.

title: Machine Learning-Based Prediction of ADHD in Bangladeshi Children Using Behavioral and Demographic Insights
authors: Sharbany Panday, Sourish Bairagi, Sinthia Sayma Rahman, Mir Sazzat Hossain, Md. Rashedur Rahman, Ashraful Islam, Farhana Sarker
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: ADHD is a prevalent neurodevelopmental problem, but in Bangladesh, early recognition is not only minimized due to low awareness but also because of limited diagnostic accessibility. This study seeks to close that gap by constructing a DSM-5-informed questionnaire designed to identify behavioral, familial, and socioeconomic antecedents of ADHD in childhood. Participants were school-aged children from various schools, ranging from 4 to 17 years old, and aggregated for a systematized machine learning pipeline. The most predictive variables were tagged using feature selection through inception of classical classifiers like Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, Logistic Regression, and Decision Tree. The study shows demographical and attentional features of importance in predicting the risk of ADHD. Ensemble techniques were the most stable among all models, suggesting that the screening questionnaire may offer useful classification. Although the findings suggest potential for using surveys and machine learning to provide a cost-effective early screen, this research is limited by a small sample size, imbalanced demographics, and a reliance on parent/caregiver/teacher reports. In the future, increasing and including gender-sensitive tools will be crucial to scale up culturally sensitive ADHD detection in Bangladesh.

title: Emotional Flow and Authenticity: A Comparative Study of Ai- and Human-Generated Comment Sequences
authors: H.P.D.C Thilakarathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT and Gemini are increasingly used to generate online text, raising questions about how their emotional behavior compares to that of humans. While many studies focus on static sentiment or emotion classification, less is known about emotional dynamics how emotions change across sequences of text and whether AIgenerated content exhibits distinct emotional flow patterns. This study analyzes emotional patterns in AI- vs. humangenerated text using an AI-Human dataset, a RoBERTa-based GoEmotions classifier, and several emotion-flow metrics. We (1) annotate each text with fine-grained emotions, (2) construct fixedlength pseudo-sequences of texts, (3) build Directed Emotion Graphs (DEGs) to capture transitions between emotions, and (4) define an Emotion Flow Consistency Index (EFCI) to quantify the smoothness of emotional trajectories. We also measure persequence emotional diversity using entropy, and compute lexical diversity and text length as simple proxies related to authenticity. On the full dataset (1,910 texts: 1,375 human, 535 AI), we find that human and AI sequences have very similar EFCI values (0.1565 vs. 0.1553; difference not significant, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.902$</tex>), and similar emotional diversity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.669$</tex>). In contrast, AI-generated texts show significantly higher lexical diversity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p \approx 2.4 \times 10^{-11}$</tex>) and significantly greater length (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p \approx 7.5 \times 10^{-25}$</tex>) than human texts. These results suggest that, in this dataset, emotional flow patterns at the sequence level are broadly comparable between human and AI text, while noticeable differences lie in lexical richness and verbosity. The paper presents a reproducible methodology for comparing emotion dynamics in hybrid humanAI text and identifies directions for more fine-grained measures of emotional authenticity.

title: Using a PIR Sensor to Wirelessly Actuate a Garden Pest Deterrent
authors: Andrey Mosin, Cheol-Hong Min
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The goal of this project is to develop a wireless sensor network that connects multiple independent systems to enhance garden health. Two separate submodules are created, each serving a different purpose. The first is an actuating scarecrow. It uses a Passive Infrared (PIR) sensor to detect motion in the garden and sends a Wi-Fi signal to a scarecrow mannequin, which then activates to scare off pests. This design includes two nodes: the detection node with the PIR sensor and the actuation node with the mannequin. The second submodule is a camera-based pollinator monitoring module. These nodes can be placed at any distance apart, as long as they are within Wi-Fi range, and still operate correctly. This allows the mannequin to be moved to new locations, preventing pests from becoming accustomed to it. Additionally, at regular intervals, such as once a day, the visitor count is sent to the central hub node to track visitor counts. A prototype was successfully developed and tested for home use.

title: Bangladeshi Folk Musical Instrument Classification Using Deep Learning and Transformers
authors: Sourav Bhowmik Joy, M. Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Musical instrument classification has been extensively studied in Western and classical music, yet it remains largely underexplored in the context of Bangladeshi folk music, despite its rich and diverse instrumental heritage. In this work, we address this gap by constructing a dataset of 7,509 audio samples across 10 different Bangladeshi folk musical instrument classes. We first develop a convolutional neural network (CNN) using Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) which achieves a test accuracy of 97.27%. Transformer models, widely used in speech and audio tasks, have not previously been explored for classifying Bangladeshi folk instruments. Hence, in this research, we fine-tuned two state-of-the-art transformer architectures, Wav2Vec2 and Audio Spectrogram Transformer (AST), achieving test accuracies of 98.4% and 99.06% respectively. These results surpass the traditional machine learning methods reported in prior work. Overall, the compiled dataset and models introduced in this study not only provide a benchmark for future research but also contribute to the preservation, analysis, and digital documentation of Bangladesh's rich musical heritage.

title: Energy Characterisation of a UWB-based Indoor Positioning System
authors: Ioannis Sofianidis, Vasileios Konstantakos, Vasileios Serasidis, Kostas Siozios, Spyridon Nikolaidis
abstract: Ultra-wideband (UWB) technology offers centimeter-level accuracy for indoor positioning, but its viability in battery-operated devices depends on energy efficiency. While research has focused on UWB’s ranging performance, its energy consumption characteristics are less explored. This work presents a detailed energy profile of an indoor positioning system based on the commercial DWM1001 UWB module. By analysing the current consumption of tag and anchor devices under various operational states — including active measuring state, idle, listening, and low-power sleep — we quantify the significant energy cost of continuous operation. Additionally the implementation of accelerometer - triggered sleep modes reduces the tag’s energy consumption per measurement cycle by 86.11% compared to a standard idle-wait approach. These findings provide essential data and strategies for optimizing UWB-based tags, enabling multi-month battery powered operation in applications such as tracking and behavioural analytics.

title: Threshold Voltage Control Techniques for Advanced Nodes
authors: Yuxiang, Wang Yi Yuan, Yue Zhang, Wei Sun, Xin Sun, Hsien-Cheng Wang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Multi-Threshold-Voltage (Multi-VT) technology serves as a cornerstone for balancing performance and power in advanced CMOS nodes. This work systematically reviews scalable VT control techniques for FinFET and gate-all-around (GAA) architectures, with a focus on work-function metal (WFM) thickness tuning and interfacial dipole engineering. For GAA devices, the stringent Tsus constraint necessitates "zero-thickness" dipole engineering, categorized via integration strategy and assessed in terms of VT shift efficiency, EOT penalty, and reliability. Novel dipole materials (rare-earth oxides, ZrO) are also addressed. This study offers critical insights into Multi-VT implementation for sub-5 nm technology nodes.

title: Scabio: A Deep Learning-Based Mobile Application for Scabies Screening and Community Surveillance in Low-Resource Settings
authors: Partha Roy, Topu Biswas
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Worldwide, scabies remains a significant and persistent public health concern, disproportionately affecting underserved and resource-limited communities. A patient without a diagnosis or expert counseling often self-medicates, which makes the condition worse and spreads through skin-to-skin contact. Accurate early-stage diagnosis is challenging in busy hospitals and costly in private hospitals. To address this problem, this study proposes Scabio, an intelligent deep learning-based mobile application capable of detecting scabies directly from photographic skin images. First, we constructed a dataset of 800 images collected from the outpatient dermatology department (OPD) of Chittagong Medical College Hospital (CMCH) using multiple devices with complete ethical clearance and permission. We evaluated state-of-the-art convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, including MobileNetV2, InceptionV3, and DenseNet121 for training this dataset. The best-performing MobileNetV2 model achieved a strong testing accuracy of 87.5%, a recall of 83.3%, and an F1-score of 86.96%. Grad-CAM heatmaps were used to demonstrate that the model was making the right decision. The primary limitation of this study is the small dataset; the majority of cases had altered lesions as a result of previous self-medication, making clinical identification challenging even for specialists. Because of this, only a small number of identifiable scabies lesions could be gathered for the dataset under the supervision of a dermatologist, properly annotated, and cross-verified. This study shows that it is a userfriendly diagnostic tool for early-stage treatment and helps AI adaptation in healthcare.

title: Reproducible Machine Learning Pipeline for Predicting Cosmetic Chemical Carcinogenicity and Reproductive Toxicity Using Molecular Descriptors and Fingerprints
authors: Shah Mohammad Rizvi, Rimo Bhuiyan, Ferdaus Anam Jibon, Sumaiya Alam Shorna, Md. Aman Uddin Siyam, Nur-A-Jannat Shuchi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Ensuring cosmetic safety requires a reliable toxicological assessment. Traditional animal studies are resourceintensive and ethically constrained, while existing computational methods suffer from limited coverage, unrealistic labels, and nonreproducible workflows. We present a transparent ML framework predicting carcinogenicity and reproductive toxicity using 11,555 validated structures from ToxCast and Tox21 databasesrepresenting a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 0}$</tex>-fold expansion over prior regulatory studies. We corrected severely skewed 88.7 % cancer prevalence to a realistic 2.55 % using IARC and Proposition 65 classifications, establishing 12.96 % reproductive toxicity prevalence from validated Tox21 endocrine assays. Addressing extreme class imbalance (cancer 1:38.3 ratio), we applied Adaptive Synthetic Sampling (ADASYN) exclusively to training data with endpoint-appropriate evaluation metrics: Precision-Recall AUC for cancer's extreme rarity, ROC-AUC for reproductive toxicity's moderate imbalance. Random Forest and XGBoost employ 2,069 features (21 descriptors plus 2,048-bit Morgan fingerprints). XGBoost achieved cancer PR-AUC 0.0505 (4.4-fold improvement over naive baseline, realistic given data scarcity); Random Forest achieved reproductive ROC-AUC 0.705 (2.6fold baseline improvement). Feature analysis revealed structural fingerprints capture cancer-specific toxicophores while physicochemical properties (LogP, molecular weight, topological surface area) drive reproductive toxicity prediction via receptor-binding mechanisms. SafeSkinAI provides preliminary screening complementing traditional testing. Complete pipeline documentation with preserved artifacts ensures auditability while explicitly acknowledging limitations, including the absence of scaffoldbased validation.

title: Decision Support Systems to Organizational Effectiveness
authors: Ibrahim Hatamleh, Adel Mahmoud Al Samman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study aims to identify and clarify the importance of decision support systems (DSS) in enhancing the effectiveness of organizations operating in engineering projects in the Kingdom of Bahrain. Data was collected from a variety of relevant sources—both direct and indirect—such as recent books, academic research, and scientific conferences. These scientific sources played a crucial role in revealing the impact of the independent variables—the dimensions of organizational effectiveness (operational efficiency, strategic alignment, organizational coordination, and human performance)—on the dependent variable. The results indicated a strong positive relationship between decision support systems and organizational effectiveness. Accordingly, it can be concluded that the methodology followed in this research paper, in achieving its objectives, relies primarily on an in-depth analysis of information related to decision support systems (DSS) and their impact on organizational effectiveness.

title: RF Signal Recognition for UAV Detection Based on Multi-Order Correlation in Spectral Domain and Deep Learning
authors: Qiuyu Lu, Ronghong Jin, Liu Yang, Xiaonan Zhao, Huan Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: In order to effectively recognize wireless signals from unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), a radio frequency (RF) signal recognition method based on multi-order correlation in spectral domain and deep learning is proposed. Two types of spectra for signal representation are utilizes as the frequency spectrum and multi-order cyclic autocorrelation functions. By extracting spectral characteristics and cyclostationary features, this approach achieves accurate signal recognition while maintaining robustness against frequency hopping. In the experiment, results show that the max accuracies of 99.71% and 99.36% are achieved respectively by the proposed method and spectral method when applied to the public dataset. Furthermore, the benefits of the proposed method would become more pronounced when confronted with limited training data, where the mean accuracy can maintain 98.79% in contrast to 69.63% with spectral method.

title: Integrated CAN-Bus System: Advanced Data Visualization for Vehicle Performance Optimization
authors: Georgios Kourtis, Paris Kitsos
abstract: Motorsport-focused cars rely on standalone engine control units that are often not compatible with the stock instrument cluster and diagnostic system. This limits access to important vehicle information and diagnostic analysis without expensive proprietary solutions. This paper introduces a low-cost software-defined instrument cluster aiming to replace the standard units for enhanced real-time display, diagnostic analysis, logging, and telemetric support. Modular software design supports deterministic analysis without modifying the firmware of the Engine Control Unit (ECU). Signal and limit flag analysis are done on the application layer to provide more in-depth analysis beyond stock capabilities. Has been successfully integrated into a vehicle with a standalone ECU to provide a stable and compatible real-time system that works in conjunction with external telemetric analysis software such as Grafana.

title: Effective Minimization of Failure-Inducing Tests Using Convention-Aware Slicing
authors: Sara Baradaran, Mukund Raghothaman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: The minimization of failing tests is among the first steps in the debugging process. In this paper, we propose a slicingbased approach to this problem. The technique utilizes a new data flow analysis which leverages coding conventions to efficiently approximate the behavior of callee procedures without being aware of their code. This allows us to quickly derive smaller test versions which trigger the same failure as the original tests. We also incorporate static and dynamic analysis to preserve necessary control dependencies, which further guarantees the well-formedness and executability of the output tests. We have implemented our approach in a tool named FITSLICER, which performs test case minimization for Java programs. When applied to real-world failing tests from the Defects4J dataset, our evaluation shows that FITSLICER reduces the size of these tests on average by 40%. Moreover, when the minimized tests are executed, in 53% of the cases, they create failing traces which are on average 24% shorter than the original traces. These simplified test versions also allow DStar, the most effective spectrum-based fault localization formula, to identify 11% and 7% more true faults within its top-1 and top-5 predictions, respectively.

title: ALPR-Based Vehicle Access Control and Visitor Management for Sri Lankan Campuses
authors: Jeevathuvarakan Thevarajan, Vaishali Ravi, Vijayakanthan Ganesalingam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of vehicular traffic within Sri Lankan university campuses has necessitated the transition from labor-intensive manual verification to robust, automated access control architectures. However, deploying standard Automatic License Plate Recognition (ALPR) systems in this context is challenged by high intra-class variance, specifically the concurrent circulation of legacy plates (containing provincial codes) and modern formats (devoid of regional identifiers). This paper presents the design, implementation, and empirical evaluation of a domain-adaptive access control framework. The proposed system integrates passive infrared (PIR) triggering with a YOLOv11n-based detection pipeline and a novel adaptive string parsing algorithm to reconcile heterogeneous plate formats. To validate this approach, we introduce a manually curated dataset of 800 newly collected images, specifically designed to address the operational imbalance between legacy formats (approx. 69%) and modern formats (approx. 31 %). These images were combined with 502 publicly available images to form the full training corpus. Experimental results demonstrate that this adaptive framework achieves a recognition accuracy of 97.1 % and an inference latency of 45 ms per frame, effectively mitigating OCR errors caused by regional code interference. Furthermore, the system incorporates an automated visitor profiling mechanism, offering a contactless, scalable alternative to traditional tokenbased security.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Model with Trainable Quanvolutional Layer for Image Classification
authors: Ayon Kumar Dea, Himel Saha
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In this paper we propose a lightweight hybrid quantum classical model for image classification. The model introduces patchwise trainable quanvolution to extract local features applying a quantum circuit to 2 × 2 patches of the image. It then mixes them with a 1 × 1 convolutional operation. We benchmark the approach against a classical baseline and a fixed quanvolution variant on standard dataset under matched training settings. The resulting layer from our model is tiny, easy to integrate, stable while operating on CPU. This approach offers a solution for resource constrained environment maintaining a small parameter footprint.

title: Channel Length Scaling of Oxide Semiconductor Ferroelectric Thin Film Transistor
authors: Wei Zhang, Chen Sun, Xiao Gong, Xuanyao Fong
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Oxide-semiconductor-based ferroelectric thin film transistor (OS-FeTFT) is a promising technology for monolithic 3D integrated back-end-of-line (BEOL) logic. Their performance under channel length scaling is explored in this article using an experimentally corroborated modeling framework. We show that the asymmetric subthreshold swing (SS) of the OS-FeTFT in the programmed and erased states arises from the transition in channel transport mechanism that is induced by the ferro-electric (FE) polarization. Previous models for the OS-FeTFT were unable to capture this phenomenon, and our results show reduction in the SS of OS-FeTFT is robust down to L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">CH</inf> = 40 nm. Furthermore, our results imply reconfigurable BEOL logic gates formed using arrays of OS-FeTFTs should follow a scheme whereby OS-FeTFTs are deactivated by erasing them and steep slope OS-FeTFTs in the programmed state are used as active logic devices implementing the desired logic function.

title: Progress and challenge in ultrawide bandgap semiconductors
authors: Dao-Hua Zhang, Xian-Hu Zha, Jiaxiang Chen, Rong-Jun Zhang, Tianhe Mi, Yu-Xi Wan
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Ultrawide bandgap semiconductors, including gallium oxide, aluminum nitride, and diamond, have been attracting great attention due to their unique properties suitable for high voltage and high temperature applications. In this paper, we discuss the challenges of the ultrawide bandgap semiconductors and present the recent progress made in materials and devices of the ultrawide bandgap semiconductors. These include improvement of p-type doping in gallium oxide, new technique for surface treatment and effect on device performance of Gallium oxide, high performance AlN/AlGaN HEMTs, and high breakdown voltage diamond SBD devices.

title: Three-Layer Stacking Technologies Using Cu-Cu Hybrid Bonding
authors: Nobutoshi Fujii
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Cu-Cu hybrid bonding is a key technology for fabricating stacked semiconductor devices. We have successfully enhanced the functionality of complementary metal oxide semiconductor image sensors (CIS) using layer-stacking technology. To meet the demand for next-generation advanced CIS, we developed a three-layer stacking process using Cu-Cu hybrid bonding for two configurations: wafer-on-wafer-on-wafer (WoWoW) and chip-on-wafer-on-wafer (CoWoW). In both processes with fine Cu-Cu connection pitch, precise alignment during bonding is essential. We investigated and resolved various misalignment issues, with a particular focus on scaling factors for WoW and CoW. In the WoWoW process, the scaling misalignment depends primarily on the initial bonding process, whereas in the CoW process, it is governed by the chip thickness rather than the bonding process itself.

title: Enhancing Polyp Segmentation in Endoscopic Images Using Attention-Enhanced U-Nets
authors: Kahandaker Rakibul Hasan, Kazi Sanir Ahmed, Efat Khan, Umme Fawzia Rahim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate automatic polyp segmentation in endoscopic images is essential for early colorectal cancer detection. Existing models, however, often have a lack of trade-off between segmentation performance and real-time inference speed, limiting their clinical applicability. In this study, we proposed ECA-U-Net, a U-Net backbone with Efficient Channel Attention (ECA), and compare it against baseline U-Net and SE-U-Net on two widely used benchmark datasets: Kvasir-SEG and CVC-ClinicDB. The models were assessed using the Dice coefficient, Intersection over Union (IoU), accuracy, precision, recall, and inference speed (FPS). Models were trained using Binary Cross-Entropy, Dice, and a combined BCE-Dice loss. ECA-U-Net provides significant segmentation performance, with a Dice of 0.857 and IoU of 0.751 on Kvasir-SEG, and a Dice of 0.939 and IoU of 0.886 on CVC-ClinicDB. These results show clear improvements in mask alignment and boundary accuracy over the baseline. While SE-U-Net incurs a larger computational cost, U-Net attains the highest inference speed of around 120 FPS. However, ECA-UNet provides a strong balance between precision and efficiency, maintaining real-time performance above 90 FPS, achieving competitive segmentation metrics with fewer parameters than SE-U-Net. The results demonstrate that deep learning can provide fast and accurate polyp segmentation in endoscopy. The developed ECA-U-Net has great potential to be applied to realtime colorectal cancer screening, as it achieves a satisfactory balance between accuracy, efficiency, and model compactness, and can also be extended to other medical image segmentation tasks that require both precision and computational speed.

title: Clickbait Video Detection: A Comprehensive Study Using Transformer
authors: Rehena Sultana, Tajnim Jahan, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Nowadays, individuals spend an abundance of time on the web, and they are becoming increasingly reliant on it every day. However, some dishonest content makers exploit this and create a captivating title to deceive individuals in general. As a result, the content quality is lowered and their time and credibility are wasted. In order to distinguish between clickbait and non- clickbait videos, we propose a framework for detecting clickbait videos. Maximum existing research consider only title but we comprehensively explore several modalities like- title, thumbnail and audio. In our research, we utilized a publicly available Bangla dataset and we add audio column in this dataset. Furthermore, we applied a variety of preprocessing techniques to clean the data. We apply several deep learning and transformer models with fine-tuning. After an rigorous experimentation, BanglaBERT and ViT perform better as it contextaware and captures high-level features.

title: Pattern-Aware Graph Neural Networks for Handling Missing Data
authors: Minett Tran, Taehee Jeong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Missing data is ubiquitous in real-world datasets. Traditional methods either discard incomplete samples or apply imputation techniques that ignore potentially informative missingness patterns, implicitly assuming that missingness occurs randomly. However, missingness patterns might provide additional information. We propose pattern-aware graph neural networks that explicitly encode which features are missing alongside observed values. We used four encoding strategies-learned embeddings, frozen random embeddings, statistical features, and hierarchical representations-across seven UCI datasets with naturally occurring missingness. Our Pattern-aware methods achieve substantial improvements over baselines, with an average improvement of $17 \%$ in balanced accuracy and $22 \%$ in F1-macro across all datasets. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/TranMinett/pattern_aware_GRAPE.

title: Cybersecurity: Opportunities and Challenges in the Context of Digital Transformation and Its Role in Strategic Decision-Making
authors: Khaled Al-Mhasnah, Abedalrahim A L-Arqan, Mahmoud Khalifa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)

title: OcuXAI-App: A Deep Learning Based Mobile Application for Eye Disease Classification Using Ensemble Technique with Enhanced Interpretability
authors: Tasnia Haque Kheya, Shaikh Prothomaa Binte Minhaz
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is emerging as a powerful tool for the classification of eye disease, offering significant potential to improve healthcare outcomes. In developing countries such as Bangladesh, this technology is particularly salient, where a high prevalence of visual impairment and vision loss is often linked to insufficient diagnostic infrastructure. The core issue lies in diagnostic processes that are neither reliable nor readily available, leading to delayed or incorrect diagnoses and a subsequent rise in avoidable blindness. AI-driven solutions offer a scalable opportunity to address these systemic deficiencies and ensure more people receive critical, timely treatment. Our proposed work comprises a four-stage deep learning-based framework for the automated eye disease classification. The initial stage involves a comprehensive image preprocessing pipeline designed to optimize image quality and enhance the model's robustness and overall effectiveness. Subsequently, the second stage focuses on the extraction of intricate, fine-grained features, enabling the model to learn complex, pathology-specific patterns. For improved predictive performance, the third stage incorporates an ensemble approach, combining predictions from multiple pretrained deep learning models. Finally, to erase the limitations of black-box transparent models, our proposed fourth-stage model employs explainable AI (XAI) techniques. This provides constructive feedback on the model's decision-making process and makes the model more interpretable and trustworthy for clinical use. The model achieves a validation accuracy of 97 % and has been integrated into the android application for practical use in real-life and ease of use by end-users.

title: Comparative Analysis of TDOA and SRP-Beamforming Algorithms for 3D Sound Source Localization Under Variable Acoustic Conditions
authors: A. T. S. Amasith, G. A. D. K. N. Galagamaarachchi, K. D. Y. T. D. Senevirathne, A. M. Harsha S. Abeykoon, D. Kasun Prasanga, R. M. Maheshi Ruwanthika
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Sound Source Localization (SSL) is a core capability in robotics, human-machine interaction, and acoustic monitoring, where the accuracy of localization is strongly affected by noise, reverberation, and the spectral structure of the source signal. Among signal-processing approaches, Time Difference of Arrival (TDOA) methods are attractive due to their computational efficiency, while Steered Response Power (SRP) beamforming is widely used for its robustness in adverse acoustic conditions. However, comprehensive benchmarks that compare these paradigms in 3D space while also isolating the impact of different deterministic excitation waveforms remain limited. In this work, we present a systematic comparative analysis of a GCC-PHAT-based TDOA framework and an SRP beamforming framework using a four-microphone tetrahedral array. A dedicated simulation platform is developed to generate time-synchronized multichannel signals for controlled 3D source locations, enabling both algorithms to be evaluated under identical conditions. Robustness is assessed across a wide range of signal-to-noise ratios, and waveform sensitivity is investigated using chirp, pulse, and sinusoidal excitations. To validate whether simulation trends generalize to practical deployment, experiments are additionally conducted using a physical prototype built with four ICS-43434 digital micro-electro mechanical systems (MEMS) microphones interfaced to a Teensy 4.1 microcontroller.

title: A Dual-Branch CNN Fusion Approach for Robust Chest X-ray Disease Classification
authors: Amjad Alshahrani, Abdulmohsen Algarni, Eman Alqaissi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Chest X-ray (CXR) imaging is one of the most widely used modalities for the diagnosis of thoracic diseases, yet manual interpretation is time-consuming and prone to inter observer variability. Although convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have achieved notable progress in automated CXR analysis, their performance still requires improvement, particularly in capturing the complex and overlapping visual patterns present in multi-label chest disease classification. Relying on a single CNN architecture often limits the ability to extract diverse and complementary features necessary for robust diagnosis. In this work, we propose a dual-branch CNN fusion framework that integrates DenseNet-121 and ResNet-152 feature representations for chest disease classification on the CheXpert dataset. Preliminary experiments demonstrate that the proposed fusion approach achieves improved macro-AUC@5 compared to individual CNN baselines, highlighting its potential for more reliable and generalizable CXR diagnosis.

title: Thermal Comfort Process Control
authors: Pavol Belany, Zuzana Florkova, Peter Hrabovsky, Roman Budjac
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The control system of intelligent buildings forms a core element of energy management, coordinating heating, cooling, ventilation, and lighting to maintain thermal comfort and high efficiency. Continuous monitoring of indoor parameters together with meteorological data (wind speed, outdoor temperature, solar radiation) enables real-time adaptation to external conditions and reduces thermal losses. Lighting significantly affects thermal comfort: artificial sources generate radiant heat that alters local temperature, while daylight introduces variable solar gains. Intelligent control of lighting intensity, operating time, and shading maintains a balance between illumination and temperature and reduces the need for cooling. The Comfort Sense subsystem integrates regulation of temperature, humidity, CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration, and lighting to create a stable microclimate for long-term occupancy. In laboratory environments, where equipment and lighting produce substantial heat gains, automated air exchange and adaptive light control prevent temperature fluctuations and glare. Mobile indoor monitoring identifies local irregularities and supports the optimization of equipment placement and lighting layout. System performance is assessed through temperature stability, humidity deviation, CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> trends, mean radiant temperature, illuminance uniformity, HVAC response time, specific energy use, load reduction, comfort indices (PMV/PPD), and lighting-related heat gains.

title: PrivGNN: Privacy-Preserving Graph Neural Network Inference Based on Collaborative Computing for Cyber-Physical-Social Systems
authors: Jing Wang, Tingyu Wang, Shenghao Liu, Yudi Zhang, Libing Wu, Debiao He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) are gaining significant attention for enabling “cognitive intelligence” by processing complex, non-Euclidean data, particularly in cyber-physical-social systems (CPSS). These systems rely on real-time decision-making to optimize interactions across physical, social, and digital environments. Collaborative learning further enhances GNNs by allowing distributed entities to learn together from shared data, improving scalability and intelligence. However, the intricate structure of graph data and the increasing use of GNNs raise serious privacy concerns, particularly when sensitive data from distributed artificial intelligence of things (AIoT) devices of CPSS is involved. Although several privacy-preserving GNN schemes have been proposed, they often incur prohibitive communication and computation costs. To address the challenges, we propose PrivGNN, an efficient and privacy-preserving system for GNN inference with the help of multiple cloud servers. This system leverages novel variants of secret sharing techniques to develop secure multiparty computing protocols that safeguard data privacy. Our security analysis and performance evaluations confirm that PrivGNN ensures robust security, high efficiency, and scalability in cloud-based inference services. Almost each subprotocol reduces protocol communication costs from 33% to 40% and computation times from 25% to 30% in the LAN without compromising security. Benchmarking on the Cora and Pubmed datasets shows that PrivGNN cuts overall communication expenses by at least 30%, highlighting its practical value for privacy-sensitive, large-scale applications in AIoT and CPSS.

title: Infrared and Visible Image Fusion Using Bimodal Neuron and Dynamic Receptive Field Mechanisms
authors: Shaobing Gao, Minjie Tan, Shun Lv, Yiguang Liu, Yongjie Li
abstract: Infrared and visible image fusion (IVIF) significantly enhances scene interpretation by integrating broad-spectrum information. Drawing inspiration from specific snakes that possess an evolutionarily optimized bimodal sensory system capable of parallel processing infrared and visible radiation, we propose a novel IVIF framework incorporating two key elements: nonlinear cross-modal interactions across six distinct classes of snake bimodal neurons and dynamic center-surround receptive field organization. These biological principles are mathematically formalized and integrated within a deep neural network (DNN), optimized through an object detection region-guided loss and a frequency-dependent fusion loss that enable data-driven fusion strategy learning. Experimental results demonstrate that the optimized model effectively emulates the infrared-visible information integration observed in snake bimodal neurons. Critically, the nonlinear bimodal neurons capture a significantly greater amount of edge information and finer mid-to-high-frequency details, which are essential for the subsequent reconstruction of the fused image. Furthermore, a comprehensive evaluation of visual quality, encompassing both qualitative and quantitative assessments on six datasets, along with extensive object detection and semantic segmentation experiments using the fused images in both daytime and nighttime scenarios, demonstrates that our model outperforms traditional biologically-inspired IVIF algorithms, achieving performance comparable to SOTA DNN-based methods.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Algorithm Design for Coupled Phase-Shift Optimization of STAR-RIS
authors: Su-Chang Lim, Jong-Chan Kim
abstract: Simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) enables 360-degree full-space coverage by controlling both transmitted and reflected signals through each element. However, the coupled phase-shift model, where the transmission and reflection coefficients are physically interdependent, transforms the phase optimization into an extremely complex non-convex combinatorial problem that classical algorithms struggle to solve efficiently. In this paper, we propose a hybrid quantum-classical framework that employs the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) to optimize the coupled phase-shift configuration of STAR-RIS. Each discrete phase configuration is mapped to a qubit-based Ising Hamiltonian, enabling QAOA to explore the exponentially large solution space through quantum superposition and entanglement. To address the scalability challenge of simulating large antenna arrays (e.g., 8 × 8 = 64 elements), we introduce a variable grouping (tiling) strategy that partitions the RIS into manageable sub-arrays of 2 × 2 elements, each requiring only 8 qubits for 2-bit phase quantization. Our comprehensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed QAOA-based optimizer achieves an approximation ratio exceeding 0.94 with 10 QAOA layers, maintains over 86% fidelity under 2% gate error rates in noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) environments, and exhibits robust performance under imperfect channel state information with less than 5% degradation at 30% CSI error variance. The energy efficiency analysis confirms that the QAOA-optimized STAR-RIS achieves approximately 1.7× higher spectral efficiency per unit energy compared to conventional reflect-only RIS. Furthermore, the proposed tiling framework scales linearly with array size, enabling deployment on arrays up to 16 × 16 elements while maintaining constant per-group quantum resource requirements.

title: Direct Switching Current Control for Series Resonant Dual Active Bridge DC-AC Converters Based on Time Domain Analysis
authors: Yangyang Feng, Jiatao Yang, Liuyu Zeng, Rui Li
abstract: Optimal control of series resonant dual active bridge (SRDAB) converters has attracted significant attention in recent years. Existing control methodologies primarily rely on either offline optimization or online calculations based on simplified models, which inherently involve a trade-off between computational burden and control accuracy. To address this challenge, this article proposes a novel direct switching current control strategy for SRDAB DC–AC converters based on accurate time-domain analysis (TDA). Unlike conventional methods that primarily focus on power regulation and have difficulty in accurately modeling instantaneous switching currents, the proposed strategy directly regulates the commutation current predicted by the TDA model, thereby enabling reliable ZVS over a wide operating range. By establishing a high-fidelity time-domain model, the state trajectory of the resonant current and voltage is analyzed, and the analytical relationships between switching currents and control variables are derived. Building on this analysis, an online direct switching current control strategy is designed to precisely regulate critical turn-on currents. Dedicated zero voltage switching (ZVS) and backflow power minimization schemes are proposed to optimize soft-switching performance and reduce switching losses. Finally, a 500 W hardware prototype is developed and the experimental results validate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed strategy.

title: A Two-Stage Pareto-Driven Framework for Adaptive Resource Optimization in Lightweight Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Goce Stevanoski, Aleksandar Risteski, Nevena Serafimova, Marko Porjazovski, Mitko Bogdanoski
abstract: The lack of adequate system resources can significantly affect the safety and security of intrusion detection systems implemented to work within the Internet of Things, edge or fog settings. Deployed devices in these environments should balance the operational and security requirements of the systems. This issue is critical since operational environments are stochastic, and deploying devices that have static configurations or scalarized optimization approaches cannot adequately respond to the changing conditions. This work proposes the use of A2DAPT - A Two-Stage Pareto-Driven Framework for Adaptive Resource Optimization in Lightweight Intrusion Detection Systems. This new scheme divides the proposed framework into an offline and an online stage and adapts the resource utilization of lightweight IDS to resource budget levels. In the offline stage, the exploration of the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) is used to determine the Pareto set of optimal IDS settings. In the online stage, A2DAPT enforces a deep reinforcement learning controller based on a Dueling Double Deep Q-Network with Prioritized Experience Replay that adapts the intrusion detection system’s configuration in relation to the non-stationary traffic by taking actions from the Pareto set determined in the offline stage. The use of the Pareto set as an action space ensures the use of safe and interpretable decisions by the intrusion detection system during non-stationary traffic. Therefore, the evaluation of the A2DAPT, in this study, focuses on its runtime behavior while operating in a resource-constrained environment under non-stationary traffic consisting of benign and malicious denial-of-service traffic. The evaluation demonstrated that A2DAPT continuously achieves savings compared to a competitive static baseline with approximately 15% CPU, 10% memory, and 7% bandwidth, all of that without significant degradation of the detection performance. The results confirm that the combination of Pareto optimization solutions with deep reinforcement learning agents enables efficient intrusion detection operation in resource-constrained environments.

title: Design of Molecular Communication Amplifier Based on Mesoporous Silica Nanoparticles
authors: Ling Zhang, Huiyu Luo, Carolina Alventosa, Yiming Tang, Estela Climent, Antoni Llopis-Lorente, Ramón Martínez-Mañez, Lin Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Molecular, Biological, and Multi-Scale Communications
abstract: Molecular communication (MC) employs molecules as information carriers. However, molecules inevitably suffer from concentration attenuation and molecular degradation due to diffusion, convection, and chemical interactions with the environment. To address this challenge, we design an MC amplifier based on mesoporous silica nanoparticles (MSNs). MSNs feature highly ordered porous structures, enabling the encapsulation of a large number of molecules and regulated release in response to specific chemical inputs through functionalized molecular gates. In the MC amplifier, incoming signal molecules chemically interact with molecular gates on the MSNs, triggering the opening of mesopores and the release of the stored molecules. The molecules then propagate through the channel and serve as the input for the subsequent amplification stage, where they induce gate-opening reactions, thereby enabling cascaded, multi-stage molecular amplification. The amplification process is modeled using chemical reaction kinetics equations, and the system performance is evaluated in terms of an amplification factor, demonstrating effective compensation for molecular attenuation during propagation. This work provides a potential strategy for long-distance molecular communication.

title: A Pseudo-gradient Perturbation Guided Meta Adaptive Orthogonal Network for Fault Diagnosis under Unseen Conditions
authors: Haojie Chen, Yongyi Chen, Dan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In the domain generalization fault diagnosis task, the entanglement between fault features and domain features severely hinders the learning of domain-invariant features. Therefore, a pseudo-gradient perturbation guided meta adaptive orthogonal network (PP-MAON) is proposed in this article. Firstly, virtual perturbations are constructed based on the gradient information derived from pseudo labels. By guiding the model to resist such perturbations during the iterative training of meta-tasks, its ability to learn domain-invariant features is enhanced. Next, a dual orthogonality constraint mechanism is designed to further reduce feature redundancy and facilitate feature disentanglement. Then, a dedicated meta-loss learner is introduced to monitor the status of meta-tasks in real time and dynamically adjust the loss weights of the model, thereby achieving an optimal balance between diagnosis accuracy and domain invariance. Finally, comparative experiments are conducted against existing domain generalization fault diagnosis methods under multiple variable conditions on two datasets, and the results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Extracting sea ice topographic features from triangulated surface models_supp1-3690366.pdf
authors: Yunting Song
abstract: Pressure ridges are the dominant topographic features of sea ice, playing a critical role in momentum transfer between the atmosphere and ocean. However, characterizing their three-dimensional morphology remains a challenge. Traditional methods, applied to linear profile data from laser altimeters, cannot extract two-dimensional features, specifically the horizontal orientation, extent, and connectivity of deformed ice. To address this gap, this paper introduces a novel strategy to extract pressure ridges directly from altimeter swath data by utilizing Triangulated Irregular Networks (TINs) and surface topology. Unlike raster grid-based models, TIN-based surface models do not require interpolation, thereby preserving the original point dataset and effectively retaining line features, which are essential for ridge extraction. Our algorithm integrates discrete Morse theory with surface roughness constraints to identify ridge structure lines from level sea ice. Additionally, we discuss how to address data dropout issues in the generation of TIN-based surface model, which prevents the correct extraction of ridge structures. The point cloud used in this study is high-resolution swath data from the NASA Operation IceBridge (OIB) Airborne Topographic Mapper (ATM). The validation against two independent datasets, the linear profile data collected by Ice, Cloud, and land Elevation Satellite (ICESat-2) and optical imagery captured during the OIB campaign, demonstrates that the proposed ridge extraction method can effectively detect ridge lines from TIN-based models. Such ridge features can provide more information of sea ice ridges, such as length, average height, and orientation, which are not possible when using linear profiles or gridded surface models.

title: Research on Transformer Partial Discharge Pattern Recognition Method Based on Multi-Modal Information Enhancement and Fusion_supp1-3690690.docx
authors: Li Junhao
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) pattern recognition is a critical component of transformer fault diagnosis, providing a technical basis for equipment maintenance by identifying specific defect types. However, PD signals are frequently corrupted by complex field noise, and single-sensor approaches often fail to comprehensively characterize discharge features, leading to limited diagnostic accuracy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a transformer PD pattern recognition method based on multi-source signal enhancement and fusion diagnosis using current, ultrasonic, and electromagnetic wave signals. First, a Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement (MSFE) network is employed for noise suppression and feature reinforcement to mitigate the impact of interference on subsequent diagnostics. Subsequently, a Multi-Scale Depth-Wise (MSDW) convolution network is employed to extract fine-grained features from the three categories of signals, while a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network is integrated to model the temporal dependencies of impulsive non-stationary signals. Furthermore, an Adaptive Graph-guided Cross-modal Attention (AGCA) mechanism is designed to adaptively learn inter-modal correlations, enabling precise identification of diverse PD patterns. Validation was performed on a laboratory dataset obtained from a 110kV transformer based multi-modal data acquisition platform involving four typical PD defect models, namely creeping, corona, void, and floating discharges. The proposed method yields a remarkable improvement in diagnostic accuracy. This study provides an innovative solution for the intelligent pattern recognition of partial discharges in power transformers.

title: Deep Learning-Based End-to-End Underwater Structural Crack Detection with YOLOv8 and Synthetic Augmentation for Enhanced Visibility
authors: G. Sumithra, Nithyashree V, Purushothaman Karthikeyan, Navaneetha Krishnan K P
abstract: Underwater dam crack detection is critical for ensuring the structural safety and long-term durability of hydraulic infrastructure. However, accurate detection remains challenging due to poor illumination, color distortion, and water turbidity in underwater environments. This work presents an automated underwater crack detection framework based on the YOLOv8 deep learning model for precise localization of surface cracks in dam structures. To enhance dataset diversity and improve model generalization, Mosaic and MixUp data augmentation techniques are employed. Image quality is further improved through a comprehensive preprocessing pipeline involving grayscale conversion, anisotropic diffusion, contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), sharpening, and Lab color space transformation, where L-channel enhancement significantly improves brightness and crack visibility. The proposed system is evaluated using standard performance metrics, including Intersection over Union (IoU), precision, recall, F1-score, and mean Average Precision (mAP). Comparative experiments with YOLOv6, YOLOv8 without fine-tuning, and fine-tuned YOLOv8 demonstrate the clear superiority of the fine-tuned model, achieving a precision of 95.1 percent, recall of 88.1 percent, and an mAP @ 0.5 of 91.4 percent.

title:
Impacts of Temperature on the Performance of Ge
                    <sub>1−x</sub>
                    Sn
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    -based Mid-infrared Light-Emitting Diodes on Si Substrate
authors: Harshvardhan Kumar, Yash Bijawat, P. Susthitha Menon
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work presents silicon (Si)-based double heterojunction light-emitting diodes (DHLEDs) that incorporate the Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1−x</inf>Sn<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> alloy within the active layer. This study examines the effects of temperature (T) and Sn concentration on performance metrics, including terminal currents, emitted power, and emission spectrum. The results demonstrate notable and promising outcomes at room temperature within the short-wave infrared (SWIR) range (λ = 1.7-2.4 µm). The results indicate that an increase in temperature leads to a corresponding rise in emitted power and the intensity of the emission spectrum. For T up to 250 K, a redshift occurs in the peak wavelength of the emitted light. In contrast, when the T > 250 K, a blueshift is observed in the peak wavelength of the emitted light. Moreover, temperature also significantly affects the turn-on voltage, particularly in that the turn-on voltage decreases with rising temperature. Furthermore, the increase in Sn concentration leads to an increase in the magnitude of the emission spectrum; however, it leads to a decrease in the emitted power. When compared to earlier reported GeSn LEDs, the proposed GeSn LEDs exhibit a low turn-on voltage and high emitted power at T = 300 K. This benchmark demonstrates that the proposed GeSn LEDs could be a potential light source in the SWIR band for both cryogenic and room-temperature applications.

title: Smart Farming for Bangladesh: An IoT and Machine Learning-Based Crop Advisory System
authors: Tasmin Akther, Md. Saif Uddin, Mohammad Osiur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Agriculture is vital to Bangladesh's economy, yet farmers face challenges in selecting suitable crops, fertilizers, and understanding soil types due to the need for specialized expertise. This paper presents an IoT and machine learning-based Crop Advisory System to address these issues by automating crop recommendation, fertilizer prediction, and soil type classification. Using an ESP32 microcontroller, the system collects real-time data on environmental and soil parameters (NPK, temperature, humidity, rainfall, pH, soil texture, moisture) via sensors. Six machine learning models-Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Support Vector Classifier, Naïve Bayes, Decision Tree, and Random Forest-are trained on Kaggle datasets, with preprocessing and VGG16 feature extraction for soil type classification. In addition to the initial train-test evaluation, model robustness was validated using 5 -fold stratified cross-validation, with results reported as mean ± standard deviation. The crop recommendation task achieved consistently high cross-validated performance (e.g., RF: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9959 \pm 0.0027$</tex>; NB: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9945 \pm 0.0018$</tex>), while Logistic Regression and ensemble methods remained strong for fertilizer prediction (e.g., RF: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9821 \pm 0.0264$</tex>). Soil type classification showed moderate but stable performance (LR: 0.660 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.009$</tex>), reflecting dataset limitations. An Android application, integrated with Firebase for real-time data retrieval, provides user-friendly interfaces for farmers to access predictions. This system enhances agricultural decision-making, offering a scalable solution for precision farming in Bangladesh.

title: A Low-Cost Pocket-Sized ECG Monitoring Device with Deep Learning-Based Atrial Fibrillation Classification for Rural Healthcare
authors: Md. Jubayel, Joyosree Ghosh Joya, Rakibul Hasan, Md. Atiqur Rahman Anik, Md. Muzahidul Islam, Md. Jamiul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: If not identified early on, cardiac arrhythmia—especially persistent and paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (AF)—poses major health concerns. A low-cost portable ECG monitoring system with machine learning models integrated for real-time cardiac rhythm categorization into three categories—normal, paroxysmal AF, and persistent AF—is presented in this study. The gadget uses an ECG sensor and an ESP32 microcontroller to record signals, which are subsequently wirelessly sent to an analysis platform on the web. The results are shown on the mobile/web application and directly on the device using user-friendly LED indicators—green for Normal, orange for Paroxysmal, and red for Persistent—after a trained deep learning model processes the ECG data. Without technological expertise, older and rural users can grasp potential cardiac dangers because to the system's dual feedback mechanism, which makes it easy to operate. The suggested method promotes early arrhythmia identification and enables users to promptly seek medical consultation when required by fusing affordability, portability, and accuracy.

title: RBEC: A Dataset for Emotion Detection in Resource-Constrained Bengali Language Using Optimized Transformers and Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Md Shohanur Rahman Shohan, Khondoker Muntahin Hassan Ankur, Md Nasit Jakoan, Md Farukuzzaman Khan, Md Ibrahim Abdullah, Md Habibur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The lack of a standardized and high quality emotion-labeled corpus is a major limitation of Bengali Natural Language Processing and it hinders the construction of effective emotion classification systems in this low-resource language. In order to fill this gap, we have created the Robust Bengali Emotion Corpus (RBEC) which is a well-chosen set of 8,076 text examples which each are manually screened and assigned one of the six core emotions. We perform a full analysis of the classical machine learning algorithms, FastText-based deep neural networks, and transformer models using RBEC to identify the capability of these methods in Bengali emotion recognition. The experimental results show that the fine-tuned XLM-R model shows the best performance, with 84.83% accuracy and F1-score of 84.77% with high Cohen's Kappa and MCC values. The results are a new standard in the classification of Bengali emotions and the significance of high quality and strong datasets in low-resource contexts. The RBEC corpus and comparative findings provide a background resource that will be used to facilitate future studies in emotion-aware NLP applications, domain-specific pretraining, and general improvements of the Bengali language technologies.

title: Performance Evaluation of Cryptographic Technology on Electrocardiogram Monitor for Data Security and Regulatory Compliance in Cardiovascular Disease Risk Management
authors: Zahra Aqilah Mudrikah F, Istikmal, Rina Pudjiastuti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases are a major global cause of death, highlighting the need for continuous heart monitoring. Wearable ECG devices collect sensitive health data, requiring robust protection. This study evaluates Advanced Encryption Standard (AES)-256 encryption on ECG data from Philips Efficia devices, focusing on performance (latency, memory, power) and regulatory compliance with Indonesia’s PDP Law and the U.S. HIPAA. Simulations show AES-256 processes data with acceptable latency (1.79s encryption, 1.52s decryption), memory, and power use. While PDP emphasizes data protection principles, it lacks specific technical standards, unlike HIPAA. The findings confirm AES-256’s suitability for securing wearable ECG data, supporting both technical efficiency and legal compliance, and underscore the importance of more defined national data protection regulations.

title: DermaXNet: Benchmarking Explainable Deep Learning Models for Skin Cancer Diagnosis Using Dermoscopic Images
authors: Musaddiq Al Karim, Yeasmin Ara, Razuan Karim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Skin cancer is the most prevalent form of cancer worldwide. Early diagnosis is essential for improving patient outcomes. This work benchmarks three state-of-the-art deep learning models, namely GoogLeNet-InceptionV3, EfficientNetV2B3, and Swin Transformer, for skin cancer classification using the dermoscopic images from the ISIC archive. Data augmentation addressed c0lass imbalance; the dataset was split in a 70:15:15 ratio for training, validation, and testing. The best performance was achieved by EfficientNetV2B3, with 93.8% test accuracy, 93.68% validation accuracy, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1>0.93$</tex>, followed by Swin Transformer: 90.67% test accuracy, 90.33% validation accuracy, and F1 score of (0.90 - 0.92) and GoogLeNet-InceptionV3 (88.17% test and 83.38% validation accuracy). All models reached high AUC values ranging from 0.97 to 1.00. Grad-CAM improved interpretability by identifying class-discriminative lesion areas. The study provides a broad conceptual framework that includes several deep learning models, data augmentation, and explainable AI. The present study can provide novel insights for the development of reliable AI-assisted skin cancer classification.

title: A Low-Cost Portable IoT System for Environmental Monitoring: Assessing the Impact of Microclimates on UV Radiation
authors: MD. Rakibul Hasan, Md. Shafiqul Islam, Kurratul Ain, MD. Tanvir Ahmed Akash, Md. Hasibul Islam, Indrojit Sarkar
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Urban microclimates show a lot of variation in the environment. This makes regional weather forecasts inadequate for determining personal ultraviolet (UV) radiation exposure. To tackle this issue, this paper discusses the design, implementation, and validation of a portable, low-cost Internet of Things (IoT) system for real-time environmental monitoring. The prototype includes a dual-mode feedback system. It gives immediate on-site data through an integrated LCD and allows for remote, long-term analysis using the ThingSpeak cloud platform. We conducted a month-long data collection campaign by deploying five units in different locations within the Dhaka division. This created a detailed dataset that captures data over time and space. The main contribution of this work is a quantitative analysis of this data. It confirms that while meteorological conditions are strong predictors (sunny: +0.39, rainy: -0.40), local micro-environmental features significantly influence personal safety. Importantly, we measure the protective effect of urban tree shade, which has a notable negative correlation (-0.21) with the UV index. The analysis also shows that cloudy weather does not reliably reduce UV radiation. It presents a weak positive correlation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+\mathbf{0. 1 4}$</tex>), suggesting the influence of scattering effects like the broken cloud phenomenon. This is important for public health awareness. This study validates a practical solution for personal environmental monitoring and provides a solid dataset for developing future predictive models based on machine learning.

title: A Novel Approach of Skin Lesions Segmentation Using Hybrid Polar-Attention Transformer U-Net (PATU-Net) from Dermoscopic Images
authors: Taki Tamim, Md. Sozib Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Automated dermatological diagnosis requires the real time and precise segmentation of the lesions on the skin where classical methods have difficulties with irregular lesion boundaries, artifacts and low-contrast. This paper will enhance the segmentation strength by proposing a hybrid Polar-Attention Transformer U-Net (PATU-Net). The approach incorporates polar-coordinate-based transformation of lesion geometry to normalize the geometry, SCSE-based attention to emphasize informative regions, a metric-sensitive hybrid loss that combines BCE, Dice, Tversky and Focal terms to minimize false negatives. The test-time augmentation of ISIC-2018 data that is ensembled with four single-trained PATU-Net models is used to make stable predictions. Experiment outcomes depict high values of Dice of 0.9351, which is better than most of the current segmentation methods. The results suggest that the suggested PATU-Net ensemble is a valuable and efficient tool to skin lesion segmentation and has a high potential to help analyze dermatology in practice.

title: Physics-Informed Vs. Traditional Neural Networks for Fluid Flow and Heat Transfer
authors: Md Mohsin Kabir, Mobyen Uddin Ahmed, Shahina Begum
