publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deep learning has recently been widely used in fluid dynamics to solve complex partial differential equations. This study presents a comparative study between neural networks (NNs) and physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) for two benchmark problems in fluid dynamics: forced convection in an enclosure and flow past a cylinder. These problems were selected because they are canonical cases in computational fluid dynamics. These are widely used to evaluate numerical methods due to their well-understood physical behavior and relevance to practical applications. The primary goal is to evaluate the performance of both methods in terms of accuracy, generalization, and physical consistency in predicting key fluid dynamic variables, including velocity fields, pressure distributions, and temperature profiles. In the forced convection problem, PINN demonstrated superior performance by accurately capturing the temperature field and streamlines, and by enforcing physical laws directly through the governing equations. Traditional neural networks exhibited limitations in learning the underlying physics. This resulted in asymmetric and less realistic temperature distributions. In the flow past a cylinder problem, PINN effectively modeled the pressure field and streamlines. It exhibits better physical consistency and captures the pressure gradient and flow separation. In contrast, NNs produced periodic and unrealistic flow patterns. It fails to capture critical features such as vortex shedding and recirculation zones.

title: TEAQNET: A Vision-Based Deep Learning Framework for Expert-Level Tea Region Classification Using Standardized Visual Evaluation Environment
authors: Pasindu Jayasekara, Thosini Kumarika, Dakshika Wanniarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Tea is one of the most widely consumed beverages globally. Among the various tea-producing nations, Sri Lanka, known for Ceylon tea, holds a prestigious place in the global tea industry. The market value, quality, and global branding of Ceylon tea depend on its growing regions. The classification of tea is important because the growing regions directly affect tea quality, chemical composition and authenticity. Traditionally, tea tasters use sensory-based methods to manually classify the tea regions based on their expert judgments. However, this approach introduces bias in correct classification due to the human error. This study introduces a standardized vision based Deep learning framework to automate tea region classification based on tea liquor color. The research has been constructed in a controlled vision evaluation environment to collect tea liquor image dataset. The dataset contains 700 images, with 100 images from each region. The dataset was preprocessed to remove the background reflection. It achieved expert level classification using different Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models which were trained on region labeled samples and evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score and Intersection over union (IOU). The experimental results demonstrate that Deep learning models such as ShuffleNetV2 are the most effective model, having a mean accuracy rate of 99.99 % while MobileNetV2 got 99.49 % and RestNet-18 got 98.98 %. The Models demonstrate strong potential as a non-destructive, scalable tool for real-time industry deployment, particularly when integrated into mobile applications for on-site testing.

title: Fast, Fault-Tolerant, and Affordable: A Modern Data Lakehouse ETL Pipeline for Emerging Economies
authors: Sazzad Hossen, Aritra Das, Md. Rakibul Hasan, Mohammad Rezwanul Huq
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Today, enterprises generate massive volumes of semi-structured data from IoT devices, applications, and web services. Traditional ETL systems struggle with such workloads due to rigid schemas, limited scalability, and weak fault tolerance, slowing analytics and machine learning. This paper presents an automated data lakehouse pipeline integrating MongoDB, Apache Spark, Apache Airflow, MinIO S3, and Apache Iceberg. The system extracts data from MongoDB, performs recursive type conversions and schema normalization using Spark, and stores them as Iceberg tables on MinIO. Airflow automates and monitors workflow execution with scheduling and retry support, ensuring robustness and reproducibility. Experiments show notable improvements over Hadoop-based ETL: ingestion <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex> faster, ETL time reduced by 64 %, query latency decreased by 68 %, and CPU usage lowered by 30 %. The pipeline guaranties ACID compliance, supports schema evolution, and integrates with Trino, Superset, and Kubeflow for analytics and machine learning workloads. Compared to cloud warehouses, the architecture is significantly more cost-efficient by eliminating licensing fees, avoiding idle-compute costs, and using low-cost object storage, resulting in lower Total Cost of Ownership (TCO). Overall, the proposed pipeline offers a scalable, fault-tolerant, and futureready solution for modern big data ecosystems.

title: A Large Language Model-Embedded Evolutionary Framework for Wideband Pixelized Patch Antenna Design
authors: Lanhaoyue Wang, Yu Kuang, Yu Deng, Yiqian Kong, Qingsha S. Cheng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper proposes a large language model (LLM)-embedded evolutionary framework for the wideband design of pixelized patch antennas. The proposed method integrates an LLM into multiple stages of a genetic algorithm (GA) to enable knowledge-driven evolution. Three embedding strategies are explored: (1) LLM-based parent selection, (2) LLM-based genetic operations, and (3) LLM-based GA optimizer. All three variants achieve notable improvement over the baseline GA in both convergence speed and bandwidth performance, with the fully LLM-driven version achieving the fastest optimization. The results demonstrate the potential of integrating LLM reasoning into evolutionary optimization as a promising pathway toward knowledge-guided antenna optimization.

title: An Open-Source Audiometer for Hearing Evaluation Using the Arduino Platform
authors: Elisabeth Alexiou, Vassilis Fotopoulos, Konstantinos Giannakopoulos
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost, Arduino-based audiometer that generates pure tones of selectable frequency and sound level and visualizes results both on the serial monitor and a 32×8 LED matrix. Built on an Arduino UNO R3 with PWM audio output and two push buttons, the system guides users via serial prompts to step through tests and displays left/right audiograms for immediate interpretation. The prototype covers 125–4000 Hz; sound level uses relative PWM levels rather than calibrated dB HL, so outcomes are qualitative. Pilot tests show normal thresholds in a 20-year-old and high-frequency decline with left–right asymmetry in a 50-year-old, consistent with presbycusis. Bone-conduction is not included, so loss type cannot be confirmed. The platform suits education and prototyping; future work targets calibration, extended frequencies, data logging, bone-conduction, and user interface (UI) refinement.

title: Charge injection for 2D electrical contact and its contact resistance
authors: Lay Kee Ang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper provides a short review of charge injection models for its transport across 2D materials based electrical contact (or 2D electrical contact) under DC condition. Three different mechanisms are reported, namely thermionic emission due to temperature or heating, field emission due to electron tunneling at barrier lowering, and photo-emission due to light-induced tunneling or multiphoton absorption. The traditional emission models are not valid for 2D electrical contact and the power scaling law in the pre-factor must be modified. For all three different mechanisms, universal scaling laws are obtained for both lateral and vertical 2D electrical contact. Using these charge injection models, we calculate the DC contact resistance which have shown better agreements in comparison to experimental measurements. Extension to other materials such as Rasbha material and contributions of lower energy valence band electrons will be shown. Preliminary results of AC contact resistance will be discussed.

title: The Talking Hands: A Comprehensive Survey and Framework for Real-Time Multilingual Sign Language Recognition with Integrated Natural Language Processing
authors: A. Shri Poornima, A. Sonali Mitra, R. Yuvanithi
abstract: Persistent communication asymmetries between the hearing-impaired community and the general population constitute a significant barrier to social inclusion, a challenge that is further exacerbated in linguistically diverse regions. To address this, we introduce “The Talking Hands,” an end-to-end real-time translation framework that synergizes computer vision, deep learning, and computational linguistics to convert manual gestures into synthesized speech. The proposed system deploys advanced webcam-based hand tracking algorithms to robustly localize and identify both unimanual and bimanual gestures across a spectrum of sign languages. These gesture sequences are analyzed using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) trained on comprehensive datasets comprising both alphabet and word-level lexicon. Distinct from conventional recognition-only approaches, this framework couples gesture classification with adaptive machine learning models for enhanced contextual understanding. Deployed as a browser-based web application, the system eliminates the requisite for local software installation, thereby ensuring ubiquitous accessibility across varying device form factors. The system's high-fidelity processing capabilities are designed to augment social interaction for individuals with auditory impairments. Furthermore, recognized gesture tokens are processed by a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) based Natural Language Processing (NLP) module, which reconstructs grammatically coherent sentences. These sentences are subsequently converted into audio via a multilingual Text-to-Speech (TTS) engine, supporting output in English, Hindi, Tamil, and Russian, with configurable voice profiles. This holistic platform aims to foster inclusive communication ecosystems and facilitate seamless interaction across multiple linguistic modalities.

title: Emotion Detection from Banglish Social Media Texts Using Transformer-Based Models
authors: Md. Mahmudul Hasan Rabbi, Sakib, Samiha Maisha Jeba, Jabunnesa Jahan Sara
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Emotion detection from text has become an important area in Natural Language Processing (NLP), enabling applications in social media monitoring, human-computer interaction, and mental health analysis. While significant progress has been made in resource-rich languages, Banglish (code-mixed Bangla-English) remains a low-resource and underexplored domain. In this work, we present an emotion detection framework for Banglish social media texts using transformer-based models. We constructed a humanannotated dataset of 10,000 Banglish texts labeled into five emotion categories: Joy, Anger, Sadness, Fear, and Surprise. Four transformer architectures—mBERT, BanglaBERT, XLM-RoBERTa, and RoBERTa-base—were fine-tuned individually, and their outputs were further combined using ensemble techniques based on majority and soft voting. Experimental results show that BanglaBERT achieved the highest singlemodel accuracy of 77.77%, while the four-model ensemble attained 77.76%, providing more stable predictions across classes. The findings demonstrate that transformer-based models, particularly languagespecific ones like BanglaBERT, are highly effective for handling the noisy, informal, and code-mixed nature of Banglish text. This study introduces one of the first transformer-based benchmarks for Banglish emotion detection and provides a foundation for future research in low-resource and code-mixed language processing.

title: Enhancing the Fraud Specificity of Deep Q-Network (DQN) Agents for Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Md. Nadim Sheikh, Rashedur M. Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study examines the recall value ascendancy of credit card fraud detection performed by Deep Q-Network agents, which is 86 % as opposed to 74 % using ensemble techniques like Random Forests. An enhanced DQN agent is suggested, which uses a two hidden layer neural network to update the target frequency and explicitly penalizes false negatives using an L1-based reward shaping linked to the temporal-difference (TD) error. Additionally, by learning from replay buffers utilizing Synthetic Minority Over Sampling Technique (SMOTE) base oversampling, the DQN agent shows improved adaptation to changing fraud patterns. This study provides insights into the creation of efficient DQN agents for fraud prevention and highlights the potential of Reinforcement Learning (RL) based techniques for increasing the probability of credit card fraud specificity.

title: Explainable AI for Heart Failure on AWS: A Scalable Big Data Approach
authors: D. D. R. M. Jayawardhena, Adeesha Peiris, J. P. Ranaweera, W. W. D. G. A. Perera, Achala Aponso
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Although heart failure is still a leading cause of morbidity and death, risk can be decreased by promptly identifying patients who are at high risk. We introduce a cloud-native, scalable pipeline for heart failure prediction that combines explainable machine learning with big-data processing. The architecture makes use of Apache Spark for distributed feature engineering and model training, along with Amazon Web Services—Amazon S3 for long-term storage, AWS Glue for schema discovery and ETL, and Amazon Athena for serverless querying. We compare three classifiers—Random Forest (RF), linear Support Vector Machine (SVM), and a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP)—under a uniform preprocessing regime (encoding, standardization, and vector assembly) using the publicly available UCI heart-failure dataset (n=918). On held-out data, models are assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and ROC-AUC. All of them achieve strong discrimination (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.91-0.92$</tex>), with MLP obtaining the highest AUC and RF producing the best balance of recall and F1. We use SHAP to quantify feature attributions, emphasizing physiologically plausible drivers (e.g., ST-segment depression/Oldpeak, ST-slope, chest-pain type, and MaxHR) to support clinical trust and decision-support. The findings show that transparent machine learning combined with cloud-first data engineering can produce accurate, repeatable, and cost-elastic heart failure risk stratification. They also offer a workable example for expanding to larger longitudinal EHRs and real-world implementation.

title: Experimental Assessment of Quantum PWM Performance in DC Motor Speed Control Applications
authors: Sohaib Saidat, Rami Bounekhata, Anis Lounes Tazerout, Abderraouf Kouici, Mehdi Fazilat, Mohamed Tadjine, Nadjet Zioui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Power electronics has played a critical role in improving motor drive operation during the last few decades. More precisely, the pulse-width modulation (PWM) and its derivatives have piqued the scientific community's interest due to their straightforward implementation. However, as precision and power quality requirements increased, techniques and approaches became more time-consuming and complex. The introduction of quantum computing proved to be a potential answer to many complicated issues that traditional computers are unable to tackle. The intrinsic quantum features of superposition, entanglement, and interference provide several possibilities to implement quantum and quantum-inspired solutions, which are transforming multiple engineering domains. This paper provides experimental validation of the quantum version of PWM, namely the QPWM. The method includes an inventive real number comparator. The experimental results show that the quantum technique surpasses its conventional counterpart in terms of precision and speedy convergence, but at the cost of slightly higher switching, which represents a step forward from the hardware- based implementation of quantum-inspired solutions, which have the potential to change a broad spectrum of technical and industrial domains.

title: Smart Gas Leak Detector: A Student Prototype for Safer Homes & Automotives
authors: Zeashan Hameed Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of an IoT-based gas leak detection system that uses fuzzy logic for the assessment of critical situation aimed at enhancing home and industrial safety. The system utilizes an MQ-2 gas sensor for detecting combustible gases, a DHT11 sensor for monitoring temperature, an OLED display for real-time feedback, and an ESP8266 microcontroller for wireless communication. An alert system using an LED indicator and potential mobile notification enhances user responsiveness in hazardous situations. The project was developed as part of the LearnOBots Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Math (STEAM) Summer Camp, where school & college (Age group: 12-17 years) students explored product design, hardware prototyping, and branding fundamentals to foster future entrepreneurship. This work highlights the effectiveness of hands-on STEM education in promoting innovation and problem-solving among young learners.

title: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Bias Detection in Bangla Job Advertisements
authors: Sayed Hanzala Abdullah, Sifat Samin Sarker, Mahmudul Hasan Badhan, Rayhan Mahmud Ansari, Ashraful Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Job advertisements often contain explicit or implicit discrimination that reinforces inequality in recruitment activities. While prior research has focused mainly on English texts, no systematic attempt has been made to study bias in Bangla job postings, which play a critical role in shaping labor-market access in Bangladesh. This study proposes a deep learning model to identify and categorize prejudices in the advertisements of jobs in Bangla language. A manually annotated corpus of 1536 sentences based on 200 job circulars was prepared and it is divided into five bias categories: masculine, feminine, age, experience and religious. Six architectures were considered, namely recurrent neural networks (LSTM, BiLSTM, GRU) and transformer-based ones (BanglaBERT, mBERT, XLM-RoBERTa). It is found that the transformer-based models significantly beat recurrent models, with MBERT showing the best results as far as test accuracy of 93.51% and F 1 -macro of 93.71% and minimum Hamming loss of 0.0138. The strength of the models in all categories of bias was also further validated by class-wise analysis. The results indicate that multilingual and language-specific transformer models are effective at detecting discriminatory patterns in Bengali advertisements and offering practical applications for fair recruitment monitoring and policy enforcement.

title: Deep Learning for Soil Type Classification: A Comparative Study of ResNet-34 and EfficientNetB0
authors: Noopur Tyagi, Monica Gupta, Chetan Jain, Jayashree Mohanty, Eshika Jain, Manik Rakhra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Accurate soil type classification is a critical component of precision agriculture, and it can help farmers make informed decisions about crop selection, irrigation management, and soil management. This work addresses the development of a deep learning-based structure for the classification of four types of soil, namely Alluvial, Black, Cinder, and Red soil, based on image data. A balanced dataset of 350+ annotated soil images was curated and pre-processed using advanced augmentation techniques like MixUp, CutMix, color jittering, and Gaussian blur to increase model generalizability. Two state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks (ResNet-34 and EfficientNetB0) were trained and evaluated comparatively. EfficientNetB0 was better than ResNet-34 with a higher accuracy for all samples (92.7%) and higher class-wise F1-scores, especially for Red and Alluvial soils. Training and validation performance measures, confusion matrices, and classification reports validate the robustness and consistency of EfficientNetB0. The results show the effectiveness of color-enhanced preprocessing, scaling of the model, and on-the-fly augmentation for improving the classification performance. This work lays the foundation for deploying lightweight and high-performing models for real-time agricultural monitoring systems to contribute to scalable and data-driven soil assessment strategies.

title: Sustainable Decision-Making for Mini-Wastewater Treatment Plants: A Review of Models, Engineering, and Management
authors: Abdullah Hassan, Mohammed Abdulrazzaq Alaghbari, Aawag Mohsen Alawag, Saleem Ahmed Al-Azazi, Maryam Althawadi, Ahmed Harouache
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Amidst a growing global water crisis, decentralized wastewater treatment plants (WWTPs) are emerging as critical infrastructure for sustainable water management. This paper critically reviews decision-making models for mini-WWTPs, structured around the pillars of decision-making frameworks, engineering perspectives, and management strategies. The review analyzes frameworks including Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA), sustainability assessments like Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), and data-driven Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) models, identifying a clear shift from economic-based decisions to holistic sustainability criteria. While MCDA offers structured stakeholder input and LCA provides quantitative evaluation, emerging AI/ML models show promise for real-time optimization but face data-scarcity challenges in small plants. The analysis highlights that optimal technology choice is a complex trade-off between advanced biological processes (e.g., MBBR, MBR), nature-based solutions (e.g., FCWs), operational complexity, and local socio-economic context. This paper proposes a novel integrated and adaptive framework that synthesizes these pillars to guide stakeholders through these trade-offs. Ultimately, sustainable management requires a holistic solution that balances robust engineering with viable economic and management strategies, rather than merely selecting the "best" technology in isolation.

title: Metaverse-Driven Customer Management: Reinforcement Learning Approaches for Sustainable Urban Engineering
authors: Nidhi Dua, V. Haripriya, Shruti Bajpai, G.I. Shamini, Nimesh Raj, Ashu Katyal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Smart City Customer Management leverages advanced digital technologies to enhance urban sustainability, efficiency, and resilience. By integrating reinforcement learning with the metaverse, an intelligent, immersive, and adaptive urban management system can be developed to optimize the use of city resources. There are a lot of bad things that happen to the environment when traditional Smart City Customer Management systems are used. These include not enough data integration, not enough promotion of urban sustainability, and limited real-time adaptive resource allocation. The objective of this research is to develop a digital twin architecture based on the metaverse, integrating Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) to evaluate and enhance urban resources. Cities could be able to profit from using self-organizing agents in areas like energy, transportation, and waste management, as this hypothesis suggests. These agents decide things based on the specific information they have. The method uses MARL-driven metaverse virtual situations to teach AI agents how to make decisions in the present. For this to work, the agents need to be able to talk to both real and fake things. You can do this with the system. The suggested method can help local governments be proactive and sustainable by cutting down on traffic, making energy use more efficient, and getting ready for changes in demand. The experiments' results show that using this model for real-time optimization could improve the health of city dwellers, the environment, and the way city resources are used. The framework does a better job of making the system more resistant to urban problems, improving its operating efficiency, and setting sustainability standards than older systems. The goal of this study is to show how the combination of metaverse technology and reinforcement learning could change the way customers are managed in smart cities in the future.

title: Applying Pretrained Vision-Language Models for Visual Question Answering and Answerability Prediction on VizWiz Dataset
authors: Mir Hasan Mohammad Ilham, Showrav Dhar, Mohammad Jobair Hasib Joy, Tanvir Azhar
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Visual Question Answering is an application of multimodal AI that can be leveraged to provide automated real time responses to visual questions asked by blind people using assistive technologies. Answerability Prediction of a visual question determines whether the image-question pair contains enough information and is legible enough to generate an answer. In this study, we apply six pretrained vision-language models on the VizWiz-VQA dataset for Visual Question Answering and Answerability Prediction tasks. We benchmark the performance of the models in these two tasks and provide a detailed comparative analysis of the results. The best performing vision-language models were ensembled using a weighted logit averaging strategy. Our model ensemble outperformed the baseline used in this study for the VQA task and obtained <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 4. 0 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy on the VizWiz test-dev split and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 3. 2 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy on test-std. We also obtain competitive scores for the AP task with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 2. 5 4 \%}$</tex> average precision on test-dev and 82.02% average precision on test-std.

title: Fault Detection in Transmission Line Connected to Grid-Forming Inverter Using Variational Mode Decomposition and Chaos Metric
authors: Behrooz Taheri, Seyed Amir Hosseini, Hossein Askarian-Abyaneh, Mehdi Mohammadi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources and grid-Forming IBRs in power systems, rapid fault detection and classification in the lines connected to these sources is of critical importance. In this paper, a novel protection algorithm based on the combination of Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and chaos theory is presented. In this method, the current and voltage signals of each phase are combined before decomposition to include the dynamic and non-linear content of both quantities in a single signal. The combined signal is then decomposed into its intrinsic components by VMD, and the index mode is selected for chaos analysis. Using logistic mapping, the chaos metric for each phase is extracted and compared with predetermined thresholds, leading to fault detection and classification. The studied network is implemented in the PSCAD environment with a Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) controller, and the proposed protection algorithm is implemented in MATLAB software. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm is able to detect and classify single-phase-to-ground, two-phase, two-phase-to-ground, and three-phase faults with high accuracy across different locations and impedances. The combination of VMD and chaos theory increases the sensitivity and accuracy of the algorithm in transient and steady-state network conditions, making this method an effective solution for protecting IBRbased power systems.

title: CyStoneNet: Pseudo-Color Enhanced SE-Attention MobileNetV2 for Kidney Stone and Cyst Detection
authors: Jerin Sultana, Joyita Sen, Nowshin Tasnim, Mohammed Mahmudur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Kidney disease is a serious health concern and a leading cause of death worldwide. Stones and cysts are the most common causes of kidney damage; therefore, early identification is essential to improve patient satisfaction. As a result, automated diagnostic systems are becoming crucial in assisting clinical judgment. Many neural network techniques have shown potential in the early prediction of kidney disease, especially transfer learning. However, existing research faces challenges such as inadequate preprocessing, suboptimal feature extraction, high computational complexity, and extended training times. To address these limitations, this article proposed a lightweight framework for the detection of kidney disease from computed tomography scans. By applying pseudo-color modification to grayscale CT scans, the technique improves textural and spatial information that is sometimes missed in the conventional grayscale format. Moreover, the MobileNetV2 architecture is incorporated with a squeeze-and-excitation (SE) attention mechanism to adaptively highlight the most discriminative channel-wise features. The proposed model required only 2.80 minutes for training and 0.03 minutes for testing on a dataset of 745 CT images collected from multiple hospitals in Chittagong, Bangladesh, achieving 99% accuracy. Owing to its lightweight design, the framework provides a fast and cost-effective solution for detecting kidney stones and cysts. Assisting radiologists in early and accurate diagnosis, it can support timely treatment and improve patient outcomes. This study is among the first to integrate pseudo-color enhancement with a regularized SE-attention-based lightweight framework for kidney disease detection.

title: Between Control and Safety: Purpose-Driven Location Sharing Experiences in Bangladesh
authors: Rabius Sani Jabiullah, Prianka Akter, Khandakar Nur Md. Shakil
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Purpose-driven location sharing is central to digital life in Bangladesh, yet everyday practices are shaped by uneven access to privacy controls and heightened concerns about misuse. We conducted an online survey (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=44$</tex>) and semi-structured interviews <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{n}=\mathrm{5})$</tex> with Bangladeshi smartphone users to examine how they share location information, manage control, and navigate trust across gender identities. Participants relied on informal strategies—such as typing addresses or using images—due to usability gaps in existing tools. Once shared, location data often becomes difficult to retract, which they perceived as increasing their vulnerability to screenshots, long-term storage, or unauthorized reuse. Gender shaped experiences of comfort and safety, with participants across identities adopting additional caution while still expressing discomfort and fear of misuse. As an exploratory study with a small sample, our findings offer qualitative insights into socio-technical challenges surrounding purpose-driven location sharing. We highlight opportunities for designing more context-aware, trust-sensitive, and genderinclusive location-sharing systems.

title: Exploring the Interplay between Public Trust and Policy Regulation in the Adoption of Artificial Intelligence for National Security in the Arab World
authors: Rashed Alrasheed, Nahla Elshorbagy, Mohamed Albuarki, Saleh Almerri, Meshari Alotaibi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study investigates public trust and regulatory readiness in the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) for national security across Arab countries. As AI becomes central to cybersecurity strategies, concerns about legal adequacy and civic engagement intensify. The research aims to identify factors influencing trust in AI, evaluate national legal frameworks, and explore the role of citizen participation in policy-making. Using a descriptive quantitative method, data from 572 respondents across diverse Arab states were analyzed. Findings reveal strong belief in AI’s cybersecurity potential. Over half of participants support citizen involvement to enhance transparency and trust. The study highlights the need for inclusive governance, and public education to align AI innovation with

title: Wideband Differentially Fed Dual-Polarized Planar Antenna Array Based on Metal Integrated Suspended Lines
authors: Zhenzuo Chen, Jiayu Wang, Yang You, Yunlong Lu, Yi Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: A wideband differentially fed dual-polarized planar antenna array based on metal integrated suspended lines is proposed in this paper. The antenna array integrates compact quad-ridge waveguide radiation cavities with a pair of differential feeding networks to achieve wide impedance matching and high port isolation. The quad-ridge waveguide provides a smooth impedance transition and suppresses higher-order modes, while the differentially fed couplers ensure single-ended-to-differential signal conversion. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> prototype array is designed and optimized to operate from 10 to 15 GHz. Simulation results show that the proposed antenna array achieves a -10 dB impedance bandwidth of 40%, port isolation better than 37.3 dB, and crosspolarization discrimination exceeding 57.6 dB, while maintaining an antenna efficiency above 82% and stable radiation patterns across the entire band. The proposed design provides an effective approach for high-performance wideband dual-polarized antenna arrays.

title: BanSarcMeme: A Multimodal Dataset and Baselines for Sarcasm Detection in Low-Resource Bengali Language
authors: Sumaiya Akter Badhon, Ayesha Khanam, Zaima Sartaj Taheri, Fahim Shakil Tamim, Md Nazir Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Sarcasm, a subtle linguistic phenomenon where intended meaning diverges from literal expression, remains a challenging problem in natural language processing due to its reliance on contextual, cultural, and cross-modal cues. The difficulty is further amplified in resource-constrained languages such as Bengali, particularly within multimodal content like memes, where irony emerges from the interplay between textual and visual information. To address this, we introduce a novel multimodal dataset BanSarcMeme comprising 5,000 Bengali memes, annotated as sarcastic or non-sarcastic. Our work systematically investigates sarcasm detection using unimodal (text-only and image-only) and multimodal approaches. For textual analysis, models such as LSTM, BERT, mBERT, Bangla-BERT, and XLM-Roberta are evaluated, while visual understanding is performed using architectures such as VGG19, ViT, and ResNet50. Ultimately, our multimodal framework leveraging CLIP, which aligns visual and textual representations in a shared embedding space, achieves the best performance with an F1-score of 88.89, underscoring the superiority of cross-modal alignment for Bengali multimodal sarcasm detection.

title: Optimized Glass Core Substrate as a DBC Alternative
authors: Amir Murtadha Mohamad Yussof, Puteri Nur Aisyah Abd Rahim, Mohd Faizol Abdullah, Badariah Bais, Norazreen Abd Aziz
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Direct bond copper (DBC) substrate is one of the core components in power module packaging. This article proposed an alternative for the common DBC of Cu/AlN/Cu with an optimized Cu/glass/Al configuration. The total 6000 datasets for a total of six unique substrate configurations are calculated for their in-plane (k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf>) and out-of-plane (k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</inf>) thermal conductivities using analytical calculation. Although Cu/AlN/Cu is found as the best heat conductor, the Cu/glass/Al is acceptably good with anisotropic heat conduction characteristic of k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf> = 326.4 Wm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</inf> = 8.9 Wm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, and k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf>/k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</inf> = 36.6. The optimum thickness of the Cu top layer, glass core, and Al bottom layer are 1.0 mm, 0.2 mm, and 0.1 mm, respectively. The verification using Icepak simulation suggests k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf> = 301.9 Wm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</inf> = 9.2 Wm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, and k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf>/k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</inf> = 32.9. The discrepancy in k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf> is remarkable when the thickness of system components becomes imbalanced. These findings give insight into the performance of Cu/glass/Al substrate as a substitution for DBC, but more studies are required to understand the mechanical reliability of this three-layer lamination.

title: An ML-Assisted Recommender for Multi-Shape Patch Antenna Design
authors: Pavan Mohan Neelamraju, William Whittow, Aakash Bansal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: Designing patch antennas across diverse geometries and feed configurations typically relies on shape-specific formulae, extensive sweeps, or heuristic optimizers, which do not scale when the shape and feed must be chosen jointly. We present a learning-assisted design framework that reframes patch-antenna synthesis as a frequency-driven recommendation problem, augmenting full-wave electromagnetic simulation with a data-driven recommender: given a target frequency, the system proposes a patch shape (from 13 canonical geometries) and feed type, predicts geometry parameters, and estimates key performance indicators (e.g., <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex>, VSWR, gains) via a learned frequency-response surrogate trained on full-wave CST data. The models are trained on a large CST dataset <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(>15,600$</tex> simulated designs) spanning 1-20 GHz with multiple feed placements per shape. The pipeline comprises: (i) a shape+feed selector, (ii) a multi-output geometry regressor, and (iii) a performance predictor used to screen candidates before simulation. On heldout frequencies, the recommender maintains consistent top-1 accuracy for shape+feed selection, the geometry predictor attains low relative error, and predicted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> closely tracks full-wave results across the frequency band. We report ablations against direct formula-based sizing and Genetic Algorithm (GA)/ Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) sweeps, and discuss runtime and data-efficiency aspects relevant to integration in EM design flow. The proposed framework establishes a new early-stage synthesis paradigm by explicitly learning shape and feed selection prior to full-wave simulation, and is readily extensible to generative and cross-shape transfer modules in future work.

title: Mental Health Sign Recognition in Bengali Text: An Optimized Deep Learning Transformer Model for Imbalanced Classification
authors: Md Nihad Hossain, Asif Hasan Tonmoy, Dip Karmakar, Mashkurul Alam Ohi, Maliha Tasmim, Md. Khalid Syfullah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Mental health disorders are becoming more common around the world, making early detection important. While Natural Language Processing (NLP) and deep learning have been widely used to study mental health in different languages, research in Bengali is still limited, even though it's a widely spoken language. This study works to close that gap by creating a complete system to detect mental health conditions from Bengali text. We built a diverse dataset with 27,031 samples taken from social media and translated English content, manually labeled into 12 different mental health conditions through a careful process involving multiple annotators and majority voting. Our method included detailed preprocessing, feature extraction, and model building. We tested two pre-trained BERT-based models (csebuetnlp BanglaBERT and sagorsarker bangla-bert-base), which got F1 scores of 58.01 % and 55.21 % respectively. Our six-stacked transformer model(TransMentalNet-6) with tuned hyperparameters clearly performed better, reaching a 66.35 % F1 score. Even though the dataset was very imbalanced and mental health language is complex, our approach showed strong improvements in classification. This research provides useful resources and methods for Bengali mental health analysis, which can help support early care and treatment in Bengali-speaking communities.

title: Design of a Wheeled Robotic System for Autonomous Platooning Applications
authors: Trygve Eggen, Cheol-Hong Min, Hassan Salamy
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Wireless sensor networks (WSN) in conjunction with the Internet of Things (IoT) have allowed for distributed robotic systems with continually-increasing sensing capabilities, interconnectivity, and, in turn, the capability to perform complex tasks in unpredictable environments. This paper presents the design of a mobile platooning robotic vehicle system. These low-cost robotic platforms use one-way communication from the leader to follower robots to convey the leader's velocity and operating state via Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) 5.2. The platform presented in this paper is designed for cost-effectiveness and expandability. Each robot uses an Arduino Nano 33 BLE as a microcontroller and a bi-motor differential drive. Sensing is achieved using wheel encoders and an inertial measurement unit. This information is processed on the leader robot using a sensor fusion algorithm and transmitted via BLE to the follower robots. The follower uses the received information to control its traction and follow the motion set forth by the leader robot.

title: AI and IoT Convergence for Smart Environments: Integrating Machine and Quantum Learning for Renewable Energy and Air Quality Monitoring
authors: Marowa Jahan, MD. Ridwan Al Mustavy, MD. Mobashir Tajuare Partho, MD. Helal Uddin, Somiya Afroz
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Climate change presents urgent challenges requiring innovative computational and monitoring solutions. Machine Learning (ML) has demonstrated significant potential in optimizing renewable energy systems, while Quantum Machine Learning (QML) offers next-generation computational capabilities for climate modeling, forecasting, and decarbonization. Complementing these, the Internet of Things (IoT) enables real-time environmental monitoring across domains such as air quality, water resources, and waste management. This paper synthesizes insights from recent advances in ML, QML, and IoT, highlighting their convergence as a powerful framework for climate change mitigation and sustainability. We present applications in renewable energy optimization, environmental monitoring, hazard prediction, and policy support, alongside challenges of scalability, data quality, and hardware limitations. Finally, we propose a unified research agenda for integrating ML, QML, and IoT into climate action strategies.

title: Fuzzy Distance-Based Ensemble for Mosquito Species Classification on Human Skin — An Explainable Deep Learning Approach
authors: Al-Amin Ahmed Shanto, Sahajuddin Ahmed, Rokaiya Zaman Suthi, Fahima Khanam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Mosquito-borne illnesses continue to pose a serious threat to public health, and successful vector surveillance and management depend on precise species identification. Manual, labor-intensive, and prone to mistakes are traditional identification methods, particularly when mosquitoes are found in deteriorated states like shattered or partially destroyed. In order to close this gap, an automatic categorization framework for three mosquito species in two visual states—landing and smashed—is developed in this study. By combining three pre-trained convolutional neural networks—ResNeSt-50d, MobileNetV2, and EfficientNetV2-RW-S—using a fuzzy distance-based ensemble strategy, the suggested approach makes use of transfer learning. To improve the robustness of predictions, the ensemble incorporates Cosine, Manhattan, and Euclidean similarity measures. To improve generalization, a carefully selected dataset of 1,500 photos from Kaggle was preprocessed and enhanced. Standardized optimization procedures were then used to train the models. With an accuracy of 98.33 %, the ensemble surpasses individual backbones, according to experimental data, which also show gains in precision, recall, and F1-score. The interpretability of the model is further confirmed by visualization using Grad-CAM. By providing a scalable tool for automated vector surveillance, These finding demonstrate the promise of fuzzy ensemble learning for entomological picture processing and potentially improve public health responses to outbreaks of diseases spread by mosquitoes.

title: SKINALYZE: An AI-Driven Framework for News Analysis and Customer Sentiment Analysis in Sri Lanka's Pharmaceutical Skincare Industry
authors: Karshi Thavarajah, Karishani Bamunawita
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The rapid growth of information technology has influenced multiple industries and increased the economy of the countries. The usage and adaptation of advanced technologies increases the industry's impact on the country's economy and positively improves the revenue of the users. On the other hand, the Pharmaceutical Industry is one of the main sources of economic growth in the country, especially in Sri Lanka's pharma skincare industry plays a major role in the country's income, because in Sri Lanka, the demand for skincare products has increased due to maintaining a healthy lifestyle and concern about physical appearance. Developed countries and developing countries use AI and ML to develop their business through automation and analyse past data collection, which impacts on the future. The system was constructed with Firebase for real-time NoSQL database management, Next.js for server-side rendering, and React for the frontend. Based on pre-defined keywords stored in Firebase, Skinalyze incorporates real-time news collecting via the NewsAPI and uses a pre-trained natural language processing model, “Xenova/distilbart-cnn-6-6”, to automate the summary. The “TextBlob” model, which is implemented through a Flask API, is used to analyze the sentiment of customer reviews. RapidAPI is used to obtain data. Chart.js and D3.js are used to represent this processed information in the dashboard, allowing for comparison of product reception, efficient scope evaluation, and sentimentbased strategic decision-making.

title: Framework for Implementing Green Supply Chain Management Practices in Sri Lankan Healthcare Sector
authors: Pubudini Jayasekara, Chathumi Kavirathna, Chamoda Paranawithana
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The public healthcare sector in Sri Lanka, comprising more than 550 government hospitals, plays a critical role in delivering accessible healthcare services but faces growing environmental challenges, including inefficient resource utilization, poor waste management, and limited adoption of sustainable practices. Green Supply Chain Management (GSCM) offers potential solutions through practices such as green procurement, energy-efficient logistics, and waste minimization; however, implementation within Sri Lankan public hospitals remains limited due to low awareness, financial constraints, weak policy enforcement, and the absence of a context-specific framework. This study identifies relevant GSCM practices, key barriers, and readiness assessment parameters through a systematic literature review conducted using the PRISMA methodology, synthesizing 52 peer-reviewed studies. A mixedmethod approach was adopted, combining thematic analysis with quantitative data collected via a five-point Likert-scale questionnaire administered to hospital administrators and logistics personnel. Data was analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) and an ordinal readiness assessment scale. The study proposes a validated implementation framework, a readiness assessment model, and practical recommendations to support sustainable supply chain transformation in Sri Lanka's public healthcare sector.

title: ToxiFusion: A Hybrid Semantic–Sequential Neural Architecture for Multi-Label Toxic Comment Classification
authors: Sourav Saha, Soumik Ghosh, K M Nafi Asib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Despite being the seventh-largest spoken language in the world, Bengali remains lightly explored in the field of toxic text detection compared to other high-resource languages. The growing influence of social media platforms has facilitated the emergence of toxic text content largely because of the anonymity they offer. This serves as a major factor in the development of effective toxic text detection methods in Bengali. This work presents a comprehensive benchmarking study on a manually annotated Bengali dataset, evaluating a wide array of models ranging from traditional machine learning to modern transformer architectures. Our experiments demonstrate that transformer-based models significantly outperform other approaches. Specifically, a fusion model combining BanglaBERT <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{\text{large }}$</tex> with a Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (Bi-GRU) classifier achieved state-of-the-art performance, yielding a weighted precision of 0.91, recall of 0.92, and a weighted F1-score of 0.92. The strong results from multiple transformers, including MBERT, MuRIL, and IndicBERT, further confirm their efficacy for this critical natural language processing task in a low-resource language context.

title: Dual Polarized Array Antenna with Diagonal Positioned Bubble Slots Using Both-Sided MIC Technology for X-Band Applications
authors: Md. Motahar Hossain, Showrajit Saha, Debprosad Das, Md. Azad Hossain, Nguyen Khac Kiem, Nguyen Hoang Hai
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This work introduces a novel design of a both-sided Microwave Integrated Circuit (MIC) structure implemented with bubble shaped-slot perturbations to achieve dual polarization (linear and circular) at X-band applications. The proposed antenna employs a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> array configuration, where the insertion of diagonally positioned bubble-shaped slots technique enables the excitation of two orthogonal modes in quadrature at the desired frequency. The proposed antenna is placed in a 0.8 mm thick Teflon substrate, incorporating a 0.2 mm wide slot line in the metallic ground. At the center of the design, the slot line is combined with a parallel microstrip line on the substrate to form a hybrid junction The proposed array exhibits excellent performance, with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> of -27. dB at 10.1 GHz and an impedance bandwidth spanning <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.73-10.25 \text{GHz}$</tex>, corresponding to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$515 \text{MHz}(5.09 \%)$</tex> within the X-band spectrum. Simulation results show that the antenna achieves a 3 dB axial ratio (AR) band for left-hand circular polarization (LHCP) between 9.86 and 9.99 GHz, with the minimum AR reaching 0.11 dB at 9.93 GHz. Outside this range, the antenna operates in linear polarization (LP). At the resonant frequency of 10.1 GHz, the antenna achieves a broadside gain of 24.52 dB at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\theta=0^{\circ}$</tex>. The array further provides a peak gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 8 9}$</tex> dBic and a radiation efficiency of approximately 95.5 %, ensuring robust radiation characteristics. The structure is simulated using Advanced Design System (ADS). The results confirm that the proposed design offers an efficient and compact solution for X-band applications, including radar, satellite communication, UAVs and high-frequency wireless systems.

title: A Two-Stage Fairness-Aware and Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Monkeypox and Confounding Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Md. Ahsanul Karim Abir, Md. Nasif Osman Khansur, Boshir Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Monkeypox (Mpox) has visual similarity with other viral skin diseases, and thus it is difficult to accurately diagnose them based on the images. The paper outlines an elucidable deep learning system integrating fairness-conscious preprocessing, attention-based classification, and lesion-centric segmentation. The Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) is used to equalize the light and reduce the bias of the skin-tone. An EfficientNetB0 binary model firstly divides between the cases of health and illness, and Grad-CAM heatmaps give the localization of lesions and the evidence that can be interpreted. Such lesion-directed inputs together with original images are categorized with an attention-enhanced ResNet50 with Channel, Soft, and Squeeze-and-Excitation modules. A test on the MSLD v2.0 data reveals an accuracy of 99.50% on six classes, which outperforms other studies of monkeypox classification. The findings indicate that contrast normalization + Grad-CAM guidance + attention mechanisms constitute a powerful, interpretable, and computationally efficient pipeline that can be applied in the field of dermatology.

title: Exploring Disease Mutation Patterns: A Deep Learning Approach for Mapping Mutations to Diseases
authors: Kaushik Biswas, Fariya Sultana Prity, Muhammad Hasibur Rashid Chayon
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Genetic mutations are important indications of disease, assisting in diagnosis and treatment planning. However, most existing mutation classification methods focused on specific diseases, trained on small or synthetic datasets, and have significant misclassification rates. Such constraints limit performance and scalability, especially in real-world clinical settings. To address these, we propose an enhanced BioBERT model that requires only mutation sequence as input for multiclass disease classification. This makes the prediction pipeline much simpler. The model uses BioBERT as a backbone to understand biological sequences and multi-scale convolutional layers to capture different mutation patterns. A feature fusion process and a classification head improve decision-making capabilities. Compared to the baseline BioBERT, our version achieves a reduced parameter count of 49.5 M and approximately (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2 \mathrm{x}$</tex>) faster training, providing greater efficiency without sacrificing performance. We trained and validated our model on the large-scale, real-world ClinVar dataset by utilizing 424,899 samples from 95 disease categories. The proposed architecture obtained 92.83% accuracy and outperformed existing methods in terms of scalability and generalization. Ultimately, this provides an automated and efficient way to map mutations to diseases, reducing manual effort and misclassification risks, and offers a clinically applicable framework that can support medical professionals with accurate diagnosis and scalable mutation analysis.

title: Independent Coordinate Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks: Exploiting Structural Constraints for Dimensionality Reduction
authors: TzeHoung Lee, Suthawan Prukumpai, Patrick Ghali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: When input features satisfy known structural constraints, some features become dependent on others through deterministic relationships. We introduce Independent Coordinate Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (IC-KAN), a principled framework that identifies minimal independent coordinate sets and learns exclusively from them. Given P features satisfying k independent constraints, only $d=P-k$ coordinates are algebraically independent. IC-KAN extracts these independent coordinates and learns in $\mathbb{R}^{d}$ rather than $\mathbb{R}^{P}$, fundamentally changing the dimensionality of the learning problem. We prove that sample complexity scales with intrinsic dimension d rather than observed dimension P, achieving convergence rate $O\left(n^{-2 \alpha /(2 \alpha+d)}\right)$ versus $O\left(n^{-2 \alpha /(2 \alpha+P)}\right)$ for standard methods. Furthermore, we prove that parameter efficiency gains are p times greater for KANs than MLPs, where p is the number of spline coefficients per univariate function. On equity return prediction where accounting identities create dependencies between financial ratios, IC-KAN achieves $3.2 \%$ out-of-sample $R^{2}$ using 6 independent coordinates versus 2.6% for standard KAN using 8 features, while requiring 30% fewer parameters. The framework applies broadly to any domain with explicit structural constraints.

title: GeoCaricature: A Landmark-Guided Thin Plate Spline Transformer Framework for Geometric Anamorphic Facial Caricature Generation
authors: Md. Imrul Kayes, Abdullah Al Mubin, Kaushik Deb
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Generating a definitive facial caricature is challenging, since it requires authenticity, humour, and skill. In this study, we mainly aim to propose a controlled approach that is able to produce pre-eminent, realistic, grotesque, hyperbolic portraits from empirical images by only applying computer vision techniques. In our approach, Thin-Plate-Spline Transformer (TPSTransformer) manipulates the image by implementing geometric transmutations via spatial transformations. Initially, employing a controlled noise application to augment certain landmark regions with regularisation led to stable and effective outputs. The source points are identified using a facial landmark, and noise is added separately to each of them to achieve the target points that maintain a structured embellishment. Finally, integrate the output with the original image by blending their details at multiple spatial frequencies. Hence, the tableau preserves 76.64% of the visage attributes and characteristics of the pristine image, which is crucial for precise identification. Moreover, a survey indicated that the proposed approach received 54 of 100 votes. All of the investigations were undertaken on the WebCaricature dataset. The proposed algorithm can address the limitations of earlier methods, such as over-distortion or travesty, by effectively rendering accessories like spectacles and managing non-frontal face images. In addition, we enacted a comprehensive commensuration of our proposed approach with other premier caricature models, which demonstrated the potential effectiveness of our methodology.

title: Circularly Polarized Twisted Quad-Ridged Horn Antenna for In-Band Full-Duplex
authors: Uros Jankovic, Rawad Asfour, Dariush Mirshekar-Syahkal, Djuradj Budimir
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: A circular horn antenna for in-band full-duplex communication with a quadruplet of equidistant ridges twisted to follow conical spiral contours is presented. Twisted ridges, apart from the role of extending the bandwidth, also act as polarizers. Both orthogonal linear polarizations of the ridged waveguide feed, representing transmitter and receiver sides with zero cross coupling between them in the ideal case, are transformed into the same circular polarization determined by the ridge twist direction and vice versa. This gives rise to fullduplex capability without sacrificing additional resources like different polarizations in the communication channel. For antenna designed for the frequency band 20–40 GHz, full-wave electromagnetic simulation shows Tx-Rx isolation greater that 70 dB, return loss greater than 21 dB, while boresight axial ratio as well as gain are respectively less than 2.3 dB and between 10.9 and 13.8 dBi.

title: A Fuzzy-Based Safety Analysis Approach for Air Traffic Management Systems
authors: Emine Busra Akdemir, Ihsan Kaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: With the accelerating effects of globalization, international trade and travel demands have substantially increased, resulting in a significant rise in air traffic density. This growing traffic volume has made air traffic management (ATM) processes more complex, amplifying the likelihood of collisions and human errors, and thereby placing flight safety at the forefront of aviation priorities. In this context, this study proposes a fuzzy-based safety analysis approach that consists of Decomposed Fuzzy Sets (DFSs) and Z-numbers for ATM systems. DFSs offer a refined mechanism for modeling uncertainties in risk evaluations, while Z-numbers facilitate the simultaneous representation of both the degree of uncertainty and the reliability of the associated information. Furthermore, the proposed approach integrates these representations into Functional Hazard Assessment (FHA) and Fault Tree Analysis (FTA), two well-established safety analysis techniques in aviation, in order to enhance the reliability of hazard identification and risk quantification. The integration of these approaches is expected to facilitate a more comprehensive and realistic framework for safety analysis, ultimately supporting decision-makers in managing risks more effectively under conditions of high traffic complexity.

title: Autonomous Optimization Method for Plasma Etching Physical Model Parameters based on Integrated Neural Network
authors: Hongli Song, Yuxuan Zhai, Ziyi Hu, Junjie Li, Rui Chen, Ling Li
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Plasma etching plays an increasingly critical role in advanced integrated circuit manufacturing, while etching modeling has attracted more attention for process tuning. Conventional plasma etching models rely heavily on empirical parameters that are difficult to acquire accurately, limiting their reliability and applicability. To address the multi-objective optimization problem of physical parameters in plasma etching modeling, this paper proposes an integrated neural network based Bayesian optimization method. Taking key etching reaction parameters as optimization variables, the method achieves efficient optimization of physical parameters. Experimental validation shows that the simulation results derived from the optimized parameters are in good agreement with experimental data, and more accurate physical parameters are obtained. Compared with the Gradient Descent method, the proposed optimization approach achieves a 7.18× speedup in optimization time while yielding a smaller loss function. This method enables possibilities of accurate and efficient calibration of plasma etching models.

title: A YOLO-Based Multi-Stage Framework for Bangladeshi License Plate Extraction and Recognition
authors: Shuva Podder, Md. Foisal Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a complete pipeline for Automatic License Plate Recognition (ALPR) system tailored for vehicles in Bangladesh, with specific emphasis on handling skewed license plates. The proposed system achieves a mean Average Precision of 98.43% in license plate detection and 97.82% in character recognition. The framework employs the YOLOv11 model for both license plate detection and recognition, ensuring a unified and efficient deep learning approach suitable for realtime applications. The proposed system in this paper is capable of extracting the complete license plate number of a vehicle with high accuracy. In the first stage, license plate regions are extracted from vehicle images or video frames using a YOLOv11based detection model. These detected regions are further refined through an efficient preprocessing pipeline that integrates rescaling, filtering, thresholding, and geometric correction techniques to improve recognition performance, particularly for tilted or skewed license plates. In the second stage, the enhanced plates are processed by a YOLOv11-based recognition model optimized for Bangla alphanumeric characters, which predicts characters with high reliability. Additionally, a sorting technique is applied to assemble the predicted characters into the complete license plate number. The detection dataset contains 1,844 annotated vehicle images, while the recognition dataset consists of 2,602 annotated character images covering diverse conditions such as illumination changes, occlusions, and angled views.

title: EfficientNetB4-Based Deep Learning Approach for Accurate Classification of Maize Leaf Diseases in Precision Agriculture
authors: Shinnu Jangra, Sweety Sehgal, Jasvinder Kumar, Jayashree Mohanty, Yashasvi Saini, Manish Kumar Singla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research leverages the EfficientNetB4 architecture, known for its high accuracy and computational efficiency, to classify maize leaf diseases into four categories: Crown Rust, Gray Leaf Spot, Blight, and Healthy. For this study, a pre-processed and augmented dataset of 4,188 maize leaf images from Kaggle was used to improve the model’s performance. To achieve a balanced assessment, the data were partitioned into training (70%), validation (15%), and testing (15%). The model achieved an overall accuracy of 85.26% on the test set, and all class F1-scores were high, which means that the model is quite reliable. However, the confusion matrix showed that there was strong classification since there was some misclassification in Gray Leaf Spot. This work shows how deep learning can be applied in the management of diseases in agriculture. Future work will include collecting more data, integrating disease detection into mobile applications, and integrating explainable AI to provide recommendations for precision farming. This new system has the potential to transform sustainable maize farming.

title: Improving Multiclass SVM Classification Using a Hybrid Alternating Decision Strategy
authors: Tharmaraj Thayatharsan, Amirthalingam Ramanan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Multiclass classification remains a significant challenge in machine learning, particularly for Support Vector Machines (SVMs), which are inherently binary classifiers. Traditional strategies, such as One-Versus-All (OVA) and One-Versus-One (OVO), provide baseline solutions but present notable drawbacks: OVA suffers from overlapping decision boundaries and class imbalance during training, while OVO incurs quadratic growth in computational cost. Advanced frameworks such as Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) and Unbalanced Decision Tree (UDT) improve testing efficiency but remain susceptible to error propagation, which is particularly problematic when datasets exhibit overlapping class boundaries. To address these challenges, this work proposes AOV (Alternating OVA and OVO) SVMs, a novel hybrid architecture that alternates between OVA and OVO classifiers across successive decision levels, prioritising easily distinguishable classes at higher levels via confidencebased dynamic reordering. The proposed architecture requires only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lfloor(3 N-4) / 2\rfloor+N-1$</tex> classifiers, more than OVA's linear <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N$</tex>, but substantially fewer than OVO and DAG's quadratic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N(N-1) / 2$</tex> and UDT's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(N(N+1) / 2)-1$</tex> complexity. Experiments conducted on seven UCI benchmark datasets (Iris, Wine, Glass, Vowel, DNA, Isolet, Letter), using both Linear and RBF kernels, demonstrate that the proposed method achieves classification accuracy and maintains consistent performance across varying dataset characteristics, making it a scalable and accurate solution for multiclass classification problems.

title: Innovations in Process Technology Enabling Next Generation Memory Solutions
authors: Rinus T.P. Lee, Sarah Lynch, John Grasso, Sara Aoki, Tuhin Basu, David O’Meara, Jarron Maguire, Yixin Qin, Evan Sweet, Rio Soedibyo, Kandabara Tapily, Dina Triyoso, Hirokazu Aizawa, Cory Wajda
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, we review recent innovations in process technology for next generation memory solutions. These include high-κ dielectrics, ferroelectrics, novel DRAM device concepts, emerging memory technologies and non von Neumann computing architectures. We will also present our results and discuss them within the context of these recent innovations.
